text:
I was so excited to see that I got 10% off my total just from checking-in at Oceana Grill so I HAD to give them a review.

It's hard to find decent food on bourbon street, so after failing miserably at a previous hole in the wall, this place definitely made up for it.

Oceana Grill was packed, but we didn't have a problem finding a table for three. I ordered the taste of New Orleans and it was great because it came with red beans and rice, jambalaya and crawfish étouffée. 

Good food, music and ambiance.

ANOTHER WARNING for parties of SIX OR MORE - the TWENTY ONE PERCENT MANDATORY GRATUITY is REAL! Though the food was fine, I found it extremely inconsiderate NOT to be notified of the charge by the front desk and to have that gratuity warning on small print at the bottom of a page in their menu. Teams and friend groups, just please be careful - don't even bother going in groups of at least 6 especially if you can get less expensive Indian food of comparable quality elsewhere.

Qachbal's Chocolatier is part of downtown Ybor located inside a convience store. I almost walked past it if it weren't for the sign on the sidewalk in the front of the store. 

None of the treats tasted appealing or made me want to take another bite. I was really disappointed because it looks good and appealing to the eyes, but once you take a bite, it transforms the experience into a downhill slope.  

There was nothing special about the Banana Bread. It's your standard banana bread with none of the ingredients jumping out and it was too dry!

The Croissant wasn't moist like traditional ones I've had and missing usual buttery taste. Sadly, I couldn't finish it.

The Guava and Cheese lacked authenticity and wasn't close to what I'm used to. It was just okay.

The Iced Coffee was the only good thing. The flavor of the coffee came through and wasn't  diluted by the milk. It was the best thing we got and it was nice walking outside with a cold drink.

Maybe we should have tried their chocolates, but it puzzles me if that is what they're known for, why provide other treats if they're not at the same caliber? I read good reviews for the chocolate and the pastries detract from what they're good at making. With it being so hot outside, we didn't want to eat chocolate that would be melting so fast.

I can't see them lasting much longer because nothing tasted memorable or made me want to return. I'm sorry to say, but I had high hopes. I wasn't impressed and the best reaction to describe my experience was, "Meh." I've definitely had a lot of better pastries elsewhere. It was unremarkable.

Really cute place in the old New Orleans. Food is really good and tasty. Flavor combinations are great and all tasted fresh. Great experience when visiting NOLA. But for sure it is a touristy place, good thing was I by myself so I got a seat right away, otherwise waiting time for lunch on Saturday was more than 1h... so not sure if locals go there but it is really nice for the experience. Will definitely go back!!

Excellent brunch! We had the blueberry waffle, a side of biscuits and gravy, bacon fried rice, cinnamon toast French toast, omelet and pork steak breakfast. Literally everything was spot on. The only complaint were the guys who ordered the pork steak breakfast said it was Judy ok. The rustic ambiance was welcoming and the bar options were just about limitless. Can't wait to go back and try out their lunch or dinner menu!
Happy hour is the shit here and bomb ass wings! What more would you want? Did I mention there's a shitton if TVs with all my teams I want to watch?

I wish I could give this bridal salon 10 stars. They were wonderful with me! I ended up purchasing my beautiful lace wedding dress from here. I can say with confidence that all 5-6 dresses I tried on were like nothing I had tried on before, certainly not in Indianapolis. The staff was extremely sweet but also professional. They knew exactly what dresses to pull for me without me having to shop which I know from another experience I had with a salon in Indianapolis is extremely hard to do and can't always be accomplished. 

All of their dresses are truly one of a kind and you will not look like a ''cookie cutter'' bride. If you are really looking for a dress that will make you stand out and feel beautiful try Beloved Brides.

The absolute best Philly cheesesteak I've had in my life!! My cousin told me this is his fav cheesesteak, and I trust his judgment so I tried this place out opposed to the other top contenders, even though it was out of the way. Came for lunch and the line wasn't too long, and got my food quickly, very lucky apparently. Ordered a cheesesteak whiz wit. Came out piping hot- the meat was cooked perfectly and flavorful, chopped up the right size. Onions were nice and caramelized, and they gave me plenty of whiz! Also had chopped peppers on the side which gave it a nice kick. Seating is pretty limited, and I chose to eat outside, but all the tables were taken, so I just walked down the street to the park and ate it there. If it wasn't so far from my hotel room I'd definitely have gone to back to get it again before leaving Philly.
Food was ok. But not as good as before. They don't know the recipe for a bloody mary. so I switched to well drinks...which were weak and definitely bottom shelf.

I used to go to this grocery back when it was owned by A&P. Since Breaux Mart has taken over the prices have gotten higher and the quality of customer service has plummeted. 

Worse, when visiting recently I have seen Breaux Mart's contractor illegally booting people. When I get groceries I don't appreciate being spied on by some sleazy fellow staking out the parking lot. Although the law requires Breaux Mart to make the booting decision themselves, they have left it up to this dubious individual who isn't complying with various provisions of the city's boot law. I've complained personally to Breaux Mart but they refuse to take any action. 

 The prices are bad enough, but when combined with this kind of callous disregard for the law and for their customer base, it's clear that Breaux Mart isn't the type of place I want to shop.

So I go in to the Wawa to get a couple of Sizli's and some altoids the bill was a little bit more than I thought, I looked at the price for them and I was charged $2.59 when the price says $1.19. I tell the cashier no problem (rushing to work) so I had no time to argue, but I do tell him to change the price because someone may lose it.
So when I go back there a couple days later what do I see the same incorrect price.

Two stars EARNED.
We love the Reef! They were busy but made a table happen for us. Molly was an awesome server, super attentive and personable. Best quesadilla ever! Great place to be on a Saturday night!

This is a great little (very little) local breakfast and lunch place. It used to be The Hut. 

Most recently I had a bacon, egg and cheese on a kaiser roll and a small cheesesteak. Both of them were delicious. Hot, with plenty of cheese and meat on each. And they use excellent rolls, which we all know is very important. 

They also have great coffee and a surprisingly good beverage selection. 

Fast, friendly service consistently make it a pleasant experience as well. 

I'm looking forward to more visits.

The food was great and the place was busy. I sat at the bar. If you want a sit down dinner, I recommend calling ahead. I ordered the special - ahi skewers with rice noodles and loved it. Beers are awesome, too!

I went here two years ago and it was terrible. Two years later, I decided to give it another chance. The foods definitely improved, though I still think they aren't great. However, I will recommend the Spicy Garlic Chicken Wings since that was my favorite out of all the dishes that I ordered (General Tsao, Fried Rice, Peking Pork, Tofu & Veggie).

If you dine in, be warned that the air is polluted with their oily smoke since their venting isn't good. The place isn't fancy and it is small, so seating is limited.

We haven't gone to China garden in years and did tonight for convenience.  Now I know why we don't go there - it was horrible.  The sushi (rock n roll maki) was fishy tasting and the green curry Thai chicken was disgusting - mushy eggplant and veggies and overall not appealing at all. I feel badly giving review - never do it but this was a really bad meal

I've only been to Rosalitas for dinner a few times, but I've never been disappointed. The food is fantastic and the wait staff is very attentive. We made a reservation  each time we've eaten here, which I recommend because the dinner rush will have you waiting for a while. I ordered the shrimp quesadilla on our last visit and really enjoyed it. The margaritas are fantastic--we ordered a pitcher of the house margarita on ice and they are so good! The chips and salsa are very tasty and you can tell they're made fresh. I can't wait to try some more items on the menu.

Worst experience ever. Went in to have lunch with friends & was not greeted by staff. They sat us at a dirty table & never offered to clean it. I had to personally removed the plates after waiting for 10 minutes. 
They refused to allow a highchair, claiming it was a fire hazard. When we asked to see the fire hazard, the manager said he did not know where it was. Completely against federal law. Horrible staff & service. Highly recommend avoiding this restaurant at all

Great staff.  The Produce is not always the best in town but I can count on they 100% for all my other needs.  Personally think they have the best wine selections and goat cheese.  The family just loves this place.

I live around the corner from this place. I love the beer. They always seem to have some great dark beers and a decent IPA on tap. Their bar service is quick. The different types of hummus are really good, but it isn't a place you'd go for dinner. I'd say it's a great place for a drinking snack. 

Definitely check this place out. Their beer is excellent and their atmosphere is great.

Went out on a cold front and a windy day, so I knew going out it was gonna be slow. We still managed to snag some decent sized fish and had a great day on the water. While Im not as big of a fan of inshore fishing as I am offshore, this was still a great experience and I recommend it.

Spacious indoor rock climbing gym, awesome for the kids and experts, but not for adults who are not experts.
They have many rock walls at the front and boulder climbing without ropes at the back.
It was a very warm muggy day in June, we headed there with some friends, the space was nice and cool and a large birthday party was leaving, it was not too crowded on a Saturday early afternoon. It was the perfect place to escape to get out of the hot and humid weather, and have some exercise and fun.
We were three adults and three kids, we bought the kids a 15 punchcard with 5 climbs each for $60 which we split 3 ways. We had a belay expert that worked solely with our group (since they were not too busy), normally they told us the belay expert will take up to five kids. She was nice and good with the kids.
The kids had an awesome time, BUT us adults were not permitted to climb. Here at this gym adults must take a class in belaying and do it themselves. It was a shame, because all three of us adults wanted to climb and the place was not too busy, so they missed out on some cash and we missed out on some fun.
Go vertical is a rock climbing gym in the city and they do allow non expert adults to climb. We will have to go there next time.

I couldn't possibly have any more great things to say about this place. The venue is small but for good reason. Nothing like a small family owned Italian restaurant. The food is absolutely out of this world good. I suggest you try it, it's a little on the pricey side and the fact that they don't do reservations kinda bites, but the food is well worth the wait and the price. Please show support for this restaurant!!!

Great spot right off Bourbon! Definitely a nicer atmosphere. The food was fantastic, and the entire waitstaff was very accommodating of our various allergies (dairy, gluten, etc). They had plenty of options for us to choose from, and everyone loved the entire meal. Would definitely go back. 

The restaurant did seem pretty busy, so I would recommend a reservation. We had one and we're seated exactly on time even though we were early.

Eating here last night was nice.  The atmosphere is definitely Italian, down to the music being played.  Was quiet when we arrived, but filled up quickly.  It is a small place...cozy.  The wait staff was good, but we waited far too long for our check.  Not a big deal, but worth mentioning.  The food was good, but the sauce had a background taste of too much dried basil, which ruined the wonderfully garlic filled tomato marinara.  I know it was dried basil because I use it in my sauces, as well, however I don't over do it so the taste doesn't overwhelm as this did.  This restaurant claims that everything is made on premises by the 2010 Tucson Iron Chef winner, so that led me to believe it would be all fresh, including the spices.  The salad was good, the veggie soup was also delicious.  The garlic bread was good.  All in all, a pretty good meal, but my hubby and I both decided we prefer Carrabba's (I know, it's a chain, but it IS good), where they use fresh basil in their sauces and we get dipping oil for our freshly baked bread.

Great gift shop!  It's that go-to place when you're looking for a fun gift. Especially great for people with a sense of humor. 
While it's not local for me, I'd definitely go again if I'm in the area!

First time at Growler. I have to admit, after reading previous reviews I was a little concerned that we might have slow service, but it was a terrific time all around for the four of us. We arrived 4:45ish. Snagged a table on the patio which had a gorgeous view of Pusch Ridge. Beer selection was fun, food was really good. Two of us got salads. Two got sandwiches. We all commented on how good the food was. Were definitely coming back. Plus, I logged onto their WiFi while I was there and was sent a $5 coupon to come back.  See you soon!!

Life saver! My mommy has been going to Terri for her pedicures for years now; so when I realized I had a chipped nail and was overdue for a gel manicure while home for the holidays, naturally, I decided to try this shop...beyond happy I did! I called on Saturday (before Christmas) around 6:15 pm and asked if I arrived by 6:45 pm did they have time for a gel mani, she said sure, come on in. I got there right at 6:45 pm and I was greeted and seated immediately. They were friendly and once they realized who my mom was, they raved about her and how they'd heard all about me. Warm and clean environment and very reasonably priced! Lighting is also a plus, as it is adequate but not too bright (I get migraines). I had gel on my nails and she was gentle when removing that polish! My manicure and polish look fabulous! Definitely recommend trying this place and this will be my go to when I'm in town!

The place is unique in that Argentian resturants are not the most common.

One issue we had with this place is although it's kind of funny in retrospect is how cramped the place is, as we could literally hear everyone's conversations that were next to us.

Aside from this, the Croqueta's with codfish were really impressive, and not as skimpy as I expected although definitely not filling.

The picahna really was amazing for the price, and my partner got the NY Strip and the same can be said. Definitely packed with flavor and as someone who doesn't enjoy steaks too much, I really liked the food. Great yucca fries too.

Only reason I can't give 5 stars is due to the insanely cramped feel, and this is a ~6 month delayed review so it was when COVID was much worse, although I understand they only have so much space.

Aside from that, the food is 5 stars and I great date place, would highly recommend.

Phenomenal!  I'm very picky when it comes to gumbo (no one ever makes it as good as my nana) and my nana would be proud of this place!  I got the Mumbo Gumbo and a fried chicken po' boy.  I left so stuffed, but I wanted to keep eating!  I'll take anyone visiting the city here for sure!
Great live bands and cold beer. If you are looking for a historic dive bar you found it.

This studio is the best! I love the space, it's a large room so it doesn't ever feel cramped and there's lots of natural light. I've had a few different instructors and had a great experience every time. They lead challenging classes, but always make sure to offer modifications if it's too hard. There are people of all shapes and sizes so no need to feel shy!

I like this place. Clean. Good view. Breakfast buffett is good. Warm food. Pretty classy actually. You have to pay $50 valet mandatory as there is no other parking around. Unless you park far away and walk.
Great spot to eat or just hang with friends and have some cocktails while listening to great music.  awesome food and cocktails
The best restaurant in Indy by far!!! Loved the wine pairings with the food, great service, couldn't ask for a better place to go to for dinner.

I don't know if the food is good. The lack of hospitality was so huge I didn't get a chance to find out. ACME across the street has a line so we thought we would try Felix's instead. Apparently that was the wrong decision. When we walked the place was fairly empty (perhaps that alone should have been a clue.) The waitress wanted to seat us at a table but I asked for to sit at the bar to see them shucking the oysters. When I moved a chair one foot to the right the bartender yelled "No moving chairs!"  Look, I'm not thin-skinned and I don't get offended easily but let's be honest, I'm the customer and she was rude. We end up just going to ACME. 5 minutes later we were being welcomed in and treated like family. Bottom line, Felix's is empty for a reason.

So the 2 stars say "Could have been better". I can't even say it was "ok". I have been here twice before within the last year and it was much better. However, dinner was very disappointing this weekend. I want to say though, our waiter was amazing as was the hostess who seated us. But the food was really off. For anyone with an actual gluten allergic or celiac such as myself, the menu was very limited. Pretty much every other restaurant in St. Pete have more GF options. What was surprising to me was that things like I rice weren't gluten free - I was told the "seasoning mix has gluten". Why would seasoning have gluten? It shouldn't unless it's low end quality. But for an average price of $28 plate I would hope I'm not getting low end anything or any sort of spice mix or pre-packaged "seasoned rice mix". Yuck. Beyond that everything was just bland. I don't have dulled taste buds if you are wondering. The green goddess salad sounded amazing on paper, but was wilted due to way too much "dressing" on it yet had zero flavor! I read through some of the lower rated reviews recently and was surprised that several other people felt as though the food had come from some pre-packed mix rather than a chef created meal from start to finish. I have to agree this seems to be the case. Also as other reviewers have mentioned, it's possible there are some limitations to what is available due to the pandemic, but I find it hard to believe that this means everything has to have gluten and taste bland. Regardless, please add more options to your menu for those with allergies - we deserve nice meals too and shouldn't be told that the only way to make something GF is to basically take away any seasoning or sauce whatsoever and have a plain piece of fish and a steamed vegetable and plain rice lol. I can stay home for that!
Quite simply the best pastrami in Tampa bay. This little gem is pure homemade, Jewish, magic..Deliciousness! Do yourself a favor an GOOOO!!!!!!!
It was pretty good, speaking as a Chinese American who has eaten alot of dim sum in her life. The rice "jook" was pretty good with thousand year old egg in it. I will be back.

Don't miss the BYOB opportunity! There's a wine & spirits right across the road by ShopRite, but nevertheless, the food itself here is enough to leave you happy. Authentic Sichuan dishes comparable to Chili Szechuan and Han Dynasty, but slightly cheaper and just as flavorful. 

Our group of 5 came on a Saturday night and found the place quite occupied. Service was attentive from the start, and the two middle-aged waitresses were happy and accommodating of our needs. 

FOOD: 
Dan Dan Noodles - heavier on the sesame/peanut sauce, which is authentic. Comes in a small bowl so perfect for sampling as you won't get full on the carbs! 
Beef and tripe in chili oil - I thought this was perfect! Not drowned in chili oil and flavored just right. And plenty of meat, which is always a plus. 
Spicy boiling fish filet - delicious! Plenty of filet and comes in a huge pot loaded with dried chili peppers. It looks different from what you'd typically get from other places, but it's actually quite authentic in terms of what you'd receive in China. 
Cumin lamb - AMAZING flavors. Meat is tender and juicy. Favorite. 
Peking duck - the only letdown of the night but it's an out-of-place dish at a Sichuan restaurant so what do you expect. No points deducted. 

We were comfortably fed - no leftovers and no food coma. Happy to return!

I love this place! Their chicken udon noodles are my jam! I just found this place a couple weeks ago and have ordered from them 3 times since. Every time I have to get the udon! I haven't yet been inside the restaurant(Chinese is usually when I'm too tired to cook) so, I usually DoorDash it. I will have to update after I get a chance to go in.

We went through the drive through because it's closed inside. But they messed up our order completely. The line wasn't even that long and they were completely incompetent. We ordered sides and never got them. We ordered biscuits and meals that came with biscuits and never got them. They gave us tenders instead of bone in. They obviously only care about drive through times because they just threw together a bag. My kids were disappointed and not fed all the way. I will not be going back and I will make sure everyone knows this restaurant is not somewhere to go during this time. Taco Bell and McDonald's and all other restaurants are stepping it up and Popeyes is not. Unfortunately it won't let me give 0 stars.

Beautiful place, tastefully decorated and intimate. This is the second time we're having brunch here. After last year's great experience we made it a point to make reservations for my birthday weekend in lovely New Orleans. The staff is great and the service is without flaw. The food... Is not from this world! Had sweet and savory menu items but I couldn't make up my mind on what I liked the most. You have to try their Grits though, I think they're the best I've ever had. Simple but amazing! The place is small and the sitting is close with the next table, so be sure to be friendly with your neighbors if they're open for a light conversation. We got great tips from other diners and found it interesting to interact this way.
Everyone from the call taker to Joe who serviced my unit was fantastic.  Joe diagnosed my problem and had my unit up and running at a great price!
Hamburgers are out of this world. Served with a fully loaded baked potato. Dark but they play great music.
Nicely's Jamaican food is soooo good! This is always my first choice for Jamaican food. The young lady behind the counter is always friendly, nice and welcoming. You will not be disappointed.

I've been here several times but have never written a review. This place is consistently good. I love the Italian Kitchen gourmet pizza. Great burgers, cheese steaks, and wings. The turkey hoagie I ordered once kinda skimped on the turkey though. The service is always good.

Me and three friends when there for Korean BBQ. We all ordered the all you can eat.  I guess all you can eat has a different meaning to this place because they cut us off. The food was great but that's because you cook it your self and we had a professional chef with us.

I don't know if this is the new theft prevention program but this store doesn't encourage people to shop.  My husband went in yesterday to shop.  He looks at things and buys them.  As he walked in, he was asked many questions and said it was 5 hours before someone could help him.  He explained he just wanted to look around.  After many more questions, they allowed him..  He did decide to buy something.  He asked someone for help and since there is no counter, was told to stand in a specific area.  He did, nothing happened. He left.  Too bad because he said I should have a new desktop..........

It is fine to be a burger snob, but you can't turn your nose at an Awful Awful. When you tell yourself "I could eat a whole cow"...PLOP here it is in all its savory meat(wad) goodness. The place is nothing to look at...dinky and always a line.  They are as quick as they can, and boy do they deliver, good food that is.  I wouldn't rate them on their bed skill. ;0)
Good spot near our home when we're craving Asian food. Sushi was decent and the chicken basil was delicious. Polite service and quick. Will definitely come back.

Garbage. 

I stopped in today for SNS- it's not quite everywhere yet, I needed a quick fix, and saw that they do it. When I arrived it was quiet, occupied but not busy. The man at the desk told me it would be $40- typical. So I sat and waited a few minutes, and before the tech started, she said something (I couldn't understand) to the man- and he told me it will be SEVENTY FIVE DOLLARS. I literally laughed at them- I thought they had to be kidding, especially since he'd already quoted me $40. He asked if I usually go "here-" I said no- and he said "Ohhhh, well rittenhouse very expensive." HE GAVE ME THE SNOTTY SALES LADY IN PRETTY WOMAN TREATMENT. 

OH. Well excuse me. I'll retreat back to "my side of the tracks." Yknow, I don't get out much, I had no idea that rittenhouse place is so expensive. 

Thankfully I was able to get in last second with someone who's done my nails a few times, and takes great pride in doing it meticulously. I didn't bother trying originally because it was so last minute, but she squeezed me in- for $37.
Hands down the best bar in all of Nashville. Bands are always amazing and the bartenders even with the huge crowds always have your drinks full.

Went in for my rapid pick up, got back to the office and items were not in the bag. Chicken on my sandwich is SUPER dry. Asked for delivery of missing items, literally down the street said they couldn't get ahold of a driver? So instead of getting refunded, I get rewards and not all our food??

I was there at a normal afternoon appointment, to get laser hair removal. What a nightmare. Words can not explain my tragedy. She gave my face second degree burns and I spent 6 hours in the ER, also had to follow up with a dermatologist. All out of pocket by the way. I did not get so much as an apology, reimbursement on the $5,000 I paid as well. Don't ever go there, unless you want someone to destroy your face without so much as an apology. I've been depressed, can't wear makeup and have PTSD. I cannot even afford a psychologist. This has taken a big toll on my life. Please save yourself and don't ever go here!!!!!

OMG I echo Emily A.  I just discovered the Italian Market (I know, I know -what took me so long) and I went to Claudio's for home-made ricotta (so creamy, fresh and good - and CHEAP - $2.99 a lb.) and mozzarella.

Can't weait to pre-order a Burrate for later this month.  Burrata is Mozzarella with a creamy-buttery cream in the middle - so decadent.

I have FOUND my source for mozzarella.  Hooray!

THIS PLACE IS AMAZING! 

I grew up eating here. family ran and the food is great. as for vegetarian options ... your decision to be the vego... complaining isn't an option. The pork here is great. everything is great

The food was mostly awful.  The hamburger was like shoe leather.  The soft shell crab legs were like little twigs.  The French fires were overcooked.  The service was mediocre.  Very disappointing considering its general rating.

I recently renewed my lease for my apartment at the Adelphia House, and this year would make the 5th year of staying here. I love staying here and couldn't imagine moving elsewhere unless it's a lakefront home with five golden retrievers and a garden in the back. Okay, I digress. 

Here are five great reasons why I love it here:

1. Maintenance has always been very responsive to my work order requests.

2. I don't need to be home to receive packages; the front desk signs for them and keeps them safe. I never had an issue, and I order a lot. #dontjudgeme

3. My floor and neighbors are quiet. A little too quiet, but I'm okay with it.

4. Friendly staff! There is nothing worst than dealing with nasty attitudes when you're trying to rectify an issue.

5. Location, location, location! Post office, Target, Reading Terminal Market, Trader Joes, great bars & restaurants, and let's not forget the Wine & Spirits stores are within walking distance.

If I had to think of things that I don't like, it's the occasional fire alarms set off by people who fill the dryers with too many clothes. Like, c'mon folks, is that complicated.  Several times last year, the water had to shut down due to pipe upgrades. Although it was a minor inconvenience, I understood the need. Okay, that's all. Thank you for reading. :)
Nice atmosphere and good food! Always crowded but they keep the flow in s good way! Recommend and will go back again.

Wow - we are 1 week into a 7 week trip from Socal to Banff in our 89 Westy. While in Reno, we noticed antifreeze leaking from underneath the van. It's a Friday before a 3 day weekend and we are in unfamiliar territory. Put the word out in a VW Facebook site and was referred to this place. I called and spoke with Spencer who advised that he could take a look at it around 9:00...it is now 10:00 and we are back in our hotel with a completely fixed vehicle. Spencer and his son were so friendly and helpful!!! Also, very knowledgeable about vanagons and VW's in general - had a beautiful Porsche in his shop as well as other VW types that he was working on...I wish we lived in Reno so he could be our regular mechanic....Can't thank you enough Spencer (and son) for getting us back on the road - a big shout out to Matt for the referral!

I was really looking forward to this. The line was fairly long outside the restaurant, but it went quick. I had the mild chicken tender plate with mac and cheese and greens. Not a fan of the pimento mac and cheese. All in all, it was just okay.

Visited on a Saturday around 11.  We waited nearly 40 minutes for 2 crepes. Multiple tables were left with dirty dishes sitting for a while. It seems they need more staff on. 

You also tip when you order so you're not able to tip according to your service experience. Kinda backwards. 

Food was pretty good once it finally made it to the table.

It's been quite a while since I've visited this theater, which is by far the closest to home for me.  I was pleasantly surprised on this visit, as it was the first time I'd been since they redid the whole theater some time ago.  Much to my surprise they have reserved seating like some more pricier theaters and the seats even have electronic recliners, which is a great touch.  The overall movie watching experience was great here and I'll definitely be back in the future.
This is a great place for tea and Raman. Good prices and good atmosphere! The pork ramen is one of my favorites.

What a fun way to see a different side of Nashville!  The crew was fantastic...especially with the dumping of water on the heads of people who got too hot.  Being able to bring your own food and booze on the boat was great too!

Seeing how my fiance has been burning up in Hell's Kitchen, I decided to treat him to a winter retreat in Santa Barbara.  I was a little skeptical at first when I found this B & B deal combined with dinner at a local French Restaurant.  (Last time I found a hotel online, I swore I would never put myself through that again...) But I digress....My man and I arrived at the Cheshire Cat Inn and were swept through the rabbit hole (so to speak).  Our room was amazing upgraded to a beautiful suite complete with an in-room Jacuzzi and a cozy fireplace.  From the apricot homemade scones, to the friendly staff this was an inn to remember.  Now I have to find new holidays to warrant another escape. (I don't celebrate Valentine's Day but Memorial Day is so far away!
Real good duck and chinese broccoli. All their is good and well known locally. Don't let the modest strip mall location fool you.
Love the drip coffee and the baked goods.  Great atmosphere too.  Day old pastries are usually half priced.

It's insanely loud and I guess I forgot how overpriced an arcade was. But overall the layout was nice, they have games, bowling and pool. The later you come it becomes a kid free zone so definitely plan on coming back for a fun adult only game night. There is a bar as well for anyone who would like to enjoy a drink with a game.

My mother and I took a super shuttle from Sarasota to the Tampa airport on Wednesday. I had taken one down a few days prior and had a great driver but our trip back was absolutely horrendous. The driver reeked of cigarettes, was swearing, going 30-40mph above the speed limit (for example on a 10mph trim ramp he was going almost 50), had gone over the white and yellow lines 3-4 times, was complaining about his boss and the company, etc. I'm not a nervous passenger but his driving was completely unsafe and erratic. My mother submitted a complaint and emailed but received nothing back.

I usually eat here all the time. I love their Tuscan tomato soup. I called today to order  to be greeted by a rude lady on the phone. She put me on hold for 10minutes and didn't have the one and only soup they served. Very displeased with this service !

First  let  me  say ,  this  company  has  a Facebook rating  with  the  better BBC , second ,  they  towed my  Daughter's  car in a place  that  has a  4th hrs visitors  parking  pass   she  was  only  there  24 hrs.. billed  her  $155.00 then reduced  it to   $100.00. The  apartment  complex  said  they have towed several  they  should  not  have . They  will  not  return  phone  calls  neither  will  the  apartment  complex . This  does  not  speak  well  of either  company . I looked  up  the  rating at the Better  Business  Bureau.

Love this place. Probably have been here 10-14 times and I am not sure why I haven't done a review yet but I guess today is the day! The food is so delicious. The staff is all friendly. I have pretty much always had great service, but today we had Carol I believe her name was and I would say the service was outstanding! She was everything I would expect a server to be and it was nice to have that good of service! We will continue coming back.
I've actually never seen more rude people in my life. I understand if you hate your life, but don't take it out on your customers. Thanks.

Andres and staff are excellent and make this one of the friendliest coffee places in town. I love their iced lattes. They also have live music that provides a lovely atmosphere for the neighborhood. Esplanade wouldn't be the same without The Flagpole.
Disgusting not a Sonoran dog they aren't even from Mexico they have no idea what a Sonoran dog is worse thing I've eaten ever!

There are a lot of bad jokes that can be made about the optimist and its name but its simply not a good restaurant. The place is nice and the service was good but thats it. First we ordered champagne. The waiter puts two wine glasses on the table.  When asked about champagne glasses he said they dont have those.  He then put a plastic bag of ice on the table to put the bottle in. So all night we slurped champagne out of a wine glass while staring at a wet plastic bag. The oysters were very good but then the burnt octopus came swimming in a vat of some red sauce. The halibut was gross. Frozen and undercooked with a green moss on top. Horrible experience.

So fresh and delicious. The Salmon Tropical is where it's at. The sauce is amazing!! The rice and beans are delicious. I got a crispy beef taco on the side and it was ok, but there specials are amazing. Get the horchata for sure. Flan is ok - I like ones with a creamier consistency.

The food is good but they were out of a lot of stuff. They didn't tell us that they were out of the kind of chicken we ordered until after we paid which was kind of annoying but the employee was there alone and very nice. He gave us a few extra pieces of the one kind of chicken they weren't put of and apologized for the inconvenience.
I was also sad that they were out of their edible cookie dough bites. I have had them before and they are so good!

Overall the customer service and the food is good, it was just frustrating that they were out of so much so early in the morning

Espresso made from beans aged in former New Day's breakfast magpie mead barrels?  Damn amazing and don't dare add any flavoring or sugar to it.  I still dream about that espresso, and its complex, vaguely alcoholic berry taste.   Bee Coffee Roasters offers you the chance to step out of your caffeine comfort zone...taste a cardamom and shagbark hickory syrup latte, freshly roasted espresso or coffee, or other creations (I happened to witness the menu remnants from special Gencon offerings, including Doctor Who and Groot lattes). But, overall, I recommend anything with those breakfast magpie espresso beans...hopefully it was not a temporary offering.
Easily the best, fattest oysters I've had here! They were juicy, not gritty and tasted delicious.

What a disappointment ! It's hard to have a sub par experience in an area that has so many amazing Mexican eateries.  This was by far one of those places that you experience and wonder how the heck they keep the doors open?!   We walked in for a late lunch on a Saturday afternoon and wished we had walked right out.  The server was also the bartender and the only person waiting on tables.  Needless to say, service was slow, atmosphere was the equivalent  of a library and food was meh. While in the restaurant, I saw business being turned away because the server was too overwhelmed to take on more business (she had 3 tables to help). 
Do yourself a favor, the name is deceiving, this is not your resort town Señor Frog's experience.  I don't actually know how they are able to use the same name.  It's misleading and false advertising.

When my wife and I started looking for a paver company to replace our old concrete driveway, we started by looking on the BBB website for highly rated companies.  That narrowed down the field pretty quickly.

We asked three companies to quote, and as the prices were all pretty similar, it quickly became evident that service would be the deciding factor.  One of the three immediately eliminated themselves by not responding to a query regarding design.

We next went to look at some recent jobs in our area (Wesley Chapel / Lutz) done by the two bidders, and were told one of the companies had repeatedly promised to start the project, but failed to appear.  

Then we visited Bay Brick Pavers showroom to select the material for our job.  The owner and his wife were very cordial and professional, so we left a deposit and scheduled our installation.

They touched bases with us a couple of times, and reconfirmed when they would start.  The bobcat operator was prompt and capable.  He removed the old driveway and prepped the area so the installers were ready to go the next morning.

The installers were just as good, leaving the finished driveway neat and clean.  The entire job was completed in less than two days.  

The price was exactly as quoted, with no hassles or problems.  My wife and I can't speak too highly of Ted and crew.

This is a hidden gem, people. If I lived in Nashville, I would probably be addicted to this place. Not even exaggerating. I highly recommend the falafel plate. The yellow rice that it is served with is incredible. I'm not sure what they do to it. This is not a fine dining restaurant by any means, but the food is so satisfying. You will be full for days.

*This is a Margarita Review!* I didn't have time to eat an entree but wanted to  bike over to try their house margarita since I personally consider margaritas a vital life essence. Anyway, I order the Festiva Margarita with Casa Pacifica Reposado, Caballero, Citrus and a salt rim. While they were making my drink my waitress, Lex, gave me a  little cast iron skillet of chicharrones with a generous helping of hot sauce - not a bad bonus. 

The margarita was in a somewhat difficult to hold on to glass with a very thick rim of salt. Turns out the salt rim was super necessary as this marg was v sweeeeet. I like my margs on the savory side so I was a bit taken aback. It was good, but if you're a margarita enthusiast like me, you like your margs true to form. 

Def coming back to try the food, but most likely won't make this my go to margarita spot.

Nothing really special about this place. The decorations were different and catchy but with the restaurant part not really a place for a nice sit down dinner. This place is more of lounge than a restaurant. The music is too loud for you too enjoy your food. Nothing special about the menu. I ordered chicken wings (mild) and Hawaiian BBQ Flatbread. The wings were not good at all. It does not taste mild at all it tasted just a regular wings. The chicken were not that cooked as I bite down my third piece the meat were still red. And even one of my friend noticed it too. I did not finished the wings after that but offered it to the rest of the group. The flatbread is good as I love pineapples so that's a given that I will like the flatbread. 

They also have sushi in their menu so if you like a place that offers ceviche, sushi, flatbread, wings, sandwiches at the same time then I guess this is a place for you. The service is okay.

Worst Starbucks ever. Problems with their order system supposedly. Caused all the girls that ordered after me and some other guy to get their drinks before us. Like we didn't exist the barrista kept calling out what the newest people waiting wanted. Finally we went up to the counter and made a point to demand our drinks

Let's be real, the Nat Caf is great, but it's not that great. There are plenty of other restaurants that will give you much more heart and soul than Nat Caf. This place is really for old people that have problems with their digestion so they need boring food with a sprinkle of cheese. 

I've had the chicken stir fry and the fish tacos and both times I walked away feeling very full, but knowing that they were multiple other places that would have been better. It's a fine meal, but if you're looking for something that will make you happy with intense flavor and creativity instead of just a full belly than this is not my jam.

Had my first experience at MedExpress this past weekend. I usually go to a different urgent care type place but this one is closer to my house and members of my family had been pleased with their visits here.

The check in process was super simple, I liked that you signed the papers at the desk on a signature pad instead of  having to bring them back to your seat. I also appreciate that they e-mailed me after the appointment. 

There were a few people in front of me so I had to wait maybe twenty minutes before getting called back to the first room where the nurse took my temperature/blood pressure/ wrote down my symptoms. 

Next, the nurse led me to a different room where she gave me the flu and strep test. The doctor then came in shortly after and was very friendly and checked out my nose/throat/ears. 

After my diagnostic of the flu (meh), the doctor sent me on my way to the checkout area where I received care instructions as well  as my prescription. 

All said and done, I was in and out within an hour. This location was very clean and all the employees were pleasant. I'll be back here next time I need to go to the doctors quick!

No doubt this is the best place to get a steak on the Illinois side if the river. This location blows away the Ofallon location and the prices are better that less quality places to eat. The bread is awesome and the best thing on the menu is the prime rib. The bananas foster is off the hook as well. Again Andria's Countryside in Edwardsville has it going on and you will not find a better steak than here. I have eaten there at least 20 times and have yet to ever have nothing but fantastic food and service. Don't waste your time trying other places just stick to the best.

Meh. I really really wanted to like this place but it just falls short on so many levels. 

Food: Bland, tasteless and boring. Do they know how to use some simples salt and pepper even? Doubt it. I had the calamari and really all I could taste was half cooked white flour, I have never been one to eat straight flour soooooooo needless to say I did not finish this and I did NOT ask for a box. My boyfriend had chicken tortilla soup which he specifically ordered because that type of soup generally has at least a small amount of spice and he wanted to clear out his sinuses. The broth was pretty much watered down tomato sauce, not a pinch of seasoning of any kind to be found. 

Service: We were seated right away but then I think the waitress just kind of forgot about us. Many times. 

Atmosphere: It's pretty inside, but that's really about it. The atmosphere itself was kind of dreary and again, pretty lonely since the waitress kept forgetting we were there. 

I really don't think I will ever go back unless it's just to grab a drink or 2 before the game. I can guarantee I will not waste my money on food there again.

They serviced MUC (squid)! I'm amazed! Winner over here. LOL

$10 per hour for the pool table. Decent food that they served and pretty quick service too! We also got a few buckets of beer and a bottle. Smoking is allowed inside. 

Overall, it was a chill experience.
Small gem. Great wings w/ unique homemade sauces. Deep dish is great...better than Chicago. I will come back every time we are in town.

We ate there for the first time this weekend.  
We had a veggie supreme which was outstanding.  
Love the thin crust pizza, the toppings were perfect, and the price is great.  We still have half the pizza left over and I can't wait for lunch when I can have the leftovers.
Great place for Vegans!

Excellent. Different from a lot of the burger shacks that  have popped up lately. Apps- spicy pork eggrolls - for the money, not a very good deal. Overfryed, with too little filling. If it had been a few bucks, fine, but not at that price. Not enough to share.
Onion rings - perfects. Excellent, light, not "refried" in terms of texture/flavor  Obviously cooked to order. Main burger - had the lamb burger as a recommendation of the "morning bartender A". Awesome. Don't see that on a menu frequently, was as good as expected. Chocolate shake - could have been a hair thicker, but I didn't ask either, so... Still a good flavor. Unknown "famous ice cream" brand but didn't matter. Waitress was excellent, just the right divide of watchful but not overbearing. Definitely recommend. Not as cheap as a 5 guys/smashburger/etc., but a much better burger (not in same class)

Butchertown Hall is a great restaurant in Germantown. It's got plenty of outdoor and indoor seating and a pretty cool bar. Get the burger, it's amazing. The whole chicken is pretty awesome too. Make sure you try the beers from the left side of the menu, those are (apparently) exclusive to just this restaurant. The drinks on the menu are almost exclusively tequila drinks but they will substitute other spirits.

Ignore the negative comments! Found this place on a top ten Nashville breakfast places to visit list. It absolutely deserves to be on that list. Many great looking menu items - hard to decide. Settled on Prime rib hash. It was incredible! Perfectly cooked yukon gold potatoes in the hash and topped with 3  PERFECTLY cooked eggs. Normally don't eat this much at breakfast,  but I ate every bite because it was soooooo good. My husband said his French toast was pretty yummy.  Great service - Eric was our waiter (I think) - food came quickly. Good coffee and cup was always full. Relaxing place. Everybody, including the mgmt, was very attentive- especially for a busy New Year's day. The restaurant was clean and orderly. Waited tables in a fine dining establishment many years ago and I am quite impressed with what 417 has to offer.

Best pizza on planet earth! There are a tremendous pizzas shops that say authentic NY Pizza and 9 out of 10 LIE! This place is the REAL DEAL! First one in Brooklyn and they have a franchise in Tampa! What sets them apart is the coal fired brick oven, thin crust, fresh buffalo mozzarella, sauce, coin pepperoni. A must for any pizza aficionado or novice.

I ordered carry out this evening - cod platter.  When I opened the box at home, it appeared nothing more than fish sticks.  I've been with family members when they ordered cod that was much better, just a few months ago, from this Sahm's (Allisonville & 116th street).  Overall - I am very disappointed in my meal and will think twice before dining with Sahm's in the future.
Best rib Rub I have found so far in Idaho. Meaty Ribs very juicy . Service was fast and Friendly staff .

I love this place..  I love their chunky fried dumplings, sesame balls, n more... never disappointed when I go there...  you even call them in advance if you have a  party , they can even make extra fresh bakery for u!
Food is awesome here! The food is an excellent value. And the workers here are the nicest ones among all the mall court restaurants. Would highly recommend.

I called BAKED ON 8th after the concierge at the JW Marriot said they were fantastic.  YEP!  Fantastic customer service, local folks that love what they do. 
I needed a cake for a 50th birthday, and spoke to Grace on the phone for a while,and she talked me through suggestions.  I told her a little bit about the woman, that she was Canadian, and recently moved to NYC for a great job, and the cake they did was marvelous! Chocolate and delicious.  The apple on the top is actually cake... it's not a real apple.  I was so impressed. Great value, great local business!!  A++++

I like complete nutrition much better than GNC. 
Never once when I asked about a product did they say, "I'm not sure" or "let me ask someone who might know." They really took pride in their product knowledge. 
Another thing I loved is the fact that they care about your success. Your first time in, they take your measurements and as you keep coming in track your progress. 
They have a club that's $11 a month. That money builds up so you can use it towards supplements in the future. I like it because I'll always be needing something.
Ordered 2 Big Macs and only gave me 1 meat. At home so too late to turn around for a refund

I try to come here every year since they opened up and for me it gets better every year.  They do only have beer but it's good beer and they do have finger foods.  They do have a few houses that you can wear a necklace and this means the monsters can touch you and scare you extra. I do not do that because I'm a chicken when it comes to that.  I would recommend everyone trying this out and to me it's better than howl o scream at bush gardens.  But I love Halloween and it's one of my favorite holidays.

The options for Chinese in Pasco are pretty bad - 
I was pretty excited to see such a wonderful presentation of food packaging. We ordered sesame chicken (4). Chicken cashew(3) bourbon chicken(3) chicken & broccoli (3) 

Wasn't to fond of the bourbon chicken tastes to much like Chinese spare ribs

The chicken and broccoli had a different sauce then all normal places and full of carrots not enough broccoli 

Chicken cashew would be much better with the chicken from chicken and broccoli instead of the boiled shreds

We loved the plain chow mein and ragu - the egg rolls were pretty good as well . 
All said and done we would try more items next time

I used to love this place, but I just picked up take out from there and I am really disappointed. It seems like they are trying to cut costs and my salad had very little in it besides spring mix! I asked for dressing on the side, salad was covered in it. Very little cheese and no kalamata olives. Just disappointing! The worst part is that the tablet that processed my visa asked for a tipping option and I tipped 20 %, as I assumed it was going to the lovely girl who brought me my order. As I was sitting and waiting. I saw a sign that said " only cash tips go to employees" which means that I just gave the business an extra 20% ! Why even have the option to tip? Seems dishonest and creepy. I heard this place switched owners, and that seems evident. Won't go back there. Too many delicious comparably price options in the area.

I was excited to try this place when I heard that Nhu Y had been resurrected as a such.The bahn mi was better than I had remembered . The chicken (B4)$4.75, was superb! I do believe the search for the best bahn mi in Philly has ended.Chicken was moist and well- seasoned. Nice amount of mayo, carrots & daikon. Just the right amount of jalapenos for some heat. The bread was the real star. Dense and chewy; a cross between a baguette & ciabatta.

They also offer, sweets, steamed buns, spring rolls, croissants and more!The veggie spring roll ($1.25)  was great! Not greasy and the filling had so much flavor, dipping sauce(not provided) was unnecessary. For dessert the steamed banana cake ($2) served w/ a side of coconut milk was a taste and texture sensation.Gotta try a steamed bun($2) next time, for which I cannot wait.

Great octopus appetizer. Short rib entree good but not great.  Nice oyster selection and good martini - very dry.

Not worth the food poisoning we got though

I've been here four times and every time I leave happy but not satisfied. I love ciders. Been drinking them for years and have had them around the country. I think their ciders are good but not great. They have interesting combinations with various fruits or herbs in their beverages but many of them are semi-sweet as opposed to dry. I prefer dry cider and they usually have only one option. I don't think their ciders are bad, just not what I prefer. 

Also, every time we've come in and sat we are never sure if we are being served or need to go to the bar. The last time we went we sat down at a table, ordered drinks, chilled and drank, but as we were finishing, there was no server in sight for a solid 10 minutes. We didn't know if we were to go back to the bar to pay or not. Then another server came over, asked if we needed anything, so we just said the bill. She gave it to us and while we were waiting to pay our original server finally returned and had no idea we were paying. It's always confusing so hopefully they can figure that out within their staff. Usually I stand at the bar but since there were 4 of us we sat down, but we never know what to do when we're sitting... so I'll keep coming back and hopefully I'll see more dry options and better service.

We took the New Orleans Walking Tour of the French Quarter with Suzy. She was a great tour guide. Very friendly and knowledgeable. She took her time with us in terms of pace especially since we were traveling with my friend's toddler. We went to about 6 places and had 3 mini dishes and 3 bites. They were all tasty. In my many years coming to and eating in NOLA, I never had a muffuletta. This was one was nice. We also had beef brisket with a red horseradish. This was my favorite. My next favorite was the jambalaya. It had a nice balance of heat to it. The boudin was also something surprisingly nice. It almost tasted like a hush puppy with meat. The toddler loved that one too. My only critique would be to have 1 more dish vs a bite (we had a cookie and praline). I wasn't as full as I would have thought given all the spots. However, Suzy was excellent. We learned about the city and the food as we went along the tour. It is a nice way to get to learn about the area and the food of the French Quarter.

Started off poorly as we called and the gal who answered had an attitude - "what do you want", lots of negative vibes in every detail of our simple order for two hamburgers with fries.  We picked the food up in the little van out front which was convenient, and at least the pick up person was pleasant.  The food.....not so great.  Just OK food, but it was hot and the fries were good.  Wouldn't go back.
This shop is beautiful! I could spend my entire pay check here! It's  must go to spot, Sarah is amazing and goes above and beyond for her customers!

I went to Carrabbas's Restaurant yesterday with my friend Maggie. I ordered a Johnny Rocco salad that had shrimp and scallops in the salad . By the time I got my salad the shrimp and scallops were cold.  I was very disappointed.  The server Toni should have served me my salad right away. My friend Maggie ordered a sirloin steak which took awhile to cook.  Toni should have served my salad right away instead of waiting for my friends steak to be cooked and serving both together.
Not good service. I don't like cold seafood in my salad. The salad should have been served hot. Then bread was served in plastic wrap instead of the wire bread basket. Table that came in after us got a wire bread basket for their bread.  Excuses the wire basket needed to be washed. This was at 2pm in the afternoon I give this visit rating and of a two. Disappointed with visit this time.
P.McGorman
Great burger place! Lots of options and they will do lettuce wraps for gluten free people!

Easily in top 3 best pizza I've ever had. I got the Bourbon Special Pizza. Crazy simple to order off here and pay for and showed up on time. The front desk loved the smell so much they ordered there own. If the world comes to an end in New Orleans and you haven't had this pizza you'll regret it. And completely reasonably priced. 2019 ...Christmas Review.

I dined here with a friend, my treat. I ordered the chicken Caesar salad and he got the bay platter (or something like that). Drinks are too expensive at RL so I had water. Good service and food. Happy, happy, happy.

Came in with a large group on a reservation so you'd expect there would be a plan to serve everyone.  Such was not the case.  Once food was ordered,  it took a full hour for us to start getting fed and then it trickled in over the next 20 minutes. When it arrived my mashed potatoes were luke warm and had likely sat in the kitchen for 15 minutes before coming out.  Waitress never returned to refill drinks. Food could have been great but execution was atrocious.  Service was beyond bad. Waitress had to come back and walk around the table and ask how many beers people had because she had no idea. 

Will not be back.

Posted this almost 30 minutes ago... Still waiting on checks.

Worst service place I've ever been. I'm even upset with myself for buy my jeep there. All the people are rude and act like they are doing you a favor. Customer services is horrible. Would not recommend going here.
Loved the atmosphere and decor,  amazing service and friendly staff.  Great delicious food in a fun environment.

Good basic noodles and rice dishes, nothing special but nothing bad either.  

Had to work a late shift and went to the Bakery for a let night dinner.  The crowd there is an eclectic mix of people from all walks of life, there were a few trendy young partiers there, a few families with young ones, a few gentlemen who have seen the bad side of the tracks, a few city workers who just got off work...  

Service wise, those ladies who work there are super fast but may be a tad on the rude side.  There was no "hi, how are you..."  you walk in, they motion you silently towards an empty table, generic tea and menu got placed on your table.  They reappeared when you are ready to order; food were brought out quickly but dont plan on seeing them again for the rest of the meal.  Do not expect fine dinning service there.  Avoid using bathrooms too, the non working fixtures and foul odour can be a little offensive.

Ordered the $7 Beef Brisket Lo Mein and the Seafood Chow Mein.  The Beef brisket Lo Mein came super fast, the beef was nice and saucy, tender and tasty, the boiled lettuce... not so good.  The accompanying clear broth was pretty tasty.  I would definitely give this dish 4 out of 5 stars.  Some better veggie would bring this up to 5 stars for sure.

The $12 Seafood Chow Mein came later.  The noodles were very chrispy, a plus.  Lots and lots of bok choi...  should have called in bok choi chow mein.  Seafood was scarce, 4 shirmps, half a scallop and 2 pieces of squid.  The sauce was blend, needed soya (rare that I add soya to anything) and chili (I always add chili to everything  :-)).  Definitely not the best chow mein I have ever tasted, only gets 2 out of 5 stars here.

Portion wise, both dishes were huge, great value for the money.

The average for me is 3 stars out of 5 for food.  Would I come back, yeah, great place to come for a late night snack or late dinner.  But I probably wont order the Seafood Chow Mein again, I will try something else.

Although I'm from an hour away, my brother and sister-in-law live in Mt. Holly, so I finally got them to go.  They will now go back even without me because they enjoyed having a place where you can enjoy fresh beer, sit for a long time, and not have food pushed onto you.  They also have a really nice outside area, so when the weather is nice I will sit out there and enjoy.

On the way out, I grabbed a six pack of cans.  Canned beer really has come a long way.  They canned it just a few hours before I picked it up.  Boy, did it taste fresh when I got home!

Small menu. Out of items. Portions need to be larger for price point. The taste is good. Ingredients seem quality and healthy.

I frequent quite a lot of organic, healthy, pure, veg/ vegan restaurants around LA and wherever I travel. While this has a great thing going for it, I'm really not sure what all the 5 star reviews are for. I went and had a ceremonial matcha latte. I think the fact they offer sprouted almond milk is great, although I wish they had more alternative milk choices (hemp, coconut guys.. these are standard for vegan joints.. not everyone likes almonds, they consume a ton of water and some people are intolerant to nuts). THE PORTIONS need to also be larger. I also ordered an orange smoothie and it was too much money for the size I got. The matcha was made perfectly. I order matchas most places I go and it's rarely made this well- no chunks, nicely frothed a little, slightly sweetened. It was a good matcha and that portion was pretty good. The guy mentioned below made it.

The customer service: the guy with dreads or braids was really sweet and helped patiently answer all my questions since all ingredients weren't listed on the board (I'm thinking of the flavored chais and maybe a couple other things) and it was my first time in. The menu was extremely small and limited and most items contained things I don't eat. There were a couple items that looked really promising but they were out (and it was like 10am). So all in all it was kind of a bust for me.. not sure I'll return. There was another girl with tattoos and she was cold to me, seemed like I was unwelcome there.

9-17-18
Gave it another try and the same thing. The girl who rang me up (same girl again) was slightly less chilly but acted so annoyed that I was even there it looked painful for her to act polite. Smoothie was again very small and this time too runny. For what is charged, it's not worth it. I don't plan to come back.

I was very impressed with the quality of food, but even more so with the service. The server was attentive and even grabbed Naan from the back for me because the ones on the buffet was a little dry. I will definitely be coming back!

I was just there on Friday night. Josh Arnold was fantastic! Jake Baker had a hard act to follow. He was good, too, though. Wait staff was good. Pre ticket sales: Whoa! Posted price for General Admission tics was $10. We were charged $13. I went to the ticket window and told the guy that he overcharged me. When I told him that the price posted was $10..he rudely replied...WELL THEY'RE NOT! He asked me where I saw that price. He then stormed out of the office, went over to the posting, tore it off the wall and trashed it with much flourish, and then went back to his little ticket office. No apology, no refund, no offer of a free drink. On the upside, it gave me a crazy story to tell the rest of the weekend.

Food is very good and super fresh.  The variety of menu options is great from Malaysian, to Japanese to Indian.....  The Rainbow roll is amazing and their cheese cake is to die for!!  Our new favorite restaurant.  Can't forget to mention the is service great and staff is so friendly.

This place is a wonderful hidden gem here in Carmel. Whenever we go in we're the youngest by a few decades, and it's part of what makes it great. The tenderloin is fantastic and definitely one of my favorites in Central Indiana! The wait staff has always been nothing but pleasant and the service is always efficient. The restaurant itself is dated but it works. I love coming here for a quick delicious dinner with my husband!

I've eaten here before, and the food was good. However, today, I encountered a very rude and pushy cashier/manager! First off, the menus that one picks up are not quite the same as the menu above the cashier. I ordered a veggie burger, and he said that I wanted a veggie club. I said yes, thinking that there was only one veggie burger as was shown on my menu. I later found out that I could have gotten a classic kind of veggie burger for less. Since I went along with my order, I couldn't complain too much. It was a different story for my husband however. He ordered a small salad, but when he sat down and looked at his bill, he saw that he got charged for a larger salad. When he approached the cashier about it, the cashier insisted that my husband ordered a large salad. So when my husband asked to see the manager, the cashier replied that he is the manager, and  he's too busy to bother with him right now. We left in disgust, but I do remember being there the past few times when I saw some people leave this place and say something like "I'll never come here again!" I thought they were isolated cases until now.

My pictures just say it all YUMMY!!  the sushi is fresh and the customer service was amazing.  Sushi one after another - best part its ALL YOU CAN EAT SUSHI!   very affordable for how much you can get!  Also i recommend the mango ice cream and why not do some saki bombs while youre at it.  :)

First off, when places tend to mix two cultures' worth of food it rarely ever goes well. Second, who chooses Tex-Mex and Creole/Cajun to go together? Odd.

We ordered the steak/chicken fajitas and the chimichurri steak. Both were decent but really nothing special. I did get a side of jambalaya and it was easily the worst jambalaya I'd ever had. I guess it'd be fine if you'd never really had authentic creole food? Who knows.

This place is wonderful. The husband and wife are so nice, and are always genuinely happy to see you. The prices are reasonable, and the quality's great. 

A bit out of my way, but well worth the trip. Also, there's 20 minute loading just down the street, so it's easy to drop off/pick up without having to pay for a meter.

Accepts credit only over $30.

My front tooth has been needing treatment for a long time. I was very fearful of how the treatment and cost until the staff walked step by step what would take place. I would reccommend anybody to come here who has dentist anxiety, the staff is very friendly and try to make everything stressfree as possible.

okay, so the drinks are cheap and if you like to people watch (point and laugh) then this is the place for you. i don't know what it was but man, there were a lot of bachlorette parties going on. if you want to get trashed for cheap and have a bunch of nasty boys try to rub up against you one last time.. this might be the place for you.

oh.. and i don't know what what up with the DJ, seriously, if the record keeps skipping, you might not want to play that song.

meh.. i had a good time, but that's only because i spent most of the time attached to my boyfriend's hip. it help close out the nastiness around me.

The food here is okay but we typically eat in about once or twice a month because we live down the street and it's super convenient. Yesterday we went to the take out window and ordered a shake and cup of water. 40 minutes later I returned to the window and asked for a refund (they obliged). The girl--who spent five of those 40 minutes greeting my dog and another puppy in the area with the benches--seemed surprised that I hadn't received my shake. In some ways I blame myself as I am all too familiar with their poor service, particularly at the window. 40 mins for a shake is next level incompetence/disregard though. I am never returning. Metro Diner on Kennedy is infinitely better.

One of my stops while I was in Florida over the Thanksgiving holiday was Sloppy Joes at the Bilmar Beach resort on Treasure Island. After a day recovering from our Disney trip, my sister and I headed out to the beach. We grabbed breakfast at the Waffle House, which I have already reviewed so be sure to check that one out, and afterwards, explored the area. I was scouting out potential gems to stay at and also did some gift shopping. We spent a lot of time walking on the beach and were getting tired and thought a seat and a drink would be nice. My sister suggested Sloppy Joes because she and my brother in law had eaten there before and loved it. Honestly, I wasn't that hungry but knew I had better eat something. Sloppy Joes is on the beach and has amazing views of the gulf. I'm a sucker for a good view!

We followed the signs to the front to check in and get a table. Being it was a fantastic day, we asked to sit outside. We were quickly seated and given the food menu as well as a drink menu. Being in Florida and at a quirky beach resort restaurant, the drink menu was exactly what you'd expect. Lots of Rum and fruit... otherwise known as beach drinks! All of the drinks came with a souvenir glass. I wasn't that interested in a souvenir glass and asked our server about other options. She told us that all of the drinks were available without the souvenir glasss so it was Rum Punch for me! I haven't had a beach drink in a long time and the Rum Punch from Sloppy Joes was just what the doctor ordered! Cold and delicious.

Since I wasn't hungry, most of the menu wasn't what I was looking for so I settled on the sticky, sweet, chili wings. They were highly recommend by our server and she was spot on. They were sticky and sweet with just the right amount of heat, oh... and nice and crispy. I hate wings where the sauce runs off and creates a pool of liquid underneath the wings and doesn't stick. The wings were exactly what I needed and I am glad we stopped.

If you find yourself in Treasure Island, Sloppy Joes in the Bilmar Beach Resort is worth a visit!

Brisket is horrible! save your money and time. It's dry, but with a greasy taste. The taste overall is nasty! They may offer better choices, but I'll never know because you couldn't pay me to eat there again.
Tastes like it was soaked in dirty dishwasher. Before you get skeptical,  I'm easy to please.
Waitress never asked how my food was and had to wait 10min for a straw. Restaurant was completely empty at dinner time, kinda know why now
Brady himself I think was sitting two chairs away and read my comment on ticket, yet did nothing.
There brisket is, well, gross and they don't seem to care what you think of it. Walk down to Barfly and get something good to eat!
Their food is outstanding. Our relatives in Quakertown introduced us to this restaurant and we have been back a few times and have a positive experience each time. Their helping are very generous!

This is place has the best sushi I have ever tried. Nothing will ever top it. I've gone 3 times this week and I am not ashamed. I leave full and within two minutes, I crave the sushi once again. I highly recommend the Crunch Spicy Tuna Roll and the Bonfire Roll. Amazing place with amazing service and amazing food. 10/10.

I can't believe I'm just trying this restaurant. Very quaint, cool vibes. Great spot to bring the family. Definitely recommend getting a few apps for the table to start. We had the chicken dish which was fab, the steak was so tender and delicious, and the burger was simple yet so tasty with the meat being from Porter Road Butcher. We loved our server too! Very funny and engaging and not to mention attentive! Will definitely be back.

I have been a costumer for a few months. Today as I was drinking out of the cooler I noticed debri in the water. After looking closer I noticed there was a frog. A FROG! 

I don't know what kinda company provides filtered water with frogs in it. 

I would never recommend this company to anyone. 

Horrible! 

Note: they were completely insulting over the whole incident.  Suggesting I put the frog in. Typical low life response.

Best place for live music! Cool venue, neat shops, good food, try the mussels!
Mango Margarita Rocks!

Beautifully laid out and extra clean l am in love! Met the owner who was as sweet as pie and who believes in trying to make Willingboro a better place. Haven't tried the hot food but will be coming back this week to pick up lunch as everything looked appealing and fresh!
Definitely a local spot! Make sure to make a reservation beforehand. Food  and Atmosphere is good but can get kind of loud.
Great place, friendly staff and awesome food. One of my neighborhood staples that I always reccommend!

I went here  on Christmas Eve with my husband and mother in law. This was there 3rd time going so, some comments made are also based on their experiences.

First of all, the outside is absolutely stunning, they had small bonfires lit leading up the mountain to the restaurant that was super cool...the view and landscaping at the top made me feel rich and fancy..and then walking in - Holy shit, it's amazing.  Being from the midwest, perhaps I have not been in a lot of Hacienda style places, but every detail was to perfection.

The food.  Well, my husband and I do eat seafood but don't eat other meats such as chicken, beef, etc.  Though there were 3 fish dishes on the menu, only one was only seafood.  That was really disappointing, as my husband had to get the risotto, which ended up ...  well adequate.   

My mother in law had the duck, which she said was really chewy and just okay.

I had the swordfish which was really awesome.  The pureed  cauliflower kind of made the dish.

The wine list is intense, and though it's really impressive to have a catalog of wine, it would be nice if it were organized somewhat better, or even had a featured wine list of the night.  

The service was okay,  but as my husband, whom is a waiter at a fine dining restaurant pointed out, the food runners had their thumbs all over the inside of every dish.  yuck.  

The desert was pretty good, but the espresso was super sour tasting.

I was so impressed with atmosphere that it overshadowed the mediocreness of the food.  But in the end..it was really expensive.  The night before at Wildflower the food blew me away, and was much less.  They also said that the menu seems pretty much the same as it has the last 3 years on Christmas Eve.  Unfortunately, it sounds as if the tradition will be broken next year, as they will be looking for a new place for their special holiday dinner.  Both my husband and mother in law are pretty intense foodies, and I think really wanted to be blown away by the food, but it just doesn't cut it.

I was visiting from San Francisco, CA on business. The warm welcome was very genuine. The Nica unique cuisine left me speechless, I savored every bite. I had to meet the chef and personalized how wonderful my meal was. This is far the best Nica Cuisine I have experienced! Please visit and allow your tastebuds pure pleasure.
Heard about this place and went tonight with the family. Great food! Loved it all and great people! Some of the best BBQ I have had!

I recently stayed here with a friend and chose this hotel based on location and price. Yes, the elevator is small and slow and reminded me of the ones in Europe. Take the stairs when you don't have your luggage. The front desk staff was very friendly. 

We were in town to see my cousins band play so we had a late night. Before 9am I woke up to construction in the room next door. This included persistent hammering and screwing and cutting right next to or into our shared wall. They mine as well have been in our room. I called down to the front desk 3 times asking about it and requesting it to stop. Although the man apologized and claimed he went up stairs each time asking them to stop, they didn't. Just when I thought they did, I would be jolted from my bed again. 

This place would have gotten at least 4 stars from me if it wasn't for the construction. I understand they need to improve the building or rooms but then you shouldn't be placing someone so close to the work. 

I am traveling to PA very soon and would have definitely stayed here. Now, I would only stay here if they gave me a discount and assured no unwanted wake ups.

Always busy there but the last couple of visits my car interior just isn't getting clean. Dirt left on the doors, carpets and seats, very disappointed.  For the $25 I spent (including tip) I expect better. I won't go back anytime soon.

Came here after some shopping on a Sunday afternoon and the first thing that got my attention was the "fresh" in Doc Bs Fresh Kitchen. I sat at the bar and ordered the Tiger Shrimp Salad with an avocado dressing, feta, peppadew, and red onion served with arugula. Omg this may be the most fresh and delicious salad I've had in a long time. It was a little pricey at $15 but well worth it as there were lots of shrimp in my salad that made every bite delicious and I didn't want it to end. All the flavors paired so nicely and it was very well balanced. I was also surprised by the great happy hour that started at 4pm on Sunday which all of their wines and wells are $3! Can't beat that when all the wines are from $8-$15 a glass! I really enjoyed my experience here. Fresh and delicious food! Service was also excellent!

3rd visit in a year.
1st visit: met friends there for drinks. Arrived to get the last pint off a pitcher and they had several before me. Was told we all were cut off and no more beer for anyone, even after the waitress acknowledged I just arrived. Dumb move. Understand dram shop laws, etc. 

2nd visit:  Met friends with girlfriend for Sunday lunch. Waitress brought our beer to the table and spilled a full beer on my girlfriend. Their answer was to give a free beer! Didn't even offer to comp her meal or anything, instead she gets a beer while wearing one. 

3rd visit: went today and was told sit anywhere. 80 degrees and sunny so we sat at the patio bar beside 4 other people.
