After 13 mins no one acknowledged us till we picked up our keys and started leaving. Was told by the bartender they were trying to raise the garage door on the bar. If she can say that when we are leaving, could she not say that when we sat down?

Will never go back. 3rd strike means they are dead to me.
The best banh mi in town !!!!!! Omg I want to try the whole menu ... but I only can try two cause I cannot eat it all plus it's better when it's fresh !
Beautiful restaurant, and the food and service are always fantastic. Will recommend to friends!
Ordered takeout and Ryan was super helpful and professional.   Food was delicious.   Don't forget to get a cupcake too.  They are SO GOOD!

Thank god for Yelp. I just discovered I can leave a review. Note this review is from 6 years ago but I cannot and will not forget the service I received from here. I came in for a relaxer for my prom back in 2010 (18 at the time) and was attracted to the salon because they had offered the Phyto brand of relaxer at the time. Upon entering the salon, the staff was somewhat aloof, however I believe the owner of the salon was more cordial when my service was started. After my service however, I was largely ignored and while waiting in the lobby area to be picked up by my parent, I inquired about some products for hair upkeep. The front desk woman laughed at me and started to mock my word choice repeatedly asking what did that even mean. I remember being confused by her reaction and I explained what shampoo or conditioner would you recommend I buy. She just laughed and said "yeah, right. go look for yourself." and walked away to go to the back of the salon to sweep away clipped hair. I just remember feeling depressed after the visit and not knowing what to make of it. It was my prom coming up and I already was self-conscious and this didn't help with any underlying self-doubt young adults tend to have about their appearance prior to such a landmark event. I had initially liked my hair but that service left me feeling horrible inside and I never returned. I'm happy to see they have a yelp page up so that I can now share that story. They have probably changed some things in the past 6 years so giving the benefit of the doubt but I still look back on that day and wished I had spent my money somewhere else (I spent about $200 that day after saving up for a few months) where respect of guests is routine and the focus is on building up the confidence of the people who enter your doors.

I like this bay looking restaurant. It is on the water. They have plenty of waiter and waitress. The time we have visited were Sunday lunch time at 11:00 am. It is a bit cold seat at the outside next to water for December. This is our first restaurant for progressive lunch. 

I got the grilled grouper sandwich with pretzels bun. The side we have the coconut rice. It was great. The grouper is fresh. They do give good pieces of grouper. We also has the lobster roll with sweet potatoes. The sweet potatoes were crunchy. The lobster is chunky and has less mayonnaise which show more lobster meat in the rolls. I like this way better. 

Our service is good. Food come out quick. We were able to finish our meal and paid less than 50 minutes.

I did try to like Miga. In fact, I was quite excited about it. Going into the restaurant, I was also at first pretty impressed. The space was large and clean and the waitstaff were courteous. But for some reason, the food just didn't measure up (part of me just really misses the Korean food in LA).

The dolsot bibimbap is pretty average. None of the BBQ meats really tasted right. They definitely did not satisfy my Korean food craving. The soft tofu soup (soon du bu), also did not really measure up. 

Unfortunately, I'd rather drive out to the outskirts of Philadelphia (Cheltenham or Upper Darby) for Korean food than dine at Miga.

This was my first time axe throwing and it was so
Much fun!!! This bar is actually in the back of Fergs so it was kinda hard to find we had to ask someone. They are taking Covid precautions which is great too. When you arrive they give you the rundown and you sign a safety waiver. You can't wear heels and they prefer closed toed shoes. Then they spend some time teaching everyone how to properly throw which is awesome because it was def harder than it looked! Then they have different games on the score sheet for you to play. It was such an awesome experience I can't wait to go again!

Ups and downs, gutters and strikes.  Overall, it was a good brunch, but I'm sure the unlimited mimosas had a factor in my laid back mood right now, that and the ridiculous view that every table in the house has the privilege of enjoying.  I do love a good view of the bay, and this is a great one.  None of the dishes really stand out to me even after a couple visits.  They were going back and forth, and last time, they had a small a la carte menu which was cool but again not impressed by the quality that came out.  It didn't seem like it was made to order, more like they made a bunch and let them sit in the back until "made to order".

There were as many positives as ehhhs along the way.  My across the board gripe would be nothing was actually HOT.  Warm...  Sure, but hot nothing.  When it comes to mashed potatoes and grits, that's kinda a must.  

There wasn't one dish that will have me running back to this place, but nothing so terrible that I would warn anyone to stay away.

Service was great, I sat at the bar this time, so I never was in need of a drink or to order one of the small plates they offer.

Really good food. Very filling. Service was slow and forgot most of our requests such as straw, Splenda, slay and pepper etc but overall food was good. Drinks could are good but I have had better ones.  Price doesn't really match with serving and flavors but looking forward to their happy hour next time as that has better offers. Trust

After hearing several recommendations from friends my husband and I came here for brunch at 1pm on a Sunday. Parking was easy with a large lot behind the building and we were seated right away which surprised us a little bit. Maybe it was an off day, I really wanted to like this spot but things just did not add up. 
We were seated at a table for two that had a booth on one side and a chair on the other, when we sat down there was no one sitting at the tables on either side of us. As soon as these tables filled up it was extremely awkward, they were so close we could hear what everyone else was saying word for word. There was no music being played in this area of the restaurant and you could hear every part of the conversation at the tables next to you. 
It was also extremely hot in this part of the restaurant, it did not feel like they had any air conditioning on which was unfortunate considering it was in the 80s outside. 

Now onto the food...I ordered the Keg Omelette which had Serrano ham, Swiss cheese, tomatoes and came with home fries and a biscuit. The best part was the biscuit. My omelette had almost no filling, I think I had one bite that actually had ham and mostly it was just plain eggs. It took quite a while to find our server and ask her for some ketchup, it was not severed with the meals and not on the table. 
My husband had the 1/4 Keg Omelette, he said it was alright but nothing special.

Drinks from the bar were fine, but we had to beg anyone to refill our water glasses throughout the meal. 

The bill came to $28 for two omelettes, one mimosa and one manmosa. Doubt we will be back.

For starters, there are better options in Edmonton. We stopped by for lunch because of the Yelp reviews. However, the service was almost non existent. 

We were seated right away and menus were provided. Tea was dropped off but the tea pot was never refilled. Our server mistaken an order and we corrected her so one person was left waiting for his food while everyone else was eating. Food was okay. I ordered the vermicelli bowl with grilled beef and spring roll. The spring roll was made with the wheat wrapper. I always prefer the rice wrapper instead. The beef was grilled alright. There was a good amount of veggies. I tasted the pho broth and it had a strong star anise flavour. The broth was just okay. There was a good amount of meat and noodles.

We never saw our server once food was dropped off until we got up to pay the bill. The restaurant only had a few tables of customers dining during lunch. They accept credit card payment which is always a plus. There is ample parking available on the plaza.
True professionals. These people know what they are doing. Great customer service!

Dear Wishing Town thank you for being the icing to my rushed civil wedding ceremony cake.  I came to you guys two days before the event and had a lot of questions and was rather pushy.  I wanted things changed and modified and you had no idea why.  Despite this the person helping me was patient, professional and had a very rosy disposition, she even called the baker to make adjustments.  The result?  A perfect cake and a lot of people I know asked where I got the cake (and it was just about $40 with all fresh fruit add on).  Needless to say I can't wait to order more fresh whipped creamy chiffon strawberry shortcake.

My dad told me about this place that they have this amazing sandwich that I must try, so here I am trying his uh-mazing sandwich. This place is right on a broad walk. The inside was kind of cramped and noisy, they have only a few tables to sit outside. I sat outside for the ocean view and breeze and to people watch. Our server was really nice and was helpful on recommending items on the menu and explaining what had what. I order the fried grouper po boy, which was actually not that bad. The sandwich was pretty filled, so I was only able to eat one half of the sandwich. The fries, I didn't think fries could taste that good. They were perfect. My friend ordered the lobster roll, it was okay for being one of the popular items. It was more like a lobster salad on bread. We felt that it lacked flavor, taste bland. I would come back again.

This place truly lives up to its name. I had the Spanish omelette (the best) and the biscuit comes with a maple butter spread that's to die for.Don't forget their signature Bloody Mary. I must visit if your in the area!

This place is very expensive and the quality of the food is horrible even a simple fried vegetable spring rolls they don't get it right... crispy from outside side but gooey inside... very disappointing!!! Definitely NOT coming back.

We showed up without reservation; we may have gotten the last available room. This place was jammed; cars packed the lot. We ended up getting the only available spot right in front of our bottom-level hotel room.

Be careful with the sink water; it comes out of the faucet at about 1400 PSI and splashes back when hitting the bottom of the sink bowl. Raise that pressure gradually until you get what you need. The water pressure of the shower was lacking. The pressure is there they just need to pull some it from the sink and put it in the shower. Poor balance of pressure.

Nice big king-sized bed; it was huge. Pretty comfortable and warm. They have a nice soft, cushy top layer. 

The room itself was nice. Lots of space. We watched some crappy Adam Sandler movie, one of the new ones(I strangely liked it though), on the nice flat screen they provide; the microwave blocks the infrared TV sensor for the TV remote. I had to move the microwave to be perpendicular to the TV to get it working again.

This place has the most awesome pizza. And their meat and cheese platter is to die for. The mixologist downstairs make the best drinks in downtown Tucson as far as I am concerned.  Always enjoy drinks here before heading out downtown.

I recently took my diesel truck into A1 on a recommendation by a friend for a transmission issue. The tech (maybe one of the owners?)  took my truck for a quick drive (without me) and looked at the transmission fluid.  With this limited info he told me that the ONLY fix was to replace my whole transmission at a discounted price of $2500.  He said that he could rip the whole thing out and take it apart (the only thing he could do) and it would be the same price to fix it as to replace it.  I took it to Arrow Transmissions who fixed it for $360.   All that was wrong was it needed a new switch.  Really A1?

Their pizza is always a favorite of mine, but at this location I find myself in a situation that required me to ask for more help than I deserved. Manager Stephanie was very accommodating and eager to provide excellent customer service.
Went here for a holiday dinner with some friends recently.  Love the décor of the place.  it is a very cool setup.  The food was good but not great.

Seriously a GREAT spot for sushi AND Thai food. 

We were up bear 54/Vet and decided to look on Yelp for some options for an early dinner. We chose Bangkok Thai and were so happy we did. 

It's located in a section of shops and a Residence Inn. In the plaza is a Mexican place, a beer garden, Carrabba's and various retail shops. Upon entering you realize just how great this place is going to be. There are linens on each table and the inside is ornately appointed. Each table is adorned with fresh flowers. 

Our server was extremely attentive and brought out our drinks very quickly. 

We decided to split a sushi roll and order an entree each. Our server was kind enough to bring out our roll while our entree was cooking. 

The roll was spectacular-the shrimp was fresh and adult warm from the tempura frying. Our entrees came out piping hot and were decorated with a fresh orchid. The vegetables were FRESH-crispy fresh, which I like. The sauce on my cashew chicken was spot on and even the rice was shaped into a heart. 

If we are ever in this part of town again, I would definitely come back. It is a great addition to the Odessa area.
Great zoo. The apes have nice exhibits. The Insectarium is awesome. The grounds are lush. Lots of places for kids to play. All for free!

Nothing makes my tummy happier than an "All That Jazz" po-boy from Verti Marte.  It's a layer of tomatoes, ham, turkey, grilled shrimp, cheese, and mushrooms all covered in wow sauce, which is some sort of creole mustard/ mayo hybrid--but better.  I think that every single layer of this sandwich is integral.  The shrimp philly, also lathered in wow sauce with a melty layer of cheese, mushrooms, and peppers comes in as a close second. 

They don't use the traditional Leidenheimer bread here and instead use the sesame seed Italian bread, so I kind of put their po'boys in a class of their own.  I've had a lot of the different po'boy combinations here and they're all pretty good, especially the ones with melted cheese and mushrooms!  And if you're looking to enjoy some food porn up close and in person, just stand in front of the glass counter that displays all sorts of hot and cold entrees and that will literally send your belly and mouth into foodgasm. 

Resisting the urge to eat your po-boy right there on the street is hard, so make sure you have a fast route to your eating destination or you'll end up dining right there on the street. It's cash only, so be prepared, but there is an ATM in the front.  I will literally speed walk here from the Marigny/ Bywater limits and can get here in about ten minutes; it's that good.

I've heard nothing but bad things about this place, but I figured since it's across the street from where I work I'd let my taste buds make their own decision. Welp, the reviews were spot on. I got the cheeseburger and fries combo, which from initial appearance had me at 3.5 stars. After the first bite I thought, okay this is about a 3 star burger. It tasted very similar to a burger I could make on my own grill. As I continued to eat it, I hit patches of the burger that tasted like they dumped pepper or some kind of seasoning all over it. So now I'm down at 2 stars for the taste, because I can make a better burger at my house.The fries were pretty good, but nothing I haven't tasted before. 

What brought these guys down to 1 star is the price for the quality. Spending over $10 to eat something that mediocre is robbery. Don't be like me...DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY!

I have eaten here twice since it moved into our building, and I am very disappointed by the quality of their food, especially for the prices they charge.  I have ordered the grilled cheese sandwich and the bacon, lettuce, and tomato sandwich.  Both sandwiches had very little content.  The cheese sandwich had a small glob of goat cheese in the middle, with most of the bread uncovered by any type of sandwich filling.  The same was true of the bacon and tomato sandwich.  There were about 3 half slices of bacon in the middle, two small tomato slices that were the hard tomato core.  I tore off most of the bread from the middle portion.  Also, the outer crust of the bread was tough as though it had been microwaved.  I'm usually not given to complaints about food, but because the price of their food is high (over $10 for a sandwich), these sandwiches deserve attention.  Very disappointed.

Order from them every week using GrubHub.  My favorite is the Seafood Drunken Noodles.  Spicy, and fulfilling.  I've had the sushi a few times, and like the creativity of the rolls.  Staff is nice and the actual restaurant is small, but cozy, with a nice ambiance.  I dislike that they don't accept AMEX in person, but since I order delivery from GrubHub anyway, it doesn't really matter.

We were in Santa Barbara for the day and choose Moby Dicks - big mistake.  We all had he fish and chips and if we weren't in a hurry would have sent it back.  The fish tasted as if it had been cooked earlier and had been reheated.  Very chewy and rubbery.  Would not recommend this place

I met some friends at Craft Street Kitchen for dinner. We did outdoor seating due to Covid. We were seated quickly and our server was friendly.  We ordered the beer cheese everything please and the short rib sweet potato tots for our appetizers. The everything pretzel  and the beer cheese were huge and shareable. The beer cheese soup tasted good as well. We loved dipping the pretzel into the beer cheese soup.  Our friends enjoyed the tots with the short rib. The appetizers were well portioned and shareable. We would order both appetizers again.

For entrees we ordered the farmhouse grilled cheese, the angry pig sandwich, and craft burger. All were enjoyed. My friend out in specific requests for his burger and the kitchen kept messing up his requests. The waitress had to go back to the kitchen twice to rewrite the issue. She was great and fixed the issues promptly. 

While we were at our outdoor table we witnessed two different waitresses dumping leftover water and beer into the bushes adjacent to railing of the out door seating section. I have never seen any server do this at a restaurant. It grossed us all out and I believe it is a health code violation. I would urge management to fix this issue. This disgusting and makes me not want to dine outside at Craft Street Kitchen because I do no what to see staff dumping leftover water and beer into the bushes when I am eating. That should be done inside in the kitchen.

Overall, we enjoyed our experience and food. We all had a good time here!
Dined in and at the front desk you can see the guys in the kitchen making the burgers and gloves were not used not very sanitary

Ive been to this place a few times as I travel to Clearwater Beach every 6 weeks or so.  The Service is horrendous.  The waiter we had the other night had no clue what he was doing.  There was a party of 10 of us and we advised there will be split into 4 checks.  When it was time to pay the bill he had everything on one.  Then he began to ask people what they ordered to redo the bills.  A good 20 minutes goes by and I see him inside serving other tables.  He finally brings out 2 of the bills and says he can't figure out the computer for the other 2.  We wait another 15 minutes I go inside to inquire as to whats going on, he was confused and acted as if he didn't see me before.  He then stated we could pay the bills that we received and we didnt have to pay the other bills as its a computer problem.  The place was not even busy, he just was clueless.  The tacos are pretty good if they come out hot, the margaritas are inconsistent and the service is VERY SLOW.  If they could fix the service issue we would give this place another.

Made a trip out to Villanova so we decided to give this place a shot.  Food actually not bad (ordered the pizza & a burger) but the service was just horrible.   Not very attentive, didn't have silverware when apps came out (calamari) just not a pleasant experience.  Just constantly had to wait for service. Would probably not return

Meril is one of my recommendations anytime someone asks "where should I go eat in NOLA." The food is delicious and perfect sizes for sharing! The atmosphere is great- one of those places where you can dress up or dress down.

Anthony was professional from start to finish and did a great job on the glass in our two remodeled bathrooms. He is honest and delivers on his word. I'd use him again and recommend him to anyone looking for glass cutting and installation.

Love The Nugget.  Been coming here since I was a baby.  Its one of the best casinos and hotels in the Reno/Sparks area.  They always have their bloody mary bar on saturday and sunday! Its soooo good.  I love their tournaments also.  If you ever get a chance, please sign up for a red white and blue tournament.  You will catch me and my family there.  Its also great to see Mr. Ascuaga and his wife at rosies cafe on sunday mornings.  They are so cute and down to earth.  Great place.

Came here yesterday for happy hour. The server had an attitude up until it was almost time to leave. The spinach dip was sour and had to be remade ( wasn't done when I was there) The broccoli balls had no taste and didn't taste like the normal recipe. Overall bad experience , won't be back.
Service was pleasant even during Covid restrictions. I was seated fairly quickly. Order was taken and food arrived in a timely manner. The biscuits are very delicious!

Service was top notch...food was incredible. The French pull apart bread they serve was amazing. My SO and I split an entree of steak and scallops. Steak was awesome, scallops were average. Expensive but worth it.
Service was perfect.  Food was unbelievable.  I had the lasagna and it was the best ive ever had. I am recommending to everyone.

Went to NaBrasa for Valentine's Day. It was a really nice atmosphere for Valentine's Day. The food was amazing, as always with Brazilian, we left stuffed. We were a little early for our reservations so we got a drink at the bar. Bar tender was really nice and the three of us had a lovely conversation about tequila. Waiter was great and so were the gouchos. The salad bar, which shouldn't even really be called a salad bar since it has so much on it was amazing. Of the three brazilian places I've been so far, Na Brasa is quite posibly the best.

Chuck was trustworthy and gave me the best rate compared to others. He did a good job and was very nice. It's hard to find such a mechanic these days and I will definitely get back to him when I need other repairs.

Everything we ordered was amazing except the sweet and sour pork could have a bit more flavor and cooked a little bit longer. Other than that, the dinner special items and the chicken broccoli are amazing!

Phenomenal!! Fantastic! Not sure how many more adjectives I can add that do justice. Nashville hot chicken and waffles were on point. $5 bloodies! Courteous staff. Will absolutely visit again when traveling to Nashville

A very nice hotel diner serving breakfast, lunch and dinner. My wife and I are serious foodies and seldom eat at hotel restaurants. However in this case, we were pleasantly surprised at the lovely décor, the delicious food, and the attentive service at Café Milano.

The menu offers a very broad selection of items and it caters well to international guests. We enjoyed both breakfast and lunch here and found the food to be reasonably priced and a cut above what we expected. The service is prompt and friendly and our waitress was even patient with the neighboring table of gluten-free food neurotics.

We found Café Milano to be an inviting and moderately priced hotel diner suitable for singles, couples or families any time of the day. The atmosphere is welcoming and the food is well prepared and quite tasty. We would dine here again and recommend this nice hotel restaurant to others.

Awful!  Service was slow, and stupid. 
Can't toast bagels, asked for butter they said ok. No butter in bag. Asked for butter again. They were out. Told me to go to another restaurant and ask. REALLY!?!  I would have spent my 25$ at the other restaurant had I known. No cream for coffee had to wait another 5 mins for them to refill it after asking and they made it sound like I was asking for them to donate a vital organ. Ugh. Not a great way to start a travel day.

This restaurant is simple, charming and has a mom & pop's old world feel to it. I felt like I was in another country. Our waiter, Randy, was the only server on shift, but he was phenomenal and worked that place like a rockstar. Very personable, helpful in explaining the menu, and very attentive to our needs. The food was incredible, much better than I expected. I had the milkfish braised in coconut milk, ginger and garlic with shrimp, served over garlic rice and topped with spicy red cabbage. I felt like literally licking the plate clean, how I wish it was socially acceptable to do so. It's a must try for all you yelpers out there. The dessert Sampler was also good but didn't really blow my socks off. Would I order it again? Yeah, I believe I would. To go to the bathroom you have to walk through the kitchen. I thought it was cool, it adding to the feel of being in a restaurant located in a different country. My final thought, cool place and a fun experience, loved it!!

Fairways are in awful shape. Made the tee time over the phone and was not told that they were in the process of replacing them. The website states "You will notice that some of the fairways appear to be discolored and others may still be wet. This is all part of the process of providing a better turf for your playing enjoyment." Discolored isn't even close. The condition is awful especially for the price of $60.00. Tried to discuss with the staff and GM. Asked the gentleman working the pro shop if he would pay this price for the shape the course was in and he didn't answer. Went to discuss with the GM who said if we didn't like the repairs they are making then we might not be the types of golfers they want. Over all horrible customer experience.
Pretty awesome place staff is friendly makes you feel at home went for a cleaning and came out feeling clean the doc is awesome straight forward highly recommend this place

Our first time playing here. Awesome course. We used a Living Social deal to play. Absolutely beautiful scenery and the grounds are immaculate. Very challenging course with lots of sand, water and elevation changes. Got windy in the afternoon and could use more info on each hole either at the tees or score card. A very friendly, polite staff. They even cleaned our clubs after. Some construction going on while building a modern, elegant clubhouse to be completed early next year. We just moved here and absolutely love the area and the golf course. Can't wait to play here again and for the clubhouse to be completed. Now, if I can just talk my wife into becoming members?
Best happy hour in town. Great food. Awesome people. All the bar sports including volleyball! The day wilhagans closes is the day i move outta Nashville!!

Wasn't really packed when I came. Cant review the food since I was waiting almost an hour just for an appetizer so we winded up just paying for drinks and walked out!! Gave an extra star for waitress being empathetic about it. There's no reason why one should be waiting so long on tacos and chips.

This place appears to not be in business anymore.   The website link takes you to a porn site.  When you call the number it is for Fulton Bank.  After reading the reviews we were looking forward to trying this place. :(

Last couple of reviews were concerned with lack of Covid-19 protocols. Seems they got the message. All staff was masked up correctly on our visit. 

We were greeted as we walked in. He let us know there was a store-wide sale through the end of the month. Store and outdoor lot seemed pretty well stocked. Prices were "okay" with the discounts. 

We didn't buy anything at the time because we needed to go home and take measurements. (We are in the market for larger pots for the front of our home.)

Came for a special anniversary dinner and it was a great experience. I appreciated how they had non spirited cocktails which was refreshing. We split the deviled eggs and crab dip appetizer to start. Both were amazing the dip was my favorite. The main course was rainbow trout which consisted of chewy ancient farro and barley with the toasted hazelnut, the perfectly cooked fish, and the green sauce. The flavors all complimented each other. We picked one dessert after havinf a hard time deciding and the waiter was so kind to bring us the other dessert we wanted on the house! Thank you for the hospitality and amazing food.

Thanks to one very convincing review here, we made our decision to finally come here for lunch one day.  Why did I wait so long to try Viviano's?  I have missed out for so long, but I intend to make up for lost time.

Everything I have tried has been great.  The salads, The Festa, the pizzas, but one sandwich and one pasta stood out.  Go there on a Tuesday for their Reuben Tuesday.  It will blow you away.  The bread, the kraut, and the melt in your mouth corned beef.  It is amazing, probably the best Reuben I have ever eaten.

The Tortellini is surprisingly one of the best I have ever tried.  I didn't have high expectations for it, but wow, very fresh and flavorful.  It isn't a gooey cheesy mess, but rather a rich cream sauce.  

So the food here is just amazing, one of the best Italian places (for the price) I have ever eaten.  Thank God I live so close, I will be going here often.

Always a perfect experience for me. Today for brunch I had Chorizo Benedict. They either have a world class chef there or somebody got really lucky with this dish; eggs cooked perfectly, avocado and tomato at peak ripeness, chorizo spicy but not overly so as with so many places, every detail of the plate was on point. Even Gordon Ramsey couldn't find fault! Yeah, they can get busy and sometimes you might have to wait longer than you would like. Get over it. The wait, if any, is always worth it. Prices are quite reasonable for this quality of ingredients and waterfront setting.

Breakfast was disappointing. It took 45 minutes for our food to be served. There was no explanation for the delay and the restaurant was not busy. The food was decent but the timeliness was ridiculous.

Very good dinner generous portions, nice tasty salad comes with your meal. Nice bar with good crowd. All new inside, nice booths, great service in the restaurant, our waiter was really good. Great location. Only thing parking limited

I give 5 stars because my experience was beyond great. Yes I was there in the middle of the afternoon after the lunch rush. The staff were friendly and all working very hard. The employee suggested the chicken avocado BLT which was delicious. Bread was fresh, the lettuce tomato and avocado was extremely fresh. Chicken could have been more seasoned however that's not allowed in public restaurants because of the public's many health issues. The restaurant itself was very clean considering the construction going on next door to them. I had a wonderful healthy lunch for under $10. Also it was such a hot day and the mall was not very cool but this restaurant was very cool. The owner obviously cares about the comfort of his customers and employees. I will return.

Why did I come here without reading the reviews??? I am so disappointed. I saw their $2 Tuesday special and got excited. I should have known discounts aren't always what they are cracked up to be. My drink tastes like dollar store juice. Its kinda dirty in here and the service is lackluster.... What a frickin waste! And the crazy thing is everyone here seems quite content with mediocrity... I like Chilis but THIS location SUCKS!!!This will be my last time at this dump! Thumbs down!

My first time at this tiny jewel of a spa, inside of the charming Monteleone. Lance worked some magic to get our party of four accommodated in less than 48 hours on a holiday weekend. 

My full pedicure was expertly and lavishly administered by Millie. I'm no stranger to pedicures, but this was several levels above the usual fare! 

Also, I received a one hour massage at the magic hands  of Trudy, during which my soul left my body at least twice. Trudy's skills are beyond relaxing and highly beneficial; I felt ready to take on the hot NOLA afternoon with renewed vigor. 

Thank you to all. HIGHLY recommend.

Not a family environment at night.  This is the worst Hooters I've ever been to.  My wife and I both had buffalo chicken sandwiches and they were burnt,  so were my son's chicken nuggets, and other son's burger.  We should have left as soon as we were seated at the bar section and a drunk chick at the bar made a scene. Next time we are going to wing house.

Hit or miss with this place, tried the Deluxe and it was DELICIOUS, next time tried the Tuna Deluxe and again DELICIOUS. Went back today to get the tuna, had some Mexican lady help me (its ok im Mexican too). I asked her for a Tuna Deluxe, she gave me tuna with olives and tomatoes, wrapped it up so when i got to my office i was super disappointed and disgusted. Next time I'll have to ask for one of the guys that work there. This lady seemed to be pre occupied talking to I understand she didnt hear or care about what I ordered

What a fantastic new spot!! Everything ordered was absolutely delicious. A group of four of us shared the ricotta appetizer and the pub board, and the mushroom pizza. Ambiance is excellent....Great addition to Boise ! We

Best Italian in the area......'nuff said!

Best to visit on a week night - weekends are a bit too crazy.

Good food, friendly service, clean, good portion size for price.  My husband is a lasagna fanatic and he enjoyed their lasagna. I had the eggplant parmesan and it was good.  This place was good but we also recommend maggianos in Tampa if your over on that side of the Howard Frankland bridge.

Latin night is ridiculous the guy who teaches is a fake and how would any one know really unless your a latin american?
All salsa lessons are a scam people. No one dances this way in any latin american country. These are lessons for a real dance competition!
Argentina Spain and USSR are the best in Salsa. Don't get laughed at please stop. Your being used in a made up world a tourists idea of making quick cash
Stop being a follower and stop this business

Came here to get something made for a loved one who passed away.  They came through while I waited and put together a great piece for the widow of a Navy veteran who passed away.  The owner here has passion in his business and it explains why he's been in business for so long serving our community.  I'll be back for future orders.  Thanks again you've made this holiday season special.

WOW!!!!  What a show!!!  The Grand Ole Opry has been running longer than Forrest Gump and it has more country than a sweet potato pie! I saw that the Steep Canyon Rangers were on the schedule so I bought tickets immediately. The night filled up with other great stars of counrty music, Steel Magnolia, Jim Ed Brown, Dierks Bently and a little girl witha  big voice named Carrie Underwood. The Del McCoury band played for us as well as Connie Smith. I really enjoyed the show from start to finish.

For those of you that haven't been... GO. We are lucky to have the Opry so close to us and they have regular shows so there's no reason not to go. Go now before it's gone!!!
Very unassuming and almost missed it.  Fantastic trip through history and renewed my passion for genealogy.

When M&M (the owner) is in the house, you are in for a treat. What kind of a treat ? A treat like you have been friends forever (which to us, that was our first visit), a treat like you are the most important customer to him. I understand sometimes, a restaurant can have a not so good review about their customer service on some particular day, but during our visit here, customer service is everywhere, even the Chef (M&M's wife) came out to say hello to us and to make sure we are satisfied with our meal. 

We have been driving by this place for a few times and always say let's give them a try someday ! Well, we finally did on a Saturday morning. It is located on Wells and some calls this "A hole in the wall" place, and it is exactly that, if you don't know it is there, you will pass by it everytime! They were just open and getting everything ready for the day when we arrived, place is very colorful, cheerful, very clean, and spacious. We picked the table next to Wells. Browsing through the menus while we listened intently to M&M about his story. It didn't take long for our breakfast combos to arrive, it is first time for me to try fried plantain and it was good, even Dad loved it too! My Huevos Rancheros was saucy and got characters, I almost cleaned the whole plate which I rarely do. Most of the ingredients here can be used in Mexican cooking, but our meals were served with El Salvadorian love. 

I already look forward to coming back here to try their lunch or dinner !

Meal was perfect, just as I asked!
Haven't had my eggs cooked liked I wanted in a long time and the potatoes were just liked I asked!
Wait may have been long but it was worth it.
Brandy at the counter was most attentive and couldn't have been more charming!
Love the dimples!
Thanks for a great breakfast!

All four of my children have gone to Flanagan's Preschool in Plymouth Meeting. The staff is great and they have a really high retention rate with their teachers. They are very accommodating to every child's individual needs and have a great curriculum. My two older ones that graduated entered into kindergarten already reading.  My kids are always happy to be dropped off in the morning because they have so much fun.

Disappointing. We had good memories of dining at Maximo's over the years and this last time, it looks like everything- owner, chef, decor- has changed. It's still lovely but the food was nothing special and the service, while friendly, was just poor. Ordered a salad that came after the meal. Ordered Brussels sprouts that never came (although we were charged for them). Another salad was soggy. Ordered the fish of the day seared (supposedly with habanero infused oil)- had no taste and didn't come with the pasta. When reminded, the waitress brought the pasta much later - it was to have been my sons dinner and needless to say he was cranky. Another diners pasta with white sauce had no sauce- again, no taste. We honestly couldn't wait to leave. For that price, we certainly expected more and won't be back. Sad, especially after all the good memories.

We made a reservation for 7:30. We arrived and were quickly seated. We were given menus and I selected a filet mignon while my husband selected a porterhouse steak. We also ordered sautéed mushrooms. We asked for the fried ravioli appetizer and it arrived in good time.  I took one bite and did not like it. I was expecting cheese filling and it was a meat filling. In fact it tasted like a meatball. My husband said they were very good. However I did not like the taste and did not eat any more of them.
My husband ordered ceasar salad and shared it with me.  It was delicious. We both felt it was the best ceasar salad we had ever had.
Next came our meals.  I had asked for rare with the butter sauce on the side. Instead it was poured over it.  It was very heavy on the garlic side.  Our waitress asked how I liked it and I told her.  She took it back to the kitchen. The owner or maybe his wife came by and apologized and asked if there was something else she could get me while I waited.  I said no because I had a beautiful baked potato in front of me.  I had maybe taken 2 bites of potato and my new steak arrived.  It was perfect.  My husband said his steak was very good. He also had the baked potato. We both ordered the potato with butter and sour cream as well as the bacon with cheese toppings.  All of this was very good.  The service was wonderful.  We ordered desert to go - tiramiso and whiskey cake. They look yummy. I could only find one very tiny flaw and that is some of the foods were a little too salty. One interesting item is the light in the middle of the room.  It is a Dale Chuhuly  piece according to management. Very beautiful.  There is also an extensive wine cellar, which we did not take advantage of. It was a nice touch to this restaurant. Overall, a very nice dinner.

Stay away from the buffet!   They lured us in with a two for one price.  It was a joke.  We paid 33.00 for an overdone slice of prime rib and a small serving of Asian food.   I had such stomach pains an hour later I thought I was going down.  
We stayed in a nice room, loved the shower.  Visited the pool but one drink was freaking expensive!

Now my largest complaint is the lack of class regarding the clientele.  This casino used to be nice and attracted a good group of patrons.  Now I was in watch mode from the many apparent meth heads to young men just scoping out the place

Stumbled upon Russell's today during my lunch break at work and decided to give a try.  It is an awesome inside and even has a mini fire pit!  Decided to go with the Chipotle Chicken sandwich and it was very good!  The service was very attentive and friendly. I am definitely going to try it again and try there amazing looking cheesecake

I ordered a very simple large cheese pizza two hours later after two calls I was told it would not be coming. I was told that the driver was there which was baloney cause I left the front door open with the screen door closed I was sitting in the living room and I have a dog so if anyone came to the door I would've known about it very easily. A major disappointment,  I was home sick I couldn't leave to go out and get anything it was a nightmare and all I got was excuses and lies I won't use them again.
Changed an inner tube on my wife's bike in 5 min 'flat'.  The guys were friendly, give them a try!  They sell cool t-shirts too.

The worst service beyond the hostess. Took forever! I ordered medium steak, got rare. The waiter was downright mean! An asshole! No smile, no how is your food? Wow! The gnat flying around our table seemed to be enjoying itself though.
Dennis- the owner of the shop is friendly, very knowledgeable and helpful. I'm a fan of Bike Friday's folding bicycles and Rincon Cycles is one of the few dealers in the country.

Leylin was absolutely amazing! She was able to find my dream wedding band within our budget and delivered the best customer service we've received, including from other jewelry companies. We will definitely be back to work with her for all our jewelry needs!

Stopped by with my little foodie group for a delightful brunch. The space is cute, in other words, I like the decor. 
And there is ample parking behind the building... in case you're not finding any out front. 
There are some favorite menu items but also those that you can not always find on the typical Mexican menu. So that's a big plus. And the brunch items are not that easy to find around town. 
I enjoyed having chicarrones for starters instead of the usual chips and salsa. Big plus for that. 
I had the breakfast torta with potatoes on the side. Btw, those breakfast potatoes are delicious. There are other veggies in there, like a hash of some sorts and then pan fried I believe. Either way I totally recommend those. My huge torta was also quite yummy, lots of meaty goodness. 
The portions were hearty and the prices reasonable. Could you eat cheaper? Yes, but would you get this quality of food? Probably not. 
Why not 5 stars you ask? The service was friendly but quite sketchy.

I've never eaten here before, but I did come for their bar and Cuban night club. It was so much fun! The drinks are not expensive, and you can either watch the people dancing or tear up the dance floor yourself! The inside is just amazing. They did a great job of decorating to make you feel like you're in Cuba, without being too tacky at all!

Every half hour or 20 minutes or so, they clear the dance floor and a professional Cuban dancing couple comes out and dances to a song. They even change their costumes for each dance!! It's beautiful and very entertaining.

Love this place and will be back soon!

The car wash shut down operating while i was in the wash. It really freaked me out but I was not trapped by any machines. I did want to get out of my car but the attendant insisted I get back in my car. System started and then stopped again. I again asked to exit my vehicle but was sternly told to remain in my vehicle. Eventually the car was washed and I was on my way. However, after waiting about a month later I went for a wash and I freaked out when the machine came around the car. Major panic attack that it would shut down and I would be unable to get out of the car...Cancelled my "over a year long" membership 3/19. Very disappointed as I really liked keeping a clean car. I would say 7 out of 10 car washed were a decent result, other times hit or miss on quality washes.

A great lunch spot or informal dinner spot.

Very tasty. I got the new "Pro Bowl" which is Paleo.

Just meat/fish and veggies.

It wasn't the heavy Indian that you think of when you hear "Indian."

One of the owners, Robby, came over to our table and asked us how we liked it. Excellent customers service.

I also got a fresh naan bread and it was very hot and freshly made out of the clay oven.

A very clean environment with a high-tech hand-washing machine.

Will definitely go back and will recommend it to my family members who usually don't like Indian food.

The sauces in the condiments bar were also very delicious!!!

Thank you, Robby, for a great experience.

Excellent service. We were a large group and our waiter went above and beyond to ensure a great meal.
I had the tortilla soup and it was excellent, the small pieces of avocado made it quite nice.
The red  chili shredded beef was a hit as well as the taquitos!
In a previous visit I had the posole' it's a bit more of a stew here but quite good.
The fact that they limit the free chips to 1 bowl is kinda of sketchy, no Mexican restaurant I have ever been to has ever charged for a refill of chips, So there's that as a negative.  .
But overall the food is good and the service is great!

After reading other reviews, I am glad that other people know Famous Fat Freddie's as well as I do.  The food is terrible.  Whether it is the pizza that is undercooked and covered with a tasteless watery sauce or the mediocrity of their steaks that somehow get served cold or the hoagies that have warm lunchmeat in them, it's all garbage.  The only thing worse than their food is their delivery service.  When you place an order and are told that the food will be there in forty-five minutes to an hour, you have the right to expect it in or around that amount of time.  When you are kept waiting for two to two and a half hours, that's another matter.  To top it off, when you call to complain about the amount of time the order is taking and speak to a manager, they respond with, "what do you want me to do about it?"  Here's what I did about it....I am spreading the word that Famous Fat Freddie's Pizza is a complete waste of  money.  The food is terrible, the shop dirty, and their service is a joke.  How this shop manages to still do business is beyond me.  The fact that this filthy establishment hasn't gotten someone sick yet is a miracle.  Look elsewhere for food.

The Omni Majestic is truly a three-star boutique hotel, but the excellent service my mom and I received while staying here bumps this place up a star.

We both had been flying from opposite ends of the country (me since the night before) in order to attend my grandmother's wake and burial. The funeral home was close to the Omni Majestic, and from the reviews I chose it as our home-base while we were in St. Louis.

I had phoned prior to our arrival to let them know we were coming. When we arrived Mark and someone else who's name I cannot remember (but she was the sweetest woman ever) greeted us. We were early, but they got us into a room quickly. They stored our bags while we got some food in our stomachs and off we went upstairs.

The hotel is rather dated. The elevator is old and the smell is not that great. It still smells like it's legal to smoke indoors-that's how long it's been since the carpet was changed out-or perhaps it was never changed out.

The room was nice. Bathrooms are tiny and the televisions are the old tubes. They also offer complimentary shuttle service within 5 miles.

The $25 a night parking fee is a bit steep considering the location and city. I'd expect that in Chicago, Miami or NYC, but not a boutique hotel in St. Louis. 

If you want nice, centrally located, clean-though dated and great service I'd recommend the Omni Majestic for your stay. If you want big and roomy with some glamour, keep looking.

All in all, such a pleasant and positive stay. They were very kind and accommodating during such a difficult time.
This is a delightful spot! Good coffee, wonderful atmosphere, and delicious treats (try the Samoa cookie!).
I love Raley's... this is my favorite place to get my groceries, everyone is so helpful and Manager Sean is awesome. Very helpful

Holy hotness! I was in the mood for something spicy so my boyfriend and I stopped by here completely on a whim. The first thing I noticed was the sign in the window that urged customers to call ahead. I'm a BIG fan of signage! This was obviously a hint that it may take them a while to cook my order. (I'm ok with quality food taking longer to cook) Honestly, I would much rather read a posted sign and make a decision based on the sign, than stand around like an idiot wondering where my food is or why it's taking so long. I appreciate that they at least took the time to post that sign. 

Moving right along...

I love food that makes me cry, so I went straight for the HOT tenders. Normally, when spicy food reaches a certain hotness, you can no longer taste anything (except pain.) These tenders were perfect! The seasonings were SO delicious! There was a gentleman that came from the kitchen to ask us how everything tasted and he mentioned that they just recently adjusted their recipe. So, for anyone that may have not been impressed with this place before, it might be worth another trip. In my opinion, yes I had tears running down my face, but I could still taste those awesome seasonings. THAT is great hot chicken! 

My boyfriend and I will definitely be returning for more!  (I'm eating my leftovers as I type this and shockingly enough, they are still delicious even after re-heating in the toaster oven.) 

My ONLY complaint (and it's a non-issue), one cup of ranch dressing is never enough for 6 tenders. Perhaps offer two?

We wanted to have lunch, few folks there..pulled in to asked where to park (not Valet) they said by Gil Dogs! So we came to one of our favorite spots Gil Dogs!! Sorry unless there is Reasonable parking I'm not going.  thank you for responding the Valet Guys
Didn't mention anything about Free Valet...?

Great for lunch or dinner, casual or formal. Pasta made fresh in house and the octopus appetizer is amazing, step outside of your comfort zone and try it! Sometimes parking can be tough but there are a few spots behind the building too.

Some of the best BBQ I've ever had in my life. Scratch that, THE BEST BBQ I've ever had in my life. 

About 5 feet from pulling in the parking lot you can smell smoked goodness in the air and it only gets better as you get closer to the building. 

Pro tip: get here early, because once those meats are gone, sorry Charlie! One guy in line said he had to come back because the line was out the door and wrapped around the building the last time he went. 

After you place your order, it's not much of a wait before you get your food. I ordered the BBQ sampler which came with chicken, ribs, brisket, and pulled pork and a side of Mac and cheese and collard greens. The meats were so amazing, perfectly smoked and bursting with flavor. I liked the sweet bbq sauce as an addition to the meats, the spicy sauce was a little too hot for me. 

As for the sides, the Mac and cheese was great, flavorful but not too salty or rich. The collard greens were okay, they have a strong spicy vinegar kick to them. I wish they cooked them using a chicken or vegetable stock back to truly give the greens flavor. 

Other than that, I'm definitely coming back. No doubt!
Fixed my cracked screen in about 15 minutes and it looks and works as though it's brand new.   Extremely courteous and the price was great.   Thank you!

My mum asked me if I wanted to have lunch here today. I was really surprised! Last time i went years ago I was not impressed nor thought it was fine dining. It reminded me so much as a sportsbar. Keep in mind this may have been decades ago! I haven't went since I was a teen.

Basically I wasn't expecting much.

The service was pretty good- the waitress was polite but didn't come across as friendly. 

The cheddar biscuits were okay. I like Red Lobster's more because they taste more savory than the sweet version here.

My mum had grilled salmon which was really delicious! And I had grilled shrimp (it was meh). 

We both had tomatoes in balsalmic vinegar as a side. Simply satisfying! 

And then we had the double choco cake. Tasty though I've had better. The icecream was really good. I wonder if it's homemade because of the colour and taste. :,) I was bad and ate more of it than I should have (I'm lactose intolerant). It was worth it though.

I look forward to going again. If me and my boyfriend have the munchies sometime I'll suggest a visit here!

in and out.  Lead dentist guy is super nice.  The rest of them are all business. No frills.  Dumb receptionists with no customer service skills.  Tiny waiting room with a tv.  Don't go here if you care about ambience.  Cute, funny murals.  Like the dentist version of every other south philly business. Kinda ghetto but they get it done.  Funny dental assistants.  Attitude problems galore.  Really helpful for the insureance-less.  Cleanings for a decent price.  I like it here.

What a shame.  I bought an oven thermometer here that did not work.  It got stuck the first time I used it.  I tried to return it (after being coughed on by the rude cashier and scolded for grimacing after said cough.)  I was told that they wouldn't accept the return without the packaging, even though we had an itemized receipt.  I had thrown the packaging out assuming that the product would not be faulty.  "What am I going to scan?" was his response, along with, "there are receipts all over the place!"  (Not quite sure what that one meant.) A person with the least amount of pride in their business would take a minute, look up the product, and help a new customer at all costs.  Unfortunately, this is not that sort of place.  We left all of our potential purchases and future business at the store and are never going back.  We are new to the neighborhood and so sad that we can't frequent our neighborhood hardware store.  Avoid this place at all costs.

One of those classic South Philly institutions that I'm surprised that I hadn't reviewed a few years sooner! But it took enjoying New Year's Day dinner with good friends to realize this as I reveled in delicious pasta!

Ralph's is thee timeless Philly Italian restaurant that has been around for over a century in one form or another. And while there may be a few Philly Italian restaurants that may have hit higher marks in one way or another, Ralph's is that classic spot that influenced them in that direction, while managing to still have some new tricks up its sleeves.

Both floors of Ralph's are great for enjoying old world eats. But the 2nd floor dining room has the added benefit of dining among some fantastic murals of iconic Italian landmarks. For drinks they have a solid wine list, and it doesn't get much better than an ice cold Moretti or Peroni for beer. But they also have an impressive cocktail list that's worth a look over.

For food pasta is very much on the menu! And it's bound to be a good decision whether you order it as spaghetti, ravioli, manicotti, penne, cavatelli, or other! But they also have plenty of old world classics like saltimbocca, braciole, linguini pescatore, chicken cacciatore; and their famous and HUGE veal parm! But for me I also like the selections from the portion of their menu that adds new twists to old recipes. The fettuccine alfredo with shrimp, pancetta, and peas I ordered was as phenomenal as it looked! But no matter what you get it's sure to be a solid Italian meal!

Arrived back in Nashville to pick up my car and the lot was closed. I called several times and left emails but nobody will return my call. I'm stuck in Nashville without my car and can't get to Jackson,Tn. Somebody please contact me so I can get my car

Let me start by saying that all the food was very good, but that was over shadowed by the extremely poor service we received before being seated. Since there seemed to be an extremely large group in the bar area my party who arrived before I did were seated down by the chef's table for their cocktails. once I arrived it took several minutes for the server to even acknowledge that there was even an additional person there. When he did eventually notice it took several more minutes for him to come and take my drink order and correct the wrong drink that a member of my party received. shortly after the replacement drink and a drink for another member of my party arrives but not mine. Then 10 minutes no drink, 15 minutes still no drink. I'm starting to fume at this point and the person whom I assume is the manager keeps walking by but not making eye contact. Appetizers which were ordered before I arrived have not arrived either. Said manager stops by to ask if the replacement cocktail was correct still no mention of my cocktail. The server was asked 3 time where my cocktail was and all he said it was coming (I ordered the punch which the menu states it's on draft) at this point I am ready to leave but since we are there for a monthly dinner date with friends that is not an option. my drink eventually comes delivered by said manager after a member of my party having a conversation him and we are ready to be seated for dinner as our appetizers arrive and the manager tells me the next one is on him( that was a relaxing cocktail before dinner shot to hell). we get out table and proceed to carry our appetizers to our table( yes I said that WE carried the appetizers to the table not a server, not the hostess, not the manager). the service during dinner was better but you have to think was this because we complained to the manager about the earlier issues. it is sad that because of bad customer service I was not able to enjoy my evening.

Great food, the atmosphere is a little cramped, but good for a casual night. I ordered the Blackened Alligator Po'boy. It was not on the menu, but suggested by the bartender. Definetely full of flavor (calories too, I'm sure). Try it out. The prices were not too bad for a tourist area.

Beds could be a bit softer but that is personal preference.

Overall, nice hotel, clean, friendly staff.

If you forgot something, you can call the staff for numerous things including slippers, woolite, make up/nail polish remover, and hair spray.

Took my son for graduation and the rest of the family. I was impressed they had Habachi bar made to order,Sushi bar, carve your own BBQ ribs and carve your own Prime Ribs all the seafood you ever want shrimp Blue crabs,mussels,clams,oysters and more. The best thing I liked was BYOB would go back and would recommend it. My family enjoyed it as well.

Pros: 
- Convenient for when you're looking to feed an overworked sleep-deprived film crew at 6AM on a Sunday on a tight budget

Cons:
- Food is so bad that even overworked sleep-deprived film crew would rather starve 
- Eggs tasted like paper, toast tasted like cardboard, potatoes felt like decomposing fruits (not that I would know)

I cannot understand how they can mess up the most basic sides.. but hey, I guess they're not exactly known for their eggs, toast, and potatoes

Paying a bit more for Dunkin' Donuts sandwiches/croissants is much more worth it.

Very disappointed with their service.  Went in for an oil change - it took an hour and 40 minutes.  They ran out of an oil filter and had to order one from elsewhere - rather than telling me, I was stuck waiting and waiting until I finally said something and was informed it would be "only a few more minutes."  So the customer service was less than stellar.

The actual servicing was questionable as well.  When I left, my check engine light was still on and my front right tire still looked underinflated.  I did not want to go back in for fear that it would be another half hour for them to do something they should have done anyway.  Avoid this place at all costs!

Update: Turns out I had a nail in my tire that was causing the underinflation and causing the check engine light to come on.  Turns out these guys either suck or do not bother doing a good job because they completely missed it.  Terrible.

Fido is a great stop for breakfast, lunch and coffee alike.

Located in the heart of Hillsboro Village, in an old pet store (the sign remains--I love it!), the space is ideal for nearby Belmont and Vandy students. While there's a ton of space, the restaurant is nearly ALWAYS full, from my observation--doesn't matter if you traipse on in at 7:30 a.m. or 2 in the afternoon... always packed.

I don't dislike their lunch options--I just prefer their breakfast menu. They always have a ton of great meals, as well as a case of scrumptious baked goods (muffins and carrot cake are my faves), beckoning to be snatched up. For coffee, the Bulldog is my go-to: espresso with coffee poured over. Before I moved to Nashville, my boyfriend and I would swing by, late at night and share a slice of cake. It's a sweet late night date night locale.

Service isn't bad--parking can be a pain... there's a paid lot behind the restaurant, just be forewarned that while the first 1.5 hour is free, you still need to go through the motions of obtaining a slip for your dashboard (I'm a small town girl--this is frivolous to me). 

Definitely swing by when you get a chance! One of my favorite coffee shops in Nashville.

Advertised incorrectly. Says we will get complimentary breakfast, there will be a jacuzzi, and none of those things are true. My key to get in my room doesn't work and the woman said she would have to downgrade me (AT THE SAME PRICE OF $200 A NIGHT) to be able to have a functioning key???? Just shitty service and policies at an unfair price. Really sad I chose to book here. Trying to celebrate my friends getting engaged and we are being bombarded with bad news from this hotel.

The food here was off the chart good. We had the baked Brie appetizer and it was fantastic. It was served with roast garlic and stewed apples, yummy! The duck with the pepper jelly glaze was possibly the best duck I have ever eaten, if you are a duck fan, get it (even if you aren't seriously consider it). We ordered three of there desserts and they were the perfect size and the best desserts we've had on the trip. The almond and chocolate mousse is something really special and I highly recommend it. The beer expert in the family gives this place high marks on beer. The only reason this gets four stars and bit five is that the live music they advertise on Friday and Saturday nights is not great and we hardly noticed throughout the meal. Go here for the food not the music

Todd, Aaron and the crew are awesome. They want to make sure you are happy and do great work at a reasonable price. I bought wheels, tires and have had service at this location and always experience perfection. Everyone gets along great and you can tell this crew and management like each other and really care about the customers. If you have to deal with the drama of a flat, they make it easy. And Todd and Freddie are really funny....I may be the only one that thinks so.....
Best sandwiches I've found in Florida, and the people working and making my sandwich were very friendly and helpful! I will definitely be back here another 5 to 5000 times!

The service was cheerful and attentive without being overbearing or insincere. They were very accommodating to an ever-expanding crew of females on a Saturday night. My sister-in-law got a beef dish with sweet potato mash that she said was very excellent. I loved the spring rolls. Thank you for a memorable evening!

Overall, very happy with the sizeable portions, reasonable pricing, and interesting tastes.

Started off with the shrimp summer rolls - these were your typical vietnamese rolls, with rice paper wrapping around carrots, cabbage, noodles and shrimp. Good taste with the peanut sauce (they also provide a honey vinaigrette which tasted pretty good). One order is also enough for 4 people!

Next were the thai sweet chili wings and spicy curry wings. These were a hit with our table, and definitely something we would order again the next time we come! The chili sauce and curry mayo tasted awesome, and the chicken itself was fried perfectly while keeping the meat tender

The lemongrass pork belly banh mi is also a must-order. One sandwich is enough for two people. The pork comes in thin slices (like bacon), and is rich with the lemongrass flavor. 

We decided to try both the crab fried rice and lobster fried rice. Each one comes with plenty of the respective seafood meat cooked within the rice, as well as a fried soft shell crab and lobster tail, respectively, on top. 
My only complaint is that the lobster tail was probably overcooked and too tough.

For dessert, we decided to forego that fried cheesecake and tried an interesting dish where sweet bananas were wrapped in spring roll dough, and then fried and paired with ice cream. Nothing mindblowing, but fun to eat and a nice way to end the meal!

Didn't expect it but, Byrne's Grilled Pizza was really magnificent. Clean and cute atmosphere. Byrne's Grilled Pizza has a fantastic Byrne's Specialty Pizza, that was my first time trying it here, and I was surprised with how good it was. Team ensured that everything was fine. I definitely recommend and would come again!

What a fantastically splendid experience. Everything from the ambiance to the service and especially the food was on point.

Online reservations were easy and we were delighted to get in New year's Eve at 7:30 p.m. We actually did arrive a bit early but they were willing to accommodate and seat our party of four at a comfortable table near a window. This place is gorgeous. Simple yet elegant and thoughtfully decorated. 

We tried an array of cocktails. All were good. My favorite cocktail of all time is a New York sour. This is not on the menu but they were able to recreate their version and it hit the spot. 

For our appetizers and entrees:

Chicken liver paté 5/5.  Really, really enjoyable. Portioned out on four pieces of wedged toast and caramelized onions. Geez it was delicious.

Pimento cheese 5/5. Again, they did this right. I'm a southern girl so I know my way around this dish. They used the right amount of pimento and the perfect consistency. The house made chips held up nicely and were a great accompaniment.

Octopus 4/5.  I've had more tender renditions of this dish. It did not pack a ton of flavor but overall enjoyable.

Bacon wrapped dates 4/5. This has never been a favorite of mine but the table did enjoy this.  I would probably skip this on my next visit but it's worth a try. 

House special black grouper with cauliflower puree 5/5. My friend and I were filling up pretty quickly from the appetizers so asked our server if it was possible to split this dish. No problem, not only did the chef split it and plate it perfectly, they also split the bill as well. 

Tamarind glazed pork shank 5/5. This was my husband's dish and he cleaned his plate.  There was a flavor explosion in this dish.  The pork was so tender and slow cooked. 

Speaking of service, Amy was phenomenal. Very personable, she took her time at the table with us discussing our best choices for cocktails and food dishes. She really is a pleasure. 

This experience was perfection. Very happy to have rung in the new year with this as my last dining experience of 2020. Barterhouse is going down easily as one of my top 5 restaurants in Tampa Bay.

This AMC is pretty on par with other major movie theaters I've been to. This location offers 3D movies, comfy recliner seats, online ticket purchases and a selection of movie theater concessions (including a bar). However, the movie options are pretty limited: they only ever offer about six options whereas the Regal UA will have about 12.

Good but some problems. 

The ambiance in the courtyard is stunning. The tables are cheap and wobbly though. The interior space is tired and cramped. 

The service was not good. Everyone seemed disinterested. It's never a good feeling to feel like you're burdening the staff by being there. 

Their waitlist system was a mess. 

Expensive for what it is. 

Food ok but not standout in general, especially for a city like New Orleans. 

Won't be coming back.
SLOW AS SHI*. They couldn't be more curteous and friendly. But they need to hire more than one person to take care of all car problems. Took 30 Minutes To Tell the Lady I Needed an Oil changed.

I am a retired occupational therapist. I have suffered from spinal stenosis for many years.  I went to Action Therapy for physical therapy since I had gotten recommendations from my colleagues.  Action Therapy was mentioned being one of the best places to get Physical therapy in the area.
From my experience with them, I am now referring my friends and colleagues to them. I found them to be extremely caring and knowledgeable .I was complaining about my hand. The therapist questioned what my pain was from. She questioned if it was from the Spinal Stenosis or something else. She wanted me to get an evaluation from a hand therapist to make sure that the pain was from my spinal stenosis .She helped me not only as a diagnosis but as a whole person, Action Physical Therapy also followed up by sending me helpful information to remind me of specific exercises to help me with my problems. I highly recommend them as the best!

We walked in at 9:00 and the waitress yelled across the room they were not seating any more tables , we drove from ashland city to come to this ihop location , i run a retail store and seeing that place empty and the way we were treated , i wouldnt have a job anymore , the management in this store really needs to be trained in customer service this was unexceptable

My wife's first mother day dinner! Place is nice very rustic! Parking is sufficient. Our server was absolutely horrible not a single smile through out the service, slow service! Food was good would have definitely gone back had it not been for the server!

I just visited this store, and walked out. My incredibly generous sister always brings me chocolate from here, and since I was in the area I decided to stop by. After waiting for 10 minutes to be served, these two ladies who walked in 5 minutes after me went up to the store employee and asked to order a gift certificate, the employee asked how much. I was so upset that the lady skipped me that I left without saying a word. Someone needs to take a course on how to properly serve customers!

A very well-rounded price fixe lunch menu. The oysters are great. Lovely service from Brandon and with such enjoyable food. We appreciated the 25-cent martinis for our group of seven. (Where can you get 23 martinis for under six dollars?) Great ambience in this historic place and highly recommend.

Such a great location! Right near the Villanova Campus. A fresh offering of farm to table menu with healthy options for anyone to indulge in!! 
I had multigrain pancakes w blueberries and maple syrup, others in our party had avocado toast ( delightful!) and we could not stop raving about their bread!!
Will definitely be back again!!

Back from when I was on tour with the Trio:

This is a true find.  I've eaten BBQ all over this fine country, and this is some of the best I've ever had, certainly for Idaho (no offense, Idaho).  

It's a Mom and Pop store in the best sense of the word.  If you want an haute cuisine experience, go somewhere else.  If you want a high value BBQ experience, stay here and get the Monzello.  The ribs are great, and the pulled pork is even better.  Tri-tip is just OK by me (although very tender), but I'm partial to brisket, therefore biased.  

--------

Small sidebar - there BBQ philosophy is a little nuanced for me. I like a very concentrated smokiness and overall flavor (I think Hickory is great); their approach is more delicate and balanced (for instance, they smoke with Pecan only).  Whatever.  Even though I'm more of a Syrah guy, I know a killer Pinot when I taste one, so I've "gotta respect."

No comments on the Vegan fare.

This was recommended as one of the best Chinese places by someone coming out the door.  The server was very nice, the food, on the other hand not so much.  They had just ran out out hot and sour soup and settled for egg drop- was cold.  Rice was burnt and the egg roll was over cooked and greasy. Would not recommend to anyone.

Good food. Reasonable prices. Speedy services. 

The lady owner does not like to smile, appears like she's angry all the time. But deep down, if you chat with her, she's friendly in her own way. 

Still ... that kind of angry face takes away two stars from this review. 

Recommended dish: shrimp fried rice. 

Also: Pho soup is delicious, and I don't even like Pho in general but I still find it delicious here.

After a long day on a Friday, I decided to head to old Chicago for dinner since I never bothered to make a reservation anywhere. My waiter after asking me if I wanted my margarita blended or iced and with salt and lemon, apparently forgot about it completely. Then when he got my drink finally (after almost done with my food), I asked him for dipping sauce for the fries. Again forgets it.!  I understand it's Friday, but I went there to celebrate something. So I did call out to tell him that he forgot my garlic sauce for the fries (anyways, I finished my fries by then). I decided to write down that the service was bad when I got the check (though these things tend to happen, I was mad that I din' get to enjoy my food,with me being a foodie.) But guess what, when I was about to leave, his manager came to my table and gave me a coupon for next meal. The waiter might have as well left it, but he did tell his manager about it I guess. 

Bottom line: lots of courtesy, good food.

The atmosphere here is amazing. Customer service is friendly and helpful. My friend and I sat at the bar and our food came in less than 10 minutes. This was our first time at the Grub Burger Bar and the bartender was very helpful in assisting us in looking over the menu. My friend got the Texas Luau burger and DEVOURED it! I had the mint chocolate chip milkshake and the Mac & Cheese Cheeseburger (I had to add the skinny fries, they didn't come with the burger but they were very good). I thought the milkshake was amazing and would definitely come back for another, but the burger was average and could have used a lot more bacon. Our bill came out to about $26 - a decent price. Overall our experience was pretty good and I would recommend to a friend!
Great place to eat! All of the tacos, burritos etc.. contain fresh ingredients. Also love the chipotle hot sauce.

Definitely the best wine-tasting deal in town -- $5 for 6 wine tastings (2 whites, 4 reds), and it included the glass!  For every other tasting room, I shared a tasting w/a friend, which worked out great - got to taste more wines, and still remain standing.  But here, we each did one, so we'd each have a glass to take back to our room. Cute stuff to buy there, too.  And in the afternoons, they have free olive oil tasting (we got a bottle of the mushroom/garlic one - so good - the basil one is great too).

Nice new Walmart Supercenter. Well stocked. The problem is not with store, the problem is with leaving Walmart. Exit on 73 South near impossible during rush hour unless you want to enter Fellowship Rd lane. If you are trying to get into the 73 South lane you have to move over two lanes. It's four lanes of traffic. The first lane is Fellowship Rd the second lane is for the NJ Turnpike. Basically the traffic in the Fellowship Rd lane is non-stop. You have to find someone who will let you onto 73 first. No one stops. Then you have very little time just to get into the NJ Turnpike lane just to have a shot to get into the 73 South. The Fellowship Rd exit from the Supercenter is no bargain either. There is no light so you have fight three lanes of traffic. One is for 73 North, the middle is for Fellowship Rd West and the third is for 73 South. Walmart knew there was going to be issues. There are plans for major redesign of 73 and Fellowship Rd. It is a massive project that will absolutely cause headaches for several years. The 295, NJ Turnpike and Fellowship intersection is one of the biggest bottlenecks in the entire state. Try making a left turn out of the Greyhound terminal during rush hour to get onto 73. I gave up after 5 minutes and went right and out of my way to get onto 73. The only vehicles that can force their way out and make a left are the buses. They block Fellowship West and cars will stop to let them get into Fellowship East.

Prompt service. Roughly 350 dollars to do an assessment. Nice peace of mind to have their specialized equipment to assess if there was a potential for a leak. Two gentlemen came to the house, wearing masks, and were polite and on time. Would be a great service to have when purchasing a new property. Very thorough.

Very good food and good service. If you are in a hurry this probably isn't your place as this place is always busy!! To me that is a good thing!  Great crepes and biscuits and gravy is very good also.
Wonderful food and our waiter Semaj was great!! And the manager Amanda was great also.

I have only been here once but apparently it was on the soft opening. The staff was friendly and helpful. I like coffee but I don't usually find one cup different from another. I had an Americano with coconut oil and it was the best cup of coffee I have ever had. I can't wait to go back!

I called Automotive Sound and Protection in Norristown PA to inquire about installation dates before I purchased my Groupon.
