They informed me of install availability and told me to purchase through them instead of the Groupon site and took a $50 deposit from me.  They then informed me the price for my vehicle would be significantly higher than the $119 Groupon price due to the vehicle type.  I told them to cancel and refund my $50, Heather who is one of the owners then told me "to go F*** myself".  To this point there has been no refund.  I wish I would have read some reviews prior to calling them as it is easy to see the consistent pattern of complaints.  As a business owner myself I always want other businesses to succeed but I think it is time for Automotive Sound to look in the mirror and make some serious changes if they wish to survive in a competitive industry.  I would run from this business as it is obvious they have serious issues!

Hattie B's is an above average fried chicken joint. 

Don't buy into the hyperbole of the Yelp reviews. I read these reviews beforehand and the image was conjured that some magician-turned-cook was reaching up into the high heavens and pulling down Fried Food Fit for the Gods and selling it for $10 to $15. The reviews are so hyperbolic that you'd think this can't even be chicken anymore - maybe Hattie B has discovered some animal in the Smoky Mountains that tastes like chicken yet is even better. 

Nope.

Lets start with the positives because you won't corral this many Yelp Elites without something special and distinctive. 

The secret sauce is the fried breading on the chicken. It's special and above average. It's thin and crispy in a distinctive way. The heat doesn't burn the tongue. It's a flare and then simmers - but still noticeable. Similar to Sriracha. You should probably get a drink. 

The sides are above average too. I got the black bean salad, potato salad, pimento mac and cheese, and banana pudding. That's a lot of food and only cost $17. I figured yolo. When you see this many Yelp Elite reviewers raving, it's kind of like a cosmic star alignment where you push the boundaries. 

The mac n' cheese is creamy without being too cheesy. Highly recommended. Black bean salad. Good with curious accents. Potato salad is just solid and good. 

To keep this in perspective, Hattie B's is a hole-in-the-wall restaurant that serves fried food fast (sans line). High customer volume, high turnover, with short preparation time. It's a good distinctive fast food experience. It's one of the better fried chickens I've tried. It's not a transformative experience that redefines your understanding of fried chicken. At least for me. 

There's a good chance I'll be back again, although I am in no rush.

Now here's why I give this place a B: My two main gripes: Outside of the breast, there ain't enough meat (that's why you should get the tenders or chicken breast meal). And although I got the medium it's pretty spicy and that distracted me from my enjoyment. The spiciness is also not a perfect blend with the fried chicken, where two essences mingle perfectly.

Here's what you need to know: Expect a wait, regardless what time of day you come out. Gratefully, I only had to wait 40 minutes. You have to hunt for parking so bring quarters. There is cheap metered parking but some parking lots charge $10. Don't use a parking lot.

It's good and worth the wait - but I don't think this is a bucket list foodie stop.

As a self-regarded high connoisseur of crap food (junk food), I appreciated the stop because it shed new light on fried chicken. But I don't feel it accomplishes the majesty and heights and full potential of what fried chicken can be.
I love this place. They don't just make pizzas. They have a wide selection from wraps to salads to desserts.. If I want something late night I choose this place!!

In-N-Out makes the best burgers ever! 

The place is clean and the employees are friendly! Their menu is simple. Three burgers to choose from. Hamburger, cheeseburger, and the double double. They have fries and shakes on their menu as well. They do have a secret menu. Animal style fries and burgers, which consists of grilled onions, cheese, and spread. The animal fries are OMG good! I love animal fries! There's other items on their secret menu (4x4 etc.) but their simple menu is perfect. Their burgers are my favorite!

SO WONDERFUL! 
I've had plenty of traditional Japanese ramen to know that this place is truly a gift. The staff are very kind and helpful and the food is delicious. I had the spicy miso ramen - which I've had elsewhere - and it was so worth the order. The crab ragoons were good as well. They homemade their ice cream everyday and it sells out fast! 
The big paintings on the walls are blown up versions of the owners tattoos! They support local artists and the restaurant definitely has a unique and special flare to it.
Happy hour 3-6pm for $5 .the buck O chicken is worth it 4 big pieces for $5 and came with crunch fries is very worth of a meal !!!
La Parada is a hidden gem. They have great prices and amazing food. Generally, they are your typical Mexican restaurant but with lower prices. The service was also very good!

First time here and I will definitely be back again. Almost all of the pasta is made by hand, which you can definitely tell. The bread, the desert, just everything was great! Also, our server Anthony made our experience even greater. He went through the ENTIRE menu with us and described popular items and just details about the entrees. He was also very attentive, funny, and just a really nice person overall.

Despite a rocky booking process, hotel monteleone more than made it up to us. After trouble over the phone, an apologetic manager not only upgraded out room to a beautiful top floor room, but even sent us a wonderful tray of fruit and water as an apology. The room itself was spacious and clean, no complaints. Courteous staff, an ideal location, and historical quaintness make this hotel a great place to stay. 

Spa Aria, the hotel spa, is excellent and does a top notch mani pedi, massage, facial, and more. Great prices and friendly service!

In the ideal location in the french quarter, I can't see staying at some large chain hotel over hotel monteleone for the price, and I would def return for their very affirdable summer discount deals!

IF YOU ARE ON VACATION and you need a place that has good coffee this is where to go. if you are looking for something that can serve a good cup like intelligentsia or handsome, come here. handlebar isnt as good but i think theyre on thier way. 

lattes are great.
they dont offer pour overs here.
but they have a decent drip machine that is above standard for a commercial drip machine. (we all know nothing beats a v60, chemex, etc.)

The Coil is always so well maintained, quiet, and clean. It's very modern and in a great location. I also like that it's so dog friendly and the front office staff are always so welcoming. I'd highly recommend living here!!

The food is okay, by no means terrible, but for some reason I go here at least once every couple months

The blood orange margaritas are usually to DIE for, and although the food (esp. the guacamole) can get pricey, they are pretty large portions.

I'm not sure what they marinade their mushrooms in, but it ruins the texture of the portebellos that come with the vegetable fajitas

They do have sweet velvet booths, and a little picture booth that my boyfriend and i take pictures in every time we go

This company  is a fraud. They came to my home and said that they did a repair that was not done.  My machine is under warranty and now they are billing me for the service. They have no people skills. They are a  dishonest company.  As a stay at home mother I will be sure to let all of my friends and family in my various groups and organizations know how dishonest this company is.  Please save yourself the stress and don't use this company.

Majestic. Longwood never fails to amaze us. Fountains. Beer Garden. Luminaries. The conservatory, walkways, tree houses and everything in between is breathe taking.  Must see- every season if possible.  As someone put it: if you don't have the luxury of visiting the world, you get the benefits of the sights of Longwood to give you a glimpse.

After hearing about this Deli from my German friends I was finally able to check it out.
Very happy they opened this place. Now I can get my specialties from them. They have Rollmops ( marinated hering ) one of my favorites. They also have quite a good selection of bottled beer and groceries.
I tried their cold Leberkäs which was really yummy. I had the Hackfleisch ( basically a larger spiced hamburger patty ) with potatoes and mushroom gravy. It tasted really good  but I was expecting the Hackfleisch to be made from mixed pork and beef, this one was only beef. I my opinion the pork would make it tastier.
Tomorrow is ribs and sauerkraut day, hope I can make it.
To start off not the cleanest place definitely below average. The service was poor, very rude workers, would not recommend to anyone. Not worth the trouble for the cheap price.

Hello. This shop saved me. I was on vaca from Cali and didn't have any gas with me. Tried their CBD flower and a few CBD concentrates, and it kept me right until I could head back. These folks have class and style. Gave great advice and got me set in the right direction, because I'm not knowledgeable on CBD products. Thanks to all the staff that helped me. I will definitely be back when I get back to FL!

Just getting around to figuring out Yelp, so I apologize for the late review. I was in town for a short weekend and managed to sneak a private city tour in with Will. I was pretty reticent as I've lived in New Orleans before, but some of our group wanted to do it so Livery set us up on short notice. What an amazing experience - it takes a life-long resident to really show you around somewhere and Will's knowledge was truly "insider", taking us to cool places I'd never heard of. Very informative and fun, I'd highly recommend that you ask for Will.

Great decor and ambience. This restaurant is tastefully decorated with tapestries, lanterns and elephants. When we first entered, the gentleman asked us if we had a reservation then left us for about 10 minutes to search for a table when 3 were immediately available from our viewpoint. We were seated at one of those tables.

From here, the situation improved greatly. Waiters were polite, prompt and attentive. The seating may appear crowded but once seated, there is ample space.

For appetizers, we had the Money Bags and Chicken Satay. The Money Bags are steamed shredded crab in a fried crispy shell, which were excellent with or without the sauce. The Chicken Satay was tender and the peanut sauce was delicious. The amount of chicken on one skewer was very generous, and there were three skewers.

The main courses were less impressive. The Pad Thai was too sweet and somewhat disappointing. The Green Curry was good but the sauce could have been thicker to make it perfect. Finally, the Drunken Noodles were excellent. Perfect spice level and flavor. All the main course had generous portions, and the cook on the meats and starches were just right. 

Overall, we had a great time and would come back again.

Best steak in Indy and great service too.   

Valet is $10. Unless you park on the street you'll pay this in a garage. Use the valet.

Epic failure.  Scary as they come.  Convoluted bureaucracy with messed up computer systems & call center, bizarre policies & processes.  The list of issues myself and colleagues have had over the years is right out of a SNL skit.  Express Scripts should get it together or shutdown.

I really loved this adorable bagel shop my sister brought me to when I stayed at her house a couple weekends ago. If I lived in the area, it would undoubtedly be the place where I would set up shop a couple times a week. It's cozy, with the brick oven cooking bagels and lots of comfy seating and coffee tables. Looks like they have lunch options in addition to breakfast.

Love that they serve La Colombe, and loved even more that they had bags of beans for sale as well, since I needed to stock up. While I didn't get my own bagel that day, I did try a bite or two of my sister's, which was delicious. Can't wait to go back next time I visit and eat all the carbs.

Amazing restaurant!!!! My favorite vegan place by far! I went before seeing Cats with my mom and grandparents. My mom and I are vegan and vegetarian. My grandparents are neither, but they will eat some vegan alternatives. I liked that the menu had both vegan and non-vegan sides. That meant that my grandparents could get real meat if they wanted it, and my mom and I could get vegan alternatives without having to pick out what on the menu was vegan. Our server was extremely accommodating to my mom's citrus allergy and to my grandfather's dementia. Overall 100/10!

The quesadilla was delicious! They make it with an awesome garlic sauce! 

The decor and atmosphere were weird, but the staff was friendly!

Don't stay here....Terrible front desk! Stood at the front desk for at least 10 minutes waiting to check in. Lo and behold,  the front desk person was the woman I passed on the way in. She stayed outside sitting on a bench and smoking a cigarette while I waited for her to finish. She checked me in with a 1st floor room. Before I went to the room I asked if she had something on a higher floor.  She said no. The room was terrible (landscaping lights shone in window/noisy/ no privacy). I went back to the front desk and said I wasn't going to stay in the room. She argued with me saying the room was fine and I had to stay in it. As I started to leave the hotel she said she would find me another room on the 2nd floor.  This clerk named Dawn was horrible.  Never will stay at a Holiday Inn again.

Location 
Parking 
Staff 
Halal Food 
Cleanness 
Atmosphere 

Paprica is our number 1 family favorite Restaurant in Philadelphia.

We go there almost every weekend to enjoy the Food with the Friendly & Lovely Staff.
Thanks to the Owners who won the trust of their staff and customers.. The owners always make sure everyone is happy and content before leaving Paprica

P.S Love the Te

Thank you from our Heart  

Can't wait to try the Turkish Breakfast on September

OMG i see results after my first session. I purchased a Groupon for the laser removal. I mean it was an amazing deal for a Brazilian treatments. I believe it is mid $300s for a huge area. I gave it a chance and it's working. I am African American so I'm always skeptical when it comes to spa treatments especially laser treatments since there are so many horror stories. I have 5 more treatments and will keep you updated eeekk!!!

What a pleasant surprise. An elegant bistro with a full bar! I had a terrific whole wild bass, filleted (not that tasteless pretender, 'Chilean Sea Bass' otherwise known as common Toothfish).

My wife had a spicy pasta dish, and we started with some lamb sausage and hummus.

Thanks, Yelp users for such a nice little find only 25 minutes from home!

Went on a Friday night. Staff was ridiculously in the weeds. Took 20 minutes to get a drink at the bar,while the bartender served everyone around us,after we ordered. Food was overpriced, but my ahi salad was delicious.
The poor staff looked like deer in headlights throughout our dinner. Will not be back.

Rich provided excellent service for us. We just bought a home with a gas Jen-Air cooktop/range that didn't seem to be igniting properly. He cleaned out the burners as best as could be done (the previous owner had clearly never cleaned or cared for the stove at all). The stove performance is greatly improved. Rich walked me through all the steps of what he did and how to clean them. He was also very honest about the fact that to get it working like new would cost as much (or more) than the cost of replacing the entire stove (due to the brand and cost of replacement parts). He was friendly, polite and definitely came prepared and competent to do the job.

Horrible customer service. I paid for a pick up order through the website and when the delivery guy arrived, I could not receive my food. He said my order had not been paid for. I paid through the app online. I also showed the delivery guy my bank statement showing the amount for my food being drafted out my account. The delivery guy then puts me on the phone with someone who works there. I thought she wanted to verify my card info but instead she just wanted me to pay AGAIN. NEVER will I order anything from this place again. I never had this problem before when placing an order online. I was on the phone for at least 5 minutes trying to get her to understand the food was paid for. I even gave her the shopping number for my order. She then proceeded to act like the phone was breaking up and she could not hear me. Extremely disappointed in how the situation was handled. She refused to refund me as well. Never order anything online or from this business. They do not appreciate their customers.

Poor and rude customer service... Including management who wouldn't even give the courtesy of speaking with us after I specifically requested to speak to management but instead they passed conversation between the front desk clerk and myself. Due to catastrophic flooding and storms that passed through Houston the day of our departure, our flight along with 500 others that day were cancelled. Our hotel reservation for our first night stay at another hotel happily refunded our stay. Hotel Preston (which was booked through Rocket Miles) and stated on there that we could cancel within 24 hours of stay refused a refund. We provided flight cancellation details from our airline and Rocket Miles requested the cancellation more than 24 hours but Hotel Preston still declined a refund. We've stayed there before and planned to stay many more times as we travel to the area often for work. Needless to say our company has decided to remove them from our list of hotels. Let's hope the $120 they made off of that non-refunded night is worth the loss of business that they will no longer receive from our company.

Beware !! Check your lug nuts before leaving they forgot my front passenger lug nuts they never tighten them which after 3 days of tire wobbling my hub bearings in the passenger front tire started making real bad sound which they said I need it replaced which they claim the loose nuts would not cause it to go , so it cost me $300 
  Plus alignments are not done there they drive your vehicle elsewhere to have it done and don't give any print outs on your alignment , just found out another customer tire fell off because they forgot to put lug nuts on his front tire

Whether I'm looking for somewhere nice to have a sit down meal, or looking for a quick to-go breakfast on my way to work, this place is always one of the first that comes to mind. The portions are huge! The service is quick and friendly. The food is amazing. There's not much more I could possibly wish for from The Cup Café!

The crunchy French toast with a side of their thick cut bacon is as close to perfection as it gets.
Authentic Chao Zhou Restaurant in St. Louis

Waited 1 hour to get our appetizer and another hour to get our entree. I ordered the pistachio flatbread pizza, and it was bland and tasted bad. Our wait staff was poor. The hostess began serving our table because we were not being served by the waitress. We left all of this!
When one succumbs to the fears of government propaganda it will look like this place/ They've fallen for every scare imaginable. Masks required at all times! No thanks, bye.

On March 9, I made an appointment with BMCI to have the roof on my Florida room redone.  I went with this company because they were recommended by Vivint Solar and I was getting their solar panels.  Alex comes out a week or so later and making a long story short, walks around the entire roof before I stopped him and said I was only looking to have the roof redone on the Florida room.  I have zero idea how that could have been misunderstood.  The roof on the remainder of the house didn't need to be redone.  In hindsight, I should let him have it and try to convince me it DID need to be redone.  I took time off work on my second job to meet this guy and the company was recommended so I didn't have any misgivings whatsoever.  Alex was not capable of giving me an estimate at that point but told me after studious measuring that someone would get back with me 'shortly.'  I have no idea what 'shortly' means to BMCI but almost two weeks later, I called to get the estimate and Ken asks me what the measurements were on said roof.  I told him Alex should have input that info and I was assured that he did but gosh, could I confirm those figures?  Ken wants to apologize profusely and somehow managed to bring Vivint into the conversation.  He assures me that Vivint customers are treated 'special'.  Yeah, like I don't think so but anyway, I'm not sure what the problem is... The Florida room has nothing to do with solar panels and English sounded like it was the primary language of every person I spoke to at BMCI.  To add more hilarity to the situation, Ken wants to come out again and get me an estimate and I don't have to take time off work.  Really, kids?  Why did I have to take off work the first time?  What part of 'Florida room' gets confused with the 'entire house'?  What was Alex measuring if you need to ask ME for the measurements (which I knew btw compliments of the roofing company that did the job)?  I got an estimate emailed to me yesterday (3/28) which was approximately one grand more than the company that did the actual work with a 10-year warranty.  No, I don't think I'll be going back to BMCI any time soon.

The "girl" at the counter was both defensive and had attitude when I asked why no one was answering the phone.  She said she was "busy" and wouldn't keep people on hold for 5 minutes. I said ok and asked if I should just leave my order on the answering machine and she said no it was better to just come in.   

Considering I work in the area and do not have a long lunch break, this wasn't great on the customer service side and may cause some people to go elsewhere.   

That having been said, the place was nice and well maintained. There were about 8 other people in the place and the food was very good.   I will come back as long as I have the time.

My rating would have been higher but the wait time was a little long and the desk left me in an off mood.

Have been here twice- a great AYCE sushi place!  You first start with appetizers, and boy do I love their list...The angry bird is my favorite!  I've ordered a few different rolls both times I've gone and they have a good selection and even on very busy Saturday nights food comes out fast.  I've tried the banana tempura dessert and mango mochi and both are delicious.

I have taken my girls here for over 5 years now and we love it! We have seen it grow and switch owners a couple of years ago but it still remains to be one of local favorite restaurants. You pay a bit more for Pho compared to South Philly but it's worth it since it's only 5 mins from our home... plus it's a byo!   My girls expanded their palettes and love their fried rice, pad Thai and drunken noodles. Their green curry is also tasty! They have great service. You still see the previous owner helping out and greeting the patrons.

First time ordering from this place online and received a missed call from restaurant.  Call back and the manager rudely asks me "are you going to pick this order up? We are not doing delivery tonight."  I placed my order an hour ago and just now got a call that i will not be getting my food.

Not a great first time experience and definitely my last!

This place is amazing! First time I came here was a year ago, had the best oysters in New Orleans. One year later came back again, still the best oysters in New Orleans. I've tried them at every place I ate, nothing even compares with this place. But what truly made my experience amazing was the bartender Sam. This man should get at least a raise if not an award of some kind! He joked around with us, made us feel very welcomed! Honestly the best experience ever! I've traveled a lot around the country and have never met such an amazing bartender. He's the reason I will be coming back every time! Word of advice, if you can go sit at the bar and ask for Sam, I promise you will not regret it! And order oysters and Crawish Queso Blaco. It's truly amazing!

I know.  In 2012 I made a case for the fifth star.  I can see the logic of my argument, but after last night's experience I have to say that it is not all about the oysters.  It is also about what accompanies the oysters.  And in this case what accompanies the oysters is just too much: too much noise, too much salt, too much waiting, too much change, and too much of a bill at the end.  I appreciate that we get $12 off of the normal $22 parking costs across the street.  But if it comes with a $30 hike on the price for our typical meal, it makes the trip downtown too much effort.  

Now we only get down here once every year or so.  We are not exactly regulars. We were not surprised to find that their oysters are spectacular, as usual, and in a world where there are no other oysters with the same freshness and the same adept preparation it would be well worth the trip.  But now there is some competition, and, while it is just as expensive, it may be time to check it out.

Why?  Because the rest of the meal was inferior.  The Southern fried oysters, a classic and my wife's favorite, were overcooked and dry.  The arugula salad, which looked healthy and low fat on the menu, tasted strongly of bacon.  The brussel sprouts with bacon came with way too much bacon (and, yes, you can have too much bacon).  But saddest of all, they changed the recipe for the snapper soup making it far too salty.  Shame on the chef.

The whole meal?  One salad, two cooked oyster small plates, one bowl of snapper soup, three beers and one dozen oysters on the half shell cost well over $100.

Good oysters, though.
Great firm, great people. Darrell is the best broker in this market.

Not as good as they used to be.
A few years ago way better now not so much.
The last few times I went was not tasty. Frozen fries.

Came for restaurant week. Just horribly disappointing. $35 for a soggy bruschetta, monotone veal on a bed of mushy pasta with flavorless tomato sauce, and a tiny cannoli with gritty cheese. Our whole party was extremely disappointed with everything we got. The servers were nice and attentive, so I can't give it 1 star, but I would honestly second guess eating here if it were free. Maybe it's better when it's not restaurant week.

First, I am a pizza lover. If pizza is not my favorite food, that is only because lobster edged it out. I am very particular about my pizza and would choose something else if the pizza available is subpar. Hence, I avoid chain pizza and supermarket pizza,  both frozen and fresh. I am not interested in pizza that is "not bad" and won't give it a second chance. 

After a hair appointment that ran late and still needing to grocery shop at Trader Joe's, I decided to try Pizza Luna for a quick bite in the Joe's shopping center at Speedway and Wilmot. 

The restaurant had been a Cup It Up. The scuttlebutt was that they closed due to politics, but likely it was because the food wasn't great. I tried it once, deemed it okay, and that was that. 

Walking into Luna, the first Impression was olfactory: The garlicky tomatoey fragrance could bring any pizza lover to his knees. I was enthusiastically welcomed at the order counter and was asked if this was my first time. Since it was, I was handed a menu and offered a very helpful guide to ordering. Three different styles of pizza are offered and each can be tailored with the toppings of choice. My guide raved about the fig, but I opted to go the more traditional route of house-made fennel sausage and onion. Advised that my pizza would be ready in seven minutes., I was thrilled; it was now 2:00 and I was starving. 

Seven minutes later, my pizza was ceremoniously delivered to my table along with its intoxicating aroma. I picked up the first piece, dripping molten cheese, burned my tongue, and didn't care. The crust is perfect: thin - but not at all cardboardy - with a delightfully chewy edge. The sauce is faintly sweet and deliciously present but not dominating. The fennel sausage is, frankly, scrumptious. I'd take credit for amazing ordering, but the young man at the counter deserves a nod and Pizza Luna deserves the rest of the credit. 

If someone in your party is not a pizza lover (what?!), lovely salads and an antipasto board are available. A lunch special offers a slice with a salad. Beers and wines are on menu as well. 

Tucson had two pizzas I will eat; then there are three.

Slowly but surely I'm trying to make my way to every John Besh restaurant in New Orleans. After attempting to come numerous times during happy hour, each time being a bust due to an hour + wait, I was able to finally snag a table with a group of friends. I've never heard of a restaurant where you have to make a reservation for a table during happy hour, but due to the popularity of this place, I can see why it's necessary. Everyone at the table ordered different pizzas, half and half so fortunately I was able to sample more than one. 

-Clam. My absolute favorite. The combination of flavors on this thing was amazing. I regretted not ordering a whole one. 
-Wild Mushroom. I personally don't like mushrooms but for anyone that does I can see this being a favorite. Everything else on this pizza (tomato, fontina, caramelized onions, bacon and egg) made it delicious. Might I add this is probably the best bacon I've ever had. 
-Tartufo (burrata-yeah I had to google it too lol. It's some type of Italian cheese made from mozzarella. Mascarpone, truffle oil, and egg.) Probably my least favorite. It just wasn't hitting it like the others. 
-Prosciutto. Wasn't bad but wasn't outstanding either. I love prosciutto but this pizza just wasn't great. 
-Calabrese (tomato, spicy salami, mozzarella capers and olives). Really really good! 

Overall each pizza had its own unique taste and was pretty good. The crust is delicious too. Would I say this is the best place to get pizza in Nola? Not even. Definitely worth trying though at least once. And especially during happy hour when they are half off and the drinks are as well. I'm looking forward to coming back and trying some of their other dishes as well as the dessert which I've heard fantastic things about.

I went into BGI North this morning because the right brake lever on my Cannondale Trigger (that I bought from BGI) was broken from a collision I had with a tree. I saw how busy it was and, knowing that Covid-19 had caused a lot of people to take up cycling, I expected the turnaround time to be weeks.

When it was my turn, the gentleman at the counter looked at it and said, "Let me look up the part. It may be out of stock at the supplier." At this point, I was thinking, "I'll be lucky to get the bike back by Halloween."

One of the managers - Mark - walked by, took the bike, and started to work on it. After a few minutes, he told me to leave it with him and he will call me later in the day. At 4pm I received a call saying my bike is fixed. Mark was able to use a part he had and just charged me labor. I got my bike the same day.

It is this level of personalized service that gains companies like BGI loyal customers. I am very grateful for the service that Mark and the team provided and am a loyal customer for life.
Amazing food!!! The spice is true, medium is medium and HOT is HOT. Best Thai food in Indy!!

The only real issue I had with this course was the lack of effort by the staff to maintain proper pace of play. We spent at least 10 minutes sitting in our carts between shots. There was no starter(even though we were told upon arrival there would be) and we didn't see the ranger until the 16th hole. Other than that, course wasn't in bad shape at all. Tee boxes were a little sandy but other than that I would absolutely play it again.

My review is only about the grooming experience I had with my dogs. I am new to the state of Florida. I had just rescued my second dog a few days early and brought him here along with my other dog. After I gave my dogs to the groomer and started to walk out she let one of them go and he almost ran out of the door so I grabbed him and brought him back. When I tried to hand him to her she said "oh I can't hold the dogs." How do you possibly pick them up and put them on the grooming table then? Then, I was backing my car out and I see that she had let him go again so I put my car in park and began to run after him when I saw a police man grabbed him. Finally, she brought him back in and closed the door so he couldn't escape again (he weighs 9.4 pounds so it's not like his strength made her drop the leash) so I thought ok I will leave them there but will call to check up. I called eight times in the two hours they were there and no one would answer. I finally called for the ninth time, thinking if they don't answer this time I will just go there and see what is going on, she answered and I said how is Kronnie doing. Her reply was "who"? (She didn't even know my dogs name) I said, "the small black dog you are grooming. The one who ran in the street twice. Is he fine or should I come get him"? She said, "oh. They are almost done you can just come get them in ten minutes. When I get there I see my girl dog, Eiffie, who I had asked to be down to a 1/4 of an inch barely looked like her hair had been cut and when I asked if their teeth were brushed she said no. I explained that I had asked for that when I made the appointment. In a tone that seemed like she was incredibly annoyed she said, "you have to ask for that when you drop them off but fine I'll go do it now." So she went back and it took like two to three minutes tops, so obviously she barely did anything to their teeth. I got the teeth brushing free but did not get an apology for all the terrible things that happened. When I told my grandma and mom they said they had never heard of anything so awful. I have these two dogs and a cat and am very protective of them. I had eiffie for over a year and a half and took her to get groomed every four to five weeks before this horrible experience when I was trying to find a place in the new city I lived in and with a new puppy I adopted. Please whatever you do, do not take your babies here.

Fixed our washing machine in a quick and efficient manner. Reasonable price for the parts and repair. I was told the price before he did the repair. 4 stars because he left all the stuff he moved out of my laundry room in the hallway after rather than cleaning up after himself. But other than that I was satisfied.

I used to rent -religiously- from Enterprise until yesterday.  I picked up my full size car on 10/8 for a 2 week rental.
FIRST- the agent put my in a very funky smelling car with  a pint of windshield wiper fluid.  Upon returning it on 10/23 I left a note in it describing my issues with the car during the rental:

FOB would not unlock the doors consistently- had to have AAA  unlock it 2 times
the infotainment system was shaky at best- blue screen of death appeared 2 times
the lighting for the infotainment system would work sometimes.
The gas door would not release - 
the back up camera engaged when you are driving -FORWARD
the check engine light kept coming on and going off the entire time

What surprised me the most was that no one at Enterprise cared about my sub par rental experience.  I was/am not looking for $, since I'm sure they already charged my cc full price for the sub par rental, but they made no effort at all to contact me to discuss the issues I documented and left for them.  I am in the customer service industry on a senior level and could not even fathom leaving a paying customer with such distaste in their mouth. 

Very disappointed in Enterprise, at least it's for the last time,

Brian Jones

#-7botfj-
The food here is inconsistent. At times I have been very happy with their flavor and spice level whereas at times the food has been mediocre- no flavor at all and chicken is very chewy

This will be my last time going to boba cup everything is going downhill. From no longer ordering from a person to ordering on the kiosks the kiosks malfunction and the staff had an attitude like it's my fault. First off these tweens don't know who they are speaking to as I have a masters degree in IT I told them it wasn't working and it changed my order they just looked dumbfounded. They are so rude here must be stafff changes or basically layoffs because who needs people when u have robots.
RE: The Dry Cleaners: Good quality. Reasonably fast service. Easy location for pick-up and drop-off. Prices are average and maybe a bit high, but they do good work. Family run business.

I was definitely disappointed in this place. It's really close to our AirBnB, so we stopped in to get drinks. 

It was 6:45, and the sign on the door said they closed at 8. When we went up to the register, the employee said, "Our kitchen is closed." I replied that we were just stopping in for some drinks to which she stated, "well, we're closing up in 15 minutes, so I guess I can get you drinks." We each got a glass of wine (we knew we could drink these in 15 minutes), which were really expensive for the brands of wine they were. 

The café itself is very welcoming on appearance, but the atmosphere was very odd and not something I'd probably come back to.
I can't believe I have never eaten here. Ate here yesterday. Ordered the taco, refried beans and rice meal, Togo. It was AMAZING. Hands down delicious. One of my new favorite places to eat.
Every time we're in St Pete and crave burgers this is the ONLY place to go! Their burgers are consistently juicy, tender and cooked to perfection!

This was DEE-LISH! We are brand new to Indian cuisine.  The staff was so welcoming and hospitable.  They made excellent menu suggestions based on our preferences.  Shrimp tandoori and chicken tikki masala were our picks this time.  Was so good we mopped up every bite with fresh naan.  
Can't wait to return and try more adventurous  meals.
Good bar food!! Great location with outdoor seating and water views! Pricing is great too for the amount of food you get!

After searching for delivery for thai restaurants, we came across this one. Have to say, some of the best coconut rice and green curry I have tried! Ordered from them a few times after and ordered the same thing. Definitely recommend!
Good Food - lovely place - we dined outdoors - enjoyed service and food - good selection of beer.

Being in the spa biz it PAINS me to pay retail - but for certain items that I must have NOW (and can't or won't wait for shipping online or ebay) I get at Douglas.

They even carry Bond fragrances.  (Love 'em.)  Also my 3 year old is a girly-girl and runs straight to the CHanel display to play with lipstick whenever we are there.  She adores Coco Pink.

FAVORITE coffee place in Louisiana ! The Southern Pecan is to die for! Came here back in April when I was visiting my Family in Kenner and returned again as soon as we got in because I had to get My Coffee fix! Can't wait to come back in tomorrow- the girls Are super sweet and the coffee is always delicious. I got so excited I dropped my coffee lmao. Wish they had this  spot up in New Jersey. A must try if you are in the Kenner area.

We have eaten at this Panera Bread many times. While we enjoy the food, this store has become neglected & could use some upgrades & cleaning. Also the surrounding homeless population congregates inside the restaurant, takes baths in restroom, uses Wi-Fi, charges phones, drinking coffee (free), takes up booths & seating, etc. On 11/20 at 6p.m when we ate dinner, there were more "street people" in restaurant then customers, & security guard for the shopping center was socializing with them. It just isn't a clean environment for eating. We will choose another restaurant or possibly another Panera Bread location.

Foster's was amazing! I had to go in due to a radiator problem and they got right to it. I do not know much about cars but they found and fixed the problem right away. The price was way cheaper than expected and as far as I know, it was pretty fair. As well, I had work and could not make it in time to pick up my car before closing and the crew was nice enough to open the shop the next morning on their day off just for me to come pick up my car. Overall, a great experience and the next time I have car problems (which I'm sure will be soon) I will definitely be back.

As a complete newbie to yoga, I can say that Moksha is definitely a great place to get started. The studio is very welcoming, and there is no feeling of judgement towards a newbie. So if you are considering starting yoga, specifically hot, this is a great place.

The intro membership at $50 for one unlimited month is a great option. They also have $5 Karma classes on Friday evenings that might be a good way to wet your toes a bit, but honestly go to for the $50 one because you shouldn't just try it once. Different instructors have different styles of teaching, and there are also different types of classes which the website gives you a good description of. I have tried the Yin Yoga class and Moksha Yoga, and both are great in their own way. 

I can honestly say, after only one week of practicing yoga, it really is a zen experience. My body just feels so calm after. And it's still a great workout, as long as you really listen and give each pose your all!

Some tips for newbies are:
- the MindBody app is great to see class schedule.
- You can buy your passes online and even register online (although registering for a class is not necessary). 
- Arrive 30 minutes before to lay down your mat and you can either stay in the room and acclimatize or wander out and come back. 
- Bring a towel for your mat, and a towel for your face. If you don't have a mat, rent one for $2.
- Keep your valuables at home as there isn't much for lockers, although if you really need, they do have tiny little ones for a cell phone/wallet.
- Drink plenty of water the day you are going, and bring into the studio with you.
- Remember, you can always lay down, or even leave the room, if you need to. 
- You will sweat buckets. 
- Just listen to the instructor's voice.
Great old southern BBQ. Had thee brisket with beans and vinegar based slaw very good. My wife had donkey Ed turkey tho and said very dry. Still a good place tHose a smokers are doing their job...

Yao is the BEST. My gel lasts three weeks, never chips or peels. She takes her time during pedicures and knows when her clients want to chat, and when then just want to sit back and relax. The salon is clean and I'm sure everyone there is great, but I will ONLY go to Yao for all my nail care needs while living in Spring Hill.

Went and grabbed a small order of the buffalo fries with a side of chipotle sauce 
Small-10.56(w/military discount)

The check in process was pleasant, but some parts of the hotel are looking a little shabby like the hallway carpet. The grout in the shower is stained and poor caulking and maintenance make it look pretty bad. The bed squeaks but was still comfortable. The chair seat had a problem and was very uncomfortable to sit in.

I saw their Groupon for a $20 haircut so I called before purchasing to see if they could fit me in the same day.  The woman I spoke with was friendly and accommodating and told me not to buy the Groupon- that they would honor the price.  The space is small but clean.  I had a great shampoo, quick but efficient cut and a great blowdry.  It's only $20 for first-time customers , after that it's $38.  I was skeptical and usually pay 10x that for my hair but this worked out great!  I definitely recommend them.
Totally outdated from the reception area all the way to the rooms. The whole hotel needs renovated. Bartender at the bar was not friendly and or attentive. Convenient location though

My wife and I ate here 2 nights ago. We were celebrating our 52nd anniversary. I ordered the Ribeye and she the Tenderloin. 

The good: Service was excellent and friendly.

The bad: My Ribeye was tough and flavorless. Tasted like a steak from Walmart or Winco. I sent it back and got the Tenderloin. The Tenderloin was tender but had very little flavor. 

On our last visit here I had the Halibut which was very good.

If you want a good steak I would not recommend The Cottonwood Grille.

This was a fabulous experience!  Check in was very quick and the office staff was very pleasant.  The doctor, Dr. Keith Graham Williams, was wonderful! Friendly, knowledgeable and resourceful. I got my prescriptions right on site quickly and out the door!

Over the past year the service and quality has gone down.  Dip nails only last 10 days and they chip.  Most of the girls are sloppy with filing and just try to get to the next client.  Try somewhere else... Like Dazzling Nails on Haddon Ave.

Pinefish's happy hour is great! You get some awesome deals for food and drinks. You also have buck a shuck oysters, which are great. The best thing is that their happy hour still happens on weekends! 

We ordered a variety of small plates. My favorites were the truffle mashed potatoes, brussel sprouts, and calamari. We also had their chocolate cake for dessert, which was great!
Fun clean atmosphere! Tara at the front desk was very helpful. Good food and drinks offered right on property! Close to Busch Gardens!!

Our family loves Mama's Pizza! We have never had a bad experience at this location. We have attended in big groups as well. We enjoying sharing this place with friends & family. Food comes our quickly & hot even of busy nights! 

The breadstix are one of my favorites in town! Always come out so hot you can touch them for the first few minutes. Cheesy garlic bread also good. 

The pizza this is so good! They have a family size pizza yes it's around $34 but so worth it! We have had 10 people eat off it!  1 slice more than fills you up! I have to have ranch with mine. The ranch is so good & thin. 

The Cannoli's here are also delicious. Honestly, I haven't had anything here I haven't enjoyed.

Excellent meal, excellent service, so I am saddened to report one negative development that sort of puts a nasty dent in the whole experience for me. A short, dark haired employee (kitchen help or bus boy, I think) used the men's room and left  without washing his hands. Now, if he's not taking hygienic steps with a customer right in the room, do you imagine he ever washes his hands when alone? This may not bother others but it was very disturbing to me.
I was rear-ended in my rental and the attendant went above and beyond to complete and make copies of my paperwork quickly so I would not be late for my flight.  Great service.

I love coming here after a long day at work...The family pack is a great value for the family.  My husband likes the fried green tomatoes so I usually pick some up for him.  The staff is always friendly.  If you are not looking for fried fish they have grilled options.  They will substitute the french fries with veggies.  My kids always eat everything on there plate when we come here which is an amazing feet in itself...

I worked with Mike Manguso on the purchase of a new Highlander.  This is the second car we've purchased at Weiss Toyota with Mike and he truly made whole experience great and easy - from selecting the car, to purchasing and  driving off the lot.  We would highly recommend Weiss Toyota to anyone.

I got there as soon as they opened and the service was excellent from the moment I got there. I wish I got her name but my nail tech did an AMAZING job. It's so hard to find someone who does a seamless acrylic set but she executed as well as filing them perfectly. The best acrylic set I've had. My pedicure was also amazing. He took his time to make sure my toenails were clean, filed, all dead skin gone which I appreciated so much and to make it better the massage had me passing out hahaha. They definitely do not rush your experience and do a quality job! I highly recommend.
Worst bar in Newtown.  Too crowded and rude staff.

My experience at Dickey's was mediocre at best.  My party of three each ordered our meals at one end of the counter.  Two of us ordered side items that weren't immediately ready (waffle fries and fried okra).  The  rest of the orders were added to the trays and slid down to the other end of the counter, where the cashier entered them into the computer. Empty paper bowls were marked with the missing side items.   Drinks, silverware (nice plastic ware), and condiments including BBQ sauce were self-serve, and we carried our trays to a table. I had a 3 Meat plate and chose pulled pork, brisket, and spicy cheddar sausage.  All three meats were barely above room temperature. The brisket had good smoky flavor but was very dry. I am used to smoked brisket slices that you can cut with a fork, but this was actually chewy.  The pork had good flavor and did not need sauce, but again, it was cold. The cheese in the sausage wasn't even melted.   My sides were "baked potato casserole," which is actually mashed potatoes topped with cheese and green onions, and jalapeno baked beans, which were the best thing I tried.  After tasting all of my meats and sides, (this was about 10 minutes) I realized that my kids' fried sides had still not been brought out.   I walked back to the counter to ask about them, and the staff seemed very confused.  5 minutes later, fresh hot sides finally came.   Free vanilla soft serve is a nice touch at the end.  All in all, you can get much better barbecue and service at City Barbecue or Squealers.  I'm in no rush to go back.

Had my first appointment with Cheyenne today. She was the best! I sort of knew what I wanted, but not really....and she gave me such a great post-COVID haircut!!! I had 5 inches of just blahhhhh and she shaped me up and gave me life again. Cheyenne was pleasant and easy to talk to. The salon was clean and quiet, but a steady stream of customers came in. If you're looking for a new stylist, I recommend Cheyenne at Fringe.

We loved everything we had! We ordered the Malibu, 2 street tacos, 2 Johnny Utahs,  a Shrimp Baja bowl & the fire roasted avocado... so good we had to get it again. My son ordered a quesadilla and was amazed that anything that good could come from the kids menu.   Highly recommend!

This little place is really nice if you want simple, easy, and tasty food and coffee.  The owners are really friendly and make a lot of the cream cheese spreads themselves. The salt bagel on Sundays with lox is worth is great!

I have been visiting the area for 4 weeks for work and have ordered from here once a week so far. China 1 is easy to order, online or by phone, and the delivery is VERY fast, usually about 20 minutes. Have had Beef & Broccoli, Sweet & Sour Chicken, Wonton Soup, Fried Rice, White Rice and vegetables. Everything has been delivered hot and delicious. With enough for leftovers for the next night. As often I travel, and try foods across the country, this is a great fast Chinese restaurant.
Sushi bistro is great!!! My favorite sushi restaurant in St. Louis by far! Fantastic quality fish!

They actually gave me tzatziki this time! Of course I double-checked before leaving, but hey, can't complain. This earns them back a well deserved star. Food is delicious as always, great pricing for what you get (can't beat the falafel platter). Also feel like the hummus tasted even better this time around.
Very authentic, tasty and fresh. Food just like their other location on Tarpon Ave. but larger and more tables for dining in.

I've been fortunate enough to travel to Lockhart, Texas (aka "Hill country") to visit family and indulge myself with barbecue.  Percy Street is the most authentic I've had outside of the Lone Star State.  I love that they have both single and family-style sides and portions on the menu.  Start your meal off by choosing a drink from their plethora of beers and then get down to business with the brisket!  Don't scrimp on their house-made barbecue sauces, either.  I'm limited when it comes to their sides, but my friends really seem to enjoy them (especially the mac and cheese).  The cafeteria-style seating creates a nice atmosphere, but the bar is just as nice to sit at, as well.  Definitely a favorite restaurant of mine.

Oh and by the way, the Hillbilly Gatorade is not for the faint of heart (or stomach).  Miller Hi-Life, pickle juice, and barbecue sauce make for an interesting combo...
Love this place, very cute atmosphere, fast service, and the pho is soooooo Good! I highly recommend this place to everyone. Prices are very reasonable. I always have leftovers to take home.

My girlfriend and I were visiting Philly for a work trip and had an extra day to tool around the city today. We both love whiskey and found a Groupon for Red Brick Distillery and decided to do a tasting in between meals and historic sightseeing. 

Only 4 people showed up for the 1PM tasting, which made the whole experience intimate and really chill. My girlfriend and I shared an Old Fashioned at the beginning of the tour and got to bring it along throughout the entire 45 minute tour, which was really cool. The bartender (Nate) and the distiller (Brian) were both very knowledgeable and articulate and walked us through a 'how to' of distilling and tasting whiskey spirits. Honestly, it felt like we were having drinks with friends who were teaching us about spirits.

I asked a ton of questions throughout the distillery tour (since we were a small group) and was impressed with how patiently Brian answered them all. After the tour, we did a tasting that was easily the best 'sip-by-sip' walk through experience I could imagine for appreciating whiskey (2 types) and rum (1 type). We were told about flavors that would evolve in our mouths just before and while they did, with amazing accuracy. This was different from any tasting I've been on, and was extremely memorable.  We left with 2 bottles and 2 whiskey glasses to take back to California.

I'd totally recommend visiting this distillery, and I'd come back again if fate brought me back to Philly.  They are working on some new creations that aren't quite ready yet that sound exciting to try out.  5 stars, and thank you both for such a great experience!

Well I started my last review with "maybe I should wait...." and I should have trusted that instinct.  My interactions with the dealership were stressful and gave me anxiety.  

Claiming the Oil Changes that were worked into the sale was such a hassle, eventually I just stopped.

Trying to get a callback for something as simple as replacing my remote starter fob was impossible.  Twice they said "we'll get that information for you and call you back".  I ended up just going in to talk to them about it.

I decided to take my car back to them for an oil change in November 2021 cause I wanted them to check some other things out.  I'm not even kidding, when I walked in, I stood in the doorway while the dude walked around, no more than 5 feet away from me.  He didn't acknowledge me until my boyfriend walked in behind me.  Then he finally asked what he could do for us.

My car is out of warranty now, so as far as I'm concerned, I never have to go back there.

Mixto is has good food, with a great atmosphere and very attentive service. 

I went here yesterday for a casual happy hour and was very satisfied. We ordered chicken empandads, all their skewers, and an order of calamari along with some drinks. 

Cuban food is typically pretty greasy, and this place is no exception (however, it is not overly greasy).

The calamari was VERY good. Very soft and tender. The skewers were the happy hour special for $2 a skew. If you are hungry, these are not for you. They come with two small pieces of meat/fish with peppers and onions. They were small but delicious. All  the meats were tender and juicy. The chicken empandas were great too. The salsa they came with had the perfect kick to it that made me order a second. 

The bread served is amazing. If they don't serve it to you, ASK FOR IT. It is pounded with butter, but so good. 

Lastly, order the guava mojito. Any cuban loves guava. I've never had a guava mojito, but did at mixto, and was surprised on how good it was. 

This is not a place I would go out of my way to go to, but definitely a place I wouldn't mind going to again.

We talked this place up to our out of state visitors, only to find out you have to pay a membership to play in there now. We would have had to of paid $24 for an hour and a half until they closed tonight. At 25 cents a pop to play a game, this is pretty pricey, and I think ridiculous. I was wondering why the parking lot was practically empty. Hopefully they will switch back to opening this to the public.

The place is very cozy and beautiful. We ordered mix plate and it was adorable. Chicken and beef kebab are very good. 
Dishes are perfect, and Kunefe is the best in town

YUM! We ordered delivery, which included green papaya salad, pad prik king with beef, mo yang (thai bbq pork), and the pad kee mao (drunken noodles). The salad was tasty and I enjoyed the pad prik king, which is a regular spicy favorite of mine to try at any new-to-me Thai restaurant. I'd never had Thai bbq before, and it was a very pleasant surprise, and I found the drunken noodles to be almost impossible to stop eating. Highly recommended!

My first time here and I will definitely be back to try some of their other sandwiches. I got a tuna salad wrap. Great flavor but the mayo in it seemed a little watery because my wrap was dripping as I was eating it.  I would get it again bc it was delicious.

Even though I only pay $19.99 a month to work out I would still prefer my experience to be good.  I have been at this gym for 2 yrs now and it has only gotten worse.  If it's not staff flirting with other guest, or favoring their "regulars" you get a mumble of a hi or just a stare.  I don't know how much they get paid but it's obviously not enough for them to check the tanning beds when someone is done; in my last few times of tanning I have had to clean up after the person before me and it's gross.  The manager has talked to me about it and said how sorry she was and put up a sign but that hasn't helped at all.
Best Pizza in Harleysville by FAR .. worth the extra 5 minute drive

This is a large practice with four offices and a business model geared to more volume than a typical dentist's office.

In that mode, it is very well run, pleasant and professional and given their excellent pricing I can't imagine going anywhere else.  I had two different practicioners for a cleaning as they utilized the hygienist's time just for the cleaning portion and a dental assistant finished my cleaning with the polishing and flossing phase.  This actually makes great business sense, especially since the cost savings are passed along to the patient.

I did not experience any attempts to up-sell as others have noted, and highly recommend this practice, especially if you want to avoid the unusually high fees smaller, private dentists feel comfortable charging for their work in Santa Barbara.
Just fast food sushi. Lose of quality. Walk-in hungry with more money walk-out hungry with less money. Novel concept only!

Terrible service today. The waitress did not know what kind of soup was being served. She said " Umm... I think we have chicken soup and something with beans and uh something with spinach, I think." She could have said " I am not positive but let me go check." Also, I asked her where the meat came from that is in the French Dip sandwich because in the menu it says "farm raised beef." I asked her if it was local and she said " Umm... I don't really know." Again, she could have said "I don't know but let me find out." She was very clueless. I also noticed that when she was bringing out our appetizer she showed it to the other girl who was working like there was a problem with it and they both had confused looks on their faces and just kind of shrugged their shoulders. Then after she brought it to us and went back to the other girl they started to laugh. I don't know if they were laughing about the appetizer but it made me a little uncomfortable. This young waitress also had a very low-cut shirt on that I don't think is appropriate for a waitress and also because this girl looked like she was still in high school. Besides the young waitress being clueless, on of the female "chefs" who was at the soup and salad bar was not sanitary. First, we had ordered the soup and salad bar with the baked potato and when we went up to get the food there were no potatoes left except for one so we had to ask the female chef and she said she would have to make more. She didn't even apologize and was not friendly at all. While we were waiting for the potatoes back at our table we observed her go on break and leave the cafe. after about 10 or 15 minutes, we saw her come back in with a drink in her hand and walk back to the salad bar. I followed her back to the salad bar to see if she was going to wash her hands. She did NOT wash her hands and I saw her start to stir the soup. I went back to the table because there were still no potatoes and I was also disgusted that she did not wash her hands. A couple minutes later I went back to the salad bar to see about the potatoes and I noticed her cutting up tomatoes and touching them with her hands. I do not know if she washed her hands before she started cutting the tomatoes but I sure hope so. I also had a problem with the salad at the salad bar because most of lettuce looked like it had been sitting out for a while because they were soggy and a dark green color kind of like cooked spinach.The spinach and artichoke soup was also disgusting. The soup was overwhelmingly salty. We ate our salad first and then when we went to eat the soup, a strange layer of film or something had formed on the top of the soup and did not look appetizing or normal to say the least. Overall, I was VERY disappointed about the service and the quality of food. I do not think I will ever come back.The only nice thing I can say is that the location and view are very nice and the sign and general idea of the cafe is good but it doesn't live up to what it is portraying. I was truly very dissapointed and I really hope that they  were just having an off day but they should really take a look at their employees, the quality of your food, and sanitation (some of the utensils and cups were dirty.)

This place is pretty good! I always do the drive thru, and it's not that long of a wait. I usually get a sandwich there, and its good. My vegetarian sister gets the "impossible" whopper there, and she likes it. My only complain is that our server, Basil, put half the amount of onion rings I ordered there. Overall, very tasty sauces and food!

The easiest answer a sales person can give you is: " we do not have it in store". I was looking for a key board cover and no one was able to assist me. I even told them that these covers are advertise on their web page.

it was terrible I got kung pow chicken it was a what looked like all celery 4 mushrooms a few pieces of diced carrots 6 pieces of shaved chicken for 835 what a rip off ! the chicken lo mein had no flavor do yourself a favor and don't go there .

Delicious food and to top it all  they have great service. Will definitely be back soon. 
Very clean and always busy, it must be for a good reason.

So great ! My overall experience truly amazed me and exceeded my expectations! The mash potatoes are zesty and the mac is perfect ! The chicken was flaky at the skin and juicy and tender with the meat. The okra and the gravy were compliments to the whole thing.

A high recommendation from me if you are in the area or just visiting!

Lesley was our tour guide for the Garden District walking tour and it was fantastic! She was so friendly and knowledgeable. The tour was very interesting and I highly recommend it if you have the opportunity. The two hours flew by and when we are back in New Orleans we will definitely be booking another tour!

A perfect experience. Endless amazing music from different genres at multiple stages, huge selection of any type of food you could possibly want (hellooooo crawfish Monica), and a great freakin atmosphere. 

Jazz fest 2016 was perfect. Everyone listening to good music, having a good time, indulging in some fabulous Cajun or creole cuisine. Nothing better. Let the good times roll indeed!!

Did notice that as the day progressed the festival became wayyyyyy too crowded. I think they need to get their ish together and figure out a way to handle this as it's honestly a safety hazard.

My experience at Pacifica has been great... I graduated with a master's from a traditional setting and am already licensed and work in private practice. I chose to attend Pacifica's clinical Ph.D program because I wanted something different from my Master's program. I honestly am not sure why the previous yelpers were so harsh on the school. My experience has been nothing but positive. The instructors are well published and the best in the field. One thing I did notice though is that this school does sometimes attract people who want something a little different, with that being said, some students (not the school) in my opinion need to work on some issues before attending. I feel like some students have a lot of unresolved issues, and if you are looking at studying depth psychology without having had your own stuff resolved, you will show emotionally unstable and externalize your issues. I recommend this school, however, make sure you deal with your own stuff before applying...
I like the other location better but that doesn't change the food. The cheese steaks are excellent and they have WaWa coffee.....You know what it is if you've been in the Philly area.

I love the NPR guy and his raving review. This place is a train wreck. It is one of the most awful dealerships I have ever been to and I have been to a lot of dealerships. I also have encountered the sales manager, Craig, and he lied to my face about why an employee wasn't there to assist me after the receptionist had told me the reason. I guess appointments mean nothing to these people and I don't think Craig could have been more rude to me. And lets not forget my lovely encounter with the toothless former Honda manager.  He's quite a jewel and couldn't tell the truth to save his life.  So what I am saying is YOU can go ANYWHERE and have a salesman or manager be rude to you, lie to you, manipulate you and care only about closing the deal no matter what it takes. So why go to this dealer where I guarantee you that happens.  So where did I buy from?  Ferman Mazda and Fitzgerald Hyundai. I love Fitzgerald Hyundai's pricing system, that buying experience can not be beat and I will recommend them all day, every day.  Mike was my salesperson-former EMS guy. And Jack was not what you expect from a sales manager...very friendly and pleasant.

Came here for a haircut. Over priced but I decided to see how it would go. $40 for haircut. Nearing the end of my haircut I requested a goatee lineup. No problem. No one seemed to think it was appropriate to let me know that that would be an additional $40!!! Way way way overpriced at $80 for a haircut and goatee. Haircut was okay, goatee edges was just done with an electric razor.... sub par at best.

Really great vibe. They have some great beers that were all wonderful to try. I cant decide whether their pizza or calzones or better but TRY IT. (I had "The Roni" I honestly did not expect to enjoy the dining experience as much as I did.

I have ordered the food for delivery. When you receive the food it's great. That being said when you try to order they put you on hold for periods as long as 30 minutes. I will never order food from them again. I'm from out of town and I had better service from Dominos!

Very cool place to eat and have drinks! We met some friends here for dinner. We all enjoyed our meals and its moderately priced as well. We had drinks, appetizer, a meal and dessert all under 100 dollars.  Staff was nice enough, could have been friendlier. But since we were too busy talking with friends it really didn't matter. Highly suggest the bleeding orange cocktaill ! YUM I will definitely make it back here again.

To Start, service was poor i called in advance in regards to selling my gun and the guy on the phone said he could give me $350. i got there let them know i was there to sell a gun one gentleman was nice an notified another employee an the other wasn't so nice he argued and snapped at the other. Just a little unprofessional in my opinion

We have stayed at Two Rivers Campground twice and definitely RECOMMEND it!! The location is excellent. Very close to Opry and Opry Mills Mall. This campground is sandwiched between 2 RV dealerships. Convenient if you need anything. I do want to mention the great addition of a small DOG PARK!! What a great idea for all the dogs the hang out in campers all day. Our dog loved the chance to run!! Will stay at Two Rivers every time we visit Nashville. Bravo! Full hook up, WIFI, and CABLE TV

Free things to do in Santa Barbara

Located on the second floor of the Paseo Nuevo mall near the intersection of Chapala and De La Guerra, MOCA Santa Barbara is much smaller than expected.  I like museum browsing and I especially like it more when it is free.  Stop by if you are in the area and check out what they are showcasing.  You might find something interesting.

Beautiful atmosphere...loved the prime rib sandwich at Harry & Izzys and thought I was getting the same thing here....noooo...no great slices of beef.just one hunk of fatty meat...sorry Lounge..know you're new...keep trying...

Yeah.  It's a 5.

You should know...I don't drink alcohol, so I can't speak to their beer selection of the quality of the beer.

I CAN speak to the atmosphere, service, and food, and it was all stellar.  The menu is limited...food isn't their "main" thing, after all, but it was very good.  The service was stellar, and more than that, from the servers to the bookstore clerk to the owner himself, they all seemed like they actually appreciated  the fact that we were there.  That goes a long way.  We'll be back, and soon.

I had passed by this place a million times and finally stopped in. It was ABSOLUTELY delicious. My husband and I shared the ox tails and the curry chicken. Very tender with great flavors! I wasn't even that hungry and I almost licked the plate clean. The staff was so, so nice! Will definitely go back again!!!!

This place has potential.  There are some definite areas where improvement is possible. 
I ordered the smothered fried pork chop.  The breading was crisp and tasty, the mushroom gravy was good, but would have been better if there was more of it.  The pork chop was overcooked. A bit dry, and slightly tough. It also needed some seasoning before being breaded. 
The mashed potatoes were buttery and chunky, but once more, gravy was skimpy. 
The collard greens were ok.  Large chunks of pork, but the flavor was not obvious. 
The wife had salmon croquettes, and they were also ok.  The only real issue with them was some small bone pieces (vertebrae, not pin bones). 

We will probably go back and give them another shot, as we really want to support locally owned small businesses.

Amazing! Growing up in a town that raves about its bagel & cream cheese, I'd have to say I'd take Proper Bagel any day. First of all they let us try 4 cream cheeses before deciding anything. The strawberry shortcake was off the charts- things dreams are made of. 
I ended up getting the blue cheese chicken salad on a spinach everything bagel with pickled onions, tomato, and arugula- so delicious. Full of flavor, fresh, and crisp! 
He got the everything bagel with lox cream cheese, capers, tomato, and arugula. The lox were so fresh and silky.
Both were amazing, were traded halves of each sandwich to get a taste of each. Best idea!
We then shared the s'mores bar- it's a must!!

I've done take-out from this restaurant several times when I'm in this part of Philly. 
If you have read my other reviews on yelp, you know I have tried several different Indian restaurants, so I'm very familiar with the cuisine and what to expect.
What I like about Mumbai Bistro:
1) The prices -- most affordable Indian food. If you're on a budget like me, this is perfect.
2) Simple, easy-to-understand menu. It also tells you what's spicy, what's gluten free, what contains dairy.
3) Fast service. Even if you don't call in and order ahead for take-out, they get your to-go order ready in 5-10 mins.
4) The food taste and quality is good. Good selection of appetizers and entrees. It's not THE best Indian food ever (you get what you pay for), but it's always good, enjoyable.

There's nothing I really dislike about this place.

I have been hyping myself up for John's since I started my cheesesteak hunt way back last fall. Their schedule is a bit hard to work around being that they always close at 5pm, but I was finally able to make it just in time before they randomly closed at 2 on a Friday. 

Despite an excruciatingly long wait (they were short of chefs), it was entirely worthwhile. The steak is by far Philadelphia's best. The roll is delicious, the meat is perfectly cooked, and the provolone melts beautifully throughout the sandwich. 

I took my family here after hearing all the great word of mouth, and they could not have been happier. If you're looking for the best steak Philly has to offer, then go to John's. There's simply nothing better.

Discriminates against paying LGBTQ+ customers and paying homeless customers. Video proof a couple reviews down. Disgusting way to treat customers. 
Not to mention, food often comes out incorrect and/or cold. Not worth the money. Can't attest to the drinks since I've always been the DD while visiting here, but apparently they're about the same quality as the food. 
Don't bring your business here if you value your money and morals.

Not sure why the reviews are so high here. The service was ungodly slow an we came around 5 on a Friday. It wasn't busy at all. All I could taste with the food was salt. Very salty. It was to the point where my wedding band barely fit by the end of the meal. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

I'm also 99% sure my dinner came with a desert, yet the waitress failed to tell me.

Great burgers and beers! Always great service and there are always deals through social media to try desserts or appetizers for free-items I otherwise wouldn't have tried. Happy Hour during the week is our favorite. Check them out, you won't be disappointed!

I enjoyed the plantains, yucca, black beans and rice here. Workers were very friendly. However my partner did not enjoy his food and I later spoke to my mom and her partner and they said they did not enjoy their food from here either. Maybe I'll come back alone if I am ever in north Saint Pete again lol.

I've had the chance to try this place for breakfast and for lunch and both times i was very impressed. I have to say it's nice to have a sandwich shop that is above the simple JJ or Subway with options of a plated meal but all in a sandwich. What a brilliant idea the owner had when designing this place. The flavors are like no other you'll find in a sandwich shop. If you get the chance to try it do go! I'll be back!

Not even worth it if you're absolutely desperate. THIS IS AN ABSOLUTE SHITHOLE. The sheets were not clean and they were ripped. The whole place smells like urine and old people. And not the good kind of old people...and urine.

For convenience and a because I thought Popeye's had better quality, I spent almost $25 for a meal of 8 tenders, 4 biscuits, and about 10 Oz of mashed potatoes with a tablespoon of gravy. The tenders were mostly batter on a stringy, dry piece of almost invisible chicken. No real meat included. Then too, the biscuits were dry and potatoes tasteless with greasy gravy. Disappointing. One star is a gift. 

I will not return. I will drive further next time for quality meat and juicy tenders at Cains or Chick-fil-A.

Dropped my new couch and bent it. When I asked about when it would be replaced, I got the run around on the phone. Once I came to the store for face to face conversation, the run around continued. I would give zero stars for customer service.

I ordered a pizza online to be delivered. Aside from being 20 mins late, which I can deal with bc it's a Friday night, I paid for extra cheese and the pizza barely had any cheese on it at all. A small pizza delivered to the house was $21.00. For that price I expect my pizza to be made exactly as ordered. The only time my pizza seems to be right is when I order 2 slices. Any time I order a whole pizza something is always wrong lately. Don't think I'll be trying Jet's again for a while. There are too many alternatives in the area to have to deal with constant dissatisfaction.

We went for the food time yesterday and Diesel and Lucy loved it. It was nothing like I imagined it would be. We met the owner as he continued to make rounds to insure all was well and that everyone was enjoying themselves. We will definitely be returning

Just not a fan.  But I'm not a big vino drinker either.  Food was all mediocre at best and the place had a very "corporate" or "chain-y" vibe.

I wont be back.

Oh man this was so so good. The baleadas- kind of a burrito with a thick tortilla was so good. Got a traditional breakfast w/ fried plantains and the enchiladas which are more like tostadas were phenomenal. No English on the menu but I came in prepared to order and the owner (I think) was very helpful.

The best authentic Chinese food in the area!   Everything on the menu is spectacular.  
Henry, the manager, is always welcoming and helpful.  
Just go.

Tasty, quality food, & great deals!

I tried their borscht the other night and was pleased with it. The spices were interesting... different than my own recipe in that I tasted more cinnamon or nutmeg than the usual dill. Still very good though, and it comes with a nice little garlic roll. Only $6 a bowl!

Also tried the fries and there were so many, I shared with the rest of the table. Good crisp "real" fries, as my friend put it. Again, only $2.50!

My friends got sandwiches and pierogis, and I was heavily envying the latter. Please, Green Room... if you read this... add a vegan pierogi to the menu. Cheese messes me up, but damn, I love them.

This is the best Vegan restaurant I have been to. It has been years in the making but it's plant based AlIVE and oh so good. The ambiance is simple and stunning at the same time. The bar is a great hang out place for a large group of friends or you can hang outside and dine under the stars. I have to say this will be my new normal. Great job Oliver's , I'll be back over and over and over again.

Excellent massage! I was already planning my next massage as soon as it was done. Lizzy listened to my descriptions of my aches and pains, then tailored a customized treatment to address them. And it was great! Really loved the options of cupping and aromatherapy. The hot towels and heat lamp made the experience even more relaxing. Very calm and comfortable atmosphere, too.
