I had a basic Swedish massage a couple of days ago - Zarek was the masseuse. He did a nice job. The spa facilities were very nice and the staff was very good.

My brother recommended this place as a must do for our trip and I am so happy he did! Everything was so amazing! The chicken? OH EM GEE!!! The crunch was light and the chicken was juicy. Everything was so well seasoned. Ashley, our waitress, was very attentive to our group of seven. Next trip down to the Big Easy will DEFINITELY find me here!

Use to love this place. Seems like some recipes have changed. Over priced for quality of their food. My favorite part is the atmosphere and their tea. Everything else has gone down hill. Hopefully the next time we go back it improves to our old standards.

Friday's = time to try a new restaurant for lunch. Bonus, new lunch buddy! We decided to check out Cerdo Tacos + Tequila, sister restaurant to the uber popular Jack's Burger Shack because Mexican options are few and far in between this oasis we call Edmonton. Okay, technically Cerdo's is in St. Albert but come on, it's pretty much the same.

We arrived around 12:15pm and the place was packed. We had to sit in the bar area but eventually were able to snag a table as the lunch rush slowly filed out. Man, I don't know if this is normally the case but service was slooooow. Took forever for us to order and even longer to get our food. I don't normally complain (that much) about service but we only ordered two dishes!

Here's a breakdown of their menu along with prices (starters, huevo bowls and tacos). Seriously, I really, really, REALLY don't like when restaurant websites don't list their prices.

- Nachos ($13.50) with the Saltroom's chorizo ($3.00) - corn tortilla chips, jack cheese, tomato, green onion, jalapeno with sour cream and salsa as dipping sauces. The chips were crunchy and had that satisfying bite to it but the cheese could have used some better layering skills (it pretty much was all clumped into the middle). The jalapenos weren't spicy at all but gave the dish some colour. We added some chorizo - a tad expensive for a few pieces of chorizo, although it was tasty. The nachos were decent but we felt like you could make these at home pretty easily. Factor in the price, it's definitely overpriced for what it is.

- Chicken al pastor ($9.00) - guajillo marinated chicken, pineapple, cilantro and radish. The tacos come in pairs, topped with cabbage and onion and with your choice of either corn (gluten free) or flour tortillas. I don't understand their choice of offering two tortillas over the standard three. 

Given that, the tortillas were stuffed with a good amount of chicken. Cilantro was fresh. Pineapple was itty bitty pieces - a few small sliced pieces would have gave it a nice contrasting sweetness. The chicken wasn't bad. Overall, the taco was missing something - a kick to it, a Mexican hot sauce or something like that would have helped. We didn't see the hot sauce bottle until after we ate the whole taco though. Oops, our bad but they should have had something in it originally!

It's nice to see a more diverse selection of food options available in St. Albert. I know they have mad love for chains over here so I applaud the owners for bringing something new and different. I will have to try a few more dishes to see if my opinion changes of Cerdo's. As for now, decent spot but not my first choice in town when I'm craving Mexican food.
Owner is hilarious and food is delicious!! Definitely take advantage of $10 lunch buffet!!

I have a handful of shops that I could spend hours in just looking at everything they have, dreaming about what my closet, kitchen, or living room would look like if I had an endless supply of money. Anthropologie is one of those places. Everything in here is beautiful. The tiny little tea light candles? Gorgeous. Window displays? Perfect. Coffee mugs? Darling. Coats that cost as much as my rent? Oh my gaaaash, I'm drooling.

Aside from the endless amount of day dreams that this shop supplies me with, I love them for their sale rack. I like to think that one day their swanky coats will fill my closet, but until then, their glorious little sale room is where I live. You wanna talk about hidden gems? You'll find 'em in here! Purses, hats, accessories, you name it, they've got it. Thank you anthro for jumpstarting my Christmas shopping. Because of my most recent to their sale room, I have almost all of the shopping for the ladies in my family done. Huzzah!

So go wander around the store, appreciate all its gorgeous furnishings, buy a swanky coat, or score a sweet deal in the sale room - you might just find me here doing the same. :)

This was a very nice find. I love sushi but I do not want to have the rice with the sushi anymore. This place has that option. All the items that are with sushi but put in a salad. Awesome place. Works great with my Keto.

The food was awful. The wings were mushy and not nearly enough sauce. The sicilian pizza was cold in spots and not cooled off but cold and the crust was stale. Like it was a day old. Delivery took over an hour. If i didn't have company i would of returned dinner and addled for my money back.  I ended up throwing away more than half of dinner and $65

New to area so we relied on Yelp for a place to go for Mid-week Anniversary quick dinner! We were very pleasantly surprised because it far surpassed the ratings. Food was delicious and loved the atmosphere. We sat outside but could hear good live music inside. Nice servers and friendly management! Will be our go to place!

Oh my gosh, I had seen this place on Nashville Flipped, and it far succeeded my expectations. The people were helpful and super nice. I plan to go back now that I've figured out placement for some pieces.

Been here the last 4 mornings. Food is delicious, especially the breakfast omelette. Bloody Mary's are unbelievably good also. Service has been great every time we've been. Check your bill & ask questions. 50% of the time they will try to slip in extra charges. We are staying at Holiday Inn & have the breakfast voucher but most of the waitresses still try to charge you something. Kids are suppose to eat free, but that's only happened 1 of the mornings. We have 2 more mornings here in Clearwater Beach & will spend them eating Breakfast at Jimmy's!!!

Can't get enough of this place! The selection of premium quality olive oil and vinegars can't be matched in Santa Barbara. Great customer service and knowledge of products. Makes the PERFECT gift. Family and friends think I've become an aficionado of olive oil, really I just let the staff school me for a few minutes and pretend I'm an expert. Nobody questions my expertise, they are too busy eating.

This review is for happy hour: nice selection of food at $8 each (ahi tuna, crispy lobster, steak sand, crab blt). Not my fav place for HH drinks; prefer Sea Salt HH deal. Get there right at 4:30, bar fills up quickly. They don't offer HH on Saturday, but Sea Salt does!
New to East Nashville and visited here with our friends. They were vegan and the kitchen took special care of our friends. My Amberjack fish was amazing. Highly recommend this spot!
This was my first sushi experiance and im sold. They were helpful for a newbie and i was shocked at how good sushi is. Hope its all this good
Mahi tacos were great but the roles were the stars of our dinner. Waitress was very nice. The owner came out and spoke with us. Everyone was so nice! A prefect dinner in Meridian.
Regular of their grilled cheese and home fries, hummus and tabouli plate, great cup of cafe americano and open any time of day and night.
Great service, tasty latte and croissant. Nice music, perfect volume. Wish there were larger tables available...

I! Love! Old City Movers! We will never move with another company. We used them last year to move to a house and unfortunately our landlord sold it so we were on the move again. As soon as we found out we had to move I contacted Old City Movers before we even found a new house. They gave me a quote and were so prompt with responses. We were given the estimate of 8am-8:30am on the day of the move and the morning of we got a call at 7:45 saying they were 15 minutes away. They handled our belongings with care and made sure they understood where everything needed to go. They finished 2 hours faster than the estimate! Thank you Alex, Aaron, and Chris for a smooth move.
Excellent sushi, friendly staff and cool atmosphere.  What else do you need?

Keep it simple and you'll be alright.  Fortune Teller knows what I'm saying. They keep it simple in the vein that there's no pretentious bull to be found here.  What you WILL find here is a great selection of craft beers, wine and spirits along w/ a very nice craft cocktail menu.  Decor is simple and low key.  Service has always been friendly and prompt.  Last night, my girl and I popped in for drinks and a bite.  Together we had a portobello rueben, side summer salad, bowl of house made chili and a delicious cornbread muffin.  The rueben exuded a great flavor and had the perfect amount of mushrooms and melted cheese.  The salad was bursting with flavor and was definitely way above anything you'd consider "pub fare".  Sliced fruit w/ crumbled cheese, nuts and a great vinaigrette. The chili and cornbread hit the spot for this cold, fall weather.  Chili was flavorful and meaty w/ subtle heat and the cornbread was accompanied by a jalapeño jelly that I would spread on a roof shingle and eat cuz it's so delicious.  Cherokee Street is lucky to have Fortune Teller amongst its limits.  Well done.

Don't come unless you'd like to stay for an hour or more! 30 minutes in line, 30+ minutes to get our shakes. I got an cotton candy ice cream cone, and there was nothing remarkable about it. The ice cream is not creamy- it's not super soft, and the ice pieces make my tongue feel like it had rug burn, lol. Everything is expensive, and not worth the price for mediocre shakes and ice cream. It's supposedly good for an instagram photo op, but my boyfriend's shake looked like somebody just spilled half the shake down the sides of the glass. He asked for a to go cup and never got it. Will not return.
We love this place! So unique and I love their cider selection!! I'm gluten free and completely feel comfortable here!!

My husband and I live a few blocks from this restaurant and dine out often, we were excited to try a new place.  

We walked into CB around 6:30 on a Saturday.  The restaurant was at about 75% capacity and we didn't have a reservation.  Although there were about 5-6 tables open the hostess looked displeased and just looked down until another girl came up and whispered "No reservations?"  (We saw them turn away people later)  We suggested that we could sit at the bar, which was completely empty.  If we had not suggested this, I think they would have turned us away, even though some of those open tables were never seated while we were there.  

We were the only people that were sitting at the bar, but the bartender made us feel uncomfortable.  She was nice, but seemed like she was in the weeds with two customers.  We had to ask for water, appetizer plates (coffee saucers?), napkins and silverware.  My $10 glass of rosé was a very small pour.  I usually order two glasses of wine with dinner, but I did not feel this was a good value.  

The cheese plate was great.  There was a nice variety of cheese, but only 4 pieces of bread for the cheese.  We ordered a galette which was a twist on the croquet madame: cheese, ham and an egg on top.  There were not a lot of fillings and the crepe was soggy.  

After ten years of eating at La Creperie in Chicago, this place fell flat for me. 
The decor of the restaurant did not remind me of Paris, but yet a themed party or a Paris themed homecoming dance.

Took a chandelier purchased in London to SB Lampshades for repair.
Zack & Nancy did an excellent job repairing it, paying attention to fine details to preserve is authenticity and patina.  Have done business at this store in the past and the quality of knowledge and service has been consistent.  This well travelled old chandelier will light up our house and life.  Thank you for a job well done.

What a ridiculously awful experience.  The restaurant was freezing.  Our waiter was barely around and we waited a good ten minutes for someone to notice that we needed a box and finally gave up and asked someone. I am beyond disappointed.
Great place. Emeril owned restaurant. Pork chop was great and split the gumbo with a friend to eat as an appetizer.

Absolutely great service!! Fair prices!! Very knowledgeable staff!! When my husband and I first started going to VIP Game Store we did there monthly plan, pretty much unlimited video game rentals for about $20 bucks a month, and we loved it, the only reason we stopped is because the games we were buying from them were not getting played at home. 
I have also traded a lot of stuff for credit with them and was amazed at how much I got. 
You can pre-order with them, sometimes they get stuff on the release date and sometimes they don't but they let you know that before you put money down.
I really don't buy video games anywhere else now!

Since we knew we'd be visiting Santa Barbara we laid down money on a couple Groupons a while back to go stand-up paddling with Channel Islands Outfitters.  We bought 2 Groupons but 4 of us ended up going, so we weren't all on the cheap.  None of us had ever done this SUP thing but we didn't sign up for a lesson, just a rental.

The shop seemed well-equipped, although the SUP boards are stored in a locker a little ways away by the beach.  That would have been fine except there was one fewer boards in the locker than we needed so I had to go back to the shop and schlep one  a quarter mile down to the beach anyway (the beginner boards are extra long, wide and heavy!)

Aside from that little bit of disorganization (it was the end of a busy summer weekend) we had a very good time playing in the waves and being chased by dolphins for a couple hours.  The CIO folks were slightly overwhelmed but when they could get to us they were attentive, informative and enthusiastic.  Money.  Well.  Spent.

First time at a Japanese steakhouse was a great experience! The food was incredible and the portions were huge. The chef, Fluffy, was a great entertainer on top of being an excellent chef. I loved the sirloin steak, the fried rice was exceptional, and everything that came with it complemented the main items well. Service was great and prices were reasonable. A very cool dining experience worth your time

Great space about a mile + 1/2 from downtown Reno.  Lovely interior, long bar.  Very woodsy feeling.  I asked the bartender to make a special drink - Hemmingway Dacquari - and he was up to the task.  The place is fairly large, nicely appointed.  The ingredients were all freshly squeezed (lime, grapefruit + lemon) and the drink was very beautifully made.  They have both a large selection of liquors and many beer choices on tap as well.  Great place.

RISE is one of the great coffee spots in STL. The lattes are artfully made and presented, the espresso is rich and flavorful, and the vibe is chill and neighborhoody. If you're up for some brunch-ish food, try the quiches -- mushroom and spinach, bacon and sausage, all nice, fluffy options.
Food would have been better if packaged correctly and presentable. U judge by sight first. And this is not what I wanted to see.

This place is close to my office.  We frequent this eatery.  Burritos and tacos are great.  It's quick, good, filling and won't break the bank for lunch.  The only complaint I would have - tight corridors, and maybe it's not as clean during lunch.  They can't keep up with the clean up during rush times.   Even a cool place to swing by during the weekends for a quick lunch.  Can't get enough of the burritos!

Mention I'm from out of town. I pull up to the drive thru, getting our order all together with everybody in the car right? The person/window lady on the speaker says "can I take your order?" I don't answer right away because I'm still getting all the orders in my head to repeat it to her. Literally 2 seconds later, didn't even get me a chance to say ANYTHING, she goes, in the rudest fking way "HEEEELLLOOOO?!" Like everyone else should know if a customer does not answer right away then give them a minute to answer and be more professional with your job, aka taking someone's order. But other than that, our food is ok. Missing one of our Fries but that's not a big deal.

Love this place! I tried some tacos at the Johnny Sanchez in Baltimore's Horseshoe Casino and was excited to try more here. First of all, the space is really cool. One wall is covered in a brightly painted mural plus there are several Chihuly like chandeliers hanging in the middle of the space. 

Our group took over one of the largest tables and the server was able to handle our crowd and make everyone happy. We started off with chips and 2 kinds of salsa: tomatillo and grilled tomato. The grilled tomato was our favorite - just enough heat and a tinge of sweetness. 

I started off with the Tuna Agua Chile. It was fresh, spicy but not so much that it overpowered the fish. I loved the different textures of avocado and radish. A friend ordered the brussels spouts salad. Wow! What a dish! This would be fantastic for Thanksgiving. Crispy brussel sprouts tossed with a jalapeño vinaigrette then topped with cotija cheese and pomegranates. My goat tacos arrived with pickled cactus and queso fresco. I was in heaven. I thought the 2 small tacos may be too small, but it was perfect. I loved the extra heat in these dishes which I think the Baltimore venue lacks. 

Great space and great food!

One of the worst places I've ever been to watch a movie!! My party of 3 went to the movies on 7/30/17, my partner & I got the two for $25. The first item that came out was popcorn, the popcorn was not even hot at all. Like how do they serve popcorn in a movie theater that not hot? We sent the popcorn back 3 different times the final time I asked if we could substitute the popcorn for a different entrée the server said yes, I then asked to speak with a manager before the movie had started because it was super crazy that they could not deliver hot popcorn. The manager came out during the the first half of the movie and tried to ask what was going on I asked if we can settle the issue after the movie was over  he agreed and the rest of the food came out (after over 30mins of the time the order was placed), my partner asked for another honey mustard with the chicken tenders that came a hour after we received the food. The movie ended and I was looking for a manager to speak with about my bill and how the food was,the manager had us in the back of the theater talking to us about the bill he was not apologetic and was not trying to rectify the bill. People where leaving and looking at us like we tried to skip out on our bill  which was very embarrassing. This place needs new management, the hallways smelled horrible the restrooms are very unsanitary. I will never go back to this "place of business " ever again and I hope you as the reader won't either!!!

I wasn't blown away by the food at Spasso Italian Grille, but it was good. I bumped up my star rating from 3 to 4 because of the nice ambiance and service. The atmosphere at SIG is relaxed and unpretentious.

We had the caesar salad and calamari to start (just average tasting, but nice portion size). I had the puttanesca which was very tasty but a little too oily. The linguine was cooked perfectly though.

My husband had the gnocchi in gorgonzola sauce, definitely worth going back for. Although the sauce was quite rich (in a good way), the gnocchi melted in your mouth! I've never had gnocchi quite so delicate before. The portion was huge too, enough for two since it was so rich.

Next time I'll try the escargot, since that seems to be a fave.

I needed to some junk taken away and requested quotes from yelp. Junk Gladiators replied right away and provided a very reasonable quote. The came the next morning and 5 minutes later they were on their way. If you need junk removed, give Junk Gladiators a call. They are fast, reasonably priced and reliable!

Worst experience ever! Expect to be delayed 2-4hrs from your departure. The employees dont have the courtesy to announce when a bus is not on time and they won't refund or change your ticket for a later date or time, you can expect a voucher for free nachos.

Excellent breakfast and the location is historic. Make sure you try the Poblano's Hot Sauce with your breakfast. It's locally made in Tucson and very flavorful. 

Their deserts are really good as well.

Run away from these movers. We did a cross country move with them through Budget Van Lines and it did not go well. Since your move is based on weight, our van showed up unweighed!  So, we had no way to trust them on our household goods weight. The laborers were polite and efficient, but that is the only part of the move that went well. We waited several weeks to receive our goods, and that would have been okay, except that this company does not call their customers and let them know that the shipment will be late. When it was finally delivered, we learned that Gulf Coast had subbed out our move to another company. The delivery driver was high, the assistant mover was drinking beer out of a Slurpee cup and there were boxes missing. We also did not receive our king mattress and box springs (how do you lose that?) and some valuable furniture. The boxes marked Silver, China, and Pewter were missing along with boxes marked Tools. Through all this, I fault their communication and total lack of care. Movers are scammers.

We went for a cleaning, x-rays, and exam thanks to Amazon Local. It was the most awful experience. The assistant claimed to find 3 serious cavities and wanted us to signup for a payment plan. Based on the earlier reviews, I wanted to get a second opinion or, at least, talk to the dentist himself. No dice. The hygienist just kept saying I was making a mistake and being cheap. The proposed bill was just shy of a $1,000. I said we'd think about it and get a second opinion and she just kept bad mouthing other practices! She said anyone else would use mercury which would cause health problems later on and we'd have to wait a month for another appt. In the end, they refused to give us the x-rays, much less show them to us so we could see just how serious the cavities were. Unprofessional, greedy, rude. I wish yelped allowed negative stars.

The food was good, but probably the worst service I've seen in a restaurant in a long time.
It took them at least 20 min to just get us water after we asked for it for it twice and they never refilled it. Also, the bread took forever to get there. 
The portions are pretty small for an Italian place.
The only people that got treated well were the locals and the regulars.
Don't waste your time and money on this place.
Today i had the lamb chops with green and brown rice with a salad this food was so good and well seasoned.this place is a hidden gem if you visit Deleware you must try this food.
Friendly staff and good food! I like their salsa it's better than any I have found in the area. The nachos are HUGE!

Got there at 11 o'clock on my 21st birthday because all of my other friends were already of age and wanted to start drinking. We were told they were closing early, at 11:30, even though they aren't supposed to close until 1. There were a good amount of people already in there, and they turned down a good amount of us. They lost out on a lot of money by turning us away. Every other time I've been there I haven't had an issue. Great food and good happy hour prices.
Always great food and service. Outside tables are nice. Always had a different type pizza love them all yummy.

Heard that this was a fantastic restaurant in New Orleans. When we arrived, I soon realized this wasn't the restaurant I had seen pictures of or read about.  The newly renovated decor is terrible...a bad 80's rendition of pastels and grays. The menu is completely new so we couldn't order what we had been hoping to.  The food was just ok...nothing to write home about but we couldn't get over the ambiance of the restaurant. It felt like we were in a diner. Unfortunately, we won't be back.

My husband and I celebrated his birthday & enjoyed a wonderful dinner. Angela was our server, she definitely is a professional & made us feel right at home. She helped make the evening very special. We started with the calamari appetizer. We loved it! We had the Maui Shrimp and Jambalaya for dinner. They were both amazing. Wonderfully seasoned with jus the right spice. Then they brought out a dessert for his birthday, yum! But we couldn't resist having the banana bread Creme Brûlée. Yum!!

We are big fans of this group, St. Elmo, Harry & Izzy, Burger Study...and were happy to see a new branch on the family tree. Food and drinks? The rubber shrimp had its familiar sinus searing cocktail sauce, the  egregiously over salted filet was accompanied by the delicious beans....so, good and bad. Overpriced drinks, but so what else is new. Pleasant, competent service, if a bit slow. That is not what the issue is. This is new building, custom designed. Clearly a lot of money was spent, and time and effort and more  money.....But....Whoever did the design of the space has either great contempt and disdain for patrons paying hundreds of dollars for the experience, or  has never sat in a restaurant before, or is just plain bad at design. Chairs more suited to a torture chamber during the Spanish inquisition face blank walls. Tables so tiny they barely held two dinners. Unbearable sound levels, on a  night that was not even so busy. Lighting that left us dazed, spots so bright that we thought we were under arrest alternate with areas of  near total darkness. Sorry guys, we expect a tiny bit of comfort at an upscale restaurant, even if you can't manage any real style. Unpleasant and  uncomfortable. With all the great steakhouses and lounges within a short drive, we will not likely come back here.

Absolute best customer service I've received anywhere hands down. Stephen went out of his way to help us out making numerous calls to make sure we got our difficult to find tire and wheel fixed and back on the road as soon as possible. Would definitely recommend!! Great service and got us the best price around. Highly highly recommended!!
This place is a waist of time. From the ugly decor to the worst home fries and eggs I have ever been served. Do not waist time or money!

Mothers Day Fail:
Online ordering system is down so no curbside.  Put mask on and go inside.  NO WORKERS wearing masks.  Cashier complaining about phone orders.  Maybe if your online system was working you wouldn't have to take orders over the phone.  Too many slow moving hipsters.  Too slow...

I have been dining at Mr. B's for the past 12 years, and try to get there whenever I am in New Orleans. A bit more elegant than the Brennan owned Bourbon House, Mr. B's is decorated with warm dark woods and comes equipped with a small bar area which is where my party and I ate. It has become a little bit of a tradition for us to come to Mr. B's to chill out, pig out and have a few drinks. 

Gary and Rich were our bartenders and they set us up at the bar with some Abita beers, bloody Mary's (a crowd pleaser), and easy conversation.  Now, on to the food. For starters I had a bowl of their Gumbo Ya Ya which comes with chicken and andoullie sausage. The flavoring is delicious. My only complaint is that I wish there was a bit more rice served with it, but that is personal preference. My friend had a special oyster and pea soup, which he enjoyed but noted it was lacking the oyster flavor he was hoping for. My other friend had a greens salad, which he seemed to enjoy. Moving on, for my lunch entree, I had the shrimp and grits. The shrimp was wrapped in bacon and they were salty, plump and juicy. They were very tasty. The surprising star on the plate, however were the grits. They were insanely good. I could just eat a plate of them and be perfectly happy. The consistency and buttery taste was absolutely heavenly. I had to defend my plate from my friends attempts at stealing them. Likewise, my friends BBQ shrimp was also fantastic. Rich sauce and plump shrimp are well worth the effort in peeling them open. I swear I thought my friend was going to chomp down on his plate he enjoyed the sauce so much---buttery, spicy, perfection. My other friend had a petit fillet that was wonderfully prepared accompanied by some eggs which I did not try. For dessert we split an addicting bread pudding that was sweet with cinnamon and raisins and soppy yet firm. Man it was good. I also had a special coffee called a "Bob" which had Kahlua in it and it really hit the spot. 

It should be mentioned while we were eating at the bar there were a lot of people enjoying the Sunday Jazz brunch that was being held at the time were were there. Having some live Jazz music while eating such good food, enjoying some delicious drinks and watching some sports is a real treat, especially because the Jazz trio that was playing was quite good. As usual, Mr. B's just always delivers for me and I plan to return as soon as I am back in New Orleans.

Don't waste you money in this ripoff of a strip Club. $22 admission for a terrible "show". Dance-corners are right in the middle of the club and staff looks at you like hawks. Even the girl who was dancing on me was embarrassed because they were so looking over to us. Never again. She was a cutie tho, and the only one who was pretty. All the others are below the standard of what you'd expect in a strip joint. 
Spend your money elsewhere.

My best friend and I were in NOLA for a bachelorette party 2 years ago and ended up missing our flight out of town at the end of the weekend and therefore had to stay an extra night. After half a week of partying and staying in a fancy hotel, we decided to take the adventurous route and "rough it" that last night to save some money, and this place was nothing short of "rough". I can't begin to explain how disgusting and unaccommodating this place was. I've read all the other negative reviews and they are all accurate, not remotely exaggerated. Not sure where these 4 and 5 star reviews are coming from, but I would hate to see what these people are ranking with 1 or 2 stars. This place was truly sick and all we could do was laugh to keep from crying. Maybe not every room was as bad as ours, but I don't see how any other rooms could have possibly been worse. I'll give 2 stars because the location is pretty great and the price was....okay. If you do choose to stay here, bring all of your own sheets and towels, flip flops for the shower, and a bottle of 409 to spray surfaces down before you touch them. You will leave here with your skin crawling.

The food was great but the service was lacking. I sat at the bar and my server was not very friendly. She actually left before I finished my meal and drinks.  To make things worse another server asked me how I was doing and when I said ok she said they were just trying to clean up the bar. I noticed that they seem to cater to the larger parties much more than a single guest. This place could have a great bar service scene for a middle end restaurant but not yet.

Part retail, part museum. If you've seen the Pickers TV show you know the interesting kind of things they have here. The selection is small. If all you want is Pickers logo merchandise, I'd give this 4 stars.

I had to take my friend to the airport one morning and he was hell bent on getting to district donuts. He loves their coffee and cinnamon rolls. I've only tasted their coffee, so I decided to give their food a chance. I ordered the fried chicken biscuit. Hands down the best chicken biscuit I have ever had. The chicken was fried throughly, crispy and full of flavor. The biscuit is similar to church's chicken biscuits, honey covered, crunchy edges and a soft middle. My mouth is watering as I think about it. Will definitely be returning for another, SOOOON!

Incredible pizza! Ordered a small build your own with traditional crust, red sauce, sliced tomato and spinach. Cooked to perfection. Fresh ingredients perfectly proportioned and the sauce, omg the sauce!!! First time here, a wonderful community feel pizza joint that also offered trivia tuesdays! Also dog friendly, great draft beer and wine list! So pleased, just may go back before the week is over!

Was there for the clubbing. Not really impressed. But then again, maybe this is just Tampa's fault because Tampa is not a very happening place. 

I hope I never go back.
The most amazing and original food. Garlic bread cheese steak and raspberry peppercorn wings is a must try. Very addicting. And the ownership and staff are awesome and curteous

Everytime my family is in the mood for take out we always go here. Never have we been disappointed. General Tso's and Sweet and Sour Chicken are a must and anything that you might try will be good. Definately a must if your in the area.
Had the charbroiled oysters appetizer (amazing) and drumfish meal (great) highly recommend.  Have returned each time I've been to New Orleans since my first visit.  Can't get enough of the oysters!

I am a massive Patsy Cline fan, so this was #1 on my Nashville stop. 

I got nervous when I walked in and saw so many people for the Johnny Cash area, but once I went upstairs- I felt better. 

I truly enjoyed the detail and personalization of the museum. I learned things I haven't seen previously. 

If you're a fan, this is a must see.

I found Sharon! Formerly of Reds Salon in Yardley/Morrisville Gaslight Village.  Experienced with all kinds of hair. I can always count on her knowledge and understanding to give me the cut and style that I am looking for. RELIABLE!!!

Guac is the best Mexican restaurant in Bellevue. Elegant atmosphere, affable staff, and fine food contribute to this restaurant. Their salsa was beyond standards and their handmade tacos were decent. Definitely coming back soon!

I was referred to Stratman Family Dentistry by Dr. Delasandro after numerous attempts to get a porcelain vaneer to work in my upper mouth.  After 12 attempts by Dr. Delasandro, I decided that I had had enough so I decided to try a new dentist.  My initial impressions of Dr. Stratman were weak because of something that happened on day 1 of my experiences in his office.  He noticed that my nose was crooked and made a point of calling several of his female assistants into the room to check out my "crooked nose".  I though it was most unprofessional and ill-mannered.

I received my new denture with three implants with snap-ons for $10,800 dollars.  They were great at first but then I had difficulty taking them out of my mouth one day and the denture broke in half while pulling it from my mouth.  I told the  assistant that helped me when I first took the broken denture to the dental office that they broke while pulling them from my mouth.  Somehow, the words became "dropped", instead of "pulled them" and Dr. Stratman said that my insurance would have to pay for the repair.  I insisted that they broke while pulling them from my mouth but to no avail.  After checking with another dentist, he said that Stratman was responsible for replacing and repairing my denture.

I haven't been pleased with Dr. Stratman so I've decided to go to a new periodontist, Dr. Cole to continue dental  services.

Dr. Cole did say that my implants looked really healthy.
went for lunch. bottom of the earth house. had the navy bean soup and lentils on toast. pretty solid.

If I could rate with 0 stars I would! Placed an online order for pickup and when it was time for pickup none of the burgers were even ready and the fryer was down because they were installing new fryers in the middle of lunch time! It would have taken an extra 20 minutes to even get the fryers installed. Then we had to wait for the burgers to be made. What's the point of placing a pickup time 45 minutes early when it's not going to be done on time. Very unorganized and bad managing. I really wanted to keep giving this place a try even though they have given us the wrong orders before but this is ridiculous. I like Five Guys but do NOT order from this place!
Great food, they mix their alcohol. So when you are ordering a hibiscus marg, it's more like tequila + hibiscus margarita mix. Not a fan of that.

If tasty food is all it takes to make a place great, the Falcon Coffee Bar has it in the bag. The empanadas are very tasty and the bodega sandwich is delicious. I look forward to trying more! The atmosphere is trying to grasp onto feng shui but dying plants have it just out of reach, but hey, minor detail. The only thing holding me back from giving this eclectic plant based cafe 5 stars is the service. There was no greeting when we walked in. We sat at the bar and perhaps it was our own fault to not be aware that you can not order from there. Eventually we had to interrupt the conversation of the employees to ask where we order... just 3 feet away. We will definitely return for the tasty food with high hopes of better service and perhaps an extra star.
Easily my favorite nail place in Philly. Be sure to make a reservation: the shop is small so they do fill up. (And this is seemingly the only place that gives a back rub while your nails dry!)

Holy moly! We were visiting Tampa for a Busch gardens trip and ordered China Wok for delivery to our hotel. It was delivered fast, the portions were humongous and delicious! Would highly recommend them!
Torani syrups, 2 for $12.00. Good deals on some good looking leather furniture, too.

Came here after a couple drinks so this review may be offset towards the positive. That being said Kin is great. Definitely the best ramen I've had in New Orleans, and I'm glad that New Orleans is finally getting some good Ramen spots. 

I went with the classic Tonkotsu and the broth, as expected, was awesome, very flavorful and creamy.  The brussels sprouts were a nice hipster touch to the otherwise traditional tonkotsu. They cooked really well. The enoki mushroom I also enjoyed, although it was a bit hard to eat. 

The place is small and quaint - the perfect place for a cold night when you crave ramen (since we get so many of those in New Orleans), and the service was nice.

Also kudos to these guys for doing a vegan ramen, which is uncommon, but I'm sure is what I'll try next time. 

Kin is a catch. Worth checking out for sure.

I'm not sure why this is, but of the three or four El Rodeo's I've eaten at, this wasn't the same type of experience.  Food was just 'ok' and the service was fine, just something missing.  Not a very impressive review, just know that when I think of El Rodeo I think higher than this one, so maybe it was a one-time experience problem.

Cute neighborhood pub!  We ordered their St. Louis style pizza which was VERY good!  Can't beat that it was 1/2 price either!  Would definitely go back as long as the weather was nice and we could sit outside again.  It was really smokey inside, but they warn you with signage before you walk in.  If I smoked, this would be my bar!  Dimly lit, quaint, and neighborhoody!

None of their employees were wearing masks.  I don't think these guys have the safety of the public as a priority.  I think its some kind of political statement.    Neither I nor anyone I know will eat there or order take-out until after we are sure the staff is vaccinated or they are wearing masks.   How unprofessional.

Good local feeling place, friendly staff and good bar food. Had the wings. I would rate them a 7 out of ten. I'm a business traveler and this is close to hotels in Birchwood area. I would recommend for a casual meal.

Wonderful store devoted to pet birds and their owners.   The owner is very knowledgeable and caring towards all the animals under his care.  They offer boarding, which is wonderful since it is often hard to find someone to watch your pet parrot, and take time to socialize with the boarded parrots so they don't feel like they were abandoned.

The store sells several kinds of well known food brands as well as their own mixes, many different toys (several have been new favorites for my own fids), a large selection of cages that are appropriately sized for the parrots, some unique odds and ends including dishes and millet.  They also breed certain kinds of birds.

Of course, if you go by there you have to say hello to their friendly Caique Calypso - she really can get wound around your fingers or hair or purse, and is an absolute delight to interact with - it's also a testament to her owner (who runs the store) that she is so friendly. 

Just a wonderful store to pick up things for your favorite bird as well as to interact with other parrots and their owners.

Awesome place for music and drinks (sports too but that's not my thing). The place looks amazing, inside and out and the staff is super friendly and fast. This place alone changes the look and vibe of Ybor and has had a really good crowd the few times I've been in. Relax, play pool, dance, or watch sports... It's all here. This will be my new spot, for sure!

After years of hearing about the hype and the awful experience I had with Party City last Oct (read Yelp review!) coupled with the fact I was privy to the knowledge that a fair amount of stuff would be on sale, I made my maiden voyage to Masquerade.

After we made our way past the mostly plus size $5 corset display and stripper attire masquerading as club attire on the sidewalk in we walked. The left is party decorations as well as children's costumes while the right side is a hybrid of a Hot Topic/Adult costume/make-up counter. What I liked about this place was the variety; it reminded me largely of a place I had visited in NYC called Ricky's (Also year round if I'm not mistaken!). Wigs,hats, stockings, and petticoats galore!

Apparently the place has switched owners and the new owners are clearing out the top adult toy/lingerie section since it doesn't match their tastes. I really enjoyed the corset/pin-up/steampunk/etc section because although it was pricey, it was definitely better quality than the cheesy one-time-use costumes most Halloween places sell for top dollar.

Yeah outdoor seating! My three positives and a negative (because the world needs more positivity):
+ calamari appetizer was one of the best I've ever tasted. Crispy, sweet with a touch of heat. Delightful 
+ my seasonal short rib and Mac n cheese was scrumptious. Apparently it's the third year they have brought it back- for good reason
+ the peanut butter cookie dessert was so worth the calories
- it is a tad pricey ($40/pp for lunch w/o alcohol)
Hidden gem with kids of indoor and outdoor space

I called this company for trash removal of yard debree. After sending a picture of trash via text message I was told they can come tomorrow. A time was never given and I text them my address. I waited all day and no one ever came. I don't understand in this economy how a company can give u p good money. It was taken care of quickly by another company.

It's been a while since I stopped by and had a bite and had heard someone talking about how they had remodeled a while back so I thought it was time to take a peak. The remodel turned out good! Basically the same layout and a different color scheme. Today we tried the street tacos and finger steaks. Both were good but a little lacking. The chicken was just diced chicken without a ton of flavor but it was tender. The rest of the taco was really good with some good spice and flavors. The corn tortillas were warmed up and crispy which was a nice touch. The finger steaks were super tender but a little on the batter was undercooked and on the soggy side. Service was amazing and friendly!

Honestly, I don't get the appeal. Truly one of the most annoying places to go to. Every time I come back to try and give it a second chance, I'm always reminded why i always say I'm never coming back here. Whether it's the bitchy hostesses who act like their job is more important than curing cancer or the wait staff who couldn't find their way out of a wet paper bag. Two nights ago I ordered a steak dinner (medium rare) and the food runner just dropped it off and went on without giving me a knife. Took about 10 minutes before the server came back where I had to ask her for a knife and then an additional 5 more for her to come back with it. Do you think my medium rare steak was still medium rare?
Breakfast Cuban is pretty darn tasty here and the B.E.S.T. is named perfectly for me. You can't go wrong with bacon, fried green tomato, spinach and a runny egg!!
It is not open 24/7 on Saturday that is a bold faced lie to anyone reading this!!!!!
Fantastic Cocktails. The vibe of Square Root makes me want to hang out often. The bar selection is impressive, and the cocktails are top-tier.

Love, love, LOVE this place! I have only been at lunch time, but the food has never let us down. The catfish, the greens, the cabbage, the sweet potato....all perfection! The Asian flavors with the addition of the grill is mouthwatering. I highly recommend this place to any foodie out there!
Delivered to us hot! Super delicious. And not greasy! I love the Kona with the cheddar cheese, and the white pizza with oil and different cheeses. Drooling.

When you're looking for consistently good southern cooking, look no further than this Cracker Barrel. I'll start off with the store part - the store side has a pretty large variety of items to buy, from Tate's Cookies and Moon Pies, to classic TV shows on DVD and a talking parrot toy. The staff members at the store are always nice and helpful. Moving on to the restaurant, I'll start off with the biscuits, and boy are they good! They have a slight sweet taste and once you have one bite, you want to keep eating and eating them till you're out of biscuits. They don't even need any kind of jam, jelly, or butter spread, they're good to eat even plain. The pancakes here are also very good. They're medium sized and then with the 100% pure syrup, you can't go wrong with those, but of course you can't go wrong with their crispy bacon and scrambled eggs either. I've mostly tried the breakfast items on the menu, but for the few times I've had a lunch or dinner menu item, they're good too.  The service here is usually pretty good, except sometimes the food can take a while and the most recent time I ate here for dinner (as of writing this review) we weren't given the breakfast menu, which I thought was odd that we had to ask for it even though breakfast is served all day. Overall, my experience has been consistently good here and I will of course return.

You won't find a better TEAM to clean your house. They have all the right tools, from backpack vacumes for curtains and baseboard to chemicals made for the job.
I had just had our fireplace resurfaced with real stone and the place was covered in stone dust. The girls stayed longer and took care of the mess. I am very particular about my house and its care and maintaince but the maids to the BEST job.

This is the only restaurant in Indy my husband enjoys, as he is a bit of a food snob. The dishes are always adventurous, and we can trust that everything will be cooked perfectly.  We went last night, and were not disappointed with the food; however, the service was surprisingly sub-par.

We were seated in a booth between two parties of couples whose average age was about 60.  I'm not ageist, but I feel I need to mention this, as these parties received much better service from our server than we did (my husband and I are in our mid-thirties).  We were planning on ordering a third drink each, as well as dessert, but our entree plates stayed on our table for roughly 25 minutes after we were finished, even as our server had to wedge himself in between our table and the tables on either side of us.  I finally had to flag down another server to remove our plates and bring us our check.

We've been coming to Oakleys for years, but based on our service last night, and the ever present lack of respect for anyone under the age of 50, I don't think we will be back.

This restaurant would make Gordon Ramsay's head explode. The pages-long laminated menu with pictures of entrees lets diners know that Lincoln Square doesn't really have a specialty. If you're looking for great ambience and fresh food, go somewhere else. I had a veggie wrap that was choc full of pre-cut, formerly frozen vegetables. I hear, though, that breakfast is a markedly better experience, but I won't be returning to try it.
Delicious food, clean restaurant, and fast service. The best sweet tea from any fast food restaurant in the area!

NewsomEye is a group of top professionals.  My appointment was thorough, timely AND I was offered a very conservative approach to care.  A big shout out to Dr. Ochs and his staff.  The optical store was not in any way pushy when I chose to delay my purchase.  Super-impressed by the whole experience.

No better quality fried chicken in the state. Wish there was more options for toppings. The tatter tots though... Fire.
Ps. The ketchup on the tater tots was not my move lol.

The gardens is beautiful, and the restaurant good but pricey.  The cinnamon flavoured butter with the complementary bread was delicious. We ordered the tea lattes and the salads which were fresh.  A great place to come for a meal with great ambiance and good food

So far all I had was the New York style pizza very greasy but was really good compared to other pizza places especially the pizza chains like dominos or papa johns Angelo's pizza was by far better than those places and you get your money's worth

HALLELUJAH, HALLELUJAH!!
Rouses has fixed their security problem. Now, shoppers with large bags and backpacks check their parcels at the customer service desk. It was the most pleasant, least invasive shopping experience I've ever had that involved a bag check. 

And one of the stockers danced with me on Aisle 8. Shopping here is a dream. 

Accessibility notes: Ramps and automatic doors at all entrances. Store is single level. Aisles can be crowded for mobility devices. Shelves and coolers are quite tall; seated shoppers may require reaching assistance. All food service counters (deli, butcher, cheese, prepared food) are high. No braille signage. Carts available for shoppers needing assistance. Can get loud at peak hours.

Originally written on Google. 

Way to much to write on a cell phone. Want to see her REAL reviews check out Yelp or healthgrades.com. She can't alter those. Spoiler alert 1 Star on Yelp 1.7/5 on healthgrades. Where to start??? Hmmm well my story is similar to the other negative reviews. Went for MARRIAGE counseling and in the second appointment (after I walked Out because of false accusations SHE, the fake Dr. doctor made about me such as saying that I said certain things and used it as an excuse. Things I NEVER told her. Something generic she could tell anyone and not be wrong. Like the way a "psychic" words something to MAKE IT apply to you) she told my wife there is no hope and handed her a card for a divorce lawyer (We we're seeking advice on communication and me being better scheduling while being a full time student AND stay at home parent. If we were seeking divorce we would google that)Must be bitter ever since her husband petitioned the court and was granted divorce from her in 2003 (public records ). Anyway back to this "doctor" that's the equivalent of Dr. Pepper being called a doctor. Extremely EXTREMELY unprofessional. Brought us back late for appointment, spent the first 5-10 minutes talking about a previous client and their issues (while you might not know their name but you definitely pass them as you come and go) and her Dog. I kept Track of how much time was actually counseling and how much time I was Paying her to do her personal crap. I will be finding out from the insurance company how long she tries to bill for and will make them aware. Her dog, while friendly, is a MASSIVE distraction. It's a large Golden retriever and it likes to chew. I mention this because I had A $40 fitted hat on my knee, her dog took it to which she replied "he won't hurt it". Ummmm I don't care. It's mine, not his/ yours. Well hat was destroyed to the point she had to throw it away. Then the dog goes INTO my wife's purse and takes her keys out and starts chewing on them. Same response. I get Florida is a pet friendly state but seriously?!?!?! I will ABSOLUTELY be contacting the governing board that oversees her "PhD" and/ or her license to be a counselor.
Absolutely delicious food. Good service. A perfect restaurant. Food was delivered quickly. The waiters are very nice.

Gate 1 has quickly resolved my issue, and I am once again impressed by their efforts to work with clients. Once I was able to get in contact with them, they were able to explain my options and allowed me to select the one that best met my needs.  I'm looking forward to more trips with Gate 1 when it is safe to travel. Thank you, Jesse, for your excellent customer service.
Excellent fresh and authentic Korean food. A healthy alternative to the heavy local cuisine. Large portion sizes and friendly service.

WORST EVER.  Super rude.  I was told that I'm a "pain in the ass".  Right to my face.  Also was told I texted and called too much,  That because I had to call you back to my house 3 times in one day because the new hot water heater continued to leak.  I was charged $850 just for the installation of the heater.  If they did the job correctly the first time 4 years ago I wouldn't need to call them.  STAY FAR AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY!!!!!!

This is a delicious quirky place where the owner makes whatever he is in the mood to try. We started with a special kind of pastry which reminded me of what my great-grandmother created when I was a kid. Then we had a vegetarian stir-fry along with a sable platter. The stir-fry was totally modern and tasty while the sable platter was something I would have gotten at Miller's Deli in Baltimore when I was a teenager. All the ingredients are fresh. The atmosphere is also unique, sometimes featuring a Susan B. Anthony impersonator. You can also get a variety of food to carry out. The indoor tables have embedded parts from discarded computers. It is almost like dropping in at a friend's house for a meal or snack, if your friend happens to be a competent chef. The desserts include homemade rice pudding, pastries, and ice cream made in the store. The folks here are friendly and helpful.
Honestly the best damn chicken parm sandwich I've had.  Hands down.  Make sure to ask for long hots if you like a little spice.

First let me say that I really enjoyed the atmosphere, outside and in. The inside is dim, like a pub, but not so dark that you can't read your menu. Our server was polite and attentive. They were busy, but I never felt forgotten. 

The food was good, not great, but very satisfying. My friend had the pasty, which is one of his favorite dishes there. (He's visits there regularly.)

I had the tabouli with Mahi Mahi, blackened. I liked that it was lemony and fresh, but I did wish it had chunks of cucumber in it. It came with hummus and pita, which was also tasty and lemony. 

The beverage menu, I was a little disappointed in the beer selection, the only dark beer offering was Guinness. I also thought it was a little pricy in contrast to the food, which I thought was reasonably priced.

Finally, my friend and I don't like smoky bars. I don't even know if it's allowed there inside, because neither of us noticed. 

All in all, I'd do it again. I'm Looking  forward to trying the curry.

I really really really want to love Kunitz. It's locally owned, has a decent selection of good quality shoes, and has a (supposedly - I've never actually purchased) really great service - including a basically never-ending warranty. If you find something you like and they don't have your size, they'll gladly call the other store and order it in for you (if you pre-pay), and you can return sale items.

But I just 'like' Kunitz. Their selection is just plain not really my style. Every once in a while I'll find something I like but the fit is always super strange (what is it with higher end shoes always been so freakin' narrow?). 

But the thing I REALLY dislike about Kunitz? Their sale section. I know, I know. I LOVE sale sections. But their's is outside, over top of a grated sidewalk with an almost constant breeze coming up it from the LRT or whatever passing below. So if you dare to look at their sale shoes, you will get a constant breeze of dust and debris in your face and up your pant legs. It's SO gross.

Kunitz: There is a reason you can't seem to get rid of those sale shoes. Just sayin'.

Sat at the drive thru forever how do you forget that you have people in the drive thru? Wtf horrible service dirty inside employee always outside smoking instead of inside working no wonder it's forever this place needs to get it together it's fast for a.

Low-quality merchandise for high prices and rude customer service. I visited this shop with my boyfriend and mother. The salesman was a little pushy to get me to look at this dress and try it on. It was adorable, and I was thinking of buying it. I'm a picky buyer, and I try only to buy things that are both high quality and made ethically. It felt a little cheap, and I went on my phone to look up the brand to see where it was made. As I was doing that, the salesman saw I was on my phone and came back over and rudely said, "do not take pictures of my clothes." I wasn't and thought that was not polite. My mother said, "she wasn't; she was trying to look up to see where the brand was made." As he walked away while she was speaking, and said, "I'll tell you," but did not do so. I have no intention to hurt a small business; it's crucial to keep them in business. But that behavior was unacceptable and made us uncomfortable in the store. Oh, and as for the dress itself, it was significantly cheaper online. He was selling it for a high markup. It was also made in China, where the child labor laws are laxer, and many work in poor conditions.

I only get my nails done on special occasions and I decided to try Blooming Nails. They did a great job, but it was unenjoyable. I was very disappointed. I got a mani and a pedi and they did them at the same time. I was unable to sit back and relax and enjoy my pedi because someone was painting my nails at the same time. I wanted a design so I had to watch and explain during my amazing foot rub. If they were treated as 2 separate services they would have gotten way more stars.

Dropped by this place on 7/20/13 to check out the new ownership and just to have beer on the deck. I have to say that I will NOT be stopping by any time soon. This place has no idea what they are doing. I orderd a half Guinness /Half Cider located on the bottom of the drink menu, afte about 5 mins the waitress came out and asked if I wanted Two drinks? I showed her the menu and explained what I wanted again, when she came out I had a glass of just Cider! I sent it back and for the THIRD time told her what I wanted. About 20 mins into ordering these drinks she finall got it right. Another couple sat down next to us and waited over 45 mins for their dinner and had to cancel cause the meal didnt show and they were going to a movie in less then 15mins. THe four top of individuals on the other side of us was complaining out getting there desserts and they had been waiting over 30 minutes????? Not a single table around us was happy. It took more that 25 mintues to get our check and the waitress said you can just leave your beers are on me. Needless to say we did pay our bill and left. This place needs some management skills. Its a great location, the outside patio is awesome but service is poor!

This picture speaks a million words. Yes I have eaten pizza here more times than I probably  remember. This is the worst it's even been so burnt on the bottom that it tastes like nothing. Unfortunately the "supervisor" had nothing to say about this and said we should come back to exchange it even though we are on the other side of town. V disappointed

OH MY YUMMINESS!!! I read about this place on line- but it was the picture of the Yoda cocktail ( done as a Star Wars tribute not available the day I went :(  ) 
That made me want to try it out - 
Even without having my Yoda moment the food and drink - I can't remember the name of the cocktail but I got to keep the flamingo float  :) - it was a phenomenal experience - I had the cheesy Todd - a bacon burger that has deep fried mac and cheese hockey puck sized discs for the buns - sweet mother of pearl!! I was in my happy place- I'm still in my happy place!! Our waitress was well informed took great care of us she recommended a chocolate desert that I am driving  back to Tampa for . The decor of the restaurant is cool and a little edgy- comfortable for an older or younger crowd - I am visiting Tampa in a few weeks I will be back at Datz to try the rest of their amazing menu!!!
Jeff & Chris at SB Auto Group BMW found the car I wanted and worked out a good lease deal.  Very pleasant to deal with and responsive.  Good guys.

If you have a party of ten or more, reserve the back room or don't bother coming. The tables are high which made it exceptionally difficult for my grandmother and other older family members to sit at. It was also hard for everyone to chat with each other too.

Used a travelzoo deal for a romantic dinner. Really enjoyed the following:

- city views
- intimate environment
- food
- friendly staff

I don't think I would go here without a deal since the total $ i spent using the deal was around $120-140.

This is our go to take out at least once a week. Food is great. My kids love the burritos. I just wish they are being consistent of giving out the salsa. Most of the time we got two salsa (1 mild & 1 hot) for every take out. We usually order 2 burritos. However, some other time they only give out one or NONE at all, making up excuses for not giving them or try to charge us for it if we ask them. Well, that excuses just DOES NOT fly when you order the same thing 2 days ago and got two salsa with it.

So today was my FIRST time in Philadelphia. I wanted to have a Philly Cheesesteak. I was told by a Philly resident to go to the Reading Terminal Market if I'd like a tasty cheesesteak. There I joined a long line at Carmen's Famous Italian Hoagies! The service was great! Michelle the cashier was pleasant and friendly! Dan the server you ROCK! and Jeremy on the Grill you made a MEAN Kamikaze Cheesesteak! MY CHEESESTEAK WAS MOUTHWATERING! Right amount of heat! The flavor was on point, it was worth the wait. I can't wait to return to Philly see you guys in the near future! 

Recommendation:
If you're planning on being in the Philadelphia area, please stop by the Reading Terminal Market for the MOST DELICIOUS CHEESESTEAK from CARMEN'S! 

Sign 4 of clubs!
Well done, as usual!  Great location, very friendly staff and great food!  Hopefully outdoor seating next spring?

I've now been to this place a couple of times, and I have to say it's my favorite spot in town so far for vegan grub. My first choice was the French Dip (to which I added Daiya mozzarella) and it was reaaaally good. I love the way they cook their seitan in thin slices, which gives the whole thing a great texture. I got to try the Philly Cheesesteak as well, but the sauce wasn't very reminiscent of the standard Philly cheese I've had before, so I think it would have been better with just Daiya instead.

The next time we came I got the Cali-style burger with Daiya added and it was fantastic(it had ranch dressing and avocado, mmmm). The dill cauliflower-carrot veggie of the day was OK, but needed a little salt. 

Both of the cakes we tried were amazing, and the last one was called the "Cookie Monster Cake". The thing was massive, and pure awesome. The cream-filled chocolate chip cookie pie we snagged was heavenly as well. Just the right flavor and not overwhelming.

The staff was very friendly and I'm thrilled to have found this place! Another thing I really liked is that all of the items are vegan by default, but you can add dairy cheese and such if you want. Can't wait to go back!
Good place for a special occassion dinner - the garden dining room is so pretty!
A must stop in New Orleans! Such a quaint and historical spot. Really enjoyed the muffuletta and pimms cup. Jambalaya was so so. Overall great service and food for a reasonable price.

I really like these apartments and the people who run it. Everyone is extremely nice and helpful. Maintenance is responsive. The only thing that bothers me is the fact that they won't let the dogs use the grass to go to the bathroom. My dog is very picky where he goes and the grass is 2 feet away from where the "designated pet use area" is. He's very strong and will pull me down trying to get to the grass. Mostly pet friendly other than that hiccup.  Overall I think they're good apartments. They're built very nicely and well thought out.

Came here for a drink and was a little disappointed. Their drink specials are way over priced for what they are, and especially because they come in a plastic cup. It's the middle of the day on Monday I don't know why they wouldn't serve things in a regular glass. Also our waitress wasn't very attentive or very welcoming. The only upside to this place was the really awesome musician they had, he made the experience better.

Excellent place.  The food is delicious and authentic Italian....freshly made and the sauce is some of the best we've ever tasted.  

I highly recommend this place to everyone.  It's now my family's favorite Italian restaurant.

This is your typical greasy spoon chain restaurant that is best served just after massive amounts of alcohol, in the middle of making bad decisions, right before you go to bed. Maybe not your bed, and maybe not to sleep, though after a night of poor decision making after hanging out with friends, you should go straight to your house, alone, and go to bed, making this place not be one of your bad decisions.

Seriously though. It's not really that bad of a place. This location is on the west side of Elysian Fields between I-10 and I-610, just east and north of the French Quarter. For some reason, they tend to be filled with families Saturday and Sunday mornings. This location has opened in the last 5 years or so, which is probably a good thing. The food is not healthy, they don't serve anything that isn't slathered in butter and cooked on the grill. And not the good butter.

Some visits are good, the food and beverage order taken quickly at tables that are clean, and everything runs smoothly. When things of bad, they are stuck mid-spiral near the very end, and you are more than likely to get dirty and feel disgusted with yourself and everyone around you. The current management seems more intent on not managing, but cooking.

The best thing I can say is usually the floors are clean. But I will probably be back. I am into making bad decisions once they have started for the night. Or if I am in the area with a friend. Or wanting breakfast while I am in the area. If you want a cheap meal with no frills and no pretension, come here. Do not have high expectations of anything wonderful.
Usually great and relatively quick, but almost 2 hours for delivery is unacceptable. Food arrived cold, not even a little bit warm....  My delivery needs will be met by other places in the future.

Do Not buy a Toyota from these people! I was in need of a new vehicle when my Lexus needed a new transmission. The staff at this dealership informed me that my best option would be a new vehicle since I owed more than my vehicle was worth and that I'd need a cosigner. I brought in a cosigner to get a new Camry, after purchasing the vehicle I realized that the vehicle they sold me did not have standard safety features, blind spot warning and rear cross alert which I wanted nor did the finance personnel put any of the paperwork for the vehicle in my name. When going back to the dealership for these issues I was told I need to keep the vehicle for a year before I would be able to upgrade out of it. 3 mos after I returned to the dealership and I am told I can get a totally new camry in my own name with zero money down but they cannot fix the paperwork for the vehicle I am currently in that was inaccurately titled in my cosigners name only!!!! Save yourself the headache the sales staff and finance department at this location are rude pushy do not listen to customers and will not stand behind their deals or fix their mistakes!!!!
Great food, service and atmosphere. Their happy hour appetizers are fantastic.

It's worth the wait! While I did not have a bad beignet in all of NOLA, this place stuck out. Their beignets were by far the thickest and most dense, I really enjoyed them. The wait goes super fast, I made it a point to stop here twice. The staff is super friendly and quick moving. It is cash only!

I have been coming here for years! I've seen three different owners.

The individuals who do the pedicures take their time and don't zip through it like there's a prize at the end of the finish line. I love the massage chairs. I usually get the sea salt pedi.

I usually go to Chow or her husband. They take their time and listen to what I want and sometimes I don't even have to tell them, they just know. 

Although I have great experiences here, I didn't give them a full 5 stars due to how I have seen other customers get serviced. I have seen people walk in for appointments and are waiting 15 mutes or more after their scheduled appointment-unacceptable. I have also seen people walk out because they don't always get greeted when they walk in, sometimes I wanna say hello to the customer myself.

I got a spa facial here by Maureen. I heard about the salon from Groupon. It was the best facial I've ever had. She took her time, was very gentle, the scents of the creams and concoctions were delightful, and I really felt relaxed when I was leaving. The extractions were done sonically with minimal discomfort, if any. I'm 37 wks pregnant and I'm pretty sensitive, so that's saying a lot!They also had the cutest jewelry that was very reasonably priced, of which I bought a few pieces. The owner finished my transaction and she was lovely as well. Overall, I will definitely be back and highly recommend Wave Reviews! Good vibes when you walk in, friendly atmosphere and great services.

Ahhhhh, a nostalgic, idyllic, relaxing trip on an old train. Conductors and Hostesses (I know - the latter term is not politically correct any longer; I'm just trying to stick to the era-theme) all dressed to the nines in their 50's-appropriate regalia. What could be better?

Well, for starters, maybe the smell. Musty, mildewy, moldy? Not sure what it is, but these cars all reek. And for a trip sipping and savoring wine, where the "nose" is an integral part, that's very unfortunate. These cars need to be thoroughly cleaned and disinfected, steamed and sanitized, then completely dried.

Smells aside, the trip was also a bit bumpy, simply not as smooth as some vintage train lines we've travelled. And we stopped no fewer than 6 or 8 times on our trip to the winery while crews cleared the track of debris from the previous night's storm. Something like that can't be avoided and it simply added an air of mystery - sort of - to the excursion. 

The tasting of 12 samples from DelMonaco Vineyards? Well it was ok. Our server was knowledgable and congenial. And all-in-all it was an enjoyable sippin' trip. The wines were not stellar vintages, there was a mash-mash of styles and varietals - acceptable but limited. We tried some light, some cidic, a dry or two, some sweet, and a dessert-style. The latter was surprisingly delightful - an old-fashioned, full-bodied sweet (but not too sweet) Blackberry!

Our excursion tickets also included a foil-wrapped sausage biscuit, acceptable for a quick nibble, but hardly a palate cleanser between samples. Then again, crackers and cheese  were available for purchase in the "dining car"...(a better label might be "snack car".)

Our return trip from the winery was raucous and musically entertaining as two separate groups - a "cowboy-themed, and a jazz-themed - made their way through the cars to entertain with live performances. Nice!

I will admit that my overall judgment in this review is clouded slightly by some of the more elegant and refined excursions I've travelled by train (especially the Napa Valley Wine Train). But for the price, this nostalgic ride was fun and worth a day trip by rail.

Went here our first night in Reno. Excellent vibes and no line. We ordered the braised pork bowl, string beans with minced pork, and egg wrapped pancake. The beans were very flavorful and go well with rice. Pork bowl was also very authentic. Pancake was really good, the egg was wrapped in layers around a scallion pancake! Highly recommend visiting here if you want good Taiwanese food in Reno.

Came here on a Wednesday and had a free chicken sandwich and 2 pieces of chicken through t-mobile. The drive thru line appeared long so i mobile ordered and went inside to pick up.

Upon entering, the line for service was long (at least 8 people) and the interior was truthfully filthy. There were napkins, uncooked fries, and even a piece of chicken on the floor in the back. Employees also seemed to have no care about restaurant appearance either. I saw some in the back with no mask on eating and employees handle money, pick up food, and grab new food with the same glove.

I waited over 25 minutes inside the restaurant for a sandwich and 2 pieces of chicken. I understand some delay but 25 minutes! Many other customers seemed fed up and the drive thru line didn't seem any faster.

I asked for spicy on all my chicken, but i saw the employee grab mild pieces of chicken instead, right in front of me. The food itself was not good, the chicken was very dry and heavy (despite asking for light meat) and the sandwich seemed poorly assembled. Sandwich also had little flavor. Also the sauce portion is nothing close to what i believe you need for the food portion.

Even though it was free, i was still extremely disappointed by the atmosphere, cleanliness, service, and taste. I would not recommend this establishment to anyone and this is my first ever popeyes experience.

Tried to call to order dinner while visiting from out of state and the girl acted like she couldn't hear us and was rude. Decided to just order online. Went and picked up the food about 8pm the got back to the hotel to cold food, wings that weren't even covered in sauce, nasty tasting ranch, and not even the extra ranch we paid for! Tried calling the manager for over a hour! Called home office and they said they would just refund me and have the location call me no phone call or refund yet... while checking to see if I have been refunded yet and looking at my bill I realize I was charged 2 times! Called by American Express to dispute the charge and they said they swiped my card while in the store too! The email said to bring the credit card in on pickup to verify order and they decided to be sneaky and charge me again when we thought they were just verifying the order! Called home office again and they don't seem to concerned... we will never eat at another Hooters again!

I took my car into Eddie's because I was tired of feeling like Firestone (et al) was taking me for the proverbial ride.  First off, it is obvious that this is a mom and pop place.  I would much rather give my money to a place like that than Firestone.  Secondly, everyone I talked to, in person or on the phone, was extremely courteous and thorough with explanations.  I thought the bill was fair.  I will definitely be going back even though it is a drive for me.

After going to Nail Dior in Odessa and having a terrible experience with them me and my bridal party headed to this place and they took us all in!  They're phenomenal I 100% recommendation this place!

This is my favorite cheesestake in Philly , It's a must when you visit Philadelphia. One small issue is that they have to do something to organize the waiting lines . A number must be giving to customers and must be shown outside the restaurant so everyone can see it when they call your number for picking up instead of calling names and no one can hear it specially if you're waiting outside .

Best Saladworks location. We come here regularly. They're nice people, who care about every single customer that walks in. The salads are always fresh, and their customer service is great. Every time we go to a different location, we're disappointed by some aspect of it. This is the only one where we feel like home, where we know we'll be satisfied every single time.

IF I COULD GIVE ZERO STARS I WOULD! Just came back from an absolutely horrendous experience!!! What a WASTE OF TIME!!!!!! Made an appointment to see a 98 Lincoln Navigator, drove over an hour and also called right before we left to tell them we were coming!!!!!!! We get there and this classes girl gave us attitude and tells us the Navigator WAS SOLD THIS MORNING and that we should have called first!!!!!!!! ARE YOU KIDDING ME!!!!!!! NO HONEY, YOU SHOULD HAVE CALLED US RIGHT AFTER IT SOLD!!!!!!!!!!! WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL US WHEN WE CALLED TO TELL YOU WE WERE ON OUR WAY!!!!!!!!!! WE WASTED THE TRIP, GAS  AND TOLLS!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Just a suggestion, instead of giving your potential customers a lot of attitude, like we are bothering you, take customer service courses and learn how to treat people with RESPECT!!!!!!!!!!

I got a VIP Bottle Service for me and my daughters Birthday Celebrating in the same month. My first time there. The Director Joe was very nice. We had a wonderful time and loved the D.J. The Music was all that. Kept me and my friends and family dancing throughout the night. I wish I knew my waitress/server name. She was very sweet and helpful. From reading the reviews about how the waitress and servers are rude. I guessed I luck out! I was very fortunate to get a VIP in the back corner thank God we did bc it got super crowded around the other VIP Sections. The only issue was I was not aware of how the Bottled prices were calculated into the VIP Bottle Service bill. I was told up to 12 people liquor/Juices was included. I was shocked when I saw the menu that a regular size Grey goose is around $400. The bottle VIP should have been explained that if your VIP Bottle Section is up to 12 people and that the price is around $850 so therefore you can only get basically two regular bottles for that price since it's close to $400 for each bottle. At least they could have larger bottles! And I was not told over the phone that I would have to share a VIP Section with only two small tables. A few of us went outside to get some air and came back just to find a crowd of people sitting in our VIP Section I Thought!!!  I asked if we could have pizza and a cake. The Director Joe said yes not realizing when it was delivered that we had nowhere to put these pizza boxes bc the other crowd was still hanging around. Eventually they left and we enjoyed the rest of the night. A beautiful experience with the D.J and selection of music.
