Good and great. The pho made with beef broth very oily...the hush puppies burnt but such interesting things gs on the menu and great staff that I will try it sgsin.

Amazing amazing amazing!   I wanted something sweet during this Coronavirus lockdown so I googled bakeries nearby.  I had never been here so I stopped in.  The hazelnut/chocolate mousse was divine.  I bought a loaf of Italian bread also.  I have never had any better!  Delicious!

Yes! So glad to have another location aside from the crowded Avon one. This one is far more quiet which is surprising given the number of people that are regularly dining here.  Been a few times since they've opened and service is great and the food is consistently good!

I've been regularly going to Causeway for almost 10 years. Why? Above all else, it has all the things you are looking for from a fish store: the tanks are exceptionally CLEAN, there is a good stock/ rotation of HAPPY & HEALTHY FISH, the staff is very KNOWLEDGABLE & you are shopping LOCAL!!! 
Thank you Causeway Pets for being my trusted & dependable choice for fish & supplies!

Have been two times now and I love this place. Cute seating area, great prices, friendly staff. Chicken and beef pho were both delicious and HUGE. Only $1-2 more than the baby bowls at Bamboozle Cafe, but you probably get 4x as much soup. I will definitely be back to try the other dishes and the teas. Also, good music is always playing!

I absolutely love China House. It is just a few blocks from my office, so my co-workers and I order it quite frequently. Yes, it is a true "hole in the wall" kind of place, but the food is excellent. The egg rolls are always crispy and delicious. I usually order the Curry Chicken or Chicken Fried Rice - both are awesome. You should definitely check this place out if you're looking for a new favorite Chinese food place.

First time here and we have a great time eating an awesome ice cream!
Santa Bárbara, thank you!

First of all the coffee is so addicting, I got the horchata cold brew and it was perfectly sweet, and I got the corn tamales since I don't eat meat and it was bomb as well as a good value plus the employees were super sweet and helpful ! Green Tamale Bowl

Very friendly staff.  Good place for locals to take a break while at Clearwater Beach.  The food is reasonably priced and the drinks are good.  Tap beer is $3.00 a pint or you can get a whole pitcher for $7.95.

Great food, okay service. 

I sampled the iceberg wedge salad, such an indulgence with bacon and creamy dressing. The hot wings were the best I have had in the city, seriously. The hummus plate was better than I expected, everything was fresh and it came with a tomato, cucumber, red onion and feta salad that was outstanding. The crispy chicken sandwich was very good, the bread tasted just like my mom's and it wasn't too greasy. Fries weren't great, not terrible either.

This is much better quality and variety than the bar food we have in Fairmount. I'd kill to have North Third in my neighborhood.

I'm usually a fan of warrior because every time I've been the staff has been laid back and fairly nice. Today my service was terrible. I came in just to buy another bar for my conch because I had lost the screw on ball. The guy behind the counter kept pushing me to buy other items and I politely refused and stated multiple time I just wanted to buy the same bar (gauge and length) he then proceeded to force me to have the piercer put the jewelry in for me. It's a nice free service but I don't see the problem in letting someone leave after making a purchase. Neither the piercer or the person who sold me the jewelry showed me what the actual piece looked like so I assumed it was what I asked for... the same bar with the same length and balls on each end. It was dumb of me to trust either of the people because when I came home and checked the piece had a flat back and a shorter bar.. exactly what I told the guy I did NOT want. So I'm pretty pissed and disappointed since I came to warrior over infinite because I thought it'd be more laid back but I was wrong and it wasn't worth it.

Great place.  Excellent friendly service with good food.  I love to sit outside and enjoy the Reuben sandwich with an ice tea.  It's a great sandwich because they use thick corn beef brisket, cooked for hours, everyday.  Home made French fries are special too!

Usually do not have a problem here but Father's Day 7/17 my wife went to pick up breakfast and when she got home my steak egg and cheese bagel was a steak and bacon bagel????? No egg , not worth driving back for but from now on checking food before I or she leaves. Steak and bacon are a strange combo and tasted pretty gross.

Richard White PA is easily one of the WORST "doctors" I have ever come into contact with. I need excused absences from work so I don't get fired and lose my job and he stated that's "against banners policies" and when I asked to see this "policy" in writing he told me he does not have to show me that policy "because I am not an employee" It is well within my patient rights to have access to that material. All I wanted was antibiotics for my leg and a note so I could rest my leg and not get fired from my job for having unexcused absences. Richard even told me I could lose my leg from this infection but refused to give me more than 1 1/2 days off of work to heal. I will be contacting Richards supervisor Anabel Bevan at 520-381-6777 and filing a formal complaint against this "doctor" and the banner urgent care as a whole. In my notes it says he advised me of things he absolutely never advised me of and that I was "satisfied with my visit" which is absolutely not the case. When I came back in to the office to ask why Richard lied in his notes I could hear him talking poorly of me with co workers and the reason for my visit using my name and speaking ill of me stating that I am "just being lazy and not wanting to work" I will be in touch with an attorney for medical malpractice and falsifying official notes.

Ok this will be short and sweet because I love this place . First off comfort food and good portions I almost always get a open face turkey of it aint broke ..... also the burger and shake is a good deal oh and they have a full bar for my drinkers out there . Great spot is my go to over pegs or Lou lous or whatever that place is on kietzke.

There's a review here that says about long wait times "if it wasn't any good, people wouldn't wait"

We came, we were told 15min, waited closer to 40, seated in a section that was EMPTY except for us (why were we waiting??) and then were served food a starving dog would think twice about. So while the opening statement may be true, for me, it will only happen once.

No return planned

I went in to get a couple shirts tailored. 3 people working, but none can say hello or even look up. Finally after standing for a few moments, I was acknowledged but not helped. This place is not keen on professionalism or customer service. 

I turned and walked out, and they didn't speak a word. That told me exactly how much they cared. Take your business elsewhere if possible, you'll be happier for it.
Some of the best Chinese in the area. Solid Rangoons, General Tso's and really any other dish is good.

This new place took over the Crispers  spot next to TJ Maxx.  They have 10 banh mi sandwiches to choose from.  We chose the grilled pork.  It came warmed on a french baguette with fresh vegetables-cucumber slice, carrots, jalapeño, cilantro and mayo.  The pork was seasoned with teriyaki and was nicely flavored.  They also had a nice selection of bubble tea.  We had the peach black tea with strawberry popping boba and peach green tea.  Both delicious.  On to dessert...they had too many to choose from!  They had a refrigerated case with everything from tiramisu, to chocolate cake, to cheesecakes, to macaroons.   In the bakery case, which they claim is freshly baked everyday,  they again had a wide variety of croissants, all flavored danishes and other pastries.   We chose a refrigerated dessert, Love Heart and 3 different pastries...delish!  Along the wall with the danishes, they offered all kinds of packaged snacks. They staff was welcoming and friendly.  Only down side, I wished I could have had the pastry warmed with a cup of coffee...other than that, me and my daughter will definitely return to try other items on their menu.

First....small local. I did that. Outstanding people that own and work here! 

Second...We drive 30 minutes one way to go here, passing several other pizza joints. It's that good, and that cool.

Third...they have homemade custards. And a big pic of Bourdain on the wall. Whats not to like?

Not a good experience. Up all night because the drapes dont close and a bright light shines in the window. Not a good neighborhood could not sleep, kept thinking  my car would be broken into while the bright lights kept me up. Very small rooms, especially the bathroom. Had to use your room key to get into the breakfast room.  Hmmm something is wrong there. No regular coffee. Finally asked for some and they brought to the room full of waiting guests desperate for coffee. Scary place.

Very crusty bread and bagel. Fast service. You can place your order on the ordering station.

Cherry bread has a weird artificial flavor. I'm not a big fan of it.

Canele has a nice crunch shell with moist filling but it is missing a citrusy flavor, it's quite bland.

Egg tart. I thought it would be sweet like those in Chinatown but to my surprise it's savory. Nice creamy custard.

Chocolate croissant and chocolate cookie are my favorite from today's haul. 

Bagel is relatively small.

In the middle of the bakery there's a shelf with bread as a display. You can also pick up some coconut pretzel shortbread and burnt toast biscotti. Against the wall on the right you can grab some flour.

There's no sign of the capacity so some people are waiting outside. I suggest that there's a sign indicating the room capacity for social distancing
Great salsa bar. Spice it up to taste. The tacos are worth the 3.50-.75. They are medium sized. Tortillas were fresh.
Fun walking tour. Our tour guide Scott was awesome! Definitely would recommend to any AHS Coven fans.

South Street Barbers (SSB), I'm really impressed by this establishment, I've been a regular patron of SSB for the last 8 years at their 1302 South St. location, now I'm a regular at their second location at 1316 South Street location. 

These two shops have amazing barbers, they are very knowledgeable, whether you go in needing advice on what hairstyle to get or even if you need help deciding how short or how long you want your hair to be, SSB can help, they give the best haircuts and whenever i go in and can't get with my regular barber Will, I feel comfortable enough to utilize any of the other barbers in that day. One thing I really love about SSB is the structure that's in place at these shops, all staff members are always courteous and respectful and it's also kid friendly, whenever a child enters the shop the staff is always guaranteed to make sure the conversation in the shop and the movie on the television is safe for a child's ears......I've been to a lot of other shops in my life but SSB has them all beat by far. The owner has done a fantastic job catering to the public and making sure his staff is up to par. One other thing I love about SSB is that all of the barbers try and make sure customers get in the chair at their scheduled appointment time slot and they are always accountable for their actions so if the customer is not satisfied for any reason they will work with you and try and make things right. They always take their time and cater to each customer and focus their energy on satisfying their clients. 

I live in Delaware, but I come all the way to Philadelphia to get my haircuts because South Street Barbers are awesome. If you are looking for a barber shop that cares about you the client being happy, SSB is definitely the place to go. I highly recommend them. Everything about SSB screams customer satisfaction.
This place is very good.  I wish it had more inside seating.  Great for lunch when you want something out of the ordinary with out having much time.

The only saving grace was the bar tender- I visited April 29 and took a friend to show something that reminded us of good times- I was served raw chicken, bloody raw, and they replaced it with some of the worst bbq and beans ever- couldn't finish the nasty crap served. Biffs was great for decades- now no longer- sad. 

Bikers won't even eat the food... and that's bad!

Amazing!!! If you are looking for a delicious meal cooked to perfection in New Orleans than look no further. Literally every item we had was cooked to perfection and tasted incredible. I had the chicken and andouille gumbo to start, and my girlfriend had the Brussels sprouts. We shared the plaquemines chicken, and it was tasty and so tender. The asparagus and potatoes that came with it were also very well cooked with just the right amount of crisp. To top it all off, there is the loveliest outdoor patio to enjoy when the weather is right. I cannot recommend this place enough.

I have been going to Ninfa at Strands for almost 15 years now and I can't say enough positive things about her and the salon in general! Ninfa is amazing with color. She has serious talent and a great eye when it comes to helping her clients try something new. And when it comes to cuts, she is an absolute perfectionist! She will spend the extra time and effort making sure your new style is exactly what you've been looking for. She is so creative and always has great suggestions when I'm thinking about changing up my look! In addition to her many talents with hair, she is an unbelievable makeup artist as well. When I got married almost three years ago, it was a no brainer to choose Ninfa and her team at Strands to do my hair and my bridesmaids' hair. She and her team made us all look and feel like a million bucks. Nothing but great things to say about Strands - they are seriously the best in the business!

Worst Chinese food we've ever had. My boyfriend and I tried this place for the first time yesterday. Everything we ordered was horrible. The wonton soup had a sour taste, the chicken in my chow mein was fake and rubbery. Crab Rangoon's were sub par and my boyfriends general tsos was horrible. Even the rice was bad- dry and tasteless. We ended up sick after eating it too. I never write reviews but I do read them. I feel misled by all the positive reviews so I felt compelled to write my own. Try this place at your own risk.

I've gone to Lee Nail Spa for a few years now. Even after I moved closer to other Nail Spa's, I still drive back to go here. 

I've never had an unpleasant experience here. Everyone is very nice. Pricing is extremely reasonable. The facility itself is very clean. You can see that all of the tools they use to perform manicures and pedicures are clean and have been autoclaved. 

I must specifically give a shout out to Angela. She does the BEST eyebrows. She waxes my eyebrows and I've been asked on several occasions by friends if they're microbladed because they're so clean cut and shaped well! 

I highly recommend Lee Nail Spa. They do sometimes get very busy. Ive most often had luck walking in but you can make appointments as well.

OMG, the Kona coffee! I was transported back to my 2012 vacation to Maui! I also love that they are willing to accommodate the customers with whatever they want or need. Food was excellent and service was attentive and friendly. I will definitely be back, and specifically to this location.

We moved to the area during COVID and have only gotten to get takeout, which I am traditionally hesitant on in general. They have beyond exceeded our expectations and changed my mind on takeout being not as good as in restaurant.

They always have great service. The quality of food is great and the prices are amazing for the quality. It all feels fresh and we will continue to order from here.

My boyfriend and I went to dinner here for the first time, Saturday February 18th. We were about a half hour early for our 745 reservation. To our surprise, there was no record of our Open Table reservation. My boyfriend had screenshotted our confirmation as we have had this problem once before. He showed to shot, only for he and the hostess to both realize the reservation was for Friday, the 17th.  My boyfriend was clearly embarrassed and apologetic. 
Without skipping a beat, the Hostess told us we could enjoy our meal in the liunge at a table with a lovely view and no wait, or we could have a drink abd wait to see if she could fit us in. She suggested grabbing an app as it was a very busy night. 
Knowing our situation, the Hostess went out of her way to find us a great table and seat us within minutes of our reservation!! Well 24 hours and minutes from our reservation. Our apps were sent up to.our table and our night was FLAWLESS!!! 
The food was out of this world!!! We had shrimp cocktail. I had Ahi Tuna with lobster and crab. He had Prime rib. 
I have nothing but great things to say about the food, cocktails a VALUE. 
If a manager reads this, PLEASE, give your Saturday hostess a raise!!! She was clearly the lead, dark hair. She MADE our night!!!

I've made it a point to try chicken wings across four continents and these win hands down. 

Not for the grease-averse.

The food here is fantastic.  I had the rissoto with squash, kale and snap peas.  I ate every last bite of it.  The service was good as well.  The food came out quickly and the folks working at the counter were very friendly.  The portion size was perfect.  I also like the flavored water (today's was mint and orange) available at the bar.  Since I was on my lunch hour, I didn't have wine, but none the less, was impressed with the selection.  Can't wait to try their "crush" wine next time.

I gave Bowl a 4 instead of 5 due to a couple kinks they need to work out.  

First, the menu.  Its on a chalk board and is handwritten which is fine, but the writing itself is impossible to read.  

The other thing that is a big problem for me (which will prevent me from doing anything other than takeout for several months) is that it is absolutely FREEZING in the dining area.  I get that it's part of West Street Market so it's going to have more of an outdoor market feel but seriously my toes were frozen and my nose was red by the time I left.  Please get some outdoor heaters!

I can't comment on their repair services, but I'm always able to have questions answered and find a good selection when looking for a part or accessory. They seem to carry items in varying price ranges, which is helpful if you're on a budget, or really looking to invest in something. During business hours, they have a tire pump station set up outside. A great convenience to have in the neighborhood!

Also, I could walk in with my well-behaved dog, which is always a plus :)
Average food, nothing special. Placed order for boneless wings and 2 minutes later they're out...damn good microwave!

This is the second time we've eaten at Ms. Hazel's Kitchen.  So delicious and so much food!  Home cooking indeed!  The best part is that it's not too salty.
Actually, the best part is Mrs. Hazel!  She is a sweetheart!
We ordered 2 fried chicken, medium size meals.  One could've fed both of us; great value!  The morrel (hibiscus) drink is one of a kind!  It leaves a kick in the throat.
Minus a star because the chicken was a little dry.  The rum cake is good, but I think a little scorched.
We will continue to visit Mrs. Hazel!

I have always loved Chili's in the past.  Their food is very good, but I found the daytime Bartender to be rude to my face.  Making remarks to my husband about me, in front of me.  This was My Date Day and I was disappointed.  I'll never ask to come to this location again.  I'm sure this review doesn't mean that all Chilis are the same.  I know for a fact they are not. BYE!

This place is hip, cool, and "open" for business! It has such a cool atmosphere - very aesthetic and hipster. I went here and had one of the best lattes ever. While I love their drinks, they are on the pricier side. And you have to be a true coffee connoisseur to know what all the drinks are and what goes into them. I had to ask about a few. But they are creative and delicious!

I wanted to update my review and add a star for the Chef. He invited us back for a private tasting after we had shared our previous experience.  We really enjoyed tasting some new foods and the origin behind them. The kitchen staff were so nice and entertaining too. Businesses that are transparent and open to feedback make all the difference and we really appreciated the entire experience! It was a treat getting to know all of them.
Excellent brews and very comfortable setting.  We will return with the rest of the family soon.  Very chill brewery.

We went in here to get some relief from the scorching temperatures outside and get some water. We sat near the back and ordered water. There was a man in the restroom handing out soap and paper towels for tips, which I find extremely annoying but paid him anyway because he's probably struggling. Our waitress came up and asked us if we wanted to order anything, but we told her we were fine. She then came up again and showed us the menu as if we had to purchase something even though the place wasn't nearly full at 11:00am on a Thursday. Finally, so she'd leave us alone, we ordered a Jack & Coke on her third attempt. It was in a 6 oz plastic cup and it cost us $10. This place is kind of a joke if you just want to get water and listen to music. Be ready to spend your money.

Scott Bentley gave me the best haircut I have ever had, in all of my 43 years. He talked to me before he started the cut. He really listened to me and my concerns. He explained what he was going to do and why he would do that, which made me feel so comfortable before he cut my hair. The salon is going the extra mile to be safe during this pandemic. The haircut he gave me is phenomenal. I love it and have been getting so many compliments on it. One of my friends I have known for 22 years told me, "I think this is the best hair style you have ever had!" I am so happy I used Yelp to find the Bentley Salon and that I was fortunate enough to have Scott cut my hair. I am feeling fabulous and very grateful.

Great venue.  Even better when YELP is involved :)  

We arrived as YELP guests at 6:30 pm, got inside at 6:45, a full hour and 15 minutes prior to the general public.  As you walk in the door you are greeted with a Brew Ha Ha  / Sierra Arts logo beer cup then off you go to a variety of represented breweries.  Sock Eye, Drakes, Alibi, Sierra Nevada, High Sierra to name just a few.  No shortage of beer here folks.  Lots of choices, styles and flavors.

First stop, Dust Bowl Brewing.  Theses guys are located in Turlock, California and brew a great variety of beers.  All are excellent.  I have been to the Turlock location a few times so I know this beer to be good.  They did not disappoint.  2nd stop was Alibi Ale Works.  They are from Incline Village, Nevada and are about to open a tasting room in my hometown of Truckee.  Lucky me!!!!   Again, a great variety of good tasting beers.

I do not I believe a missed any of the breweries represented here.  Tasted them all!!

One place of note.....Record Street Brewing.  These are the new kids on the block.  They have not even opened the location yet, which will be on 4th street in downtown Reno across the street from The Depot. The beer was outstanding....be sure to check these guys out when they open later this month.

Revision Brewing....another not so new kid on the block had a table with brochures but no beer or rep.  Of course they haven't even opened the doors at the Sparks location on Rock Blvd.  If you know Knee Deep Brewing in Auburn, CA, then you will love Revision.  Jeremy was a founding partner in Knee Deep before venturing back to Reno to open Revision.....stay tuned!!!

Overall this is a great event benefiting a great cause.  Plenty of beer swag and an abundance of art to look at and purchase.  If you get the chance next year, be sure to attend, this is a well worth it event!!!   Thanks YELP for the great time!!!!

                                                                              Cheers..............................................
Bob, the ribs and chicken were SLAMMIN' this week!  Excellent smoke on the meats and mom's mac n cheese rocked!

Waitress was very nice but inattentive. Long time between getting appetizers, which were pretty good. We always order stuffed mushrooms, will go with the plain calamari next time instead of Molly's version. Entree ordered and took long time to get food. Ordered baked potato and got luke warm mashed taters. Salmon on plank hot and tasty. Chicken (naked) wings medium were okay, nothing special though. I will go back again, it's a nice place dine.

Great restaurant. The decor is rustic industrial! The food was deliciously seasoned. I just wish it would have came out fresh off the stove. My food was a warm not HOT! The staff members were very pleasant and helpful in suggesting some popular dishes. 

The manager came around several times to make sure everything was going well. Great serious!

They no longer have Elijah Craig 12 for the Manhattans, but the cocktails are still great.

They have a new chef, and the food is improved.  The Duck Rillette and Chicken Liver Mousse (chopped chicken liver to us peons) were stars.

I have to totally disagree with the guy who left the latest review. McCrossen's has become my favorite neighborhood spot since moving there a few months ago. The first night we checked it out, my roomate and I sat at the bar and struck up a conversation with the bartender. He was super friendly and even offered us 2 drinks on the house as a welcome to the neighboorhood  gesture. They have a great beer selection on tap and the food is great for a pub. It looked so good that night that we went back the following week to check it out for ourselves. Some favorites are the Sheeps milk ricotta with warm flatbread, the calamari and the wings. The wings are sooo good.  A good mix of typical bar food ( burgers, wings, fries) mixed with a few surprises ( ricotta, pork chop, pasta).  A great place to grab a drink with a friend, catch a game , or even bring the family for a nice dinner. The atmosphere is so warm and cozy.

This place is definitely the Cheers of Fairmount. Always a good mixed crowd in there and the staff is so friendly.
Very nice people and they do a great job! Reasonable prices. Took my husbands dress shirts and they came out great.

I wanted to love it here, truly. I will preface by saying the staff is amazing and the only reason for three stars. We ate on the 5th floor, and other than the hot chicken sandwich, the food was mediocre at best. The pulled pork was somehow dry and greasy at the same time, with no sauce and a few shreds of slaw. It had to be sent back. The fries were the only saving grace for the under-sized burgers. The calamari was extremely greasy, but not in a yummy, fried way, but in a heavily over- sauced way. Quality control needs to be enforced, I doubt the famous owners would be okay with lack of care and finess coming from the kitchen.

This is now hands down one of my favorite places in Tucson. Spectacular service and the best ribeye I had ever tried in my life! Also, I love the atmosphere and lighting of the place, very soothing and relaxing. I also loved that they had holiday drinks! Such a great way to get festive!

I stopped by this small gem in my neighborhood and I was pleasantly surprised! The breakfast sandwich was fresh and flavorful. My oat milk latte was tasty as well! The staff was very friendly! Can not wait to go back!

LOVE the atmosphere here! Love their flights as well. 
Their sours are the best! And I even loved their IPA's (I usually am not the biggest fan of IPA's). 
I have not seen or been on their patio yet, but I will definitely be coming back!

Brent and Chrissy are personable and extremely professional.  They have consistently worked with our schedule to tend to our dog's high energy needs.  I trust them with the care of our cherished pooch and am grateful for the high quality service they provide.  I try not to abuse their good nature as they've been willing to work with me for last minute emergencies.  Their rates are on par with most dog walking services but the personal touch and excellent care sets them apart.  I especially appreciate that they leave detailed notes after every walk.   I can't recommend their service enough.

I usually run in to return packages- which they help pack and tape. They shred papers securely fir $1.50: pound. They also laminated my COVID CARD for me. They're patient and helpful, since I'm usually running in at the last minute.

I have had a very good experience here. I broke my leg a month ago, and have been seen here 3 times as I have dealt with the injury. The medical assistants, cast techs, radiology folks, and Dr Wang have all been helpful and friendly. My only complaint (hence 4 stars) is that wait time can be long - I come for a 2:30 appt, and am not seen by the doctor until 3:15 - but I understand that this tends to be the norm in large, busy practices. I'm sorry that others had bad experiences, but I would recommend Dr Wang and his staff.

Have been wating for a Metro Diner to open on the west side - did not disappoint!  Had chicken and waffles, and a couple of desserts to go.  Food was great - and the service was fantastic!  Will definitely be back!

I am LITERALLY addicted to the chicken lettuce wraps. I will NOT share, so don't even ask. Their edamame is AMAZING so I won't share that either. I just don't understand the delivery of the lettuce wraps. I'm doing homework and I need food. I ordered PF Chang's 2 nights in a row because I needed those lettuce wraps. The first time they forgot my fortune cookies and I was heartbroken. And then how can you have lettuce as half of my FAVORITE dish, but send me broken and torn lettuce, or pieces not even a magician could figure out how to stuff?! TWO nights in a row??? The cold lettuce COMPLETES the dish with that magic blend of chicken and AMAZING ingredients. Lettuce may be a natural ingredient, but it's still half of your dish and should be monitored as such. When I order again this week, I'll expect better lettuce with your magic chicken.

I have gone to this nail salon many times but I will never step foot in this place ever again! I came in with my mother in law to have a few pedicures and at first I was really surprised that they didn't even say more than four words to us and continued talking among themselves in their language which really doesn't bother me. What does bother me is that they were talking about me, and laughing! I am allergic to mosquito bites and my legs are quite scarred. My pedicurist actually pointed at my scars and turned to the pedicurist beside her and said something in her language, he even stopped what he was doing and looked at my scars. She then laughed hysterically! I have never seen anything so rude in my life! I may not understand what you are saying but I can read body language and I'm not fucking stupid!

I'm no fan of large museums, but I have a soft spot for quirky collections--- especially when they are full of masterpieces. 

The Barnes' foundation is just that and is about the right amount of art that I can tolerate without getting overstimulated. 

It's probably the only museum in the world in which the audioguide (or some sort of introduction to the museum itself) is unmissable. It'll tell you things like how Mr. Barnes sought to improve defects in painting by matching them with other smaller paintings that drew attention away from the flaws. 

I hear it's moving. I guess the positive side is that it'll be easier to get there. The downside is that it'll probably become incredibly crowded.

Hate to do this to you guys. But when I call 22 minutes b4 you close and tell me you've stopped all to go orders, this is the type of reviews you will get. Never tasted the food because apparently, they're busy closing early. If I owned this spot I'd be pissed my managers were so lazy.

Great prices and open 24/7. They also have a wine and beer section and a Starbucks. The Starbucks doesn't have a full menu and the staff is about as knowledgeable as my dog, but they have wine so all is forgiven. They have great mix sixes and other cool IPAs.

A true diamond in the rough. Honestly one of the best and extremely affordable. As soon as you enter you are being welcomed at the door. We sit where we like and the waiter is extremely helpful and inviting. This was our first time in here so the waiter explains how the menu works and suggest on a couple of the most popular sushi rolls. Right away we were sold one them. We get our home made sangria and automatically begin to love it. Sushi came right away. We left and  usually when we leave a sushi place we leave like why did we eat so much, not here. Here we left satisfied and happy. Hope this helps because this just became our favorite place.

Crazy Hibachi has been here so long. This used to be a Hometown Buffet.

The building is pretty spacious with lots of tables and booths. The buffet area is located in the middle of the restaurant. The inside doesn't feel clean and is sometimes humid.

I am not a fan of the food here. Not fresh and too oily, even for Chinese buffet standards. Also noticed the buffet selection has shrunk throughout the years. I would avoid the sushi at this place, but that's my general rule for Chinese buffets.

The service was basic. Workers came and refilled drinks and cleaned plates.

There are better options elsewhere for Chinese food in the area.

They really do try to work with you on a transaction it's just that one of the staff members was young, probably new and not knowledgeable on the currency so they had to call the boss a few times so it took a while. The other staff member didn't seem to know as well or just didn't want to help. I'd expect the staff to know more about currency types than me but I guess not this time. Overall they got me sorted out, it just took a while.
I was staying with folks who prefer to have a healthy liquor cabinet sooo we stopped by here. Lots of choices to be had. They got rye and wines. It was interesting to browse the selections.
Rude. Got rotten egg rolls. They hung up on me when I called.

The atmosphere, the food, the portions and the service are all superb at Tufo House!!

My daughter and I really love Korean food and we really admire the culture! We were super happy to find this homey, cozy and an absolute gem! Finally, we don't have to drive all the way to Sparks for decent Korean food!

My Kat really enjoyed the tasty, and richly spiced Topokki. She craves it every week, mm ok more than once a week. My favorite was the firery, spicy, very refreshing and comforting Kimchi Soft Tofu Soup!! That great combination of flavors and textures out of this world! Seriously their food is addiction! 

Gosh! We can wait to be back!

For a place that only sells chicken tenders & fries, Cane's knows their business. Your options are chicken tenders, chicken tenders, or chicken tender sandwich. 

CANE'S SAUCE pays the bills here. This special blend of magic is a staple of the franchise. Hard to explain, but the sauce has a unique kick of spice and welcomes dipping across the board. Being a Cane's sauce fanatic, I always order inside and pump extra cups, avoiding the $0.30/xtra sauce fee. 

Crinkle-cut fries are a great friend of mine, and very respected in the potato community. Arguably better than the chicken, crispy edges with a soft interior give the fries a thorough, smooth bite. Coleslaw is another featured side, which compliments the chicken masterfully. Texas toast bread with every combo--original i guess, compared to a customary biscuit, but never know how to utilize it. Like, do I make a chicken sandwich with this? Just seems extra, given what you already have. 

***With a recent expansion of Raising Cane's across the region, this location is met with open-arms. Again, the sauce alone will bring you back. SIDE NOTE: Grab a drink, the ICE here -- top notch. Pebble-shaped marble style ice makes a nice flurry noise when almost empty; Also allows for a swift straw cut to the bottom***

Mid-day lunch. You order at the counter and pick up when your number is called. I like it here because they have different types of meats. I ordered the lengua tacos. It came with cabbage and salsa. The lengua was in nice chucks and very tender. Very good. My companion had the pollo tacos. She said it was good and flavorful. The 3 tacos with rice, beans, pickled carrots, pinion and limes was filling. Great service and clean.

Safe enough to send your children there, but there are some problems...

Nurses...good and an oops .  Everything was mostly fine until I had to go home and the nurse in charge lost my home care paperwork.  Now, how am I supposed to chase that down when I'm hopped up on drugs to numb the post-surgical pain?  I complained about this and they made excuses about how I seemed to be alert and oriented and that the nurse notes said she reviewed the paperwork.  BS!  It's not like ANYONE sane would have allowed me to drive a car, so I couldn't have been that alert and oriented, right?  Then again, they brought me a popsicle or Jell-O basically whenever I asked for it.  They even looked for Cherry, which is my favorite.  Thanks.  

Accommodations...good.  The food was good - not that I had much of an appetite.  My family really enjoyed the cheap, delicious, and nutritious eats in the cafeteria.  I've eaten there a few times since myself, and it's rather pleasant. However, the seating area could use a deep cleaning, some repairs, and someone to sweep and clear what's left behind by messy folks.

Upgrades...some oops.  The new forced air system is so strong that you practically have to wear a windbreaker and hunker down under the covers to stay warm.  Ear plugs would help drown out the noise produced by all this air whooshing around.  It was seriously a few decibels quieter in the old wing of the hospital.  What gives?  I also ended up in a ridiculously small room for the amount of visitors I had, which was only 3.  I was lucky to have a private room, but more seating would have been nice to have.  If they would just fix a few of these things, it would be a better place.

ER..oops.  When I was a student at UCSB, I went to the emergency room on a Sunday because I had an ear infection and dizziness - like falling down dizzy and having my best friend drive me there and wait with me there dizzy.  My doctor's office was closed and there were no urgent care clinics that I knew of.    I walked out with antibiotics and got better over the next week.  The report I read years later had an aura of condescension to it which I didn't enjoy.  I'm wondering "what the heck?"

Billing...oops.  It took some time to get some issues straightened out between insurance and the hospital.

Patient concierge..good.  We had to go twice and ask for printouts of all of the interactions the hospital had with the insurance so we could stick it back to our insurance.  I mean, we were even asking for the most minute details to track down any notes of calls or messages left with our insurance.  Thankfully, they had everything in the system and our insurance finally caved on the penalty fee.

Word to the wise -call your insurance or have someone in your family call the insurance to let them know what's going on just to make sure everything is covered and sent in by your doc in advance of a surgery.  I don't think my call would have counted, or even anyone in my family.  They want to know doc actual orders.  But they SAID that we ought to have called them.  Whatever.  Insurance is nasty - and I don't mean Obamacare insurance either.

Wanted a blow dry just because.     Oh, and on a Saturday morning.    Left a message through some form of electronic magic late Friday and got a call first thing on Saturday ... No problem!   Beautiful Blue washed my hair with an awesome head massage that removed all stress and Tommy blew it out giving me the volume I needed.     Good conversation during, a warm and welcoming vibe throughout this clean, modern and wonderfully air conditioned salon.    When I'm back in NOLA I'm coming back!

Cool place in the Westin, 27th floor, for drinks and apps. You'll see a lot of "reserved" tables empty. They are for hotel guests which we were but didn't know to reserve. Outside space, great city views.

If you are going to this practice, go to see Marisa, the NP only!  Unfortunately, I was treated horribly by the billing office. I sent a letter addressed to Dr. Hruza. She, nor the office, ever replied.  That says a lot.  I had already been discouraged with the treatment course in addition to the hip-shooting and diagnosing Dr. Fraggle.

Thank you Frist!!!!!!!! 

We just went to see the Fairy tales, Monsters and the Genetic Imagination exhibit this past Monday. I have to say I was thoroughly impressed. The pieces evoked a lot of feelings because fairy tales are very dear to my heart. I felt disturbed, intrigued and inspired at the same time. I also enjoyed the Phillips collection, O'Kieffe is one of my favorite artists and it was awesome to see some of her pieces. 

We are very lucky to have this little museum in Nashville and I hope to continue supporting them! 

PS I finally got a good look at your beautiful wood floors, they're lovely!!

We continue to come back...over and over..trying new dishes and new drinks! It is never a disappointment, for two reasons: the food is delicious and Olivia!! Let's talk about food, the Burnt Ends Mac & Cheese is the bomb! Sorry I didn't take a picture! But we sat at the bar....which is fun & comfortable, that's where Olivia comes into the equation!! 
Olivia is hands down the BEST bartender! She never misses a beat and is super attentive! Highly recommend!!!

If you go to Belle Meade, I would recommend purchasing a guided tour of the mansion. That gives the only verbal information (and access to a tour guide that can answer question). The rest of the plantation is self-guided and you can't see the inside of the mansion without purchasing the guided tour. 

I found the plantation very interesting, but I would have liked to hear a more balanced approach of the history of the plantation from both sides. I noticed that they glossed over the negative history and likely mistreatment of slaves and servants. They mostly focused on the story of the plantation from the owner's family's perspective, ignoring the ugly parts of it's history. (Which I think would be healthy to hear about and talk more openly about).

That being said, I thought the tour was interesting. I really liked our tour guide in the mansion--he had a good sense of humor and was very knowledgeable. However, the guide for the tour after ours sounded like a cranky old woman without a sense of humor. So I imagine the quality of tours vary, based on who you get. 

The wine tour after was included in the price, and the wines were fun to sample. Although it had some drawbacks, overall I was happy with the trip.

The best Cheesesteaks!!!!  As a transplant from South Jersey to NC, I make sure to take the trip up there at least twice a year and always have to get a Gaetano's Cheesesteak.  I even had someone bring me down Cheesesteaks and Gaetano's separated everything for them so it wouldn't go bad.  Even told them the best way to heat everything up and put it together!!!
In addition to being dirty, this place took 30 mins longer than it was supposed to. And they only gave me half the sauce for my wings. Do not go here.

Prob the most extensive Organic grocery in the Tampa Bay area.
Love the selection, the smoothies are all real fruit! The Cafe is gourmet all the way! Try it out. You will become obsrssed, like ME!

First ever yelp review bc this place is that good. 
I truly appreciated the social distancing measures they had in place. Service was great and the food was even better.

Aki has good lunch specials ($11-15) that make this an easy choice during the week. Various roll combos or sushi/sashimi platters also arrive with your choice of miso soup or salad. It's usually not very crowded during lunch either, so the chefs are able to serve you quickly. They also have a full bar, if you're feeling rebellious at lunch (or just want to go to happy hour).

If you are a sushi fiend and can't get enough, get the all-you-can-eat special. $26.95/head allows you to have unlimited apps, sushi, maki, and some special rolls (some have an extra surcharge). A notable exception to this is the sashimi plate, of which only one can be ordered per head. Totally worth it, so make sure you eat it. You have to eat EVERYTHING you order or you will be charged a la carte, so take it slow and only order when you're sure you can finish the dish. Drinks not included.

The food itself is solid, with a good ratio of rice to fillings and lots of fresh fish. I especially love their miso soup, which has a really thick broth and minimal tofu cubes. On the weekends, the sushi chefs have to rush more to serve the crowd, so the technique slips a little (but then again, when you're scarfing down all the sushi you can eat, does a loose roll really matter that much? Not to me!).

Yum!! Grilled pork was smothered in maple syrup. Oxtail pho was devoured by the boy & hubs had chicken wings pan fried in fish sauce.

Spring rolls were superb and had one extra herb that I'm still trying to place.

I don't like writing reviews for less than 4 stars, I want to point out what's good in the world and leave the rest to take care of itself.

The circumstances are a bit special though with our visit to Carnivore. We went there for my wife's birthday, and she's been doing Keto. This place is ideal for keto - with a meat entree you have the choice of a starch, a vegetable or a side salad. No embarrassing having to ask for substitutions. 

We went on prime time Saturday night. It was packed and even though I did online check in we still waited about 20 minutes. NBD. We all ordered ribeye with side salads and also ordered a side butter to melt on the steak.

The choices are Worcestershire chive, blue cheese or garlic Parmesan, and they're all good. This is key because the meat is simply grilled without much hint of spice or even salt. Once again, keto-friendly. 

Now, even though she doesn't like any pink in her meat she ordered it medium, as did the rest of us. I suspect because it was packed, our steaks were undercooked and, thus, she got a medium rare steak with the blue cheese butter on top.

She said it was terrible. Well, her tastebuds are out of whack, I reckon from the ketosis she is going through. My ribeye with the chive butter was pretty good, I thought. 

We also ordered jalapeño poppers to start and they're not bagged, they're handcrafted and tasty even though there was only 3. Of course, that got a few grumpy grumbles too from the birthday girl who was probably wishing she could be face down into a huge platter of carbonara at Cunettos but hey, keto, dear.

Ok, I thought the food was good. They have priced themselves in the area between Texas Roadhouse and Capital Grille so that's the bar to be cleared, and two out of three of us thought they had. 

Or perhaps I was just swayed by the $9 Basil Hayden old fashioned which was outstanding and severely good value. 

I'd go again, just perhaps order it a bit more done than usual.

Btw: I just went over this review and found it substituted 'Leto' for 'keto'. So my wife was doing Leto. Hucking spellcheck.
The sushi was delicious! I ordered the shrimp tempura roll and the spicy tuna roll and both were made well very and tasted great! It only took about 30 minutes from order to delivery.
Happy Hour Review: Awesome Happy Hour specials  ($5 bubbly or sangria, $4 glasses of wine and $3 beers) Our server was awesome. The dipping sauces for the fries are amazing.

This is the spot to go the people are friendly,the magic guy he's really good what he does turning one dollar bills into one hundred dollar bill.he got card game's tricks of magic I give the man a 10+ also you should check out jewelry place they have beautiful hand made jewelry bracelet etc the lady that works there very nice and helpful perfect demeanor and respectful. One the steak  is beautiful inside old cowboys Western style the chairs are cool ass hell never seen a restaurant like this one it amazed me all the pictures they also got John Wayne suit his cowboys movie days in a glass container the customer service is great and with good attitude I ordered the cowboys streaks comes with a big steak and baked potato how ever u want it side comes with cowboys beans and salad and bread on the potatoes they give on the side the chesse and green Chinese Onion and crumbs of bacon and sour cream Soo good the BBQ sauce very good .the gun show was the shit lol really the Mexican guy really funny character and side kick they funny they all put in a great show I would watch over anytime Soo if u from out of state like must see and go they got a I hunted house very scary they say they say they mess with your mind they put in a room bye yourself pinch black they professionals in this type of stuff one was an actor before I just forgot to tell what movie he came in but anyways I'm coming rite back and bring all my family in too check out this spot I give this place a 10+ everyone has a great positive attitude all the store open up cool place to come with your family and kids..thanks pinnacle's peaks

We found ourselves in Metairie and wanted to stop for lunch.  Bobby Hebert's has a generous drink/food menu.  I got the red beans and rice with a house salad.   My husband got the shrimp po boy with fries. 
The food is delicious and generous, and there is great sports memorabilia inside.

Average. I can see how this is a good place for a game. But the stars speak for themselves. Had the mushroom burger and it was ok. Sweet potato fries are not bad. The mango lemonade was the highlight!

We went to have dinner here this past sunday, believing the yelp posting that it was open until 10.30. (That is the only reason why no 5 star, although that mayen't be their fault)           While perusing the menu, we were told the kitchen was closing at 9.45.  No problem. i just hate being THAT guest.  The one who is eating while all the waitstaff shuffle around glancing if you have an empty plate so they can go home or get on with their night.  
    My boyfriend and i each ordered appetizers: some sort of roll - his with chicken, mine vegetarian.  My lord were they good!  And they came with two radically different sauces that even tasted interesting when mixed together.  Since we realized they were closing, we told them to go ahead and bring the entrees.  Already we knew we'd need to-go boxes.  
    Lemme just say this: the staff were so accommodating and patient.  Two men were our servers.  Very sweet dispositioned and not intrusive. A very gracious host sat us and checked in on our experience.  
   D got the Flag of India and liked all the differing flavours.  I got the veggie version of a new item called 67 or some other number sauce.  The next day i was sweating at work and luxuriating in the aroma coming out of my pores. Sounds crazy, but that's how good it was! I want to learn how to cook this sauce and put it on every damn thing but know that i can never replicate their magic.
    Our next plan of attack?  Lunch Buffet- where we can try a whole load of different dishes and not have our eyes bigger than our stomach.
            Namaste!

First off I'd like to start by thanking the team at Kaufman Eye Institute for an amazing experience during my appointments. I had scheduled my appointment to do Lasik eye surgery follow up appointments with Dr. Jonathan Kaufman. 

It was my first time at this location and it was also a very busy day.  I was called back for my appointment 5 mins after checking in. The technicians are wonderful and very sweet! I saw the doctor within 20-30 minutes of my visit.  Dr. Kaufman is one of the best eye doctors I have ever been to! His impeccable knowledge in this field is outstanding. He thoroughly explained everything I needed to know about Lasik and helped me make my decision for the procedure. He is very personable and trustworthy.
The best customer service ever! Brian is fabulous and this is a wonderful Yardley business.

I have always loved this place until today. Our server Chelsea T. was terrible she does not have an ounce of hospitality in her. To Chelsea defense she did not know that my husband and I had just experienced the worst week of our life and just wanted to come an enjoy the food and some alone time at our favorite restaurant. I explain to her that I had a mushroom allergy and She advise us that they did not remove the mushrooms from the appetizers. ((So what do you normally do for customers with an allergy?)) She was rude to my husband when he questioned why his tostones con queso was soupy instead of crispy And nacho like. She forgot to place my husband entrée for  paella. And when all was said and done she praised herself that she had her manager remove $5 from our bill. We did not go to your establishment for $5 off our bill. We went there to have a great experience and eat good food. Neither of those things happened tonight.

Ordered takeout, and they gave us fries that were so undercooked that they fell apart when you tried to pick them up. I couldn't even call the fries soggy, they were literally dripping. They also gave us about two bites of mac and cheese (the already small container was maybe 1/4 of the way full) and messed up one of our items.

Excuse me! How dare you be this good?! The soft serve is perfect. One of the best in the city. The shop itself is small but accommodating. The staff is beyond amazing. We only ordered two sundaes and they presented them on a tray and handed them to us. They even offered to bring them out.

Even more impressive, they have been extremely vocal about covid protections of customers and staff . Something I'm really focused on is how places treat employees. I can tell this is a healthy working environment. This is what I love about Philly businesses. 

Go to East Passyunk and just enjoy a memory on the street in these summer nights.

Been going to Lester's for several years now.  Have been to the Ladue location (near Highway 40), but seem to go to the location at 14810 Clayton Road.  I believe that it used to be a Fitz's Root Beer location before it was converted to Lester's.

We tend to get there about 11:20 AM - the place is large, but seems to never be very full.  It never gets very full - unless there is March Madness going on.  I have never been here during dinner hours; only for lunch.

I think one of their good deals is the hamburger and fries for an additional $1.  Some of their items - especially their larger salads are over priced.  I'm a huge advocate of eating out often, but eating out cheap!

Service is usually pretty good - never great, never awful.

You can open the menu, close your eyes and put your finger on anything on the menu. Chances are it is good. The prices are unbeatable,  food is delicious and rich in flavor,  the staff is very friendly. I am looking forward to warmer weather so I can enjoy the outdoor seating.

Not so good, they were out of everything I truly wanted. I had to ask them what they did have then I chose from their availabile food. I decided on something I'd never tried and it was overcooked...straight up Bo-Bo like the clown...and yeah, not coming back here!
Loved everything about it! Friendly staff, clean, large portions, and good food! The pineapple fried rice was delicious! The takoyaki was great!
This is my go-to coffee shop in the morning. The home made biscotti is a great compliment to the fresh brewed coffee. Breakfast and lunch sandwiches are unique and delicious. Definitely recommend!

Warm interior, and reliably good food. But something is amiss. The service is perfunctory. Dinner service is placed in front of the wrong people; wine glasses are not replaced for a new bottle of white. The server never asks about more water or bread (which is very good).  The woman at reception is sweet but seems new to the restaurant business--like an intern on her first day. We mentioned it was our son's birthday--ignored. 

The food good.  Duck perhaps had too much of the taste and flavor of accompanying bacon side dish.  The pork shoulder was yummy. Salmon--like it is everywhere. First courses--a bit small, but flavorful. 

There are just so many wonderful BYO's in Philadelphia--this one just needs to try a bit harder.

Every time I go to NOLA, I must come here first. Always a regular spot for my wife and I.

Gumbo soup is extraordinary.  The Gator sausage was great.  After visiting 4 times, always ordering something different, I can say that this place is really good.

The service is really good as well, very attentive and if you haven't ever been there, the servers are real good about going over the menu.  A must visit for sure!

We broke out! Our team conquered the 1776 room like champs. The game was challenging for sure, but we had 3 minutes to spare. Loved the theme of the room and how it transported you to a different time and place with the props and attention to detail. 

Our guide, Luke, laid the rules for us and was encouraging as we progressed through our clues. He gave us three free hints that helped us move forward when we were stumped. 

The only bummer to the evening was the "private" dinner beforehand. We were with a large group of 20-30 people eating a catered dinner in their main sitting/dining area. Other groups from other parties were finishing rooms and making lots of noise while we were trying to listen to presentations, and the staff ushered in new groups through our room - making it less private and I'm sure those groups felt odd, too. Which there was a separate entrance and exit so if a group is using the private space, it's exclusive.

Do not order delivery from this place. I put an order and literally waited for 2 hours and it still didn't leave he store. I have ordered take out from here. So annoying. Either hire more delivery drivers to deliver your pizzas or stop delivery if you can't live up to expectations.

Where do you start when everything on your plate is great?  I had the brunch buffet on a weekday.  There is salad which looked fine, but I didn't have any because I wanted to leave all my space for the main course, besides I can make salad.  What I can't make is the best fried chicken in the world, which is what they have at lil Dizzy's cafe.  That said, the smothered chicken was even better.  It's fall off the bone chicken in a brown sauce.  There is green beans which were very tasty and made me feel less guilty about the rest of the food on my plate.  Which brings me to the gumbo.  I haven't had every gumbo in New Orleans, but it's hard to imagine there is any better than you find here.  Man it was great.  Then there is desert, bread pudding.  Don't know what's in it, but man its the best desert I ever had that didn't have chocolate in it.  All this for $16 including a drink?  That's insane.  If I lived in NOLA, I would be here every week.  This is a must restaurant in New Orleans.

After an extended stay at Disneyland we planned to stay a night in SB and my wife found this place.  Our check out time in Anaheim was 10am and knowing we were going to arrive early in SB, we requested an early check in the day prior to our arrival .  The gentleman on the phone said there was no guarantee but that shouldn't be a problem since it was a Tuesday night and they may have vacancies and to call the day of.  Even if our room wasn't ready they could put us in another similar room.  The morning of, we called and asked again and a gal at the front desk said they couldn't promise anything but would do their best.  Called again when we were 15 minutes away around 1pm (normal checking is 3) and the gal said that the cleaners were not back from lunch and the best they could probably do was 2pm.  Then we arrived and spoke in person to her and after explaining our conversation with the gentleman  the day before, and she  abruptly interrupted my wife, saying, "Excuse me, if you let me finish, I'll explain to you how things work around here ......blah, blah, blah", she had lost us with the intro and we didn't hear the rest except for the ending of, "the best I can do for you is 3pm".  I told her that we'd be back at 5pm and to take your time! After checking in and throughout the evening we noticed that many of the rooms were empty so not sure what the power trip and push back was all about.  The reason I didn't give this place a 1 star is because the room was clean and the beds were comfortable.

Awesome!!! I can't say enough good things about Kay and her grooming! I tried preen mobile pet grooming due to connivence and previous bad experiences with "chain" groomers. Our yorkie "hank" has never looked better. We re-booked  right away! His coat is so soft and shiny and looks fabulous.

Stayed in Tucson later than planned. Needed to eat before we headed home to Phoenix.........long story short - the place looked inviting from the street!  No pre-conceived expectations just glad they were open!

LINGUINI WITH TRUFFLE OIL & ROSEMARY - linguini tossed with Roma tomatoes, mushroom, olive oil, rosemary, garlic and a drizzle of truffle oil

These are just words. Words can't describe feelings. I felt GOOD and HAPPY eating this dish along with the plump perfectly cooked shrimp.  Holy cow just writing this review my mouth is starting to water!  Pasta was perfectly cooked. Mushrooms firm. Roma tomatoes were roasted and had greate al dente texture.  It was just a Delightful dining experience.

I regret that I was driving and could not pair it with wine. Next Time!

Food was pretty good. The BBQ nachos were awesome. It was long wait to get food and have no where to sit after. So many people were holding tables and haven't even ordered yet. There should be someone asking people to move if they don't have food. Highly suggest ordering to go!

Went for the NYE party. Food was great, bands were entertaining, the event was well planned and definitely worth the price of the tickets. We booked a table and our server was very attentive making sure we had drinks and that we didn't need anything. A really great time. The portapottys outside for the lounge was really great since it was so crowded and the lines could get long for the bathrooms. Everything was clean despite the crowd. Would have been fun if there was a way it could have been planned to also include a fireworks viewing. Would definitely go back.

Went there this AM for an issue with my phone that I bought there last year. A young man, I believe his name was Vaughn was very polite and helpful and even applied the replacement protective shield on my phone.
He checked my account, and phone that this was a covered repair under the protection plan.
Very helpful staff and has a good variety.

We used to go to Pappas Ranch but haven't in a while. We decided to go tonight because I hadn't had the fusion wings in a while and was looking frward to them. 
The first thing I noticed while waiting for a table was the greasy stink in the air; kinda like cooking onion rings in too hot an oil and forgetting to turn the exhaust fan on. While we sat there with our eyes burning I looked around and watched the staff at work. I am in the restaurant biz so I tend to observe the goings on at other restaurants. The first thing that stuck out was one of the waiters; a highly tattooed guy who went from table to table using very few of his smile muscles.  I watched as he went to three tables whose guests had gotten up and just picked up the credit card slips but didn't do anything to prebus. The busboy was running around quickly doing his best to get tables reset. On a side note, with four hostesses at the door chatting and running their fingers through their hair, why did we have to wait as long as we did for a busboy to reset Mr. Tattoo's tables? We wondered why there were four hostesses. Anyway, we were sat at one of the booths and were promptly greeted by Victoria. She seemed pleasant and gave us the impression that we were in good hands. She took our drink order and delivered them promptly. We weren't ready to order at that point so she went away. About five minutes passed and Mr. tattoo came over and asked if we were ready to order.  I asked if there had been a server change and he said that Victoria was his trainee.  We ordered some appetizers, wings and guacamole. I noticed that the fusion wings were no longer on the menu but I ordered the wings with the Greek fusion BBQ sauce hoping that it was just a word change. So we continued with the order process. My friend ordered the street tacos. Mr tattoo just looked at him and asked "which ones?" as if he was just annoyed at having to prompt for it. We finished the ordering process and discussed the experience up to that point. Then the appetizers arrived. The guac was really good. The wings did not live up to my hopes. They did not resemble those big, delicious, smoked beauties that I remembered so fondly. The entrees arrived and they were pretty tasty. Our server seemed to have a bit of a personality with the table behind us but when he came to our zone he seemed to leave it somewhere else. 
We felt that the experience this evening was memorable only in that it makes us want to forget knowing Pappas Ranch. Between the stench of grease in the air, the disheartening new wing preparation and the "barely give a crap" attitude of our server, Dan, (his name on the check was Dan), we can check Pappas Ranch off the list of go to places.  Oh, the experience didn't end at door but in my laundry room where I had to strip off my clothes that stunk of grease and then dash to the shower to degrease myself. Needless to say I am not returning.
This is the best car wash around. They are a little expensive but they always have a coupon on the website. They clean every nook and cranny even the trunk. I highly recommend it.
Went for dinner, poor menu selections. If you want good Polish food find some place to eat at. I will never go back.  0 stars
Very good food. Big fan of the Nachos all Carbon...steak, chicken and shrimp on a bed of chips with melted white cheese!
This is my boyfriend and I 2nd time here! We love it! Service was great! The Mongolia beef was like no others. it was delicious!

One of my favorite places in Philly! I love Vintage Wine Bar for many reasons. As soon as you walk in, you will be struck by the beauty of the place. It's full of interesting art and a lovely chandelier. Floor to ceiling give a warm, vintage feel to the whole place. You truly forget you are sitting on one of the busiest streets in town. 
The service (Mark) was helpful in making recommendations, friendly and quick! We told him we were from out of town and he quickly made different suggestions of things for us to see and do!
The Happy Hour was superb, offering lots of treats for $7 or under. We tried the pear and brie bites on a crostoni and they were to die for! 
I will be back and highly recommend you do the same!

Such a great experience with this company. I tried two local plumbing services prior to redcap and wish I went with redcap first. They're prompt, reasonably priced, and my technician (Daniel R) was very knowledgeable. 

If you're having a hard time finding a trustworthy plumber, I definitely recommend this place.

This is my favorite Chinese restaurant in terms of flavors, presentation and cost.   The dishes are satisfying, beautifully presented and varied.  Take out in recyclable containers.  Please open a branch downtown!

I really like Sher-e Punjab.  Their dinner is better than the buffet, but the buffet is a great deal!  My only complaint is that the buffet is a little americanized - creamy and mild dishes being the predominant offerings - but what can you expect?  Tucson is predominantly white people without a tongue for spice.  

I've sat in and ordered take-out several times and have always had a pleasant experience.  All of the naan is good, I've had several dishes that I liked and when I've asked for it spicy (8/10), it comes with a good kick.  Once I was adventurous enough to order it as spicy as it came and it cleared my sinuses out.  Score! 

All in all, I would recommend this place and I definitely consider myself a regular.  I tried to branch out and try another place in town and was so disappointed... Sher-e Punjab sets the standard in Tucson!  Just do yourself a favor and get dinner there before trying the buffet.

The Appalachian Pork you DO NOT need a knife. It was so unbelievably tender and juicy. The seasoning had a BBQ twist which was perfect.  I accompanied my dinner with green beans and a side salad. 
The service by Nicole was very attentive and she has a perfect customer service aura about her.

This chain seems to be going downhill fast. They eliminated bussers to save a buck and forced the already-too-busy waitstaff to clear tables which looks awful and your table basically never gets cleared anymore of empty plates or glasses until you ask them to take them. I'm convinced the burger patties are getting smaller too as my burger this time around was more bread and toppings than actual burger. We used to be regulars but will not be coming here as much until Red Robin gets their act together.

Never really stop by Nordstrom's Cafe, but WOW! The food and ambiance is fantastic. The patio has a phenomenal view of state street as well as the shopping center. The customer service here is just as great! Our server Victoria was so sweet and darling, definitely recommend to everyone!

This outlet mall is the prettiest one I have been to, with a fountain, open space and benches u can enjoy relaxing on. The stores are well maintained both inside and out. All the standard outlet mall designers are represented here. There are plenty of deals to be had. A Yelp check in gets you a coupon book in return. The food court could be better. This is a great place to shop going to/from the Jersey shore.

Went on a Monday night and there were not a lot of ppl. Service was still slow. This was my first time at the broad ripple location and I was upset they did not have chihuahua cheese. Margaritas were still great though!

Took a bike ride to the loop and decided I would treat myself to a small portion of the delicious mac-n-cheese here--I could justify the calories after huffing and puffing to get there....

It was just as delicious as normal and I even had a free smoothie on my card so I got the low-fat strawberry with ginseng and that was a nice refreshing  treat to my day.

They have some outdoor seating but I decided to cool off inside with the air conditioning.  

I didn't feel like this location was as clean and shiny as some of the other locations so they need to step it up a little and wipe the tables a little more frequently and run the sweeper.

My wife and I had lunch here this week while entertaining a couple from out of town. And while I've been here many times before, on a day with wonderful weather, the patio dining was truly phenomenal. The staff has always been friendly and welcoming, and every time I've had a meal here, the food has been superb. Tammy and I shared the escargot and the French onion soup as appetizers and we had the soft shell crab for and the blanquette de veau (braised veal medallions in a wild mushroom and white wine cream reduction with baby carrots in a puff pastry shell - sorry little cows!) as main courses.   The food was incredible and the best part was the bill. Since Café Degas is a participating restaurant on the Restaurant.com web site, I was able to purchase a $50.00 off of $100.00 gift certificate at restaurant.com's annual 90% off sale for $5.00. With dessert, and the cost of the gift certificate, our $132.00 bill (inclusive of tax and a 20% tip) was reduced to just $82.50 (just over $20.00 per person)! And while I certainly don't eat out like this every day, it is great to have such a wonderful meal at such a cozy romantic venue for a really competitive price. I think the best compliment I can include here is that our friends from out of town who'd been eating there way through some of the city's most notable eateries for four solid days both stated that their meal at Degas was hands down the best food they'd eaten yet in New Orleans.

This is not my first time dining with Piazza Gavi.  We ordered through ubereats and the delivery took about 60 minutes to get to my door.  We ordered pepperoni pizza, cheese ravioli with a side of meat balls, garden salad, and minestrone soup for about $71 with tip.  You can't go wrong with ordering the pepperoni pizza.  It was our favorite, but this is not the best pie in Tucson for the price.  The meat balls are huge balls covered in red sauce and pair nicely with the cheese ravioli.  For some reason, the garden salad did not come with dressing.  Luckily we had some Italian dressing in the fridge to keep the theme going.  Overall the food was good, but can be pricey for the amount you get.  Pair this meal with some hard kombucha.  Cheers!

The restaurant is currently under construction so unfortunately this has tainted my first experience.  I called to order take out. Was told it would be ready in 30 minutes.  An hour and a half later,  we finally got our food. That said, it was delicious. Surprisingly, a simple spicy salmon roll wws my favorite thing.

I've been here multiple times, and it's sad that they believe they have any right to know anything about my medical status.  It used to be a fun place to go.  However, I'll never be back.  For the record, I am vaccinated but that isn't any of their business.  They are clearly anti science because at this point everyone in America has had access to a vaccine.  It's not my concern about anyone else bc the vaccine protects me.  The vaccines are for personal protection.  If you choose to be foolish and not get vaccinated, you have to live with those potential consequences.  It doesn't concern me.  It's too bad that 5 spot thinks they get to make medical choices for their patrons.  RIP Motown Monday's...I'll never come back.

Very good authentic food. The soup broth was amazing and full of flavor. I will definitely be back tito try the Tom yum soup and pad Thai. Check this place out if you want to be amazed by the flavors and authenticity.

Gorgeous studio, spacious, large bathrooms with showers! Two rooms for classes, including classic 26&2 Bikram style, mat and towel rental. The heat was perfect and the timing and dialogue were excellent. Great place for yoga! Ample parking, too. Easy to find.

Terakawa used to be Ramen Boy and I suppose owners change and along with it came a name change to avoid the bad reputation from Ramen Boy. 

First off, improvements in customer server went up with the new ownership so this is really good. The waiters are prompt and the food arrives quickly, so quickly perhaps that I think it was already pre-made... Like lighting quick. My dish came out in like 2-4 mins after ordering it. 

I had the original Terekawa Ramen, I can't recall the name. It was good. Not spectacular, not bad, just good. Like I wouldn't write home to my mom about it, but I'd probably confirm it's existence if someone brought it up. The Ramen was a little on the salty side for me and in comparison to all the other Ramen joints in Philly, I'd say it's about in the same league. So far, no Ramen in Philly has really stood out compared to other cities, but I believe Philly will reach that point eventually. 

I also tried the Takoyaki, which was 90% breading and 10% squid. I'd say this is consistent with all the other Takoyaki's I've tried in Philly which is disappointing. 

Overall, going to give it a 3, which is much better than the 1 I gave to Ramen Boy back in the days. Things are better here, but not all that much better where I will make a trip out here. But if I'm in the area, sure I'll grab a bite if I am really craving Ramen. The issue is that this is in Chinatown and in Chinatown, there are so many other better restaurants that you can go to.

I have visited this place three times, and my experience has been consistently positive. This place has enough space for big groups and more private space for couples. 

Not surprisingly, their food isn't quite authentic but that's not what they claim to be. They have an extensive menu with a lot of varieties for rolls. (Sadly they don't offer using noodle and bento boxes, which is a minus to me.) Quite honestly, they can use some of their creativity at naming the rolls for varying the flavors. Once my group ordered 5 rolls at a time. They all tasted good, but I had a hard time distinguishing one from another. There are a few options for tacos/burgers/sandwiches, but I haven't tried any. 
-appertizers: my favorite is the fried calamari. Crunchy but not dry. Very flavorful. I had to order that twice. The crab and asiago dip is also good, but better to be shared among a large group.
-specialty rolls: ehhh can't remember which ones I ordered (7 different rolls in total). They are good so my guess is that whichever you pick will be fine. 
-the bowl: I got somen noodle with friend tofu. This is my least favorite. Despite its great presentation, it is very tasteless. I had to add a lot of soy sauce to make it edible. 
- aburi sake: basically salmon seared rare on avocado sauce. This is the one! Very fresh and elegant. I highly recommend this dish. 
-desserts: nama truffle and nama nanner are both good, but I prefer the former.

The Steakhouse of Western Village, Sparks NV is the model for what fine dining should be. I am not going to spend time repeating what the other 5 Star reviews said. To that I will say "Ditto".

Servers are all in black and white jackets with ties and give a very clean professional appearance. The restaurant is very well staffed and everyone makes sure your requests are met.

The giant prawn shrimp cocktail is amazing, soup and salads are perfect and the steak is what it should be.

Since this is an upscaled, fine dining establishment-the prices reflect that-but worth every penny. It is not what I would call bring the family and kids type of restaurant, although everyone is welcomed.

Make sure you make a reservation in advance, as it is usually very busy.

The meal ends with a photo to remember the evening. I HIGHLY Recommend this restaurant!

Will definitely come back to this restaurant in the future! While we were waiting for our table, we sat at the bar for a drink. The bartender was really helpful and offered some cocktail options that haven't made it on the menu yet. The drink didn't have a name but I'm sure they'll know once you say rum, oranges and jerk seasoning! It was sooooo good!
 
We had the short ribs for our entree which was amazing with the pommes frites. The customer service, the food and "no name" drink made it a great choice for our first night in New Orleans!

I will not go again and will discourage others from going. Went today because I had a deal for a day coupon. I think they mis-represented on the coupon. This place is not a restaurant.
