It's a market/deli where you can get reheated food. Nothing tastes great after it's been in a microwave, especially naan. The samosas were soggy. The cheese in my paneer was either soggy or crunchy (microwave?). The portions were small. And as others have referenced, the service was terrible. I could barely get their attention to pay.

This is a somewhat pricey activity but worth getting to ride down the Mississippi river in a real steamboat(it's like Disney Land but with no track or fake Indians).  The captain narrates the entire journey filling you in on the historical sites you pass, and there are two full bars on board.  We didn't bother with the food, as you pay almost twice as much for the meal and we were glad that we didn't as it looked pretty mediocre.

I have been having my hair cut, colored and styled at Salon LaRue for over two years with consistently excellent results. The quality of work is superb, the consultation to assure that Rudy and I are "on the same page" is excellent. The Salon is professional, organized, always running in time. I have and would recommend Salon LaRue to anyone.

There are only two redeeming features: the fries and the $5 shot and beer combo. Imperial now offers both- so we would rather go there and not deal with the jerks that work at Our Bar. The front door bouncer has the worst attitude and obviously isn't trained for his job because he can't read the birth dates on ID cards. He let's everyone with a fake into the bar- he probably thinks it's cool to be from Florida or New York-- but if you're from Nevada and actually 21 you don't make the cut.  My advice is to experience real Reno nightlife, which doesn't include the yuppie scum that works at Our Bar
.

I took my wife here for our 3rd anniversary. We've walked past many times but never knew what the inside looked like. Very classy and elegant. The server was just awesome and it shows he really loves what he does. Very thorough in telling us what he recommends us to try. I like that. I had the rack of lamb and it was the best tasting dish I've ever had. Dessert was amazing. Overall great experience. Would recommend anyone trying it for sure!

Upon getting locked out of our house after a 3 mile run, my girlfriend and I knew that our landlord had a key on file at our local Home Depot. We swiftly ran over to Home Depot 20 minutes before closing time. After several technical difficulties while setting up the KeyHero app, the staff (Lauryn, Anna, and Robert) at the key making department were extremely patient and helpful getting through this stressful night. After finally getting the keys printed, Josh in customer service, was extremely generous in letting us take the keys for free because we had locked our wallets in the house. We are very lucky to have such an amazing Home Depot close to our home. Excellent service, would recommend!

I can't believe I haven't been here sooner!  Healthy paninis, salads, smoothies and coffee bar... yummm.  My smoked salmon panini on rye was delicious and filling.  And so many other delicious-sounding options with moderate calorie counts.

I also had a coffee with sugar-free peppermint syrup - didn't realize what I've been missing here!  You know hot chocolate with peppermint schnapps?  Obviously this wasn't as good since it's lacking chocolate and alcohol.  But the taste was comparable and wonderful, and won't pack on pounds or give you a DUI.

My solo dining experience was a little awkward, but that was my fault.  Apparently the options are to-go or full service (not fast food counter style).  Not realizing this I inadvertently sat down at the nearest table to the counter to wait for my food, which then became my private dining table... awkwardly right next to everyone ordering and me basically facing the wall.  Oh well - still great!

I've taken my car here for both oil changes and inspections: they are awesome! If there is an issue with my car, even if it is out of their wheelhouse, they will explain it to me and tell me where to go to fix it. There are ALWAYS coupons available online. 

They do take longer than they should to complete the work, but given their quality of work and their prices, it is worth the extra wait time. I recommend dropping your car off earlier in the day and picking it up around dinnertime.

I was a pretty frequent guest at Kelliann's before I burned some bridges, and then well Kelliann's burned down...no connection... But this spot is pretty solid as long as the crowd isn't whack, that said on a good night you could pretty much dictate the vibe, which is always a good thing.  The owners a drunk too, so talk to him and he'll throw you a couple of drinks.

Warning: watch out for egocentric bartenders.
I have gone here a few times for some celebratory drinks, nice back patio, nice staff, friendly atmosphere, I like.

The food is awesome! The service is great. The whole atmosphere is welcoming. The staff greet you with warning smiles and hospitality. The must try is the fruity pebblets waffle. They allowed me to personalize it as a chicken and waffle plate and I must say it was the best tasting thing I have ever had. My husband had the waffled French toast and can't stop raving about it! It's a new establishment and a MUST TRY! Also the vanilla latte was from the gods. The hands of the kitchen staff are truly blessed!

Would never use this place for anything other than strings and picks. Poor selection and only cheap brands. Unfriendly, uncaring staff. Driven way out of my way multiple times after calling to confirm that they had certain items only to show up and they say they've never had them. Overall bad experience every time I go.

More of a big box store aimed at selling to beginners.

I've been bringing my children here for years. I'm very satisfied with the professional but laid back atmosphere of the office and staff. They are very friendly and accommodating. I would defiantly recommend this office for your children if you looking for a dentist.

Favorite date night spot! This concept was genius and well-done! The theater is nice and the staff is always friendly. The best part is the food is actually really good! I had low expectations since it is a movie theater but I've loved everything and I've tried almost everything. The craft beer selection is great and a selling point for my boyfriend. This is the only movie theater we attend anymore. 

I love that they show older movies again as well!

My only complaint is that they do not have every movie and the ones they do have only show for a very limited time.

Another place with bad service.  I am real big on customer service and I did not get it initially.  The food was not as hot as we thought it should have been.  I am sure they will do just fine here, but not with my money!

Every year I go to Dickman's to get Lobster for V-Day. I usually go to the one on Oracle. This year I went to the one on Broadway. Boy did they drop the ball. 4 Lobster for $88.00. One was rotten. What a disappointment, to me and my guest. Called the next day and they are refunding me my money. So I am happy again.

The best burgers around!! Great employees as well. I noticed there was a customer snapping pics with a Yelp Expert Reviewer shirt on and a Yelp hat on it was Steve and he happened to leave a nasty review  he was just mad everyone laughed at him and some little boy squirted mustard on him and he was incoherently asking for his Yelp elite discount he was asked to leave!!

2nd Story is walking distance to a lot of great sights in Philadelphia, and it is everything I am looking for in a brewpub: great beer brewed on premises, a cool and interesting atmosphere, and a hearty food menu. My girlfriend and I both really liked our beers, the wit foe her and I opted for an American IPA. 

Though we only stopped in for a quick pint, we almost changed our dinner plans to eat here instead after we saw the generous portions, like the fish and chips, that the kitchen was serving up. We will have to come back with fierce appetites next time. 

The atmosphere is classy yet comfortably casual. I think I would describe it as rugged wood construction throughout the dining room and bar. I like the windows above that give a view of the brewing operations upstairs. I will definitely return.

We had a lot of fun at Recess over the weekend. I found the staff to be friendly and prices to be reasonable, although I will say they seem to have a light pour, so beer is probably the best value.

I enjoyed all the games and all the space to move around. I agree with the sentiments others have left in that a bigger variety of games would be nice. There are SO many air bowling setups and I think that space could be used for something else. I imagine / hope they'll continue to expand their game selection as they continue to grow. Either way, I plan to come back!

If I could, I would give a negative -1,000,000 rating.Please never ever go to Ashley.  Summary, I bought a sectional couch on 3-22-16 and it is 7-26-16.  I don't have a couch or my $1500 refund.  (plymouth meeting, pa) Ashely advertises quick delivery, its been 4 months!  Gary promised I would have the couch in 7-10 days.  I called after 2 weeks (was placed on hold for 10-42 minutes each time I called any department) they said it was there and they had forgotten to call me. I scheduled delivery-they didn't have openings for 10 days, then only had one or two options.  This was dishonest-delivery is not 7 days.  I stayed home all day on a Saturday.  I am self employed and am paid per client hour.  I did not work and I missed my son's soccer game.  I had a morning window for delivery.  I sat home all day.  They do not use Ashley delivery people.  The delivery guys explained they rent a truck on Saturdays and deliver furniture for Ashley.  The guys did not come until 8pm!  The truck they rented broke down twice.  A day later, I realized the couch was torn. I called a distribution number (22 minutes on hold.)  I was calm (not realizing the horror to come).  The woman was nasty, accused me of tearing the couch, asked why I had signed if torn, said their items are not torn, etc.  She actually hung up on me.  She told me to email photos to a 'furniture doctor' email.  She did not speak clearly and was frustrated bc i asked her to repeat email.  She said I would have a reply in 1/2 hour.  I cancelled a client.  Again, reducing my income.  Well,1/2 hour turned into 2 weeks!  I spoke to a human after 31 minutes on hold after being disconnected  as well. I was given different numbers and passed around.They said they had ordered me another piece, but no one told me.  I thought they would repair this piece.  I waited another couple weeks for the new section. I called them after not hearing from anyone.  Again, they said the couch was there, but had not been scheduled for deliery.  John,the manager of Plymouth Meeting store, promised delivery that Saturday at 8am.  I then talked to Mary Rose at corporate.  When I talked about frustration of the long phone wait times, Mary Rose laughed and said she waits on hold.  She was rude and asked why I was calling her when the situation had been resolved.  It was resolved, bc John had scheduled a Saturday delivery.  At this point, weeks have passed.  No surprise, I took off work and they no showed.I called the delivery center and was told I was not even in the computer or on the schedule.   I was unable to get in touch with John or Mary Rose.  I finally got in touch with John Tuesday.  He apologized for not returning my calls. He shockingly admitted that he had "lied" to me.  He acknowledged there is no such thing as an 8am delivery. Deliveries are later in the morning and you get call the night before with a four hour window.  Other companies allow you to pick an AM or PMwindow.  With Ashley, they tell you the night before if you are AM or PM.Now,I asked for the couch to be returned.  John gave me a hard time about this and questioned my ability to get  a refund. I called VISA and they  started a fraud investigation for an item over $1000.  John promised all 3 new pieces and I again scheduled another window for delivery.  Two nice men showed up (they rent a truck/deliver) and replaced damaged couch with new. Three days later, I tried to put the couch together,they were the wrong pieces, the couches did not connect!  I was placed on hold over and over at the offices.  Mary Rose at corporate spoke to me again to no avail. John dodged my calls.  We then tried a Saturday delivery to simply bring the new piece.  After they no showed again, I drove to Raymour Flannigan and bought a couch in 20 minutes.  It was in my house in 3 days. I begged John to pick up the couch and refund my money.  I scheduled for the couch to be picked up on a Wednesday. I took off work-they never came. over 3 months have passed. The lady at store said I was moved to thursday.  Even tho I was on wednesday.  John then said, he did not know why i was moved.  again, no one told me--but regardless-i had changed my whole work schedule for wednesday and could not simply take off thursday too.  We then went with a sunday pick up.  Well-that Thursday, i had a work cancellation-and came home. Ashley contractors showed up!  These poor guys were never told my pick up was moved to sunday.  However, I was home and the nice men picked up the couch. The couch is gone after 3 months! I tipped delivery men three times for this couch.  I had given my old couches away and had no where to sit for a while.  when i got raymour couch, I had to pay them to move the ashley couch to other room. so tips 4 times.  one time, the guys scratched my wall. so I tipped 4 times, I have a scratched wall, no couch and they owe me $1500. It has been an absolute disaster-like some sort of humorous cocktail story---Please never ever go to ashley!!

This place was wonderful!  Atmosphere was very pleasant. The service was great! .  Almost too good. :) But maybe it was because it was a Monday night and they were slow.  The buffet was a meal in itself.  Will definitely come back!
We tried unlimited Korean BBQ for $20. Unlimited sides are really good. Of course, the BBQ was the best part of our meal. We'll come back.

Great place, wonderful atmosphere ... The bartenders/mixologists are very helpful and generous !  The food is equally amazing !! The crab cakes are amazeeeee !!! The steak is perfection !!! Oh and the shrimp and grits are pure Perfection !!! Great gem for CWE !

Paid almost $900 for THREE nights only and haven't slept due to the motorcycles and other loud cars outside. Insane to me that you pay so much a night and the windows aren't even sound proof. 

Everything in the sundries is expensive. 

The rooms aren't even that clean... they basically made our bed and left all the trash which had left over food in it. 

On the plus side, rooms are huge, love the bedroom having a door. Love the location, loved the free breakfast.

Do yourself a favor and try the Crawfish Corn Bisque. You will NOT be disappointed. The orange juice is freshly squeezed and it was amazing. DH recommend the Down the Bayou omelette. It was flavorful and absolutely delish!!! Service was a little slow, but we were in no rush... and everyone was very friendly!!!

Pros:  Super great location and access to the beach, walking distance to the piers and State Street.  We also got early check in and the staff was efficient and friendly. 

Cons:  No cookie upon check in and I was too embarrassed to ask for something so petty.  We stayed in the Bogendelia wing with an awesome ocean view.  The room was very outdated and the tub looked like it was patched with bondo.  The matteress seemed like it was outdated too.  

Over all, we had a good time, but I think we could have gotten better money's worth somewhere else.

Best froyo in Reno, hands down.
The staff here rocks.  They're always super helpful, and very friendly.  They have such an amazing assortment of toppings and yogurt flavors-- which is probably why my yogurt always ends up being over $5, ha ha.

Geez.  There are too many reviewers on this site that are far too easy to please. I now take restaurant  reviews written on yelp with the proverbial grain of salt.  This particular eatery is just plain ordinary at best. Totally unappealing and unprofessional in appearance/atmosphere, this doesn't even have the ambience of a fast food place. A t-shirt with holes and a pair of dilapidated cut-offs with cheap sandals would match the decor and food.  Two beers, one appetizer, and two meals--all mediocre--cost $55!!! That for greasy food served on plastic plates by waitresses that must have learned their craft at Mel's Diner. They couldn't have cared less about any part of their job (ala, "Kiss my grits"). When the salads arrived, I knew this would be an awful experience: anytime it comes in a small black plastic bowl with iceberg and the blandest of ingredients, you can be assured this joint doesn't care about the food it serves. Sure enough, the fried oysters and fried calamara tasted exactly the same--of breadcrumbs and oil. "Where's the Seafood?" The portions were very small considering their high cost.  I wanted to order the baked potato as a side but was informed it wasn't available until 4 PM. My request came at 3:30. Upon leaving, another diner was happily plunging into one. I felt further disrespected--they should have offered me one to go if they really weren't ready at 3:45.....

Until diners insist upon better we will be stuck with glorified crap like this. I'm never going back and urge you to avoid it completely. A total waste of $55.  Remember, I'm the one you should listen to.

We reached out to Southern Funeral Care to hold the service of my mother. While there was a bit of a scheduling mix up in the beginning. The staff made sure to make it right. The set up is quite large with a lot of seating. They provide tables and podium for photos and flowers. They have a dvd video system to play a memorial video if you have one made. We did and it was lovely. There was plenty of parking as well. We are thankful for them and helping us say goodbye to such a beautiful soul.

Stay Away. My first impression was when i pulled up to the valet. No help getting luggage out nor greeted at car door. The guy just stood there and watched and I was traveling solo so it's not like I was letting other guest out and leaving luggage with them to go self park the car. Cool. I'm a able bodied young man so It's not like i was incapable of retrieving luggage from my car but still when you're in the customer service business there needs to be some modicum of CUSTOMER SERVICE. Walk up to the front desk. I'm standing right there in from of the person checking in customers. have to wait for him to finish his conversation with another co work before he greeted me...strike two.

NOISE: As previously stated in another review YOU CAN HEAR EVERYTHING from within your room: doors slamming and people going in and out. Strike three. 

The final straw that broke the camels back to write this review is that as I was returning from Mandeville for a wedding, I went to my middle compartment (where I last left my cable to charge my phone) my charging cable was gone. I'm not implying valet stole it but last i checked cables don't come with legs. Asked the guy who valeted my car upon arrival and of course he hadn't a clue. Asked the guy at the front desk and he said that the manager of valet was not in today. It was a fruitless effort. It ends with arriving to my room at 3:15 after a full day of being gone and it still had not been turned over. 

Do yourself a favor and spend your money at another more reputable establishment. 

*rooms are small, satellite cable is shoddy, wifi is ok.
They've truly thought of everything! The 2nd phase has been completed! This space is huge and beautiful! The food selections are unlimited and everything is well spaced out! It's a beautiful maze
This place is a perfect spot to chill out and do some work or just have fun with your friends. Their choices are yummy and not to mention quite beautiful. The decor is also stunning and fun.

Great spot for a killer burger and a nice brew on a cold night with your favorite magazine (at least, that's what I did). The bar was filled with some locals in the 40/50/60 age range, all relaxed and classy people who clearly make some good money (but don't flaunt it). The bartender, Danny, was super friendly (but not pushy), and guided me (correctly) to enjoying the house ketchup. This place knows its vibe, and that vibe is 1920s Atlantic City. Live jazz was starting as I left. Can't wait to return. Wish they had a gluten-free bun so I could bring my girlfriend.

The food here was good, my biggest issue was the seating.  They had a booth bench at one of the tables that was entirely too low which made eating awkward.  It seems like they try to pack way too many people around each table and it results in a lack of elbow room.

That aside, the noodles and fried rice were good, as well as the chicken and steak.

Terrible food, terrible service and management for such a great location. Who takes 30 minutes to re make soft poached eggs for benney? And then asking if we would like an over easy egg instead as it cooks faster.... They couldn't even get my over easy egg cooked correctly for my meal. What manager doesn't come out to speak to the customers regarding the issues we had with our food? Why asked how we like our eggs if you don't cook it the way we want as we are paying customers! It wasn't just our table that was having issues with the food, service and the management. Good to know as both our tables discussed how the place was not busy, food took forever to come out and manager avoiding customers. Both tables agreed we will not come back, let our friends and family know so that they won't waste their money and time.
Food was great! I had the steak street tacos with rice and refined beans. I also ordered flan. Omg! It was amazing. Sorry, I didn't take a picture of food. I would definitely recommend.

Dealing with Defender Security has been an absolute nightmare.  My Pulse arming/ disarming feature has stopped working, but the camera feature works just fine.  I've had two (one a lead) technicians come to the house to take a look at it- and both techs said that the issue is not something they have the capability to fix.  Both the techs, and other service members they called while in my house, agreed that this is a one-off issue that they have no resolve for.  

I've been calling, and calling, and calling, Defenders since June trying to get this issue resolved.  Since I've been paying extra for a feature that is not fully functioning, I asked Defenders to provide me with an outdoor camera to make up for the payments toward Pulse I've been making- since the camera feature works just fine on my Pulse, this would make up for the non-functioning arm/disarm feature.

The manager I spoke to on the phone was less than helpful, giving me the run around, trying to troubleshoot (even though I suggested she look back into the records to see the millions of times I've already tried troubleshooting).  Then, tells me that their Defenders provider in the area "temporarily does not have outdoor cameras in stock".  Okay great, so my next question was, "Well, can they order one from another branch"- and the answer was "no, they cannot, they don't have outdoor camera service yet".  Okay so, wrong use of the word "temporarily" if they've never had the capability to begin with.

Defenders has been taking my money for a service that they are not providing due to whatever the communication error is between my Pulse app and panel.  They are also offering no real solutions to the problem.  The manager (still not understanding I've been dealing with this issue for MONTHS) said they'll send another tech out.  Great, another tech who will most likely not be able to help (since the other two couldn't do anything), and I'm right back at square one.  

I would never, EVER, recommend Defenders to anybody who cares about their money being put to services that actually work.  Talking to the Defenders staff has left me with many questions about how they were hired in the first place (sounding confused, saying they're transferring me and hanging up, offering no real solutions to a persistent problem).  ADT should be ashamed of themselves for contracting with this company, it makes the ADT brand look absolutely terrible and has me looking to transfer to a different home security company.

The kitchen needs an update... My chicken sandwich was bland and the roll was stale.  I couldn't finish it..

The service and decor is nice... But the food is not up to par with typical irish pub fare in the city.  If you are a tourist only come here for drinks not food.

So great to find a late night spot that serves food (and drinks)! Nice little patio area with fans. The food was super delicious! My husband and I got the spinach dip (the best!) and a roast beef poboy with the freshest tomatoes and most savory gravy! Not to mention the Pear 75. Definitely pleasantly surprised and will be a new "regular."

I'm not one to visit cemeteries often, however, I did willingly visit this one. My visit wasn't to attend a service or to visit a family member's grave. I visited to admire the history and the beauty. The grounds are clean and clear. You can walk through and read names and dates and some of the tombs are absolutely breathtaking. I felt like I was walking within an outdoor museum. It was eerie or uncomfortable. Instead, there was a steady peace amongst us. Definitely a must-see if you're in NOLA!
We had a great experience!  Be sure to ask for Paul Wheeler!  He is extremely helpful and best of all, honest!

You know you've got a great vegetarian/vegan restaurant when your customers have no idea they're not eating meat. I came to Yelp to rave about the super friendly service and yummy Al Pastor and surprise! Great job, Guys. Delicious and we really appreciated the warm welcome and hospitality.

I was very disappointed with Dr. Quigless's practice. I actually saw one of his associates rather than Dr. Quigless himself so these are comments are broader in nature. I scheduled a cleaning but instead got a new patient screening. This is understandable, but not what I had specifically asked for. I floss a couple times a week, brush regularly, and use mouth wash. I have never had a cavity. I was immediately told I had several cavities and needed a deep cleaning. I was then referred to an individual in his office to be scheduled for these procedures which cost several thousand dollars. I just felt like they were trying to bilk money out of me rather than see me as an actual living and breathing patient.  

I have since scheduled an appointment with another doctor. My insurance only allows me to get the comprehensive x-rays once every three years. I called repeatedly to get the x-rays sent to my new dentist. Nothing happened. Finally they agree to send my x-rays to the new office. They sent paper copies. I am not even kidding. All of their x-rays are kept digitally but they refused to send them. I am not actually sure it is legal for them to deny me access to my own health records like that. Overall I just have a very bad feeling from the get go. I would avoid this practice.
The best coffee in town. French press or drip, it handily defeats the competition. Well balanced, never burned.

Do not use this car wash. Horrible!!!! They ruined the finish on my car. I had it washed and a clear coat late in the afternoon. I got home and realized that they had not gotten some sap off and instead there was now a gooey finish on my car. I went back in the next morning and they would do absolutely nothing about it. They said I had to bring it in the same day. I asked where that had been posted? They said when I went through the sprayer it was posted. But couldn't see it plus I was expecting them to do a good job. They would not even discount another car wash. Do not use this car.

Later....I also submitted a complaint to Crew Carwash Customer Service and got a quick response. They told me to go back in and give them my name and I would get a free Ultimate car wash. I did that and there was a different Asst. Manager on duty who was very kind and I got another car wash. This time, they took double the amount of time, and used a brush, and all of the goo came off. I did not get the clear coat again. What a contrast in service!

We dine at Donatello rather frequently and it is my favorite restaurant in Tampa.  I love the authentic cuisine and it's fine dining atmosphere.  You can enjoy fine wines and impeccable black tie service.  It's not the place for someone who is only accustomed to a lower price point "Carraba's atmosphere."  The price parallels the quality and service you receive.  Donatello is a family owned restaurant with 30 years of business and you will always have the opportunity to meet the owners who greet you and seat you.  I don't think there's any other restaurant in Tampa with that old school Italian charm that you will receive at Donatello.  It is a higher price point but you get the fine quality you can't get at the local "Italian-ish" chain restaurants.   This isn't Olive Garden and it's not the Macaroni Grill.  This isn't the everyday pasta mill restaurant.  Donatello is the real deal. The Osso Bucco is amazing, the grilled lamb chops are always perfect, the Barolo is always served at the perfect temperature and the Napoleon is absolutely to die for!

This recently renovated Target is a convenient place to stop for a quick shopping trip.  I love how clean it is inside and how many staff members there appear to be everywhere.  I always find what I need when visiting this Target.  

The prices are reasonable, the staff friendly and the location very convenient.

After being a long-time fan of the Lucky Dill, today's experience, after several other disappointing ones over the last few years, has driven me to review here. Our server took so long another server had to step in to greet us, take drink orders, and bring a complimentary cup of soup, which turned out to be the best part of our meal. The bottom half of the bun on my son's chicken sandwich was actually wet (like from water, not the chicken), I received a different (half) sandwich than what I ordered, and the size of the apple streudel dessert was an insult. About 75% of the COVID-limited menu is $14 or more now. To make a long story short: one half Cuban sandwich, one Metro chicken sandwich, a chicken parm dinner, and a chicken rustica dinner, two soft drinks, and two waters was over $67 before the tip. So sad to have seen the inside of a (former) Palm Harbor favorite for the last time. We're done. Should've gone to Chipotle across the street for 1/2 the price, or Glory Days about 1/2 mile north.

I was a little apprehensive about getting a prenatal massage in COVID times but my husband booked me a massage and it was fantastic. Thank You Mama did a great job making me feel safe! Everyone was in a mask and covid precautions were taken. I was able to comfortably wear a mask my entire massage. Belinda did an amazing job and was kind and professional.
The charbroiled oysters are the absolute best in the city. The shuckee duckee is my favorite entree. However, any fish on the menu is outstanding.

Hands down, my favorite place to get a poboy, in the city that founded the poboy. Interestingly, it is named Parkway Bakery because it was indeed just that back in the day: a French bread bakery. My good friend used to walk there as a kid and pick up a loaf of bread and a sandwich almost every day in the summertime in New Orleans. Oh, the history!

Great atmosphere here. Great place to watch the game, hang out and have a drink with friends, bring guests from out of town, pick up a sandwich for a picnic - you name it, this is the place!

I tend to avoid meat, though I do lapse occasionally. Therefore, I know this is the best Reuben sandwich in the city. By a wide, wide margin. Sometimes when I'm on my no meat kick, I'll get a Reuben without the meat, which is basically a grilled cheese on rye with sauerkraut. Even this is amazing. They just have the best ingredients you can possibly assemble, I guess.

The oyster-shrimp combination poboy is fantastic. Again, best in the city. I also hear that the BBQ beef is amazing, as well as the roast beef. Brent, the hubby, always gets a hot turkey sandwich. He adores it.

Tip for the uninitiated: you can order your food at the bar. Even if there's a line, and you don't have a seat, you can order from a bartender. It's much faster than the line! 

The beer is always icy cold. The only thing I can say that's not terrific is the dessert. Save your calories, and have another beer!

Many times I have used this company to move persons in our family since 2010. In the past I highly recommended this company BUT after our recent move May 2021 - I can no longer recommend this company.
May 2021 - First phone call with Debbie in ABC office - she started to argue with me when I was listing items I needed help moving. I got past this bc maybe she was having a bad day. Second phone call - 24 hr b/4 move day - Debbie tells me that I am not on the schedule. I got past this bc she continued to check and found us on the schedule.  2 days after the move, we realize we are missing a metal step ladder - We call ABC to ask if the ladder was delivered to wrong place or they have it not know who it belongs to.  They said they would ask around and call me back. No return call. 2 weeks later we call back and speak with Megan, Office Manager, she said it was our responsibility to check to make sure we have everything and they cannot help find the ladder nor pay to replace it. "My hands are tied - I can't help you".  I asked for the name of the owner and she would not give me his name (I have spoke with owner in the past but wanted to see if there has been a change in ownership bc something has changed in the way they treat their customers.)  I told her I was going to make a complaint with BBB and she laughed at me and said "Ok - make a complaint over a step ladder...really?". I am disappointed in writing this review. I always felt better about moving knowing I could call ABC but those days are over. Something has changed at this office so please find ask around and find another moving company. That is what I will do.
Mary M.
Eureka, MO

Fine service, the bar staff is fantastic. Drinks are always between correctly mixed to great. Wait staff is also above average.

The food is relatively average. It's never bad but nothing to write home about.

Do not miss mac&cheese.
Great place.  Always clean.  Nice free samples of donuts sometimes.  They also serve LeBus Multigrain bread which is my favorite!

Food is great, but nearly an hour wait for saturday brunch? They have a ridiculous table policy. No flexibility!! If there are 2 two person tables next to each other, then TURN IT INTO A TABLE OF FOUR!! Instead, they just keep seating tables of 2.

Two small plates filled me up! Surprisingly good.  Had the salmon which was combined with interesting attendents. Including being on a potato chip. Mmmmm. Tasted my dinner mate's spedini and it was tender and perfectly seasoned.

New favorite diner in the area! It's brand new and super nice inside. Say goodbye to the grungy diners that smell like cigarette smoke and haven't been renovated since 1960. I mean this place was sparkling clean. Now onto the food, the pancakes were so fluffy and delicious! We also had fries which were awesome well done. Our waitress was the sweetest lady ever and made sure we had everything we needed at all times. We actually tipped her 60% because she was so sweet. I honestly have no idea what people below are talking about as far as service goes since I had such a great experience. Maybe it's because we came during the late night shift.

3.5 stars. Bleepin Yelp.

I went here last night extremely hungry after working late. First they gave me chips with TWO bowls of salsa. So that was nice and a change from most of the other places here that charge you. The chips were allright, pretty much equivalent to those I've had at Qdoba with some lime flavor crystals/salt. The red salsa was very fresh and had some good flavor to it, though it was on the mild side. The green salsa was very sweet, maybe a little too much cilantro and/or lime. 

I ordered a Super torta, which has carnitas, chorizo, cheese, refried beans on a bun. When they say super, they aren't kidding. It was humongous. And I"m not talking about the Beyond Thunderdome guy it was that big. The meat and cheese and refried beans were so so good. And they perfectly toasted the torta buns. Total heaven.

My service was decent. My server did pay attention to me but she wasn't hovering over me at all. I think she could have come around a bit more, but at the same time I was the only guy indoors at that time of night.  And she did refill my water glass while I was visiting the Men's room.

Its not real mexican, but whatever it is it was tasty overall.

Oh yeah, if you are female, do not wear high heels and drink in this place. The bathrooms are located in the basement so you have to traverse a series of wooden stairs. I thought the drunk woman behind me was going to fall on her ass. Thankfully she made it.

I'll be back.

Oh yeah the name of this place had me singing the lyrics to that A Tribe Called Quest song "I left my wallet in El Segundo". Evil, evil, evil.

First day of school and my wife and I signed up our kids 12 years girl and 5 years boy for after care with PROtential Sports they suppose to pick them up after school at 3:15pm and  take them to the Club house in Rivercrest community (Riverview FL) where we live and have sports and homework done. My daughter called me at 4:00pm to tell me they was not pick up by ANYBODY what irresponsible people and then I tried to call them to be inform what's going on because I was working not response at all I pick up my kids from school (leaving my work early), after that I tried to call again with any response. Finally after 1 one later the Lady with name Nyree who is one of the owners called me back saying she doesn't know what happen "are you kidding me" who runs business like that, my kids was unattended alone in their school and those clowns didn't not what happen.
Hopefully this help to the people know more about "PROtential Sport" and does not sign with them your kids will be in a very high risk place with the wrong people.
Now I understand one of the paragraph that was bothering me a lot but I was not believing in that moment and says this: " I understand that PROtential Sport is NOT my child care provider and that my child is only participating in the sport clinics offer. I understand PROtential sport has an open door policy for the children and/ or parents and agree that my child can be picked up or can be free to leave at any time during or after the session or chosen clinic." So who the hell is NOT responsible for a 5 years old child?. Any way I'm glad this happen in the first day so I can't imagine what will be happen in that place. 
And for those people (PROtential Sport) go the HELL for not take care properly about children.

We had another venue cancel on us last minute for a party of15. This location opened up their back room for us and rocked the house on service, taste and presentation. Our guests were wowed by the food and spirits. Tyson was a fantastic server and kept everybody happy. A great evening!

Very sad place of business, manager is very rude, very Combative, and will refuse service to you in a heartbeat.   I would not recommend anyone to shop here, my family and I felt like second class citizens at this shop.   I would take my money else where.    They should put a sign up you are not welcome here so others don't have to be treated the way we were treated.   I would put no stars if I was could.   

Please avoid this place!!!!!!!

One of Philly's great BYOBs.  Reasonably priced and excellent service!  Our waiter was very friendly and helpful in making suggestions.  The pasta is made fresh -- enjoy this authentic Italian restaurant.

Give it up for THE best sushi spot in Broad Ripple! The once-violin shop has grown into a thriving sushi bar on the village strip.

- Minimal, modern, and comfortable interior with a small sushi bar and dining room. 
- Quiet. Great for independent diners, romantic dates, or professional lunch outing. 
- Lunch special - two rolls and soup/salad for $6.99! Pretty much everything on their lunch menu is under $10. Me likey.
- Fresh, clean, and very yummy! For Indiana, this is up there. 
- Staff is usually pretty quiet. Not sure why. Attentive though.
- HUGE selection of rolls! Like a kid in a candy store...except the candy is raw fish and seaweed.
- Parking does suck in the area. I would suggest parking near the canal and walking over. You'll need a walk after you stuff your face full of sushi.

If you're living in Broad Ripple, it's nice and easy to walk here for some rolls and a beer. If not, sad face for you. Miss you, Sushi Bar!

I really like that they always have my favorites, but I wish the market would just carry Coffee Coffee, Buzz Buzz so I wouldn't have to pay a premium just for what I want. Probably the only reason this is 3 stars admittedly. The perk is that I feel like this place is never crowded so I can always get in and out!
The pizza today was not up to Newks normal pizza. Crust was soggy. Apparently used too much oil. Do not know

The only place to go for men's clothing. The selection of clothing is awesome. So many unique options. Both owners, Alan and Jackie are extremely helpful. Jackie is awesome at color coordinating the entire family. 


After the purchase is made, they are always available for nips and tucks. This morning my son tore the buttons off his shirt. I walked in without N appointment and Alan had them expertly sewn back on while I waited. He didn't even charge me. I challenge anyone to find service like that elsewhere!

There is nothing like this place in St. Louis.  It's located in the basement of the Vin de Set building on Chouteau.  There is an L-shaped bar with stools and several bar tables with stools, along with several couches to lounge on.  They have several flat screen TV's.  There are over 50 beers on tap, along with probably over 100 more bottles, and the bartenders are very helpful and knowledgeable of all of the beers.  You can order a pint or a 6 oz small "taster" for half the price of the pint.  My wife isn't a huge beer drinker so she loved the smaller pour option.  The food comes from PW pizza which is a restaurant located above this place.  The food was delicious.  Portion sizes were huge.   The atmosphere is very intimate but fun and casual.  No complaints here.

I met some friends for Happy Hour one day after work and the music was BLASTING so loud into the courtyard that we couldn't hear each other.  We went for nice ambiance  and conversation and I was disappointed in how "casual" the staff was even though they were open for business.  Just because the place isn't packed doesn't mean the staff should make the few customers there feel as though they aren't worth professional behavior. Tighten it up, New  Feelings.  The ambiance was Killed by this miserable display of childish, unprofessional behavior.

There are far fewer options for good tasting delivery in the area, especially compared to what you can get across the bridge. 

With that being said, Dominicks is generally one of the better places to order from. I'm not from around here, so I don't have the "old owners were better" bias. I wouldn't know. 

The menu here is stereotypical of a pizza place - Pizza, Salads, Calzone, Cheesesteaks, wings etc. I don't like pizza so I'll talk about some of the rest: 

The salads are full sized and the lettuce is never wilted or warm. On a few occasions the chicken was "muscle-y" which a total turn off for me. I order the ceasar with extra olives and onions and haven't had complaints about that. The cheesesteaks were fine with extra peppers and onions and mozzarella - I refuse to consume cheese whiz. The wings always seemed under cooked to me but I order them fried extra hard and they always get that request right. 

It's better than mediocre, and probably not exceptional and Originals, the spot in the complex,  doesn't deliver. Dominoes across the street and Pizza Hut don't carry anything worth consuming - if that helps give you a feel for my discernment. 

Delivery from Dominicks is always super pleasant and they're always within the 30-45 minute window. 

I would recommend.

On of my favs!

Marsha is from New Orleans, so this was a must for Mardi Gras. She got her start with Ruth, of Ruth's Chris steakhouse in New Orleans. I visited that same steak house in the early 80's in college in New Orleans. 
We started with a couple of craft cocktails. 
Bourbon Apple Crisp with Bulleit bourbon was sweet cinnamon apple goodness. Cinnamon/sugared rim. 
Cherry Blossom tasted like sweet dark cherries. 
Their crusty French bread is served in a Marsha Brown paper bag which holds the crumbs as you tear off new pieces. Served w/sweet whipped butter and garlicky red bean hummus(I need to make this!) they bring out a bottle of New Orleans Crystal brand hot sauce. 
Shrimp cocktail. Giant U-15 shrimp. Sold by the each. You decide how many!
Gumbo. Traditional smoky andouille sausage and okra and filled w/veggies
Beet salad w/spinach, goat cheese, beets, orange slices and a tasty vinaigrette. 
Church salad. Basic salad w/balsamic vinaigrette. 
Filet tournedos. Perfectly cooked filet medallions w/peppery cream sauce, grilled asparagus and mashed potatoes. (Great as a leftover on a Caesar salad)
BBQ New Orleans Salmon(less the normal shrimp due to allergy) served over cheesy Southern grits. The New Orleans bbq sauce was buttery, spicy and yummy!  It was like it was meant for salmon as well as shrimp!

Pecan pie to go (work night). Sweet. Just a bit of salt and that caramelized molasses is almost smoky. Great buttery crust and crisped pecans.  

Sooo good!

$180, but so worth it if you are a fan of fine dining and great food and drinks. Our server was great. The Manager told great stories. He found out we were fans of New Orleans and Mardi Gras and brought us some beads. I'll be back!

I grew up only about 5 minutes from this theater. I even remember when they were building it because at the time the only theater we had was in Cottonwood mall. Which was probably about the worst theater I have ever been in. Wooden Chairs just lined up in a room with a pull down screen. It was barely a movie theater.  Needless to say it is now out of business.

I was so excited  to see a weekend movie every once and a while in this Kerasotes theaters. Now it is an Amc Showplace but that means little to me. It is still a high quality establishment for viewing films in regular film, digital, or digital 3D. 

For me, being a college student, one of the best things is the deal they have every week Monday-Thursday before 4pm. Are you ready for it? I don't know....I mean this deal is pretty amazing......alright. Well ANY MOVIE that's right ANY MOVIE is just 5 DOLLARS! Not like the 5 buck club where only a few select movies were 5 dollars. This is a deal, especially if you are in college and are strapped for cash, that you cannot pass up!

This was so delicious! My friend and I happened upon this place and it floored me with how delicious it was. First they give you a dope shot of tumeric, lime, and ginger. Then i had the butternut squash tamales which were some of the best tamales ive ever tasted and definitely the best black beans ive ever had. Also Wendy was super nice and came and said hi to us and you could easily tell her passion for making great food. And to top it all off the price was pretty grand too!
Don't advertise that you're open Christmas Day when you're closed, annoying and wasting people's valuable time.

Buyer be ware. Ashley is a franchise and each store is operated independent. If you decline to have your furniture delivered and go to the distribution center on Green park road to pick up and something happens during the one year warranty for cushion's or frame in order to enforce the warranty you must bring the product back to them in order to have it fixed. What kind of business practice is this? have a warranty but in order to have it fixed you have to bring it to them. But if you pay their delivery fee during the one year they will send their service to you. Scam.

Crappy food and service!!! Everything was bland and too expensive! $13 for a small BLT nasty buffalo wings with the damn feathers still on the wings! Don't waste your money here unless your drunk and starving! Also the fettuccini Alfredo was tasteless as well!

We loved everything about El Camino Real except the cheesesteak.  El Camino Real, big fun place.  I liked the window art.  The cheesesteak came with french fries.  The french fries were heavily seasoned and the ketchup has either a Sriracha ketchup or BBQ ketchup or BBQ sauce, whatever it was, I like the Heavenly season french fries with a barbecuey sauce.  The Cheesesteak was just a miss.  The roll was big, bready and didn't work with his cheesesteak.  They use a cheese sauce which should be able to get through all the meat but it didn't.  The meat was a little dry.  We loved everything else.  Maybe go to El Camino Real and don't get the cheese steak

Stayed here for two nights, which was two nights too many.  We've stayed here in the past and thought it was a wonderful hotel.  That was not the case this trip.  Upon arrival the staff was less than friendly which I can overlook.  The room was another story.  There was exposed electrical outlets, stains on the couch, stains on the floor, the plumbing was exposed in the bathroom - not bad just weird - shower curtain did not fit the entire shower and minimal counter space in the bathroom.  I kept my shoes on the entire stay and did not put any bags on the furniture or floor.  The door joining two rooms had an obscene picture drawn on it.  Placed 4 calls for housekeeping and never got an answer.  Also called the front desk 4 times with no answer.  Overall, the place was not nearly as nice as it was on our first stay.

We love Mexican food and were apprehensive when this opened. Now it's a regular weekly stop for us. Their arroz con pollo is so cheesy and the chicken is good on it's own. They have great drink specials and the staff is amazing. We highly recommend coming here if you love Mexican food, you won't be disappointed.

As soon as I walked in, I knew this was a college dig, or what seemed like it at the time, the staff were rude and they thought they where 'cool', couldn't wait to leave that hole, wouldn't even let my dog in it! Much better bars in the magazine street area

I don't think there is a better deal for late night after bar munchies than Empire.  Slices are the perfect way to end the nigh and you really can't beat the price!  We went there on a Friday after some bar hopping and it was way worth it!

I love going to this location because of their drive-thru! It's a close drive to campus, I come here all of the time.

Everyone working is always super kind and thoughtful. I have never had a bad experience at this location.

The drive-thru line is quick and they hand everything to you in a mug so it's keeps germs away. They also had you the credit card/chip reader so the employee doesn't need to touch your card. They are doing the best the can to keep everyone safe during the pandemic!

Why see a movie anywhere else? Ticket prices are comparable to standard theaters, but here you can recline fully, eat real food, and have an adult beverage. For the price you would pay for popcorn, candy, and soda at a standard theater, you can have a dinner for two, while watching the movie. We're hooked.
Courteous, quick, punctual, and reasonable! Great communication. Highly recommend. thanks Guys!

Ok so I have this little car, it is one I bought for my father in 1991 but since he is currently dead he does not use it much, nor do I. But this car does get dirty just sitting around so I took it down to where we get our other vehicles washed.

Even though this car is old, it is low mileage and looks as good as a new car. It is just not an expensive vehicle.

I noticed as it was surrounded by a lot of over priced vehicles being dried that those vehicles were worked on about twice the time as our poor little car. As we drove off I saw the result of this lack of attention, very dusty dash... you know like how we brought it in. the passenger side windows were never cleaned, in or outside and generally poor handling or care.

Folks give each vehicle the same care since it costs the same and the tip probably is the same... it is only fair.

Very good, was expecting a little larger pizza for the XL since they're NY style but wasn't disappointed with the taste. I do believe there are more authentic NY style pizza places in Clearwater but this place won't let you down

Attentive and very friendly staff every time! Food is always delicious and moderately priced. Ahi tuna is as good as any 5 star restaurant around. Thanks for all the hard work, your food is delicious! More people should know about this awesome joint

Finally tried this place out!

The concept is simple: a bowl or a pita! So many options to customize makes it a great place to bring everyone. The first time we went the employees were patient with us newbies and let us try sauces and toppings. I got a shawarma bowl with rice and the bf got a steak pita. 

We got different toppings but my favorites are the sumac onions and the Amba (spelling?) sauce! 

The bf "naffed it up" = got a drink and naf naf fries. They are not normal fries, some are crispy like chips and some are super soft and oily...not my fav and I wouldn't order them again. 

After our first visit I had a craving and ordered us dinner to pick up. The order system online is very strange and somewhat difficult to deal with- not sure I'll use it again, but my order was ready on time and correct! 

My one beef with this place is the inconsistency in amount/quality of meat. One time I had nice juicy shawarma the other it was many small burnt pieces with barely any "meat". There was so little I forgot it even had meat. 

Other than that- the pita bread, rice, and toppings are delicious! I'll be back but hope for more consistent quality!

Nice little spot. 
Good beer. 
Reasonable prices.
No food but Sugarfire next door will deliver.

Took my wife here for her birthday this past Sunday with my two daughters. First, the food is outstanding but there are several things that separate it from either a four star or a five star (which, I might add, they could certainly achieve). We both had the Lasagna with Lamb and it tasted as if it were made in a restaurant in Italy (if you've been to Italy you know that food is incredible every where you go). I believe it was the best Lasagna I've had. We paired it with an excellent wine from Italy (my apologies for not remembering the name). The appetizer was excellent as well. This is where it begins and unfortunately this is where it ends.
The presentation of the food was not the best (there was this very good sauce poured over the Lasagna but it looked like someone just poured over the Lasagna if you know what I mean). Food was still great but the presentation was - meh. Next the noise level was just too difficult to carry on a decent conversation. I know we had a large party near us but it's the brick walls and the narrow seating area that bounces the sound back and forth. This is not a place for an intimate dinner conversation. Next the service - wow, they need lessons in service. Not only were they not attentive, they didn't want to be. One of my daughters was running a bit late and we had some questions about the specials. The waiter curtly addressed this issue by firmly stating he didn't want to waste his time and repeat himself so we would have to wait. Then he went away. Huh?! Is this a restaurant or a garage where I was getting my car fixed? The person who seated us took our payment and acted in a very similar manner (they looked to be related so perhaps the waiter was trained this way). 
Great food is the beginning of a great restaurant but there are a lot of restaurants that have great food in Tampa. What it now needs is a more caring approach to serving the customer from the presentation of the meal to the acoustics to the service. Then this might be a five star restaurant. I'm not sure if they have takeout but I would encourage you to try this route as you can supply your own great service and ambiance.

I don't really understand these good reviews. This place is just so-so. The dining area is dirty, service is slow and food was nothing special. In all fairness, I've only been here once. I'll try again someday and possibly update the review.

Loved the brunch! We got the huevos tierra caliente and huevos a la mexicana. Served with warm corn tortillas, yum! The staff are nice here and it's a relaxing vibe. We've come here a few times now and the dishes have all tasted great without seeming ultra unhealthy (not overloaded with sauces and grease, just tasty ingredients).

This place is lovely: the atmosphere and vibe is so calming. They had a lot of drink and food options -- there were too many things I wanted to try! I ordered the milk cap peach oolong tea and it was soooo good. Some boba places make their drinks either extremely sweet or super bitter but I think Mr deer the camp hit a good balance between the two. 

It took them about 10 minutes to fill our orders, which is pretty good! If I stop by Philadephia again, I'd love to come back here.

I ordered to go a miso soup, mushroom rolls and salmon+avocado rolls. 
OMG!!!! This place reaaaaly impressed me!!! Miso soup was excellent!!!  mushroom (shiitake)rolls were on point! And salmon+avocado rolls were absolutely amazing!!!! There were so big pieces of salmon!!!!

Friends and I come here for trivia sometimes on Wednesday nights, which is always a lot of fun. I've tried a few entrees there which are pretty good. I tried the steak tacos there this time. Good flavor, good portion size. Came with chips, salsa, and guacamole, which were a little sub par, but still enjoyed it overall.
Yum yum yum owners wife waited on us & was awesome.  I'm def coming back. A cute place w good German food. I'm in Europe!

We had a great stay at this property. The staff was super friendly. The room and hotel were clean. They had board games in the lobby. And every time we walked in or out, we were greeted by the staff. Huge plus! 
THERE ISNT A POOL AT THIS PROPERTY. But you can go down to their sister property and use theirs. It's a bit small, but worth it if you want to cool down.
My first time at the salon was earlier in the week.  David did a great job on my color and cut.  I will definitely be back!

Mimi is kind, prompt, caring and truly a gifted stylist. Luce Salon is a welcoming salon with a beautiful quaint patio located on the Mesa. You will leave with beautiful results and a lifted spirit after an appointment with Mimi. Ive worked with her at a salon in the past and she has done my hair many many times and in my humble opinion I think she is one of the best hair stylists in SB. She is such a gem. I love her, and you will too!

I was here for brunch with Groupon. They said that we could only choose  to eat from a less than half the menu if we had Groupon. Not worth the money we paid.  disappointed!! But the service was good..

The tacos are delicious and authentic compared to other places. The people that work there are very nice and make sure you are enjoying everything. I'm going to recommend this place to all my friends that love tacos as much as I do! They have a bacon wrapped burrito that I'll be sure to try next time it looks amazing. Thanks you Samano's for coming to fishers!!

What a find! Located in a strip mall that would be otherwise nondescript, this little cafe does a great business with tasty food, friendly service and outdoor seating. Offering just about anything you would want for a substantial breakfast including fresh OJ, the cafe also has a nice array of tacos for lunch - with homemade tortillas to boot. I was impressed at the flavor and quality - would definitely stop by again.
Just okay! A bit pricey and bland. Great customer service. Neat building with a lot history, glad they restored it and tried to keep its original structure and fixtures.
Service and food amazing. Only question is as many times as we have stayed at the Siena why haven't we eating here before? Jovanny's service makes it a six star.

Still absolute trash, run by a seriously terrible person who has no respect for food, no respect for customers, and is willing to serve both rancid and raw ingredients to customers.  Places like this should be run out of town on a rail - the only reason garbage can thrive like this in Reno is because the locals allow it to.  Stop paying for bad food!!!

Went here for lunch with my co-workers and manager 7/19. I am happy to report this place is awesome! Tried the chicken skins and they left a smile on every ones face as they had such great flavour and the right amount of spice! For my main dish I tried the blue steel burger and boy that was great too! just enough blue cheese and the perfect cut of bacon! It was great! I am only disappointed I wasn't able to try more because I was so full haha Great atmosphere (a little warm but this was during a heat wave) Great Service and GREAT food! I will definitely be back for more!

Moe's is great for a quick lunch spot. On a strip of shops next to Vanderbilt, it can get pretty busy here. There's a large menu at the front to help you make your Southwest option--they have nachos, tacos, bowls, burritos, quesadillas, and lots of other options. 

I went with the quesadilla, which I have been craving for weeks. I normally just eat a cheese quesadilla, sometimes with chicken if I'm feeling crazy. Their quesadilla menu options offered beans, rice, lettuce, tomatoes, and other ingredients. I decided to try out some with my usual quesadilla and I was pleasantly surprised. It was absolutely delicious!

I love tipping for great service, and the staff was king and helpful throughout the dining line. They rind a bell if you tip, which is so fun!

I am now in the best shape of my life thanks to Malcolm!!  I came to Malcolm to help me lose weight and tone up before my wedding.  He spoke with me about my wants, needs, hinderances, and advantages and customized a personal workout.  Since then, he has tweaked these workouts to hone in on what I want and need the most.  Furthermore, he is very accommodating; he is flexible with my often busy schedule, but he never lets me get away too long without checking in and pushing me to work out.  He has also helped me with nutrition, coaching me on proper meals and portions, even providing recipe ideas! Probably for the first time in my life (and I'm 30), when I look in the mirror I am happy.  Malcolm has become a good friend and he is an EXCELLENT trainer.  I highly recommend Malcolm for all fitness needs and levels!

If you have small children, this gym will "change your life," as many parents told me.  The kids academy is fantastic. The facility is clean, the employees are happy to be there and kind to the children. The free (most are included With the membership) kids classes are the highlight of the academy. If you are looking for a little time for yourself, the membership is worth it for the kids academy.  You can't leave the facility, but they have several lounge areas where you can sit, if you aren't working out that day. 
Overall, the gym is very nice. 
There are two yoga instructors in particular who are great. 
The marketing after you join is a bit intense, but manageable.

Great auto shop!  I have been coming here with my family for years since they opened. Mr. Kong and his team are very efficient and honest with the repairs needed for the car. They never charge extra and are very forefront with their prices. I had my car finished within the same day to replace a broken side mirror and they even took the courtesy to buff out minor scratches on my car.  Highly recommend!

I'm not sure where the negative reviews are coming from.  I went to Cornerstone Dental from a Groupon I purchased and was really pleased.

My family and I were truly impressed at how modern the office was, how friendly the staff was, and how professional the dentist was.  I was not pressured into any services other than what I discussed as options with my dentist.   They gave very detailed explanations and were very accommodating with my scheduling needs (I have several other medical issues that make my schedule very difficult to plan).

They had WiFi while I was waiting in the front (which was only a short period), a modern TV in my room, and a very comfortable atmosphere overall...especially for nervous dentist-phobes like me.  My hygenist noticed that I was nervous but was so friendly that she put me at ease. 

Again, I'm not sure why the reviews I read prior to coming to this place were so negative, because that was the TOTAL opposite if what I experienced.  I think that the prior negative reviews were really unfair.

Walked in today with a recent power problem on my iphone 5C.  20 minutes later I was out the door without breaking the bank.  All seems to be working fine.  While there I peppered the 2 techs with a bunch of random questions about my phone.  They answered without hesitation and didn't seem to be bothered by me.  Would highly recommend them to others.

We love the pizza here and think it is among the better pizzas in center city and surrounding areas.  Our favourites are simple: a few mushroom pies and a few margarita pies.  The dough is superb;  thin, light but with just the right amount of crunch to it here and there along with a nice "bubble" or few.  The staff is super friendly and while it can get busy, you can either go on off hours (which is awesome), or just know that it is worth the wait.  We sometimes just do take out... Pull up right in front and pick it up.  We are super happy to have Vetri's Pizza in this part of Center City.  Though my wife doesn't fully agree, I think there is sometimes a bit too much salt in the dough, but of course, she is correct :)  Vetri's Pizza:  100% worth checking out.

We approached dining at Stella with an open mind, a sense of adventure, and an open wallet. It's difficult to know where to begin with this review, as so many aspects of the food were mind-blowing, and the service was so seamless that the entire experience seemed to melt together into this blissful memory of a meal.

I'm aware that the menu changes frequently with the seasons and according to what's available, so your menu will vary. I enjoyed a gnocchi appetizer, which turned me from a "gnocchi is alright, I guess" to a "GNOCCHI IS MY ONLY FOOD GROUP" kind of person. For my main course, I had the filet, which had been expertly prepared by sous vide and then a quick sear. The meat was accompanied with an Asian-influenced brown sauce, as well as several vegetables that couldn't have been more perfect or delicious. The lobster dish on the menu that night was also incredible. We had dessert, too, which included a very inventive dish that involved pink lemonade. 

In fact, every dish was inventive and fun, as you've probably heard they are, and I'd recommend going in with a blank slate and an open mind when it comes to making choices, if you're a less adventurous diner. If you want the full experience, have more funds, and are really adventurous, go for the tasting menu, and maybe add the wine pairings. I don't think there's anything in New Orleans quite like this. 

Prices are incredibly high, but you get what you pay for in artistry, an incredible dining experience, and first-class service. It's truly romantic and wonderful for a special occasion. Reservations are a must, and I believe jacket is required for men. If you've planned to go to Stella, go, and make sure at least one diner in your party orders an iced tea.
This is a Washington Redskins bar located in Ybor. They show all NFL games as well and have plenty of TV's. The wings are outstanding...all drums!  FYI they do allow smoking inside. HTTR!

Long story short: Philly cheese steak is good, roast pork sandwich is terrible. 

I really don't understand how the John's Roast Pork is considered this place's specialty. It's a strange combination of lukewarm pork, cheese, and spinach where the flavors are nothing but salt and the textures are odd. Frankly, this mix doesn't mesh well together.

The cheese isn't melted and it's chunks of cheese thrown haphazardly on top of roast pork. Maybe this sandwich used to be good? But the customer volume is now so overwhelming that the quality is rushed.

While the roast pork is disappointing, the Philly cheesesteak is actually quite nice! The cheese is melted well among the cheesesteak and the best part is the seeded bread-- it's soft yet crispy, very good. The Philly cheese steak is almost able to balance out the lackluster roast pork sandwich.

You know how to tell if a place is great - is count the number of times a person from 200 miles away returns.  I nearly stop every chance I pass through St. Louis.  The hamburgers evidently received high marks among the city burger joints at one point back in the 90's.  A friend turned me on to the place and I've become a raving fan ever since.  It's simple - it's almost like a "Cheers" atmosphere.  Just a local friendly pub with great burgers and fries.  Give this a try.

Professional craftologists are the bar. The staff here has somehow been transplanted from the city into New Hope. That said, everyone knows their menu and is friendly and courteous and doesn't make you feel like they are doing you a favor by serving you...which is pretty much par for the course at most NH spots anymore. Let's hope Stella attracts more of the same and forces everyone else to elevate their game. Thanks you Chef Graces!!
Love this place.. Unpretentious, good Italian food.  Great staff too!
Love this place! The staff and Chef James are awesome! This is always our Go-To Sushi place  :)

Had a great time here! Love the ambiance. There was a live band playing on the lower level, sounded great. We went to the bar upstairs for just drinks, extremely friendly bartender, very attentive. Nice clean place, drinks are reasonably priced for a tourist place. I had jack daniels so you can't really screw that up, my friend got malibu and coke again you can't screw that up either. Would definitely come back to have a few and relax. :)

My iPhone 6 was hacked and totally locked up. iHospital unlocked it, cleaned it and restored it from iCloud for me in less than half an hour.  They said they'd never seen anything like what was appearing on my locked screen - clearly a deliberate hack into my phone.  

Sean Odell, the store manager at the iHospital on Kennedy Blvd. in South Tampa was SO great to work with and saved my phone!  I highly recommend them!!

So why not 4 or 5 stars, Will? Why are you the first schmecklehead to afford this new and vaunted eatery anything less? Well...I care about public health and selfishly, my own health. The lack of PROPER mask wearing by staff is a great concern for me: our server and the busser did NOT have their schnozes covered by their cloth masks, rendering them useless as far as "slowing the spread." I did see one other server wearing the ubiquitous light blue procedure mask with a fully covered face, so kudos to him. While I'm an old soul, I couldn't help but notice the age of our fellow diners skewed older and thus higher COVID risk. 

As far as the food? Everything was truly good at worst and wonderful at best! 

Appetizers:

-on the advice of Yelp friend Vince, I pulled the trigger on the rice balls/Arancini. They were of perfect consistency: fork tender, a poke of the fork unearthed a center of peas, cheese and I don't know what else, but it was wonderful tasty gooey goodness with a crispy breaded shell. Not much "Sunday gravy" was provided, I wish there was a little more. 

-squash blossoms: my girlfriend LOVED these and this skeptical critic did as well. The oil in which they're fried is nice and fresh, the inside had ricotta,  perhaps even burrata based on the rich creaminess. 

-Bruschetta: if you dined at the former inhabitant of this address (The slightly pretentiously named Oliver, a Bistro), you'd likely agree that Angelo's iteration of this Italian classic isn't quite up to previous snuff. That being said, the bread on which the fresh mozzarella and tomato rested seemed fresh and had just the right amount of crunch. 

-Bread + compound butter: I cannot comment on this. It wasn't provided as we "had ordered a lot of food already." Yes I could've asked for it and taken it home or something, but I didn't wish to appear gauche in front of my date. Whatever happened to (as they say at Olive Garden): Hospitaliano!??

Mains:

-cacio e peppe: pasta was a perfect al dente and tasted as it should. You'll often find this dish atop clickbait listicles of "what not to order at the Italian restaurant" due to the perceived ease of preparation which in turn lowers the value for the money, something I'm typically all about. However, it's a restaurant, if I wanted a workaurant (shout out to Steak 'n Shake) I'd procure heavy creme, black poivre and Parmigiano-Reggiano to make this at home.  It was a winner here. 

-Short Rib Ragú atop pasta that was like fettuccine but probably another name: this was a special of the day and among the most popular. I can see why! If you love bouef bourguignon (shout out to Julia Child and my Dad) you'll LOVE this and I hope it becomes a menu staple here at Angelo's.  Rich flavor of red wine, beefy short ribs, perfectly cooked pasta and creamy cheeses (I believe Parmesan and ricotta). Half of this went home as it's very rich. 

Dessert:

Budino: will definitely order again! My baseline for this is Barbuzzo in Philly. Theirs is a salted caramel flavor, while here at Angelo's there are notes of espresso topped with mystery cookie flakes and a sweet cream. Perfect for sharing! Your stomach won't feel like lead either, a nice plus for dessert

The espresso had a nice crema and paired perfectly with the aforementioned Budino. Angelo's passes this must-have of a trattoria with flying colors. 

We arrived 15 minutes early for our 5:30 reservation on a Thursday and being seated was no problem. No, the outdoor tables aren't all 6 feet apart but that's simply not a feasible reality in areas with limited real estate and I understand that. Angelo's also can't control the numerous mask-free pedestrians walking by on the sidewalk. 

What can they control? For one, remember drink refills? Especially when it's tap water? It's a nice thing to keep an eye on and a fair barometer of service quality. I had to ask for more water after making a point of passive aggressively leaving my empty water glass at the tables edge. 

Additionally and more importantly: My hope is their staff can be properly trained in correct mask usage to ensure everyone's safety. Is that too much to ask? Should this even be a controversial thing to say? Unfortunately it is in these times. Let's hope they end soon! I think we all can agree that the "old"  normal beats this terribly clichéd "new" one. Total for 2: $100 before tip.

These guys have a serious problem here especially in the drive thru portion.   6pm on a weeknight and the cars are lined up in the drive thru.  The first sign this is a problem is when I pulled up and pushed the call button no one answered it for 5 minutes.  The other issue this place has is that the app will notify you that the prescription is ready but when you come in, it's not ready. Just as today,  they said they weren't ready. So I get to  sit here  waiting in the Drive-Thru line for about 35 minutes total. They need to hire more staff or find a better way of doing this because this is ridiculous.  I'll never use it again I'll just go in next time.

First time in Nashville! Our uber driver recommended it and we absolutely loved it! We ordered Mac n cheese with the hot chicken and we shared it. It was a good size portion and it hit the spot. Drinks are delicious too!

Wonderful experience,great food and service....owner/manager? Excellent!!

Thanks!!

We split a bagel egg sandwich with the best breakfast potatos....don't forget the apple fritters....
Highly recommend them...

Such a pretty place to see! Loved the set up! 

Got a mini lemon meringe tart and a piece of their chocolate flourless cake. The lemond curd filling was spot on with tartness! Crust and meringue weren't as memorable. The chocolate flourless cake was definitely rich and reminded me of chocolate truffles.

Their ambience and set up of the menus is what I am most impressed with this place. I'll be back to dine in more than to have their baked goods. They have a pretty small assortment at the counter but a decent enough amount to choose from. This is a cake place where you can get a slice of whatever cake you see in the display case. Lots of cakes to try!

Next time I will try some other items!

Overall, I always have a good time at Dave and Buster's.

I ate at the bar this time since I was by myself.  Even though I was at the bar, Aty still noticed and greeted me.  Christian provided awesome service.

Within an hour of me eating, it got extremely busy, not just at the bar, but overall in the dining section in the midway.  I felt badly for Christian because it didn't seem he had the appropriate backup.  But he handled it like a pro.

Not long after I paid my tab at the bar, I noticed the midway filling up with people.  The Winner's Circle was EXTREMELY busy and usually there would be two people.  However, Symone was by herself.  She is awesome and always friendly.  I also felt badly for her because she didn't have the necessary backup when the line for ticket redemption wrapped around the entire Winner's Circle, which was the first I've EVER seen it that busy since I've started coming regularly.  However, Symone handled it with amazing composure and a smile.

There are great staff working at this Dave and Buster's Plymouth location and this is why I always come back.

Sometimes I think I'm being too generous with my ratings, but I hate giving a bad review. I honestly do really like this place. I only get my hair done professionally once a year around my birthday as a splurge. This review is for May 2010 when I was last here. They were able to fit me in on a one day notice. The salon is very clean and has a very nice welcoming feel to it. My hairdresser was very friendly and nice. I came in for a cut and for partial highlights. I have dark brown hair and had highlights from a year ago that had grown out but still visible. I asked her to cut my hair and match the color and style of my highlights. The cut was great. The layering and eveything was perfect. However, the highlight came out WAY too light. I looked almost blond. She could not match them at all. She offered to fix them at no charge when I came back (they were closing), but I didn't really feel comfortable. She sent me a nice card in the mail, and the cut was very nice.This is why I feel bad not giving a 5 star, but I feel like the highlights were done so bad, I couldn't go back there.
