I went back to LA to my regular stylist to get them fixed. From now on, I think I'm just going to have to deal with the 2 hr drive to La for my hair appointments.

Every dining experience there has been awesome. My last trip there I ordered the penne pasta and shrimp in vodka cream sauce and it was fantastic. So much on the plate that I had to take some home. The Wednesday special of prime rib and shrimp is one of the best deals around. I have never been disappointed with the meals or the service. Absolutely great!!

ok, i know all y'alls hatin on sisters, but if you go in a group, and are not trying to pick up women (i guess that's the point), it's fine! the drinks are cheap, the bartender was a hottie, and lesbian courting can be fun to watch

I can't say enough about this school!  My son had an awful experience at Educare Preschool and we are blessed to have found this school!  My son's teacher, Miss. Christine is so kind and warm hearted!  She has a family photo album for the kids to look at whenever they want - she hugs my son and comforts him if he's ever sad.  My son just loves her so much!  The office staff is very informative and their attention to detail is spot on!  They have a cleaning lady constantly sanitizing the rooms and toys all day everyday!  The school smells AMAZING!!!  They have the best fingerprint system to check your child in and out and you need a special key to get through the doors.  I feel safe leaving him there all day.  It's one of those too good to be true kind of preschools but it really is true!  Bright Beginnings is a blessing

Going to bed hungry. . I usually like this place...for their ala carte sushi. Tonight we were more adventurous but our sushi order was incomplete and the 
shrimp Pad Thai we wanted to try had an odor and seemed as though it had been sitting a very, very long time. I only had a bite and hope I don't get sick.

Isabelle Rossi my BershireHathaway agent in Manasquan NJ is professional and friendly. She met with us when it was convenient for us. She took note of the requirements we were looking for in our forever home. Isabelle showed us many homes but we finally decided to buy a house that was closer to home.

Had high hopes, as this was highly recommended by locals.  Long wait for a table and a long wait for food, but there are highlights:  four of us ordered the barbecued chicken.  Very tender and juicy, but reviews of the sauce ranged from too tame to meh.  The brisket and ribs both drew thumbs up.  Barbecue Salad with brisket was declared okay and the fried fish got a passing grade.  Reaction to the sides was mixed as well: cheddar grits were scrumptious, cole slaw nicely crisp, good potato salad, great dirty rice and ok BBQ beans.  Sweet potato crisp may as well have been dessert.  Cornbread was tasty, not too sweet. 

Biggest criticism of the menu:  limited fish offering (fried or shrimp and grits), only one vegetarian burger, no fresh veggies (other than salad), no cooked greens. 

Overall, pretty good food,  but be prepared to wait for both your table and your meal!

I stopped in randomly to ask questions about the different blood donation processes. Amie, the donation clinic manager, was incredibly helpful and knowledgeable about the different types of donations. She answered all my questions with a smile and without judgment. I highly recommend this donor center.

This restaurant had been on my list for an incredibly long time, but yet I had never seemed to get to crossing it off. I am so glad that I finally did!

INITIAL - I made a reservation for 8pm two days in advance via OpenTable. Very easy process and there was a decent amount of availability for a Saturday night. Arrived and was assisted by the complimentary valet service. Once inside I was greeted warmly, and showed to the table. 

AMBIANCE - The atmosphere/ambiance of this restaurant is phenomenal with blush colored plush booths, a lit up sign saying 'irresistible,' subtle plants, and gorgeous art. My friend and I made note that the lighting was dim enough to be warm and comfortable, but not so dim that it was sitting in the dark, or gaudy 'too-romantic-for-friends" lighting. So pretty overall!

DRINKS - Lightly chilled water. Tried 2 different cocktails - The Mercedes and Essential Frivolity. Both featured grapefruit juice, which they squeeze fresh at the restaurant. Essential Frivolity was one of the best cocktails I've ever had. No strong overwhelming taste of alcohol, just a fresh, fruity, balanced concoction. The Mercedes had pear, cinnamon and grapefruit and your choice of vodka or gin. I chose vodka. The light cinnamon paired with the fruity pear and grapefruit was such a pleasant and surprising combination. I absolutely loved both. 

FOOD - We ordered the Bucheron Cheese, Shaved Bayonne Ham, Onion Soup Gratinee, and Le Burger. Each item was incredible and simple! 

The Bucheron was soft warm goat cheese, pair with thick buttery slices of sourdough bread and blueberry compote.

The Shaved Bayonee Ham was dijon mustard, thick slices of buttery sourdough, and thinly shaved cured ham, served with gherkins.   

The Onion Soup Gratinee was a classic French onion soup with warm, melty cheese and bread all baked in a ceramic bowl. 

I didn't eat the Le Burger, but my friend raved over it. It was served with Truffle Fries and she was in heaven!

Additionally, the service was incredible. Not campy or overly involved, but helpful, polite, and available! So great.

Will be frequenting this lovely restaurant. Next time will have to try the wine selection.

What an amazing find - love the food. Didn't know what to eat -  got a recommendation from the sweetest waitress (difficult name to spell started with a J) - on what to eat. May not be on the beat and path but look for it - was worth every bite.  We went back again the next day.  The grits were so creamy and sweet best I've ever tasted.  Wish they were closer to New York!!

I love going here for breakfast. Today, we thought we'd try going for lunch. We don't necessarily live close by and it's a good 45 minute drive to this location. It wasn't busy since it was around lunch time & we usually get greeted but maybe today wasn't a good day for the lady who say us to the table. Maybe she was just getting over the morning rush. Anyway, we already had our minds set on the fried okra and fried chicken but, once we got our food I knew that I had made the wrong choice. Nothing really seemed fresh except for the salad. The fried okra was soggy and it seemed like it was microwaved a little too long. The corn bread was hard as a rock and the fried chicken...well, I was hoping for fried chicken. A little bit disappointed with this visit. Next time, we will just come here for breakfast.

Our water heater died on us yesterday and today, before 11:00 AM, we have a lovely new one.  That's how fast this company is.  The owner/plumber showed up early.....that's right...EARLY for our appointment...oh and by the way...they didn't give us a window of "we will be there anywhere between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM" we were given an actual arrival time of 9:00 AM and he was early!  Sorry but I had no hot water so this was a big deal for me.  He was very clean, professional and very personable.   He got to work right away and got the job done quick.  He left the area cleaner than when he started and even upgraded some things for us and included it as part of the installation.  The price I paid was the price that was quoted (which was very reasonable and lower than some other quotes we had received).  What a great experience...so much so we will definitely be calling this company again for some other jobs we have planned in the future.  If you need a plumber, I highly recommend Joshua Tree Plumbing.  Alright bye you guys I gotta go take a shower.

Wish I could put negative stars. Y'all hire racist staff. You're slow as fuck and you're good doesn't even taste that good. Fuck this business no wonder y'all lost all the kids from that high school up the street. You guys suck at your job.

Didn't even finish my food! It wasn't all that great. I ordered the.... darn I forgot! It's the one under the portobello burger, starts with an "S". But anyway, I've had better. Way better. The fries were good, just overly salted. If you want an awesome burger joint to go to that's in the Nampa area, go to blazin burger. It's the best. Cheaper too.

The BEST bagels in NL and Fishtown, hands down. Nicest owners, their whitefish salad is on point too. Actually, everything we have ever had from here has been excellent. This is the kind of neighborhood establishment we need to support!
The staff is not friendly and the fish spread is ok, but it is certainly not the best in town like they advertise. Overall, we were disappointed with our experience.

Four words: Build Your Own Burrito. Do it soon.

Their house chips and salsa are excellent and the margaritas are yummy too!

Whoa! Don't judge this place by the store front. It should be a Diners Drive-Ins and Dives feature. Absolutely loved it! Jambalaya, Seafood Gumbo, Shrimp Scampi and Chicken Jefferson were all phenomenal. Met Chef Ron himself. My wife has celiacs and they were great with GF options. My son said this is a definite stop next time he's in New Orleans.
I've been here a couple of times, once for dinner and once for the lunch buffet and the food has been pretty good. Biriyani is excellent! A little pricy but I would recommend.

Mediocre at best.  The burger was okay, nothing special. The fries were put in the bag upside down and they weren't even that great.  I should have wasted my calories at Burger King.  At least they carry Coca-Cola products.

There used to be a Qdoba on 23rd Street in Manhattan that I liked to frequent; however, it closed about eight years ago.  I hadn't been to one since, until I tried this place in Tampa International Airport.

I had a chicken quesadilla and it was very good. The price was low and the portions generous for an airport. 

One isssue: When I asked for sour cream in my quesadilla, I was told that I could have it on the side, which I thought was odd. (Moe's Southwest Grill lets you have sour cream inside the quesadilla.)

Although I like Moe's better than Qdoba, Qdoda is still good, and I like Qdoba better than Chipotle.

They seemed far overpriced for the service I received for my wedding dress alterations. I had to come back on two occasions - first because the used the wrong stitch on the hem - and then because they forgot to sew my cups in. They overcharged for the cups (based on what I have received from other seamstresses). The three bustles ($50 each) fell out of my wedding dress and had to be safety pinned into place at my reception. They also did an expensive and arguably unnecessary alteration to the back of the dress that involved taking the zipper out. When they put the zipper back in, it changed the way the lace detail lined up. Everyone said the dress looked beautiful, so it wasn't ruined, but the whole experience made me feel swindled. On the plus side, they were always friendly every time I went in.

Super busy parking lot, crazy busy inside but they are very organized.  When we walked in the door we were directed to the next available DMV associate who was extremely pleasant and oh so friendly.  She checked us in and in no time we were called back to be assisted.  Everything went smoothly and as quickly as it posaibly could have gone.  This building was surprisingly clean and the experience wasn't the nightmare I had imagined it would be or had experienced in the past!  Yay!!

Hospitals like TGH are why sites like WebMD were created, so no one has to deal with unprofessional and incompetent medical personnel. TGH checked me in as a visitor when my girlfriend was brought to the ER while unconscious. When she regained consciousness, they told her no one came for her and let her walk home without shoes. Meanwhile, I had been in the waiting room for over two hours while nurses gossiped. TGH called my girlfriend over 15 times this week to admit her following abnormal labs and EKGs. When we called back, everyone from the switchboard operator to the nurses was rude and condescending. When we got to the hospital, we waited three hours for her bed after they made us rush for a bed "within the hour." There were no orders in place, all they offered her was a flu vaccine. Orders should have been placed by Dr. Knox of USF health, but care through him has always been sub-par and inconsistent. TGH harassed both of us for over a week to admit her for flu vaccines, apparently. She could've gone to CVS for those. We needed to remind the nurse to do the standard check in procedures like take blood pressure, check heart rate, etc. The homophobic redneck roommate has been a really nice touch, too. Don't ask me why this place is award-winning. I will slam my own head in a door before I recommend this hospital to anyone.
No wonder the rating is so low. My nuggets were saturated and gross. The person that i paid just put out her hand like didn't say hello or anything at all... not going back

All that's left is a 30-acre estate, but the mansion tour is led by a charismatic tour guide, who ends by dropping you off to some amusing wine staff for a tasting. Included as an option with our Access pass, so it just required a drive and waiting for the tour to start as we wandered the grounds.
Great food. Great service. Great atmosphere. I love this place. I think it is very authentic and makes me feel like I have stepped into another city. It is a hidden gem.

One of the best meals I've ever had.

Went here with my friend Samantha and we both thoroughly enjoyed our experience. The biscuits were awesome, and the lobster dumplings were also delicious. 

Our entrees were even better. She had the salmon and I had the wood-grilled butterfish. During this entire meal I was going through flavor heaven; the attention to detail was astounding. Very pleasant and professional service. Some very passionate and talented minds came together to create an experience that will intrigue and satisfy your most discerning tastebuds. Om nom nom. The chefs are truly experts. I was fully impressed. 

For dessert we had the Salty Malty Ice Cream pie. PERFECTION. 

We both fell in love. I don't have a single complaint.

You want to get away from the French Quarters and have some great food and drinks,  this is definitely the place.  We had the tacos and wow they were good.  We took the Trolley from the Quarters and we were here in no time. Well worth the ride up.  Certainly more family oriented than the craziness of the French Quarter's.
Quaint family owned restaurant!!! Excellent for catering  and just grabbing a taste of Cuba! Be prepared to pay $1 extra if you are using your credit card.

We went to smash burger with high hopes of tasty burgers and some interesting fries. What a disappointment.  My husband ordered the large jalapeño baja beef burger with smash fries and I ordered they goat cheese spinach cucumber  chicken burger and onion rings. 

The food came quickly but we were soon disappointed. My husband said his burger had zero flavour dispute the jalapeño peppers. The fries were okay - nothing to write home about. 

My burger was missing the cheese and cucumber. The girl was helpful and brought me some - unfortunately they were out of cucumber and suggested pickles instead (no thank you) the chicken burger was salty yet had no flavour. Bizarre. 

The onion rings were AWFUL! Nothing but salt coated onion. The texture was okay but the salt ruined it! 

I won't be going back. The 2 stars earned were only because the burgers came quick and the service was friendly.

So we made a reservation here for my birthday. When the hostess called to confirm our reservation, she was less than delightful... Skip to the day of, we arrived right on time, but were told our table was not ready and to have a seat at the bar... Which would have been fine, but all the seats were already full. We stood around wondering what to do next and luckily someone else left. Okay, fine, so we had a cocktail while we waited. Since we weren't given a time frame, we decided to request menus so we could get a head start on our selections.  Fast forward 25 and we were on our way to a great meal. Overall, the starters and entrees were amazing, the dessert was sub par. 

What we got: Ham croquettes (juicy inside, crispy outside. amazing), garlic shrimp (sizzling, lemony , fried garlic. yum), aged manchego with AMAZING truffle honey, cheese with a tart cherry/fig marmalade, scallops perfectly cooked, short rib flatbread cheesey goodness, creamy baked goat cheese, and chicken with an egg (very simple, our least favorite). Dessert was a custard of sorts and a chocolate cake... Not our favorite. Oh, and even though I made a note on Open Table and mentioned that it was my birthday when the hostess called to confirm our reservation, it was not even acknowledged... Disappointing.

We will return to enjoy tapas and just skip dessert in the future!

I'll start by saying that this tour is probably best for people who have never been to Santa Barbara or are looking for something to do with the kids on a Sunday during daylight. 

We recently used this service to celebrate my friend's 25th birthday on Saturday. I'll give it to them, we were with a hyper group ready to party and had many people bouncing about wanting to dance to music and socialize. However, there was an issue with one of our friends who had an expired ID card that was not allowed on the bus. While I fully understand there are laws set in place I also know there is a polite way to approach such issues. After the issue with the id came up the woman checking identification immediately turned sour with a huge attitude. I walked on the bus sober and with proper valid identification and I felt as if I were being talked down to as well. Really rude service from the woman with dark short hair. I've bartended in the past and I know drunk people can be annoying, but come on, your company literally allows people to bring their own booze on a boat, lighten up. I sat in my seat most of the time because I felt uncomfortable being given the death stare by the hall monitor employee. We were celebrating a special occasion and instead it felt like sitting in the principal's office. 

On a more positive note I'm sure this would be more beautiful during the day. We ended our tour around nine and it was completely dark and kind of pointless because we couldn't see anything anyway. Honestly should have paid for a table at a club and got bottle service or rented a limo to drive us around for a bit. Wouldn't recommend for adult celebrations.

Local neighborhood grocery store  Has most things you need. Parking lot is a good size. Large over hang for rainy days. Fish department is not the best but it is available Good variety of fruits and vegetables Good stock of hard liquor alcohols  nice wine selection It has a little bit of everything but not a lot of everything

I've only had beer here and was glad for it. The menu itself reads well, but I opted to stick with beer only as taps in this town seem few and far between.

The Staff was great! Welcoming and well informed.

Though I would hardly expect this on every visit, the balcony afforded us a front row seat on a building fire and 1st responders just up the street.

~m
Whenever I'm in Santa Barbara I try to stop by here, the food is great and the view is amazing. Service is always great, parking is shared with the beach so plan accordingly.

The Conshohocken IKEA probably will always be my favorite since it was the IKEA that took my IKEA virginity. hahaha. I just the atmosphere in here while browsing this " furniture museum." By the way great stress reliever walking through here when it is less busy.

Awesome! 10/10 service.  5+ stars, 2 thumbs up.  I can't remember her name but the lady behind the bar/ counter was super nice and knows her stuff. If you like vegan/ raw food then u will looooooove this.  They make these raw chocolate bars that are the best thing I've ever had... Crazy good!!  Like I didn't know chocolate could taste so good.  I bought a bar. 
Get ur vegan on!!

Love the daily specials menu, which seems to change seasonally. Fried chicken night is worth checking out and I look forward to the day they bring back their deep dish pizza night! Had an awesome burger the other night and I always like getting a salad because they have delicious house made dressings.  Definitely check the $1 day old pastries out at their counter. You never know what amazing baked goods you will score!

I was so excited to hear about a poke place finally coming to Indy! I like their concept of ordering food, it' similar to Qdoba/Subway in that you pick your seafood, veggies, rice, sauces, etc. They have a great selection, and I enjoyed the marinated tuna a bit more than the marinated salmon. I also loved their house sauce mixed with their spicy mayo and eel sauce. I felt that the "small" size was a perfect portion for lunch. I definitely want to come back and try everything, I'm sure it will all be just as good. 

I wish they a soda fountain (they have a fridge with bottled drinks and also kombucha), and that there isn't a whole lot of seating space available for the amount of people that come through. I also think it'd be helpful to have pictures of popular or recommended bowls for people who haven't tried poke before and might feel rushed or overwhelmed by the choices. 

But with that being said, the interior is very clean and modern. I will definitely make a trip back whenever I end up visiting the north side, and I think it'd be awesome if they open more locations (like on the west side) in the future.
Great breakfast, but just went there for lunch and definitely a thumbs down!! Sure knew how to ruin a cheesesteak and ham and cheese wrap.
Erika is the best! Great service:) Food is great! Will come back again! Food is very fresh

These are hands down the best doughnuts I've ever had.  We got these for our clients while we were working in Reno.  We bought at least one of each kind, and all of them were said to be fantastic.  I tried the strawberry frosted, maple bar and coconut doughnut.  They were all totally amazing.  I'm kind of glad I don't live here, because I would get addicted to this place, haha!  Definitely a must try if you're in the area.

Terrible customer service and management. Arrived at 5:00 on a Tuesday night with a party of 8, 4 tables were seated in the entire place, and we were given an hour and a half wait time.. After a discussion on seating and reminding the hostess we had called and were told "we don't accept reservations, but it won't be a problem," we were then told maybe closer to 45-60 minutes. Needless to say we left, and all the while the spineless manager watched as we tried to make things work and let the hostess deal with everything.

Decent pie's and other deserts, but sadly very poor customer service. Ordered pies here for Thanksgiving, also sent an email confirming availability, yet when I called in they said that the pies are unavailable and that no exception can be made because order date has passed. Will have to see if somewhere else has pre-order still open ... disappointing.

I was in town for a business trip and yelped this place. I am glad I did because it is now my new favorite place! I wish they had this restaurant in southern California. The server was friendly and made delicious recommendations. I had the lobster, lasagna, and lemon grass dessert pre fixe menu for $45 and everything was superb! It's well worth the price! Loved it

I had my nails done with dipping powder and a regular pedicure. I was pleased with the way they both turned out as far as the shape of the nails, the colors, and how long they lasted. However, I'm a little concerned about the cleanliness here. The manicurist did my nails over a towel that had clearly been used and there was no liner in the tub for the pedicure.

Darn tootin' good breakfast spot.  Everyone at my table was overwhelmingly satisfied with the homestyle food and the colorful waitstaff.  I had the migas and homemade salsa - it was the best I've had in N.O. by far.  Also ordered the blueberry pancakes (great).  This place is worth visiting for a non-fancy wholesome breakfast with the locals.

Cash only

This is where you take that special someone for an amazing meal! It is on the higher end of the scale as far a pricing goes, but certainly worth every penny (I mean, dollar)

Cam here for a work dinner (so luckily I didn't have to pay the bill). Between the 6 of us, we tried a large portion of the menu.

The outstanding:
pepper crusted steak (from the center of the menu, it's a combo), so juicy and flavorful!
roasted brussel sprouts (from the gotta have it section of the sides).. these are sooo amazing! even the person who doesn't like them, loved them.
twice baked stuffed potato (yes, it's a potato stuffed potato) and it is heavenly and large enough for two people to share.
steak diane (the fancy one where there is fire involved table side)
ice cream (made table side with liquid nitrogen)
lemon tart (it's tangy and perfect end to a rich meal)

The fabulous:
the lamb chops (I didn't taste this, so I cannot describe, but the co-worker was very happy)
shrimp scampi 

the bartender was delightful, and even created a special shooter for our team based on a couple ideas from the boss 

The wait staff was fabulous, she went along with our jokes, and helped us to push on one of the coworkers who couldnt make up his mind.. great teamwork! :)

I would definitely come back any chance I can (and have the extra cash to lay down) for this extravagant dinner.

Really enjoyed the meal here. Started it off with a couple of cocktails, Sazerac and a light pink drink which we enjoyed. Had the Shrimp and Tasso Henican appetizer. It was good, a bit spicy. The Commander's Crisp Romaine Salad was very good. We also had the Praline Lacquered Texas Quail. The glaze and the cabbage was excellent! For dessert we had the pecan pie and the bread pudding souffle. I enjoyed the pecan pie, but disappointed that the crust was extremely hard. My husband enjoyed his souffle. Complained to the waiter about the crust, but all he said was, "Sorry." His name is Sway and was otherwise nice and attentive.

Cozy venue for a spirits distillery and I'm happy to watch their success since opening. They probably now need a longer bar or 2 bars to keep up with the guests i think. The drinks are really good and fancy. The bartenders are friendly and helpful with navigating the menu and your preferences. The menu changes seasonally and decently priced at about $8-$10/drink. There's only 2 restrooms and sometimes it's a little uncomfortable to wait when the need hits. The Fletcher Place/Fountain Square area is fun to explore, especially during the warmer seasons. Whenever we're in the area, we find ourselves stopping at Hotel Tango.

Owned by a young business man by the name of Tj Miller..first impression was young preppy college kids as patrons.The ambiance is fine whisky and bourbon casks in the front foyer and a terrace to eat outside.farther in is agood size bar along the window .The menu is mostly craft beers and several bourbon whiskey and scotch drinks. THEY HAVE SPECIAL RECIPE DRINKS.these are unusual and many people werethere just for those..tThere is a small eating menu tapas appetizers and about10 choices for dinner.upstairs is more dining and a larger area for big groups to dine comfortably.
The place has only been open a week.stillworking out the kinks..but the young owners is very on top of it and trying very hard to give the best dining experience.
Worth a visit..
Different and unique 

I had the big bird.for instance.$12 pickled watermelon. Buttermilk fried chicken breasts on a sweetpotato waffle served with bourbon maple syrup..it is big enought to split (which we did) 

We also had cornbread pudding topped with braised bourbonbbq pulled pork topped with steamed spinach and a couple beerbattered onions..

I've lived in Rofo for over a year now and always made the assumption that this place must be a dump due to it's location. While having drinks one night with some friends in KoP, a few of them had asked if I'd ever been to FQB. They all said it was a great hole in the wall and that I had to try it. So I did the next day. I chose to dine with my friend at the bar rather than the restaurant. Bartender was friendly which always makes me happy. We ordered gumbo as an appetizer which was very, very good. I'm a Southern native with family/friends from Louisiana so I've had my fair share of amazing gumbos, and although this one was "different" in flavor that I'm used to, I was impressed. Only thing, they don't serve crackers or bread with it, which they should. My friend ordered a burger and I ordered the craw fish po-boy with voodoo sauce added on (I like it spicy). I couldn't believe how good both of these dishes were! So flavorful and just simply delicious. How have I passed this place up for so long? The other plus, maybe even the better plus, the craft beers they have on tap were sooo good. I'm quite the craft beer connoisseur and was elated to find beers that are often difficult to find. You can also take these great beers to go. Go here! It's goooood!

If you want Mexican food, DO NOT COME HERE. 
I'd rather have some Taco Bell than eat here again. The food is not good, not authentic Mexican food, and their salsa sucks.

Had a sweetpea and gram gram lunch at Convivo today and sat outside with our beautiful beach views. I live here and it was so nice to walk 10 min away to such a Beautiful Hotel to have lunch. I ordered 2 kids size pizzas to share and they were huge. Boxed up the other pizza to take home. My gram baby had CHOCOLATE GELATO and she loved it !!

A must experience!
I grew up on German food and the sauerbraten will not disappoint !
Had fun listening to the oom pa pa band and drinking , my husband got paddled by the waitress Frau- too funny 
We will be back for more food and fun !

I usually like to budget but it was a friend's night out so I couldn't say "NO" when BJ's was suggested. Lets face it no one likes to be the debby downer. For one entree, and a third of a dessert plus tip I payed $17... Eh... Is all I can say, by no means was the food bad, but for the portion size, and the overall quality I'd rather take my change somewhere else. One thing that they can boast are their pizookies. Those little chocolate chip cookies (which come in varying flavors) serves as a throne that the ice cream sits on and it melts away both in your mouth and on the cookies, and sometimes on your face as you shovel it in as fast as you can. One of my favorite fat kid fixes. For a chain it isn't bad but that's all that there is to be said.

Solid breakfast place. We first went to another spot hard on Yelp reviewed, but there was a line and did nothing sell the standard sandwiches we were expecting! K&a had all the usual listings, with a few twists like the homey moon which replaced the sausage patty for a chorizo patty and had avocado.  I enjoyed the fun mix, and would order again. We got a side of hash browns, and would have preferred they fry them harder. Overall would definitely come back!
Great service...thanks Brandon! Food was delicious.  El Camino margarita on point! Beautiful setting. We will definitely be back.

I was on a time crunch and needed to get my nails done before vacation. I went to TWO different nail salons near by and was turned away because they closed in an hour. I stopped in to Britney Nails and was seated right away. I just needed a gel manicure...nothing fancy. It turned out great and I was in and out in 30 minutes. Despite the other reviews, give this place a try if you're close by! The staff was super friendly and I will be back! Thank you for the great service!
I'm a big fan of this restaurant -- every time I go the food is fresh and tasty!  (It probably helps that my fiancee always orders our food in Mandarin!)

came out on a friday night and were able to get in free with only about a 15 minutes line to get up to the top before 9pm! couldn't get out on the patio part because it was pretty packed due to the cardinals game, but were able to get a good seat over looking the arch! service was right on (and maybe even a little OVER bearing). drink prices were VERY reasonable... coming from chicago, where you're paying upwards of $50 for 4 drinks, this was a breath of fresh air (a round of drinks for a table of 4 cost us about $30!)

Not coming here again! I gave this location three shots and each time I walked away more disappointed. In this instance, I ask if they have nachos (it's not clearly listed on their menu), the young man behind the counter takes a moment from smacking his gum to look up at the menu, he asks an employee walking by where the nachos are.....that employee smacks the bottom of the menu board while saying "here"......hmmmm, not where he so appropriately said they were. Anyway, I l order nachos with asada. A lady called out our number I get the tray, I look her in the eyes and say "thank you", she replied "yeah" after smacking her lips together as if in total disgust that she had to say something to me. 
Carne Asada must be code word for cubes of gelatinous cold fat chunks because that's what I got on my nachos. The cheese wasn't melted, the food was cold. HOW IN THE WORLD DO YOU MESS UP NACHOS!! People PLEASE!! I took a few bites, deemed them not worth the calories and trashed the meal. As I walked out the same gal at the counter was emptying the trash. In an attempt to get her to smile, I said "have a nice night".....she just glared at me. Ok then, crappy service, crappy food=me never coming back! 
I hope the owner reads this, news travels fast around here!

Excellent beef pho soup on Rt 309 near Hatfield pa. Decent prices. I get extra noodles. I will eventually try other items. There are a few other restaurants in this stop mall. They are next to a good Mexican place as well.

We have had a number of positive experiences here. Their covid protocols are very good and we have felt safe dining since. I would recommend lunch over dinner, just to steer clear of busier more crowded times. 

The servers are great, the food is good, and the experience is a ton of fun. 

We don't have much to choose from when it comes to Korean BBQ in Reno, so it's nice to have this solid option available.

I was really excited when this restaurant opened in KOP because I had heard great things about the one in DC. In terms of atmosphere and menu options, this place would still get 4-5 stars. BUT the service has been so atrociously bad (both times that I've eaten here) that I will most likely never go back. 

My first time at Founding Farmers, my table ordered the skillet cornbread as an appetizer and were presented with a different bread basket that arrived at the same time as our entrees arrived. We told the waiter, but we said not to worry about it - and assumed it was a fluke. It wasn't that busy that night, but we had empty water glasses for the majority of the meal. Our table sat for about an hour and probably were checked on twice at most. Still, the food was good so a few weeks later I decided to go back. 

My second time was what really got me. My friend and I had to ask 3 times over the course of 20-25 minutes before we were given water. I ordered the southern chicken and someone brought out the wrong entree. I understand that sometimes it happens but it took about 10 minutes to get our waiter's attention to switch the entree. It stayed on my bill which I felt like wasn't really fair given the circumstance (when my meal finally arrived I ended up taking it home in a box because we were finishing up). My friend and I both ordered a cocktail and although we finished them while waiting for our food, we weren't asked if we wanted a second drink the entire time we were there. I really couldn't believe it was that bad on a Friday night. 

Not sure if it's a combination of being poorly managed, hiring bad or inexperienced waiters, or some sort of disconnect between the kitchen and other staff. Really disappointing experiences for a place with so much potential

Reid park is a nice little zoo that is friendly, clean and pretty easy to navigate. All the "main" attraction animals are easy to get to, and they have great view points. I only took off one star because I wish they'd clean the glass to some of the animal enclosures a bit more. I've been here early once and it was still pretty foggy for the bears/lions. If you're a resident, or visiting during times where they do special events, I would save a trip and come during those. ZooLights, Brew at the Zoo, Summer Safari Nights, etc. Also, check the temperature for the days you come and bring your own water!

Old fashion layout with small frying pan greens and old growth elms lining the fairways. Modest improvements by the city  have returned the course it to some of its previous glory when Ben Hogan shot the course record.  Far from the cheapest muni  in town, $45 w cart in April, overall a challenging and fun course. Four stars because not many munis left within 60 miles of Reno as this is the best in its category. 

Pro tip: When in doubt puts break towards the Peppermill.

I love this place. We went to the Cirque Dreams Holidaze show. This is our second time going to this show. It was amazing once again. We will definitely check it out again next time we are in Nashville!

Affordable Mowers is a reliable and truly affordable place to service your lawn equipment or purchase new.  We recently took our law mower in for general blade sharpening and service. It turns out it requires additional attention. We paid $25 for the service and just $15 for the other needs.

I mowed the lawn today and was pleasantly surprised at how it turned out. I had no idea how sluggish the mower had become all due to a loose drivetrain cable.  She runs like a brand new mower!

If you live or work in St. Pete and need a trustworthy place to service your lawn equipment, I highly recommend Affordable Mowers. They are kind, considerate, and knowledgeable. You can trust their advice.

This was probably the most hole in the wall type restaurant I've ever seen. My partner and I ordered alligator bites for our appetizer, the blackened duck, and the red fish for dinner. The alcoholic cocktails  are served with dry ice making them look awesome and feel consistently cold. There's nothing bad to order here. 

The paintings and hangings around the restaurant made it feel incredibly authentic and filled with culture. Our waitress was helpful in deciphering my hesitancy with fish. She also came several times to check in on us even when we were seated in the corner.

Would highly recommend this place. It's close to Bourbon street without all the noise. After dinner, only you could decide to go right to Bourbon or left to the hotel.

Very nice 5 stars in Clearwater excellent service there .On the return to Elmira Corning in NY two stars lines were very short as was the service. I would give them one star but the ticket agent was so nice and courteous I gave two stars

Went here in October 2008

They have a very knowledgeable waiters here. One thing which stood out was how much the waiter actually knew about Spanish culture. In conversation with him, we found out he had actually spent some time in Spain before working at Tinto. His knowledge lead to some excellent wine pairings and also some much needed explanations of what exactly we were eating.

The food was top notch. This is a restaurant by Jose Garcia, so the tapas style menu was in full swing. We were a group of 4 and we all got the tasting menu selection. This included 5 or 6 courses. The girl I was with happened to be a vegetarian who ate fish. It wasn't a problem to have fish substituted into the chef's tasting selections at no extra cost.
I did include a pork entree in with my selections, and I'm glad I did. It was phenomenal. 

I greedily ordered some squid in black ink after dinner. (not much of a desert guy) I'm sorry I did too. It was sub par, and it looked terrible. 

For the 4 of us, the meal ran a little over $400

pricey , but definitely worth it if your a fan of Jose Garcia
Good pie. Especially like the three for $14 on Friday nights. Would recommend. A little on the expensive side but it's nice for a treat every now and then

My entire experience with Stonebridge was great! It took a little longer than anticipated, due to weather, but it was well worth the wait!! My roof looks amazing! Looks like a whole new house. Working with Fernando, Mike and Simon was fantastic! These guys are a great team!! Simon Carlisle was our project manager and his customer service skills are on point! He was extremely responsive and worked closely with us on every aspect of the job. His team of workers worked fast and hard, even in the 100 degree days that they worked. The roof we had done was on our rental property. If and when we need another roof done, we are most definitely return to do business with these guys!! Thanks Stonebridge  Team

Let me start by saying I love the Taco Bus downtown but this was terrible!  I ordered a chicken quesadilla.  I specifically said I only want rice, cheese and a very small amount of chicken.  What I was served was anything but!  Two of the slices were filled with onions!  YUCK!  Two more were filled with pea, carrots & corn, huh?!  And the other with what looks like leaves!!?  I don't know what's going on here but it's nothing like the DT location.

The indoor/outdoor seating is nice but it's kind of loud inside...

We had an awesome stay here. Very updated for such an old hotel. They had a nice variety of coffee and teas downstairs. We were disappoint by the fact that they only had valet parking, we had to pay each night. Also, when we checked in, they didn't mention that there were bikes for us to use. We would've had a much better experience if we new about those bikes. Overall, it was a good choice. Very close to everything and lots of places to eat.

I've been a loyal member since the early '70's-even keeping my membership when I moved away. But there has been an erosion of customer service over the past few years. First, they got rid of shared branching. This allowed you to use any credit union in Arizona for most of your transactions. I was told that the mobile app replaced all that. Well, with the lobbies closed to all but appointments, I can't use other credit unions that are open. Plus, if I want to avoid an ATM fee, I could go to a credit union and use a teller to withdraw money. Then, TFCU eliminated their drive through tellers and replaced them with virtual tellers. But the virtual tellers didn't replace all the in person tellers. So, with lobbies being closed, long lines are common at drive through lines. Then with the pandemic, they closed the lobbies to all but appointments. That was fine for a while but there is no reason that they can't be reopened with a limited customer presence. With a reduced number of drive through tellers, you have to wait in a long line for service. Walk up ATM's are gone and are now all drive up. I served on the board of a local CU for over 6 years and my email address and name was on the CU's website. There is no information about TFCU's board on the website-even though we elect them. After almost 50 years, I'm about done.

I'm a Jersey Girl at heart, so of course, I love me a good diner. BSD has a nice menu. Fries are delish, so are the wraps. My friend loved her huge omelet. Staff is on top of things most of the time. Fast service. Small parking lot is always nice.
The best massage I've had in Tampa was by Kaita! All of the staff members were very friendly and helpful. Such a great experience!

Beware of fraud. I went in for an inspection and I was aware that one of my tires had a slow leak. During the inspection I was told my brakes needed to be changed soon but the vehicle would pass inspection. I was informed that there was a nail in the tire. I refused the $600 brake job. I was charged for the tire repair. The next day when I did the brakes I noticed the nail was still there. When I called the dealership the receptionist said tech said he had no time to speak to me after I explained my issue. I even sent an email requesting a refund for the tire repair that wasn't performed. I never heard back from anyone.
Had THE BEST TIME here!  That outside garden area is just made for communal enjoyment.

Such a unique and fun venue to play some mini golf! The staff is very friendly and helpful. They're even dressed up in costume to match the monster theme. Decorations in the building are "spooky" without being too scary or weird for little kids. They've got an arcade to play in while you wait to get golfing, as well as prizes you can collect tickets to win. 

The course is made up of 18 holes, each with varying levels of difficulty and obstacle blocking your way. The staff will occasionally call out challenges for you to do while golfing, like swinging the club between your knees (granny style!) or behind your back. Tons of fun! Things usually move along pretty smoothl. There are certain holes that bottleneck up so don't be surprised if you have to wait a little while to get onto the next hole. Luckily the music is awesome so you can jam along while you wait.

The mini golf is also super affordable! I think I paid $25 for 4 games (groupon!). They also hand out free play vouchers to the loser, which is the cherry on top!
The creme Brule was too caramelized on top burned and inside had a too soft texture. I wanted to tell waitress but she was too busy. The establishment should not have even served it.

I went here about 2 weeks ago with a friend for the first time. The staff is really nice and on point. The waiter we had was attentive but with the right dose to not be annoying. The food was made pretty fast and it was so so so good. I felt that the food was slightly over priced. When I got the food I didn't care because it was made to perfection. I ordered The Chicken Tostada for an app. to share. It was fresh and refreshing. I wish I had ordered my own. For dinner, I ordered the fish and chips. They cost more then other restaurants ( Mckenzie Brew House's) in the area but it was just as good and fresh. Whoever makes the food at Black Powder cares about what they are doing. At least that was my experience.

Competent, if mediocre.  Many of the 'hot' items were lukewarm at best.  Flavors were good, but signage had not been changed since the previous night.  Pricing can also be a bit off-putting, so ask before eating.

Lunch buffet for $7.95? Sold!

It was a crappy Saturday afternoon & we wanted something fast but tasty, so when we passed by the restaurant, we made a u-turn & headed in.  The buffet was simple, only six or seven main dishes, but everything was delicious. I love raita, the yogurty lemony sauce that I generously topped on everything. The curry was good, but the standout dishes were the chicken tikka and masala, plus the fragrant rice.

Other than the grumpy staff, we enjoyed the quick meal and will certainly return.

I went to Pizzology for the first time for lunch during the week with coworkers. It wasn't very busy at the time. We sat next to windows so we had a great view of Mass Ave. I'm sure during the summer/weekends it is a fun view.

Most of us ordered the lunch special which consisted of soup or salad and also a personal pizza (of which you could get any of their pizzas). Most of us opted for that; one person ordered a full size pizza (which was enough for 2-3 people, so nice for leftovers for one!). 

I had the salad (chop salad), and it was delicious. The ingredients (gorgonzola, grape tomatoes, olives...) were very fresh, and there was an ample amount of toppings so you didn't get a lot of bites of just plain lettuce.  Others who had the soup also enjoyed it.

I ordered the Baby Cheesus pizza (lol). It was seriously a cheese lover's dream pizza! Local Foxglove cream cheese, parmesan, and mozzarella make for a delicious and uniquely flavored plain cheese pizza. I've seen others complain about burnt crust, but it is like Blaze or any other oven-fired pizza. 

Our lunch took a while, so I guess that was the only downside (although I was not in a hurry to get back to work ;)). 

This isn't the best pizza I've had (Chicago homegirl here!), butI will definitely return when I'm in the Mass Ave. area.

Carol's Sweets is a haven for anyone who has a sweet tooth. It feels like an old-fashioned candy shop with a modern twist. There are candies in glass jars, instead of plastic bulk bins like at the grocery store. They also carry a lot of imported sweets, for those who like something a little different.

They have anything and everything you're sugary taste buds could be craving, from truffles and nougats to jellies and lollipops. I grew up loving Pez candies, and Carol's has loads of them in all sorts of different characters. There are boxed chocolates for quick gifts, but you may want to take some time and try one of their truffles from their counter. It's like a little piece of chocolatey heaven melting in your mouth. Honestly, I think I got goosebumps because it was so sinfully good.

Carol's also has cookies and chocolates at their counter, and right now everything is Halloween-themed. There are treats shaped like cats, pumpkins, bats, and witches. A great idea for party treats, if you're thinking of throwing a Hallow's Eve bash this year.

I was surprised to see they also sell "passion paint", for those feeling a little amorous, I guess. Looks like there's something for both kids and adults in this candy shop!

Spent a long weekend here with the family. The 1 bedroom suite we booked was huge with 3 LCD TVs. We didn't watch much TV though. Hung out at the pool a lot. The separate kids' pool is fantastic with little toddler/baby areas too. Service was exceptional. All around this was the nicest Ritz to which we've been (Orlando, St Louis, Maui).

They accept medicaid (it's hard to find that info without just calling around so i hope that helps someone). The wait was a bit long, but there were a lot of patients (maybe fitting people in same day). There's a little area for kids to play which was very helpful. The staff was very nice. I saw Dr. Cecola, he was very attentive and took his time with me. My problem was something that was involving the inner ear, something that he couldn't help me with. However he was familiar with my condition because of a seminar he attended. He gave me information on it and a website I could use to find more about it. He was probably the most helpful doctor I've seen about it. I'd definitely go back if I needed to.
2nd time there..it's great.  easy take-out option is a plus

Went to We Dat in New Orleans Convention Center during Essence Fest and the wings were very very small. $10 .00 for 6 wings that looked like they killed the birds in City Park. I have brought wings from We Dat several times and never that small. They are wrong for treating visitors like that and only serving wings no fries or bread. I will not purchase from them again. What a rip off. Shame Shame Shame

I had a huge issue with customer service at this restaurant and asked to speak to a manager. I was told by the employees that there were no managers in the restaurant at the time. Are you serious? At 7 pm on a Thursday there is not a single manager/owner/anybody of authority in the building? Either they were lying or there is a serious leadership issue here. I tried calling back the next day and was told the same thing. I left my contact info and asked for a manager to call me and never got a call back. The food is decent enough, but obviously the owner/managers don't care about guest complaints. I will be eating sushi elsewhere in Tucson since they obviously don't want my business.

We love Caddy's!  We have been several times and the food and atmosphere is the best on the beach.  Although we have had multiple servers, Amy by far is our fave!  She takes her time, gives us a lot of great suggestions and was the ONLY server to tell you about the REWARDS program after we went over seven times!  So do yourself a favor, make a reservation and request outdoor seating and ask for Amy.

I love this place! I have been here twice, once with a bachelorette party and once by myself, and the service is always incredible! The staff are so friendly and the amenities for what you pay to have even one service done is so worth the price. I recommend taking advantage of the pool and saunas, and make an afternoon of it by ordering some food from the restaurant downstairs while you lounge. I wish I lived in Indy so I could come more often!

Oh! They got pretzels, a hobby shop with Gunpla and Transformers, a GameStop that had Gundam capsule toys,  and plenty of custom t shirts I could pay and print out for my own sake. I wish they had a Yoshinoya with all the items though, then I'd go there nonstop.

I Hope You Like Waiting!! We went on Yelp list from home at 5:00, which said it was a 2 hour wait. The Yelp wait list told us to arrive at the restaurant by 6:30. We got there at 6:25 and were told we'd be seated in about 20 minutes. Turns out we were seated at 8:20!!! Almost 2 hours after we arrived, after being told repeatedly it wouldn't be much longer. We would have never ever waited had we known. EVER! I really can't stand people who have no respect for your time. The staff we interacted with certainly had no regard, I'm sure could have cared less. Perhaps the incredible increase in prices they had in the last week will help reduce the crowds. Decent food but nothing is close to worth that wait, especially when you were deceived

Food and drinks are good.  We had a party of 5 and we all enjoyed the appetizers and dinners.  Not sure I would agree with the negative reviews.  We've eaten here several times over the yrs and was never disappointed.

There is nothing I dislike about this place except that it is a chain owned by an evil corporation.  

Setting that aside, the food is fresh and delicious and, more importantly, seasonal.  It's really my kind of food -- very simple, yet creative and tasty.  

We sat at the bar to avoid waiting for a table.  The bartender was very sweet and attentive and, even better than that, extremely knowledgeable about wine.  She helped us choose a red based on the meals we planned on ordering.  She was spot on.  

The atmosphere is very inviting, too -- very chic and elegant, but not pretentious or uncomfortable.  

Love this place!

I love the layout of this airport. The way that the terminals are organized just feels very natural, like I would walk to the right place even without signs - if that makes sense. There's also a decent amount of interesting restaurants to try. I would encourage them to consider restaurants that have more plant based options but regardless of that, this is one of my favorite airports to fly into!

Great tempura shrimp and delicious cocktails. They were slammed so service was on the slower side and the bathroom situation is downstairs and shared with other businesses so not great for the older folks I went with.

This place is the perfect little spot! It really gives me true Japanese flavors and vibes. As soon as you walk in you have to place your order like most Japanese restaurants which I loved! It reminded me of when I visited japan this year. Everything from the sake to the coconut shrimp roll was very delicious. I will be coming back !

Recommended by Javier at the local Marriot hotel. A great call by Javier!  Attentive service, excellent Margaritas expertly blended and delicious food.  The salsa cart was absolutely muy bien!  I have never seen this in the US or Mexico or anywhere else in the Americas. Fantastico!
Very good food and drinks. We enjoyed the music. Very fast and friendly service. We would definitely come back.

If you don't have reservations, don't bother. The food isn't worth more than about a 15 min. wait. With no reservation, we  were first told that it would be an hour but then we were told it would be about 45 mins. We waited over an hour and a half and the best thing that I can say about this place is the bartender Myles was terrific! The customer service at the front was terrible. It was extremely evident that they weren't used to handling large crowds. I was ready to go someplace else except I was in the minority and the restaurant came highly recommended. Once we were seated and our food came I was disappointed. It was very average. I couldn't believe that we had waited so long for average food (shame on us!). The next night we went to Oceana's where again there was a wait. Only you could tell they were used to dealing with large crowds and waiting customers. AND the food there was so much better!

Delicious! My wife, son and I are in St Louis for vacation from Rochester, NY. When traveling I love looking for local breakfast spots. This place was perfect. Quaint, in a cool looking area, great coffee and delicious food. I got a slinger. It's a St. Louis specialty that many restaurants offer but this was great because it came with sausage patties instead of burgers. Easily reminds me of a garbage plate that I get home. Staff was so friendly to us (especially with a 17 month old fussy boy). Will definitely be back next time we're in town.
Awesome instructors and facility.  Highly recommended!  Perfect for families from children all the way up to adults!

Chilaquiles Verde! Who turns down pulled pork for brunch? Not me. I would compare this to maybe a nacho style meal and I loved it. The veggies, poached egg, and a little crunch made for the perfect meal. I never think to explore brunch options, but on this Saturday I'm glad I found a new spot to visit in Indy. Shoofly isn't in the most popular area of Indy, but it is the up and coming area! My friend also ordered the biscuits and gravy. Seriously, some of the best biscuits around!! 

Great spot for Saturday brunch!

I was in need of a parking space for the coming school year and Lydia in the office went above and beyond helping me have a secure place to park every day.  She tried to find a spot for me that someone else didn't need and when that didn't work she had the trash dumpster moved to create a parking spot.  Lydia is kind and attentive to us, she always responds right away to our emails and finds solutions.  Want to feel like you are more than a rent check?  Move to a Sierra Property and try to get Lydia as your property manager!

Love this place! Beer and Food is amazing! Employees are always happy and very helpful! You can tell that they really love their job! You can make reservations, which I would suggest, otherwise you do have over an hour wait. Its also a bit on the pricey side, but much worth it on date night :)

This is easily my favorite liquor store in Indianapolis.  Rather than giving a rambling review that will end up being more of an ode to how much I love booze than how much I love Kahns, here's the breakdown:

Beer

- In the coolers they have a large selection of local micro-brews, and also micro-brews from all over the country.  You can always find a six pack of something you haven't tried yet that is cold and ready to drink.  They also have a cooler of single bottles so you can get a little of this and a little of that.  There is a very nice cider selection as well.  They carry Strongbow cans, Samuel Smith, Original Sin, and at least five other varieties that I can't think of at the moment.  They have a pear cider that is above the $20 range that I REALLY want to try.

- Out of the coolers, they have a large selection of single bottles arranged by country.  They also keep a row of sale beer in the middle of an aisle.


Wine

- HUGE wine selection.  If you want it, they likely have it.  They have one of those fancy wine rooms full of bottles I cannot afford.  Don't mind the face print on the door looking in...

- Sale wines are often really good deals, keep an eye out!
- If you have piles of money and want to turn that money into cases of wine, Kahn's has a big wine cellar and will happily store it for you.


Liquor

- Very nice liquor selection, prices a bit better than what I would find shopping at my local Meijer.  

- The best part of the liquor is tasting bar.  Yes, that's right.  The liquor tasting bar.  I once left here a bit buzzed because one of the employees went on a mission to find a gin that I would like.  As gin is one of the vilest substances on earth, that was a tough task, but he did eventually find one that didn't taste quite as much like ass as all of the others.  Anyhow, when venturing into the realm of new, expensive liquor, it's really nice to be able to sample it before shelling out the cash.

The employees have always been very helpful whenever we've gone in, going out of their way to answer questions.  In general, I've found the prices here to be comparable to other liquor stores, though they do have some really good sales. They also have a room full of stuff you need for drinking - glasses, wine screws, decanters, etc.  They sell a small selection of fancy cheese, but you'd be better off going to Trader Joes or Whole Foods for that.

Small but to the point. I was very pleasantly surprised by the selection within the store. They cater not only to the runners out there but they also carry triathlon equipment, sports nutrition supplements, other knick knacks for sports enthusiasts, and more! I was psyched to find that they carry a wide range of Tifosi sunglasses which I have been searching high and low for. Very cool store (with recognition from Runners World to boot!) and the staff were friendly and helpful.

This place is awesome! GREAT food! GREAT service!

It's only been open for 3 days, and they already have it down! Had beers, nachos, and street tacos. Everything was fabulous! Our server, Adam, was friendly and fast.

We have enjoyed the State Street location for years, but this is much closer for us. We will be back over and over! Thank you for a great afternoon! Great job!
The food was excellent.  There was so many great vegetarian options that  it was hard to chose. The restaurant got terribly busy and they were unfortunately short staffed but well worth the wait

In my neck of the woods a sudden yearning for  take out sushi used to result in a stop at the grocery store for an uninspired version of the delicacy.  Then I saw Yoshi Cafe, nestled next to a chinese place and a dry cleaner.  When my latest craving hit, I took a chance.  Walking in, I  discovered a bland and austere interior, but thank goodness I didn't let atmosphere be the final word.

 The sushi itself was very good and, at times, absolutely delicious.   The rice was perfectly cooked and seasoned. The fish fresh, the tempura crunchy but not greasy. I don't go for a lot of the heavily sauced sushi, so this review is limited to the basic masago/ unagi/ spicy tuna variety sushi which were all delicious and traditionally prepared.  I did step outside the box and order their Outback roll with grilled beef and scallions.  It  was delightfully tasty and I would order it again, especially for those in my world who don't do sashimi.

 They are a tad on the pricey side, but it's a price I gladly pay for fresh, tasty sushi.  I'll be back.
Pizza was so burnt and hard. Definitely won't order from here again. No comparison to NY style at all.
Best guacamole and best fajitas around.  Salsa is always fresh.  Great margaritas and great service.

My biggest complaint is that the food as drink here are pricy. BUT everything you will eat and drink will be delightfully tasty.

Definitely have some of their sangria. Our table split both a red and a white sangria and they are both great tasting and well made.

My favorite of the tapas was the empanadas-- YUM -- followed by the steak one we had (do not recall its name from the menu). The tortilla espanola was decent though nothing to write home about.
What a cute spot! We came here for wine tasting before dinner reservations but will be back to try the food because the ambiance was awesome and so was the service.

This event is growing and getting better for sure.  It is now much easier to get beverages and food to enjoy while spending time here.  There are lots of warm alcoholic beverage options that are great considering it is an outside event in December.  This is a German event, so pretzels, sausages, and German pastries are the main selection.  We did not try any on this visit, but they smelled amazing!

There are still a few things to improve on for this event.  1. The mud.  Half of the event is rubber mats on soil and today everything was covered in mud.  2. Sitting area.  There are only about 30 stands.  So if you are not skating, you can easily be in and out of this event in 30 minutes.  If there was a sitting area to enjoy the drinks and food, it may make it more of a destination event.  3. Some cheaper vendor options.  Most of the vendors are selling handmade German items.  Which is really cool to see, but unless you are coming prepared to spend $100 on a stein or nutcracker, you don't have many shopping options.

Incredible, awesome, fresh, hit the spot seafood. Yes, this place is the secret. 

Hidden in an unassuming strip mall you would miss it if your passenger isn't looking as you drive by. The store is very small with limited and uncomfortable seating. Its a quick lunch in and out or best for take out. That is what might be a deterrent if you didn't feel venturous. It so worth sitting on a bed of spikes to eat here.

The menu is limited but very specialize with the best of what this team has to offer. Crab Cakes is where it is at, but dont stop there. Everything is mostly a la carte. We ordered two orders of crab cakes, shrimp, and hand cut fries with two drinks came to $43, not bad when most fancy places charge you twice that, and dont taste as good as this place.

We sat outside at one of the aluminum table and chairs. First came out the shrimp on paper plate. They were beautiful, plump, in all of its shrimpy glory. They were cooked perfectly. The sweet shrimp with garlic, butter, and herbs (6 to the plate) were the perfect starter. Following were two plates with a baseball size crab cake (not cakes as advertised, more later). They were golden brown and smelled like heaven. We also received our old bay hand cut fries and they were also golden with a dusting of old bay. 

The flavors were amazing. The shrimp was scampi style and so sweet, perfectly cooked you couldn't ask for better. The crab cake was 99% crab with just enough filler to keep it together. It was sweet with flavor and smell of the ocean. A flavor and smell you will not soon forget. Finally dont forget the hand cut fries, perfectly cooked, crispy outside soft and buttery inside. They do not need anything. The food was awesome and I cannot wait to eat here again. 

There are a few things that most people would question why. Seating choice is made for one cheek seating. Almost thought about sitting in my car. Paper plates were the cheapest Ive seen, almost see through. Crab Cakes implies more than one. I am however ok with one big one as it really was two in size together. This is minor stuff which you will bypass once you taste the food. They are working hard to make the best they know how,  so maybe overtime some comforts and presentations will change with feedback. 

94% chance you will enjoy your meal. Recommend the cakes, shrimp, and fries. Oh and I cant wait to try the roast beef looks incredible.

I absolutely love the Reno area with all of it's parks and trails and Galena Creek Park is one of Reno's highlights. A hidden gem,   Perfect day, warm weather, well defined trails, wildflowers in bloom and rushing water.  There are trails fro everyone - for the strollers and for those who want more of a challenge with higher elevations and more incline.  Great place for a picnic.  Lots of picnic tables and places for bbqs.  The blue jays were in full force, along with the squirrels.  Fishing at Marilyn's Pond was great - you can get trout there.  People fly fish there and also fish from the bank.  I think the limit is 5 or 6.  Check the signs and check with your nearest game warden.  I also love manzanita bushes and there is plenty of it there.  It is endangered in California so I would just look and not touch in any of our NV forests.   I love the fact that  Galena Creek Park is right up Mt Rose Highway in South Reno  and easily accessible if you want a piece of nature and fresh air, a break from the biggest little city.

I had lunch with a client in Ybor City this afternoon and we chose to meet at Bernini's on 7th Avenue & 17th Street. It had been a while since I had eaten at Bernini's, and I had honestly forgotten about their excellent Italian food.

The restaurant sits right on the corner of 7th & 17th, and there is plenty of on-street and metered spaces to be found during the lunch hour. At night I usually park in the nearby garage.The Baroque-style of architecture and the artwork of Giovanni Lorenzo Bernini add a real touch of class to this establishment. Bernini's is a very elegant stop along the main street of Ybor, and the locals flock there for an excellent Italian meal. 

We asked to sit upstairs this afternoon since it was a bit chilly, and there was a breeze blowing through the open front door. Normally in our Florida weather the cool breeze would have been welcomed, but I certainly appreciated being able to sit upstairs along the windows and watch the people scurrying by below us. The pace of service was just right with our waiter offering several menu suggestions, and then letting us relax and have our business discussions without too much interruption.

My client ordered a delicious-looking Greco Roman wood-fired pizza which was made with green & Kalamata olives, red peppers, capers, grape tomatoes, Feta cheese, Mozzarella & plum tomato sauce. It looked great, and he seemed very happy with his choice. He boxed up some to take back to the office, but later confessed that he had a "late day snack".

I ordered the Cigar City Chop salad (affectionately known as "Bunny Chow") and this delicious leafy-green treat contained provolone cheese, marinated artichoke hearts, Romaine lettuce, shaved carrots, toasted almonds, artfully-sliced pieces of salami, red onions, asparagus and grape tomatoes with a drizzle of Italian vinaigrette. The salad was quite large, and I thoroughly enjoyed eating something a bit lighter for lunch.

The lunch menu has lots of Bruschettas, pizzas, salads, sandwiches, pastas, risottos, and full-on entrées for very affordable prices. The dinner menu has many of the same selections, but the entrées are meant to be enjoyed with an excellent glass or two of wine and some lovely company. (Great spot for date night just in case you need a recommendation.)

Don't think I'll be back here. We were lured in by $10 lunch specials, and were swindled into an $18 single meal. 

To start: food wise, the coconut shrimp was mostly just fried shrimp with next to zero coconut flavoring. Sushi roll was just okay...I've gotten better spicy tuna's for $3-4 less. 

What really left a bad taste in my mouth was the service. From one server to another: it is completely unethical for wait staff to nonchalantly offer different sides that are not included in the price without mentioning an upcharge. Granted, it makes sense that lobster mac & cheese would have been a few bucks extra...but would we have signed up for it knowing it would tack an extra $8 to our bill? Absolutely not. Though, I should have known after inquiring about the sushi and the server reporting that his favorite rolls were the two most expensive on the menu...talk about transparent. 

I digress. The correct and honest way to go about upcharging a table would go something like "the lobster mac is going to be an upcharge, is that okay?" It was simply a tacky move. I will say the lobster in the mac & cheese was delicious...and it better be for an itty bitty $8 side. 

Sneaky upcharging aside, the service was just plain slow from the get-go. Not surprised that it was nearly empty around prime lunch time. At an $18 lunch meal, I could have hopped across the sidewalk to Rocco's Tacos or Brio for a full on meal with better service. We'll know better for next time!

I had no idea where I was going when my friend decided we were eating here. She was in the mood for Pho and this was listed as open and good. So she drives us and imagine my surprise when we pull up to the old Bob Evans near where my childhood home was. It's not a Bob Evans anymore!

The food at Saigon was great. I ordered Pad Thai and she got her Pho. Both were well prepared and the ingredients were flavorful and filling.

The decor is a little odd as it's fairly obvious inside that you're in the remnants of an old American eatery. Maybe a Big Boys or IHOP, but something along those lines. But I would't say that really put me off the place. The waitstaff were friendly and attentive and the food was good.

Just had Shane tint the windows in my home, what a difference!!! Why there are not more reviews on this shop is beyond me. Shane was such a professional and came well prepared for the job, he brought his own drop cloth to protect my floors along with his own trash bags for all of his waste. The poor guy worked until 8:30 PM to make sure everything was perfect, I'm very happy with his service as well as the price, great value for the money.

Mike was our guide and he was awesome! My mother and I were joined by one other young couple for an all day adventure. We visited 4 wineries at a very leisurely pace- we could have stayed out all day if Mike didn't kindly reign us back in! The wineries were very well chosen and Mike's knowledge about everything in the area was so helpful and interesting. He had a great soundtrack too. The open top Hummer was great! We went on a weekday and lunch was included- we found it to be a great value for the money considering we were driven around all day. Yes the tastings don't cost all that much compared to the total price of the tour, but it's worth it to have someone drive you around with your hair blowin in the wind while picking up some knowledge about the area. We got along so well with the other couple on out trip we all went to dinner after. We also made the reservation very last minute, a day or two in advance, and they were happy to accommodate us. Highly recommend!!!!! Especially the Carhartt tasting room- so cute!!
As far as alcohol, they serve House Frozen Margaritas, a few Beers on tap & Bottled Beer. Enjoy.

I frequent Brown Sugar too many times to count. I usually get Cassave Pone, Currants Roll, Roti, Doubles, or Patties. The roti is great and I've had the freshest Roti on a Saturday morning. The doubles bara is a hit or miss sometimes but generally very good.

The actual restaurant is a bit small with the bakery counter and juice bar taking up much of the space.  There are two booths that you can sit at if you want to eat your food there. Then there are two windows. You order from the window on the right and pay at the window on left.

The roti prices are really good considering the amount of work that goes into preparing a roti. I highly recommend this place for take out roti or a quick snack on some doubles or patties.

Oh and they have a Mauby and Sorrel fountain.

Call ahead to order on busy days!
Excellent food, and the service is phenomenal. Definitely an awesome place for Sunday Brunch!!

We were here at the same time as the st. Patty pub crawl and the service was still excellent. We didn't have to wait long on drinks or food. Our server was personable even though she was extremely busy. Glad to stopped here for a beer!

We stayed here for our trip to NOLA. The decor is very modern and beautiful. The room was obviously a little small, but that is fine with me. The staff were pretty helpful and kind. The bedding wasn't very clean. I found a person's snot on my bedding. My sister found a lot of hairs in her bedding. We asked them to change the bedding, but she still had hairs in her bed. Along with booking the hotel room, they gave us complimentary 3 drink from the bar and 2 tickets for a Jazz Brunch at The Court of Two Sisters. The parking across the street was pretty expensive. We ended parking in another parking lot a few blocks away for a much cheaper price. The pool is amazing at night! I loved coming down there to soak my feet after a long day of walking and bar hopping. It is also so close to bourbon street! You can't hear any of the noise, but you can easily walk down there at night. You can easily walk down to the French Market as well during the day. I would really recommend this hotel.

FYI: We paid about $200 a night.

Aside from a nice neighborhood, what's then point of having a hot tub when it's cold as heck after 40 minutes of warming up...also a little frustrated with the one working treadmill, a squeaky secondary treadmill, and a dysfunctional elliptical.  Aside from that, all is well.
Had a great meal with my kids...great selection and quick service. Food tastes authentic and generous. Must try!

This place has the best macaroons in Philly! I highly recommend trying any of their macaroon flavors. Their other pastries and deserts are delicious too! I gave them 4/5 because service can be a little too slow and disorganized. I've been going here for years, and have seen more and more people crowding the small cafe. Time for another location!

Let me start by saying that I love going to the Aces games!!  Great fun. 

However.....  the last two nights the women's room by section 116 was woefully under stocked. That is just nasty. Two nights in a row zero soap in ANY dispenser. Two nights in a row no toilet paper in many stalls. Two nights in a row no toilet seat covers in many stalls. 

Come on folks!!!  These are public health issues I'm talking about here!  Clean up your act, please.

Had a horrible experience here.  I wanted to buy my boyfriend a couple of cigars for Christmas. As I do not know much about cigars, I was hoping to get some information and recommendations for good cigars. The sales girl could not even come out from the behind the counter to help me. She would just point and say, "Yeah, those ones are pretty popular." My boyfriend smoked the cigars and said that they were not even that good.  Will definitely not be returning to this establishment.

I consider myself a chocolate connoisseur and happened onto The Candy Jar after visiting another large area candy store, waiting in line and finding out they no longer made dark chocolate covered sugar free pretzels. The Candy Jar is a chocolate experience you don't want to miss. The Candy Shop is the essence of why you should support local small businesses. The proprietor greets you as you walk in asking you what kind of chocolate you want to sample while you wait. She has a passion and enthusiasm for chocolate and for her customers. She sells all her chocolate with a money back guarantee. I don't think she'll have to worry about returns. She also offered to make my husband any kind of chocolate in a sugar free version. I'm sure we'll be visiting there often.

Heard a lot of great thing about this place and it really lived up to everything! 

We called ahead to get our pie for pick up. I would recommend this if you're just looking to experience the pizza! It only took about 20 minutes!
