For to go orders you go to a small shop where you can order slices or whole pies. There is parking in the back but you have to pay (another reason why pick up is great, one person can stay in the car and another go in!) 

It was pretty packed in the shop when we picked up on a Saturday evening. The service was quick and really friendly as well! 

As for the pizza...I can barely explain it, it's just SO good! When I try to isolate the parts...the crust is pretty mediocre and the toppings aren't anything special the pizza is soooo above average! It maybe is the sauce?!? Really not sure but it's working!!! 

It's also pretty fairly priced about the average price for pizza!

I have been coming here for a long time and each time I am treated with warm welcomes and friendly smiles. Rosa and Dr. Lin are very good at what they do and show great patience when explaining things or beginning their work. If you have concerns or just need a regular cleaning this is a great place to go.

I'm a regular here and I was really disappointed with the service today. I ordered a Greek salad with chicken after waiting over an hour for my salad and being told by the server that my food was being plated I had enough and left. He also tried finding a manager but the manager wasn't around.

I attended the Nero show over the weekend, and the bartender pretty much killed my night with her rude attitude, and complete lack of bar-tending knowledge.  i wish i got her name, but i was too upset to want to deal her. 

Don't get me wrong, the venue is amazing, the sound was great and the food was pretty good for a concert hall.   But... when it comes to the drinks, the place was a complete rip off.  I order a double jack and coke- yes, a DOUBLE jack and coke- because the main act was coming on and i didn't want to have make several trips to the mini bars located upstairs.   I payed $14.50 for this drink; which is reasonable for the order.   What I got was a piss-ant 3 second pour of some watered down Jack Daniels.  The female bartender, located by the bathroom upstairs, who made the drink claimed that she gave me a double jack, but i couldn't even taste the jack.  I asked for a little more seeing as i paid FIFTEEN DOLLARS for the drink but the bartender claimed she gave me a double pour. I bar-tend in DC, and I don't know how things work in Philly, but a 3 second pour is barely even a shot.   If she is on your pay roll as a bartender, you should demote her right away and make her a dishwasher.     

I don't know if this is a company policy to short the living hell out guests or not, but please take into consideration that this can ruin peoples nights, and certainly keep them from coming back.    Besides the disgraceful bartender, the venue would get 5 stars.

I visited the Quest Diagnostics on Nightwalker Road in Brooksville, Florida. I have had plenty of experience in going to be poked, but this occasion was the most negative I have experienced in quite awhile. I walked in. Instead of a friendly face to greet me, there was an impersonal computer that did not work as expected. It looked like (and so I was told it was)an Apple, but the screen was temperamental. After a lot of frustration, I finally finished with this joke of a computer and sat down to wait. Shortly after I began waiting, a very elderly lady came in and had no clue how to use the computer. I felt sorry for her and helped her through the process. She got registered, but I couldn't help but wonder how others like her managed. No one in Quest was there to help those who needed it. What a joke of a business. 

When I finally got called back, the place looked dirty and the employees seemed disorganized. I just hope the samples I left will be correctly read and distributed. I WILL NOT go back to this facility. If you need to go to a lab, I would find another company. In the meantime, no more tests for me through this lab!!

I really like Sofra. My last visit there was exceptional. My boyfriend and I went for our anniversary. We ordered the Sultan's four-course meal for two. It was incredible and a great way to sample the menu if you're not familiar with Turkish fare. It started with stuffed grape leaves, a duo of dips with Sofra's delicious handmade pita bread, then a platter of meats, bulgar and fresh greens. The dessert is perhaps the most amazing Baklava I've tried, and it was the perfect finish to the meal. 

The chef and owner of Sofra is Turkish and went back to his homeland to be trained as a chef. The food is always perfectly cooked and perfectly seasoned. If you go, be sure to try their pita and dips, washed down with a Turkish beer. Incredible.

I've always had great service, and the restaurant is unique and cozy. I highly recommend Sofra.
Best bubble tea in philly china town!!! nice staff and nice place to chill, hang out with friends.  great drinks for the great price.

St. Louis as a city I found....uh, how do you say....shitty. 

Nonetheless, the Saint Louis Sculpture Park did not share in the St. Louis essence. 

The sculpture park was huge, and quite impressive. The park had many different trails, which all seemed to lead to a different style. 

The huge clump of deciduous trees found everywhere make this place best visited in the Fall.  

If you are in St. Louis, well, then I'm sorry...but, hey! Go here! 

This place is free and awesome. Impress your significant other: take them to a park AND to an art museum.

oooooooo
